full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, MADRAS                                                                

                                            



IIT Madras is one of the oldest and popular IITs in India. It is the third IIT to be set up by the Government of India. It was established in the year 1959. It's 2021 year NIRF Ranking was 01.

Location- 



Address- Chennai/ Madras, Tamilnadu 600036



Campus map link- https://maps.app.goo.gl/d3TK8kA3MGXnz42cA



Campus size- 617 acres

Campus facilities-



Library- 

The campus has a Central library for the students.





Guest house- 

Within the Institute's grounds, there are two guest houses.

                



OAT- 

The campus also has one open air theater.

    



Place for worship- 

There are three temples on campus for worship and there is a prayer hall for Islamic Students.

     



Halls and sports- 

Auditorium halls and basketball court, marathon ground, swimming pool etc. are also there.













Mess and canteen- 

There is 6+ canteens and 3 North Indian mess and 1 food court (north+south)







 Extra curricular activities-



IIT Madras has a wide range of extra curricular activities categorized majorly under Cultural Activities, Sports and Technical Activities. 



These activities are conducted by the students in college campus, most of the students are passionate about it, through the different clubs, activities, camps and competitions. students learn a lot from these. 



On campus, there are several regional groups that provide cultural events, get-togethers, and dinners throughout the year. Not only students, but also academics and staff, are members of the regional club. They're a terrific way to meet new people and avoid homesickness in the first year, so keep an eye out for notices on campus publicizing their events, especially for freshmen. 



The following are some of the regional clubs on campus:



Telugu Samskrita Samiti

SADINER (Student Association for the Development of India’s North Eastern Regions)

Kerala Kala Samiti

Hindi Mitra Mandal

SADINER (Student Association for the Development of India’s North Eastern Regions)

Kannada Sangha

Marathi Mitra Mandal

IIT Indore is a residential institute that offers students the option of staying in a hostel.
The 'Hall of Residence' consists of five separate hostel buildings named after famous people. C.V. Raman, Vikram Sarabhai, A.P.J Abdul Kalam, Devi Ahilya, and Homi Jehangir Bhabha are the residents of the C.V. Library:
At the moment, the IITI library has a massive collection of 35,583 books. The library houses over 575 theses and dissertations. The IIT Indore library also distributes daily print newspapers in Hindi and English to its patrons.

Sports:
In comparison to other IITs, IIT Indore has excellent sports facilities.
Swimming pools, football, hockey, and cricket fields, as well as tennis, basketball, squash, and volleyball courts, are all available at the institute
           
            

5. Extracurricular activities-

There are numerous clubs to join at IIT Indore. They provide a lot of extracurricular activities as well as opportunities. Some of the famous clubs are given below::

          

Programming Club

IIT Indore's Programming Club is for individuals who are purely interested in programming. This is the spot for folks that are passionate about programming and think that codes can do amazing things. The Club promotes active engagement in a variety of programming contests, seminars, lectures, workshops, etc. The news releases, software upgrades, web services, and other pertinent technologies in the field of computer science and technology are also covered by this club.

Electronics Club

IIT Indore's Electronics club is made up of a variety of electronics enthusiasts. It meets the demand for a platform that can introduce people to the endlessly intriguing subject of electronics. Our goal is to foster an environment that is appropriate for this subject and help students bridge the theoretical and practical divide. We hold competitions, workshops, lecture series, and seminars with this in mind. Along with theory lessons, we provide students the opportunity to get their hands dirty and discover how the ideas actually operate. This encourages the students to come up with their own inventions and new wonders. We take on projects that are exciting and represent the newest developments in the electronics sectors.

Robotics Club

For everybody who desires to put their innovative concepts into practise and dares to dream of ingraining them in robots. They may stay up to speed on all the IITI robotics competitions and events by visiting this website.

INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, INDORE



                                 



The Government of India founded the Indian Institute of Technology Indore in Madhya Pradesh in 2009.

It is one of the eight new IITs established by the Indian government's Ministry of Human Resource Development (India).





Important information related to IIT Indore -



 Location- 



Address- Khandwa Rd, Simrol, Madhya Pradesh 453552



 Campus map link- 

https://maps.app.goo.gl/1v7HVBNfbbZrh3jPA





 Campus size- 501 acres (approx)





Reaching IIT Jodhpur-

The journey from Indore Railway Station to IIT Indore takes 24 minutes. The distance between Indore Railway Station and IIT Indore is around 20 kilometers.

 IIT Indore is a residential institute that offers students the option of staying in a hostel.

The 'Hall of Residence' consists of five separate hostel buildings named after famous people. C.V. Raman, Vikram Sarabhai, A.P.J Abdul Kalam, Devi Ahilya, and Homi Jehangir Bhabha are the residents of the C.V.  3. Campus facilities- 



Library-



At the moment, the IITI library has a massive collection of 35,583 books. The library houses over 575 theses and dissertations. The IIT Indore library also distributes daily print newspapers in Hindi and English to its patrons.



 



Cafeteria- 



On the IIT Indore campus, there are seven distinct eateries. They include cafés, restaurants, dining facilities, and other establishments that serve a variety of dishes, drinks, beverages, desserts, and other items.





             



ATMs-



Three ATMs are located on campus. HDFC Bank has a branch in the school building, SBI has a branch in the workshop building, and Canara Bank has a branch. All ATMs are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.







Gym - 



 All ATMs are open 24 hours a day, seven days a week.







Auditorium-



There are two auditoriums at the Institute, each with an amphitheater. The Auditorium-1 (old auditorium) seats 300 people, whereas the Auditorium-2 (new auditorium) seats 800 people. 







Sports complex-



In comparison to other IITs, IIT Indore has excellent sports facilities.

Swimming pools, football, hockey, and cricket fields, as well as tennis, basketball, squash, and volleyball courts, are all available at the institute

           

            





5. Extracurricular activities-



There are numerous clubs to join at IIT Indore. They provide a lot of extracurricular activities as well as opportunities. Some of the famous clubs are given below::



          



Programming Club



IIT Indore's Programming Club is for individuals who are purely interested in programming. This is the spot for folks that are passionate about programming and think that codes can do amazing things. The Club promotes active engagement in a variety of programming contests, seminars, lectures, workshops, etc. The news releases, software upgrades, web services, and other pertinent technologies in the field of computer science and technology are also covered by this club.



Electronics Club



IIT Indore's Electronics club is made up of a variety of electronics enthusiasts. It meets the demand for a platform that can introduce people to the endlessly intriguing subject of electronics. Our goal is to foster an environment that is appropriate for this subject and help students bridge the theoretical and practical divide. We hold competitions, workshops, lecture series, and seminars with this in mind. Along with theory lessons, we provide students the opportunity to get their hands dirty and discover how the ideas actually operate. This encourages the students to come up with their own inventions and new wonders. We take on projects that are exciting and represent the newest developments in the electronics sectors.



Robotics Club





For everybody who desires to put their innovative concepts into practise and dares to dream of ingraining them in robots. They may stay up to speed on all the IITI robotics competitions and events by visiting this website.

campus_facilities:
The campus is stunning, with plenty of vegetation. India's second-largest campus

Internet –

The internet facilities are fine, but they will be even better now that all filters have been eliminated and there is no limit on how much time can be spent on the internet. My room has a download speed of up to 10.5 megabits per second. (The current limit is 4GB/day).

Library:
A magnificent new two-story library with air conditioning and highly comfortable reading rooms has been built.

You can also access the BHU library, which is one of India's largest libraries.

Laboratories –

 The labs are well-equipped and are constantly being improved.

Sports:
Basketball, tennis, and swimming are just a few of the sports that are available.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, VARANASI  (BHU)

                         



IIT BHU is a relatively new addition to the IIT system. The institute has received clearance from the University Grants Commission (UGC) and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).





Important information regarding IIT-Varanasi 



Location-



Address- Banaras Hindu University Campus, Uttar Pradesh 221005



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/JCLTri9vZJvy2zmj









Campus size-

BHU campus area extends over 1300 acres







Getting to IIT-BHU

By air: The closest airport near BHU is Babatpur, which is roughly 35 kilometers distant. The cost of a taxi from the airport to the BHU campus is approximately INR 600/-.



By rail- Varanasi is connected to major Indian towns by train since it is located in the core of the North Indian plains. Varanasi has two railway stations: Mughalsarai Rly. Station and Varanasi Cantt. Rly. Station (Junction).



Courses offered by IIT-Varanasi-



Courses

Eligibility

BTech

Campus facilities-



The campus is stunning, with plenty of vegetation. India's second-largest campus



Internet –



The internet facilities are fine, but they will be even better now that all filters have been eliminated and there is no limit on how much time can be spent on the internet. My room has a download speed of up to 10.5 megabits per second. (The current limit is 4GB/day).



Classrooms-



The facilities in newly created lecture halls are excellent.





Library – 



A magnificent new two-story library with air conditioning and highly comfortable reading rooms has been built.

You can also access the BHU library, which is one of India's largest libraries.







Laboratories –



 The labs are well-equipped and are constantly being improved.





Sports facilities –



Basketball, tennis, and swimming are just a few of the sports that are available.



 



Gym-

There is also an open gym for all IIT (BHU) students in the Gymkhana building.



Other plus points – 



You are allowed to bring your own bike or scooter on campus, which is not permitted at any other IIT.

From the departments at BHU, you can take a finance course or acquire a foreign language.

IIT BHU is once again unique in this regard. Within our site, there is also a vast, gorgeous, and well-known Vishwanath temple. .





Student Activities-



Many groups and committees for various co-curricular activities exist at IIT Varanasi.

Students' talents are encouraged through cultural clubs such as photography and dramatics.

Music clubs, cinema clubs, and literature clubs are among the other clubs.





Extracurricular activities- 



 The following are some of the regional clubs on campus:



NCC: National Cadet Corps (all 3 wings viz, Army, Navy and Air Force)



NSS: National Service Program



Hobbies center



Sports facilities include a pool, an amphitheater, and both indoor and outdoor activities.



competitions held inside and across faculties in the areas of music, dancing, debate, elocution, theater, one-act plays, skits, mime, and more.



While enrolled in one course, one may also pursue a certificate from a different faculty, such as yoga, music, horticulture, etc.



You can pick up a lot of foreign languages.



Additionally, BHU maintains a University Mountaineering Center.



Earn-while-learn programme for children in need.



Additionally, we have the Indian Council of Cultural Relations office on-site if you're interested in participating in other cultural activities.

Students Hostel-



The hostels are well-equipped to accommodate around 550 students in excellent housing and boarding. Wireless internet and LAN connections provide high-speed internet access.



The Institute campus has five hostels: four for boys (Mercury Wings A, B, and C, Jupiter and Neptune Houses) and one for girls (Mercury Wings A, B, and C, Jupiter and Neptune Houses) (Venus House).

Campus facilities-



Library- 



The library at the college is quite large. Aside from textbooks and recommended reading material, each course offered at the institute has its own set of requirements.



The library's collection of research monographs, reports, multi-volume reference works, dictionaries, encyclopedias, handbooks, and other materials is constantly developing.





 Guest house-

            

The guests are also accommodated by the IIT Ropar.

Outsiders/parents can stay in one of the guest houses.

           



Sports Facilities-



Several sporting facilities are available at the institute. A cricket field, three tennis courts, a football field, a gymnasium, a basketball court, and two volleyball courts are now available at the institute. 







             SAC- 

There is also a Students Activity Center at the institute (SAC).

It features a well-equipped gymnasium as well as areas for several activity clubs, including the Music Club, Dance Club, and Fine Arts Club.                    



          Bank and post office- 



The institute maintains its own independent post office, which is open to the institute's workers and students.

SBI (State Bank of India) has a branch on campus that is fully functional.



          Medical Facility- 



The institute's medical center is housed in a separate building adjacent to the dormitory complex.

         

          Canteen/Mess-



 The socializing spots inside and outside the college are one canteen, one hangout location (fuel zap), two canteens directly outside the main gate, and two messes.

A few neighboring eateries include Chawlas, Lilly motel, Sahil hotel, and HMT Resort, among others.







         Security-

                        

The institute has secured the safety and security of its campus and people.

A security agency has been tasked with the job of ensuring security to its staff and resources in order to do this.



Extracurricular Activities- 



Although IIT Ropar has a wide range of extracurricular activities, the main focus still lies on the numerous clubs in the institute. We’ve listed some of the underrated clubs of IIT Ropar below:



Robotics Club





At its foundation, the Robotics Club is motivated by a curiosity in building replicas of real-world objects or solving issues in the real world using machines or robots that are the result of humankind's collective intelligence. Our main objective is to acquire new abilities that will enable us to translate this passion into practical use. We are a group of tech enthusiasts who dream of creating things that seem to have come straight out of a science fiction novel or blockbuster film.





Aeromodelling Club



The idea of wanting to fly was passed down to us by our ancestors, who envied the birds as they flew freely across space on the limitless airway. We, at the Aeromodelling Club, IIT Ropar, are a collection of aviation and aeromodelling enthusiasts who share a love of the sky and a burning ambition to push the limits of airspace to their absolute limits.



Coding Club



The largest organization on campus, the Coding Club, plays a key role in creating the ideal environment for competitive coding in the college. The club regularly runs competitions of its own to showcase the coding prowess of the college in addition to assisting people in developing the unmatched abilities necessary for coding their way through external competitions. A community that only permits logical reasoning and does not support settling on methods, we are constantly looking for ways to improve because there is never a "best" in a competition.



Dance Club



Even though IIT Ropar is one of the greatest universities in the nation, the students still rock and roll. Dedicated students from IIT Ropar fill the dance club to the brim; these young Jacksons have already mesmerized spectators at numerous cultural festivals held around the nation. These dancers have wowed audiences worldwide with their performances of hip-hop, jazz, romantic salsa, and our own bollywood style.  Anyone who is willing to move their body a little bit is guided here by the dancing club!! The time and location are right here. So prepare to rock and roll with the IIT Ropar Dance Club, everyone.







NSS - National Service Scheme







IIT Ropar started NSS at the institute level, under the direction of Faculty in Charge, IIT Ropar, with the intention of contributing to the welfare of society and fostering a sense of social duty in the young students. Currently, NSS, IIT Ropar has over 100 active members from various disciplines who work assiduously for environmental protection, literacy campaigns, and community health care. Regular events include blood donation camps and educational lectures on human values. The group seeks to produce engineers who are nation-building workers and socially conscious.

3. Student Hostels-



The students at IIT Patna have access to housing on the college's campus. IIT Patna currently has two boys' hostels and one ladies' hostel on campus for this purpose.

For PhD and Master's students, there are two married scholar's apartments (placed close university).





Boys hostel-



The boys' hostel is an eight-story edifice separated into four blocks: A, B, C, and D.

The building has a total of six lifts. Every hostel floor has it's own hostel has  water cooler.

Girls hostel-



The girls hostel is a five-story structure divided into two parts, A and B.

The girls' hostel has a total of 108 rooms, each of which is spacious and equipped with two cupboards and a window.



Hostel facility-

The hostels are well furnished with modern facilities. 

Students have access to the Internet via Wi-Fi 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

The hostel provides a gym for staying in shape, as well as two common rooms for gatherings, recreational activities, watching TV, and other fun activities.



4. Campus facilities-



Sports complex-



A basketball court, volleyball court, tennis court, badminton court, cricket pitch, and football ground are all available on campus.







Gymnasium-



On the first floor, alongside the common area, there is a gymnasium. It includes all of the essential exercise equipment, such as dumbbells, barbells, and bench presses.

           



Mess-



On the ground level, the boys' hostel has two mess halls while the ladies' hostel has one. .

After preparing food, messes are always cleaned up by the workers.             



Medical/Hospital-



In addition, IIT Patna maintains a hospital on site that provides all medical services to students, teachers, and employees.

Inside the hospital, trained medical physicians and nurses look after all of the medical services supplied to students and faculty.

               



Guest Room-



Indian Institute of Technology Patna also offers a guest house at Agrani Infra City, Rajpur, Lai Road, Bihta, which is well-equipped with all essential amenities and attached bathrooms.

To satisfy the needs of guests, the guest home is equipped with air conditioning and WiFi.

A canteen is also on site, delivering high-quality food to all guests.



Extracurricular activities-



Cultural Committee







All of the institute's extracurricular activities are planned and promoted by the cultural committee House of Socio-Cultural Affairs (HoSCA). HoSCA seeks to advance a variety of artistic disciplines, including theater, dance, literature, fine art, spoken arts, photography, etc. Smaller events are also planned by the committee to lessen students' academic burden. This group successfully organizes intra-college and inter-college gaming competitions such as Reverberance, Nebula, and many others.



Entrepreneurship Club







IIT Patna's Entrepreneurship Club was founded with the goal of providing a gathering place for prospective business owners on campus. The club's goal is to prepare students for the path from the conception of an idea to its successful commercialization by educating them about the complexities of business and entrepreneurship. In addition to hosting notable speakers from academics and industry on a regular basis, the Club also sponsors a number of business and marketing competitions throughout the year.



Rural Technology Development Club







The Rural Technology Development Club (RTDC) was founded with the intention of identifying the issues that are prevalent in rural regions and working to develop technologies and gadgets to solve them. The Club works to close the gap in technology that exists in rural communities. Keeping this in mind, the team conducts frequent village surveys to learn about the issues that the rural population faces. The Club is currently working on a number of projects, including a manual wheat harvesting machine, a self-running irrigation system based on canals, and a solar-powered LED based reading light.



Sports Club





Sports are just as important at IIT Patna as academic and cultural endeavors. Even after a protracted, exhausting battle to get into one of India's top institutions, one can still pursue their interest for a game or sport. Under the direction of Assistant Coach Shyam Kumar, the sports club of IIT Patna regularly hosts events amongst departments and hostels to highlight the best athletes. The greatest college athletes will be chosen for the inter-IIT sports meet, where they will have the opportunity to compete in a real-time competitive atmosphere. Each IITian will be doing their absolute best to prove to the world that IITians are on par with the best athletes in the nation.

INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, GANDHINAGAR

                             

IIT Gandhinagar is on a mission to become India's top educational institute, as well as one of the best in the world.

Address- Indian Institute of Technology, Palaj, Gujarat 382355

https://maps.app.goo.gl/cckHrgpvHjou5BsM8

Campus facilities-



Library: 



The library contains around 12000 books in its collection. The library also makes its resources available online, where students can access them. It includes Wi-Fi access for quick internet access.

           

           



Cafeteria-



The campus also features eateries for its students, where they may sample a variety of foods and beverages.

For students on campus, restaurants such as Anytime Fast Food, Amul Store, Dawat foods, and Nutririch serve high-quality food and beverages.



           

           

           



Auditorium-



The auditorium at IIT Gandhinagar is available for events and seminars.

             

             



Medical/Hospital-



The institute employs a well-trained instructor to instruct pupils in sports and physical fitness.

Volleyball, football, basketball, and cricket are all played on the institute's dedicated sports field.

                 

Wi-Fi-



The IITGN campus is equipped with a LAN network that connects all of the college's departments for better collaboration.

Wi-Fi is available throughout the campus and hostels, allowing for uninterrupted learning.



Sports-



Students will be trained by a highly skilled physical education instructor on staff.

Football, cricket, volleyball, and basketball are all available on the grounds.

Indoor sports facilities, such as badminton and table tennis, have also been added.

There is a modern multi-purpose gym on site, as well as gym instructors.

           

                 



           





 Extra curricular activities- 



16 Pixels





The IITGN photography group, "16 Pixels," records all of life's special moments and shares them with the campus. People who work for IITGN are also free to follow their interests in photography. Many competitions, courses, and events are held to motivate students to develop their professional photography talents.



Abhinaya







The drama club of IITGN, Abhinaya, brings together all the theater and acting aficionados to explore the theater world, from script creation to acting. Every year, as part of the ceremonies for Republic Day and Independence Day, plays highlighting social themes are presented. The drama club regularly participates in producing short films and staging street plays all year long. Every year, "The Butler Did It," an action-packed thriller film, is made for a Blithchron event. A sitcom has come out that is centered on the daily routines of students. It has persisted the entire semester.



Step Up







The club wants to give students the chance to learn various dance styles and give them a stage to perform them on together. For the institute's students, instructors, and staff, the Dance Club provides seminars at both the expert and basic levels. The contemporary and garba dance seminars recently held garnered a roaring response. Additionally, during Jashn, a dance tournament called "Thump" is organized annually, inviting all of the institute's dancers to compete in a brutal competition. The club also hosts performances and prepares members to participate in both inside and outside of the school cultural events.











Metis







In addition to creating websites and applications that serve the needs of the institute, Metis at IITGN strives to teach students about computer programming. Weekly workshops are organized by the club with an emphasis on fundamental programming and design languages like Java and HTML. Hackathon nights are held in the institute to foster a culture of coding. Additionally, competitions and workshops are held to promote programming.



DigiS







The IIT Gandhinagar club for the development of digital sports is called DigiS. It serves as a platform for imaginative people to make their fantasies come true. The group wants to raise the bar for how people and technology interact. For Android and OS systems, the club creates simulations, applications, and augmented reality platforms. It plans frequent activities in which institute students can take part.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, BHUBANESHWAR

                    

Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) is a technical institute in India The government of India established Bhubaneswar as a public technical university in 2008. The institute accepts students for bachelor's and master's programmes via the JEE Advanced and Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering, respectively.







Important information regarding IIT-Bhubaneswar-



Location-



Address- Jatni Road Argul, Bhubaneswar, Odisha 752050







Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/EHaXReGLz8QWP66CA



Campus size-936 Acres





Reaching IIT Bhubaneswar-

It is located at the base of Barunei Hill, which is known for its historical significance.

The site offers a tranquil and pollution-free learning environment.

Student Hostels-



IIT Bhubaneswar is one of India's top technology institutes, with a fully residential campus for its students.

The Hostel facilities include three Hostel blocks, one for girls and the other two for guys.



Boys hostel-



Mahanadi Hall of Residence



Dr. A.N. Khosla Hall of Residence



Girls hostel-



Subarnarekha Hall of Residence





Campus facilities-



Library-

Students can access a vast quantity of books as well as online learning courses at this university's library.





Sports Complex:



Indoor and outdoor sports such as cricket, volleyball, and football, as well as indoor sports such as chess, badminton, and table tennis, are available on campus in well-designated areas with special amenities such as an athletic race track, long jump, high jump, triple jump, and short put, as well as outdoor sports such as cricket, volleyball, and football..



Classrooms:



The classrooms are furnished and well equipped. 



Cafeteria-



For nutritious and delicious cuisine, the IIT Bhubaneswar canteens and cafeteria are located inside the hostels as well as throughout the campus. 

            

            



Auditorium-



The IIT Bhubaneswar auditorium has a seating capacity of 300 people, making it ideal for workshops, conferences, events, lectures, and even pre-employment interviews.

             



Hospital-



The IIT Bhubaneswar medical facility on campus has a dedicated team of health workers and doctors who are responsible for students, and these services are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week, including emergency care.

             



WiFi-



Wi-Fi is available to students and staff at the college.



Guest Room-



Guests staying at the IIT Bhubaneswar guest homes can be a person or a group, such as visiting faculty or parents of students.

There are 42 air-conditioned single and double rooms with toilets and other amenities.

           



Extracurricular activities- 



The IIT Bhubaneswar Students' Gymkhana is a student organization that supports all student activities and acts as a liaison between the administration and the students. Since its founding in 2010, it has worked to develop numerous qualitative techniques among students in order to help them learn how to live better.







The Students' Gymkhana serves as the center for all of the extracurricular, technical, and co-curricular activities offered by IIT Bhubaneswar, which include a variety of sports, cultural, and technical pursuits. By promoting their involvement in a variety of diverse events, it plays a crucial role in developing and nurturing their extracurricular abilities in this context.







NEUROMANCERS





As the world is progressively being remade in cyberspace, the Neuromancers Programming Society's primary goal is to share programming skills among students. In 2013, a team of committed programmers founded Neuromancers. Members of the Programming Society are experts in a variety of software tools and programming languages. The society has been highly active in organizing numerous events, tournaments, and workshops for IIT Bhubaneswar students ever since it was founded. The members of this society engage in numerous activities and operate under a variety of titles, including web development, machine learning, natural language processing, etc.



CLIX





Clix was established with the intention of giving the Institute audio-visual data, publicizing and recording Institute events, and displaying it to a larger audience through photos and films. In 2013, CLIX was named an official component of Students' Gymkhana in recognition of the substantial contribution it made to representing IIT Bhubaneswar to the outside world and in preserving memories for the IIT family here. In the last few years, with the help of a team of talented photographers, we have evolved from a straightforward "Event Coverage Team" to a "Photography Society" by holding workshops on photography and post-processing. We also hold a variety of photographic contests to encourage student potential.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, MANDI

                     



IIT Mandi is one of the eight new Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) created by the Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD). It is a well-known major Engineering and Technology institute, with UGC and AICTE approval. 







Important information regarding IIT Mandi-



Location-



Address- Parashar Road, Tehsil Sadar, Near Kataula, Kamand, Himachal Pradesh 175005



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/saKEMXVsxpheZ5QYA





Campus size- 538 acre





Reaching IIT Mangi-



By broad gauge train, the nearest railway stations are Joginder Nagar, Chandigarh, and Kalka, which are all connected by regular bus services.



Shimla, Chandigarh, Pathankot, and Delhi are all accessible by road from Mandi.



It is connected to nearby towns such as Manali, Palampur, and Dharamshala by regular bus services.

Student Hostel-



IIT Mandi features a fully residential campus, which houses around 1800 students.

Six boys' hostels, two girls' hostels, and one married scholars' flat are available.





Boys hostel- 

Suvalsar

Parashar

 Nako in South Campus

and Beas Kund in North Campus. 



Girls Hostel-

 Chandratal in South Campus and 

 Gauri Kund in North Campus.



     

     



Campus facilities-



Library-



The Central Library at IIT Mandi has over 41,868 books and gives access to over 50000 e-journals.

A good variety of audiovisual items, including as CDs and DVDs, is also available at the library.

         





Banks and ATM-



In the main campus, there are two banks with ATMs (State Bank of India and Punjab National Bank).



Stores: 



South Campus has a provision, grocery, and stationary store, while North Campus has a provision and grocery store.



Sports: 



Basketball, cricket, volleyball, soccer, hockey, football, badminton, tennis, table tennis, and cycling are just a handful of the sports that are played on campus. Hiking and trekking are two activities that are encouraged with supervision. 

.







HealthCare facilities- 



On both campuses, IIT Mandi offers a functioning medical center that is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week, with doctors, nurses, and all essential medications. In the event of an emergency, they also have an ambulance on standby.





Cafeteria: 



The institute has two canteens serving many good quality Indian dishes along with a bakery shop. 

They also have dining halls in both the camses.



                

            

Gym: 



The institute provides students with access to a gymnasium that is well-equipped with a variety of training apparatus. Inside the gymnasium, students can also participate in yoga and aerobics.





Auditorium-



For many of the college's events and services, students have access to an auditorium.





A/C Classrooms: 



IIT Mandi's classrooms are nicely furnished and have some air conditioning.

There are four virtual classes in all..





Extra curricular activity-  



The student clubs at IIT Mandi enable the all-round development of the students. There are many student clubs that cater to diverse interests. Most of the clubs come Under the Student Gymkhana, with club coordinators for the various programs.



The Science and Technology Council (SNTC), theCultural Society, the Sports Society and the English Debating and Literary Society are the major student bodies overseeing the various student clubs. Music, Dramatics and Dance come together with Programme Management Under the CUItUral Society to provide students a platform to showcase their skills. The society has also started a Photography and Fine arts Club. In the year 2014, this society started collaborating with SPIC-MACAY and has been organizing various events throughout the year. "Aakarshan ''-introduction to CUItUral Society and''Exuberance"- Intra College Cultural Nights are some of the major events. The college band participated in Zeitgeist 2011 at 11T Ropar and stood second. The college also participated in WAVES 2014 at BITS Goa.



IIT Mandi has the Programming Club, which along with the ACM Student Chapter organizes various coding competitions at National and International levels.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, JODHPUR

                            



Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) is a technical institute in India In 2008, Jodhpur was founded to promote technology education and research in India. The Institute is dedicated to technological thought and activity in support of India's economic development.







Important information regarding IIT-Jodhpur

 

Location-



Address- NH 62, Surpura Bypass Road, Karwar, Rajasthan 342037



Campus map link- 

https://maps.app.goo.gl/XfBMZxZsFgArJXJZA







Campus size- 852 acres





Reaching IIT Jodhpur-

The campus of IIT Jodhpur is 25 kilometers from the city. You can use an auto rickshaw or a bus to get about. It is simple to go to campus from the city.



Jodhpur junction is 25 kms away from the campus. The distance between the station and campus is approximately 36 minutes.

Student hostels-



The IIT Jodhpur campus has a student accommodation capacity of 2,478. 

There are 12 hostels in IIT Jodhpur campus. In which 10 hostels are for boys and 2 hostels are for girls.

Each hostel in the institute has some extra facilities like reading room, an indoor games room, a lounge and a dining hall with mess. 

Wi-fi is available in all the rooms of the hostel. .











Campus facilities-

  

Library-

The library at IIT Jodhpur supports the institute's teaching and research activities with a large collection of textbooks, reference, and general books totaling over 12,000 volumes.





Cafeteria-

Canteen facilities are available in the college campus for students and staff both. 

IIT Jodhpur features a three-story dining hall that covers two stories and can accommodate up to 1000 students. Separate kitchens for vegetarian and non-vegetarian meal preparation are also available.



Auditorium-

For events and seminars, the college features an auditorium.







HospitalMedical/Hospital-



Residents of the IIT Jodhpur campus have access to a primary health center with 24-hour ambulance and emergency services. IIT Jodhpur features an ISO 9001:2005 accredited health center and OPD, as well as empanelment with prestigious hospitals.







Wifi-

On academic and residential campuses, Wi-Fi is available.



ATM & Bank: 



SBI (State Bank of India) maintains a branch in the residential area, as well as two SBI ATMs and one Canara Bank ATM, allowing students to conduct business with ease.





Gymnasium: 



Each hostel has a well-equipped gymnasium that is open 24 hours a day; students can use this amenity to be healthy and maintain their physique.



Sports Facilities-



There are additional sports facilities such as a basketball court, tennis court, badminton court, indoor activities (such as table tennis), and a football and cricket stadium.







Extracurricular activity-  



Design & Arts Society- 

IGNUS, the first of its kind in the State of Rajasthan, Nimble, the Science and Technology festival, Tech Expo, the flagship event of the Design & Arts Society showcasing the artistic endeavor of IIT Jodhpur and neighboring students, and Virasaat, the SPICMACAY festival are just a few of the major events held by IIT Jodhpur that highlight the institute's primary focus on the holistic development of students.



Voice- 

"There Is a New World. WE Spread the Word Regarding It." The voice interface is the connection point between the SME and the outside world. We cultivate dependable media contacts and advance SME branding. to create and release yearly/monthly publications for SME.



V-Care-

In order to instil a high degree of emotional quotient in future corporate leaders and to motivate them to be more socially responsible, the V-Care committee collaborates with social sector organizations. Through our innovative and special skill sets, IIT Jodhpur has a strong aim to give back to society and assist those in need. We collaborate closely with nonprofit organizations, host guest lecturers, and help students complete their four-week social sector internships with a variety of NGOs.



Wander-

A group of wanderers and adventurers who share a passion for exploring, travelling, and tourism make up Wander - The Adventure Club of IIT Jodhpur. By planning different adventures, adventure, and trekking events for the students, the group hopes to encourage environmentally friendly travel. The club's goal is to develop the students' personalities through travel by cultivating a respect for the natural world.



The goal of Quizzard - 

The Quiz Club of SME IIT Jodhpur is to keep SME students informed of the most recent developments in a variety of industries, including business, sports, technology, and public policy, among others. In addition to holding regular quizzes, the group seeks to assist students develop their communication skills and convey their ideas in a more organized and lively manner through exercises like GDs, Extempore, and debates.



Mint Money - 

The Finance Club of SME works hard to spread and encourage financial practises and thought among the students. Through a variety of seminars, guest lectures by leading figures in the field, certifications, and the hosting of trading simulation games, it offers a forum for the exchange of ideas in current and new areas of finance.





Toastmaster-

Students have the chance to develop their leadership abilities and confidence by joining the Toastmasters club. The club holds a number of events where members can practice, develop, and polish their social skills.

Textbooks and reference books in Engineering, Sciences, Humanities, and Social Sciences, journals in Engineering, Technology, and Life Sciences, popular magazines, and e-resources are all available at the IIT Tirupati library.

Sports:

Student Hostels-



The campus has eight hostels, six of which are for boys and two of which are for ladies.

Every hostel has the necessary infrastructure to assure the comfort and safety of its guests.

Chairs, study tables, cots, almirahs, drying stands, and other basic equipment are provided in each room.











Campus facilities-



Library-



In 2015, the Institute created a library to assist its instructional and research programmes.

Textbooks and reference books in Engineering, Sciences, Humanities, and Social Sciences, journals in Engineering, Technology, and Life Sciences, popular magazines, and e-resources are all available at the IIT Tirupati library.







Classrooms-



All of the classrooms and lecture halls are well-equipped with acoustics. Computers, projectors, audio-video systems, and wi-fi are among the classroom amenities of IIT Tirupati. There are also virtual classroom and video conferencing options.





Wifi-



High-speed internet is available on the college campus. The wi-fi facility is available to students and faculty for academic and research purposes.



Medical/Hospital-



The medical facilities at IIT Tirupati include first-aid. The college provides medical services to patients, students, staff, and faculty members in conjunction with Sri Venkateswara Institute of Medical Sciences (SVIMS)..







Cafeteria-



The college cafeteria is large and serves freshly prepared, hygienically prepared meals. In addition, the hostels have dining facilities.









Auditorium-



The auditorium of IIT Tirupati is equipped with the most up-to-date technology, such as projectors and audio-video systems.

The auditorium is rather large and can hold a huge number of people.





Sports-



Chess, carrom, badminton, table tennis, tennis, cricket, basketball, football, volleyball, and other indoor and outdoor games are available at IIT Tirupati. Students are encouraged to participate in activities that will benefit their physical and mental health.









Extracurricular Activity- 



Drama club-

On February 22, 2017, They established this group with the goal of showcasing students' acting abilities through skits, drama, and short films. This club's name, Actomania, was suggested by numerous students.

With the help of the entire cast and the photography club (IDEAL SHOTS), our first short film, "Nithya—a sad love story," was successfully finished in a week and won the top honor at the IIIT-Sri City Abhisarika Short Film Contest. Tarani, Y.Shravanthi, wrote and directed the film.



Robotic club- 

This club assists in the development of innovative thoughts towards technology. Students will design and develop new methods and ideas to overcome existing problems like aligning the stacks in supermarkets, fire extinguishing, etc. It helps in gaining knowledge of the present technology like combining Al with robotics and performing tasks like agricultural surveillance for detecting diseases in plants (by using Computer Vision). This club supports the student in understanding the use and the building of robots by organizing events like seminars, workshops, etc., where they will have hands-on sessions with the robot.



Astroclub IITP - 

The club's main focus is on Astronomy and Astrophysics. They regularly organize various Talk shows, Workshops and Competitions. The club also organizes regular star gazing sessions using telescopes, and astrophotography sessions using DSLRs during special astronomy events like eclipses, meteor showers etc. They also focus on the engineering aspects of astronomy like Rocket science and other space technology. The club also has an active Instagram page to promote awareness towards astronomy for everyone.

           AVERAGE PACKAGE OFFERED

           Rs. 11.00 lakhs per annum

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, BHILAI

                              

The thIndian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bhilai is located in the state of Chhattisgarh . It is established in 2016 by the Ministry of Human Resource and Development

IIT Bhilai offers a wide range of courses in a variety of fields.

Currently based out of a makeshift campus in Raipur,





Important information regarding IIT-Bhilai-



Location-



Address- GEC Campus, Old Dhamtari Road, Sejbahar, Chhattisgarh 492015



Campus map link- 

https://maps.app.goo.gl/wQG66jSZZm3qV9p59







Campus size- 432 acre





Reaching IIT Bhilai-

Raipur Junction, the nearest railway station, is one of the important railway stations in central India. New Delhi, Mumbai, Bhopal, Jabalpur, Chennai, Bangalore, Kota, Ahmedabad, and Jaipur are all easily accessible.

The transit facility is roughly 13 kilometers from Raipur Junction.

Student Hostels-



Within the IIT Bhilai campus, there are two hostels: Castle Ena and Castle Dio. At the moment, about 80 female students and 450 male students can live on campus full-time.







Hostel facility-



There is a large dining hall, a night canteen, and a well-equipped leisure area with an indoor gaming area and a fitness center in the hostels.



Campus facilities-



Library-



At Indian Institute of Technology, Bhilai, the central library is the epicenter of knowledge generation, with over tens of thousands of instructional books and research papers available.

It is a learning centre that supports students', research researchers', and other staff members' teaching and learning activities.





Sports-



Apart from academics, IIT Bhilai features a variety of sports facilities where students can play and practise a variety of sports.

They provide indoor and outdoor game facilities, including a badminton court, basketball court, tennis court, volleyball court, football field, carom, chess, and more.











Gymkhana-



Gym facilities are also available to help students maintain their health and fitness.





Cafeteria-



A cafeteria is offered for both hostellers and normal students. Students and employees have access to meal menus. To protect students' health, meals are prepared in the most hygienic manner possible.







Medical/Hospital-



The institute is concerned about the health of its students and employees, and hence provides for their requirements. There is a health center on campus that is open 24 hours a day.

By offering exceptional medical treatment, the doctors and staff are dedicated to the students' health and the well-being of the campus community.





Extracurricular activity-







The IIT Bhilai student body, the Council of Student Affairs (CoSA), actively directs and oversees all student-related events. CoSA organizes a number of organizations on campus that give students the chance to develop their abilities in dance, music, drama, oratory, creative writing, painting, and photography. 



In addition to these general interest groups, students can join specialized groups focused on robotics, coding, electronics, and space exploration. 







These clubs regularly have events, and the campus is bustling with activity. On campus, National Service Scheme (NSS) events are routinely hosted where students are urged to perform unpaid community service. 



The IIT Bhilai students have also demonstrated their athletic prowess by competing in and winning awards in.









Placement statistics-

.

   







OAT- 

The campus also has an open-air theater which is quite large with a sitting capacity of 5000 people.

All the major functions including the annual fest, convocation etc. are held here.

 Extra curriculars activities- 



The extracurricular activities are at their zenith in terms of fervor and caliber. For instance, the college enrolls some of Delhi's best dancers as pupils. They attend various colleges each year and consistently land first or second positions.

  

   

In addition, we have a variety of clubs for cultural pursuits. The BRCA trophy, awarded annually to the best hostel in cultural events, is contested by all 13 hostels. These occasions include:

The Institute places a lot of emphasis on student involvement in a variety of indoor and outdoor games. The students are urged to take part in a variety of games and sports, including athletics (for both sexes), badminton (for both sexes), basketball (for both genders), cricket, football, gliding, gymnastics, hiking and mountaineering, hockey, judo, kabaddi, squash, table tennis (for both sexes), tennis, volleyball, yoga, weightlifting, multi-gym, swimming, and water These pursuits make students' free time productive. Student Hostels-



IIT Goa includes two separate hostel complexes for females and boys with a total capacity of 320 students, as well as a dining hall with a capacity of over 250 students.

The hostels are spotless and well-kept.









Hostel facility-



Hostel facilities-

24 hours water supply and electricity

Common room

Separate mess

24 hours security

Wi-Fi connectivity

Medical facility



Campus facilities-



Cafeteria: 



The IIT Goa campus features a very clean cafeteria that serves delicious food on a daily basis. Both students and employees can eat vegetarian cuisine, with some non-vegetarian options available.









Gymkhana: 



IIT Goa's gym is well-equipped with a variety of training devices to help students maintain their physical fitness.





Hospital / Medical Facilities: 



The school contains a medical center that caters to the medical needs of its students, instructors, and employees, such as medications.





Wi-Fi: 



Free Wi-Fi is available on campus for students and faculty to connect and utilize for their needs



Library: 



The Central Library at IIT Goa is a completely air-conditioned and well-stocked library where students and faculty can get their essential study materials.







Auditorium: 



IIT Goa offers a number of air-conditioned auditoriums that are available for a variety of cultural and academic events.



A/C Classrooms:



 Many of the classrooms and lecture rooms at IIT Goa are air-conditioned. They are also outfitted with cutting-edge learning tools.





Sports: 



This campus allows students to participate in a variety of sports.

Because the campus has an indoor badminton court, a lawn tennis court, a table tennis court, a basketball court, and a volleyball court, this is the case.











Extracurricular activity-  



MERAKI – 



When you dance, you let go of yourself. Greater in size, beauty, and strength... This is yours to possess; it is power and earthly glory. One of the most opulent and dedicated clubs is Meraki, the dance club at IIT Goa. Numerous workshops and events are held throughout the year, and the performances are eagerly anticipated by everyone due to the tremendous engagement displayed by committed students.



ORION –



Everything is given life by music, which also provides the universe a spirit, wings for the mind, and flight for the imagination. IIT Goa's Orion music club is a gathering space for students to jam, study, and create a variety of music. It aims to unite everybody who is passionate about a certain musical style. As one of the busiest clubs, it hosts fruitful music workshops and competes fiercely with other musicians.



PANACHE – 



The sole goal of oratory is persuasion, not fact. IIT Goa's Panache oratory club seeks to improve students' abilities to express themselves by helping them better comprehend their own emotions. The group offers a venue for the speakers to meet and practice their public speaking, leadership, and debating talents.



QALAM- 



Every writer's innermost thoughts, experiences, and mental faculties are vividly depicted in their works. The goal of IIT Goa's literary club, Qalam, is to help students hone their literary abilities. The club encourages students to become readers and also works to broaden their knowledge of both spoken and written language. The foundation of the club is the use of the creative abilities needed to be a writer.

INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, JAMMU

                           



The "Institutes of Technology Act" of 1961 designated IIT Jammu as a "Institute of National Importance." IIT is an autonomous public higher education institute that is sponsored by the Indian government and is governed by the IIT Council.







Important information regarding IIT- Jammu 



Location-



Address- NH-44 , PO Nagrota, Jagti, Jammu and Kashmir 181221



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/YiJtd5ADghXdqoFA6





Campus size- 25 acres





Reaching IIT Jammu-

The nearest railway station is IIT Jammu, which is approximately 13 kilometers from Jammu Railway Station. You can either take a prepaid cab or an auto rickshaw from Jammu Railway Station to Katra, which will drop you off in front of the IIT Jammu Jagti Campus, or you can take a bus to Katra.

Campus facilities-



ATM-



ICICI Bank and Canara Bank ATMs are located in the residential area.





Dining Service-



There is a dining facility on campus. The Dining Hall provides good quality food at an affordable price, which is frequently inspected by the Wardens for hygiene and nutritional benefits.

Additionally, there are canteens on campus.







Gymnasium- 



On campus, there is a well-equipped gymnasium that is open 24 hours a day.







Sport Facility-



Since its foundation, the Institute has prioritized physical recreation through games and sports.

As a result, at IIT JMU, we actively encourage students to participate in a variety of sports and games, for which excellent facilities (football field, tennis court, basketball court, cricket practice pitch with nets, badminton, volleyball court, etc.) are provided.











Library-



The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu library serves as a resource center for students and teachers. It has a substantial collection of books, periodicals, and publications, among other things.

For students, the library also has ebooks and e-journals.





Medical/Hospital-



Medical insurance is provided to all university students.

A paramedic officer is on duty 24 hours a day, seven days a week at the university's medical center.

At regular intervals, a senior doctor pays a visit to the medical center.





Wifi-



Wi-Fi is available across IIT Jammu's campus for students and teachers.

It facilitates student research by allowing for quick browsing.



Classrooms-



 IIT Jammu's classrooms are well-appointed and equipped with cutting-edge technology.





Extracurricular activity-  



There are currently two festivals held at IIT Jammu each year.

Infinito is a small-scale gaming and culture event.

the sports festival convoquer

It is anticipated that the formal cultural festival and technical festival would begin during the following academic year.

In terms of clubs, the robotics and coding clubs are both busy, and many student-run workshops take place. The SAE club was founded in January of this year and is operating effectively. This year, there will be an electronics club.

Extracurricular clubs like dance, music, and theater are active and present performances around the community.

In addition to this, there is a social club that promotes the wellbeing of the kids of construction employees. It participates in a variety of other social pursuits.

INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, DHARWAD

                      

The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Dharwad is a self-governing major science and technology institute. The Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) of the Indian government established the institute.



The institute is currently working out of a makeshift campus on the campus of the Water and Land Management Institute (WALMI).



IIT Dharwad's future transit campus will also be housed on the WALMI campus.

Library-



 The library now includes over 1500 volumes and will be adding several hundred more in the near future.

We want to make sure that students have not only text/reference books for various courses, but also literature that will help them develop their complete personalities.

. 







Medical facilities-



Local hospitals and a visiting doctor serve the IIT campus. IIT Dharwad students, professors, and staff will have access to the general medical services provided by the allocated local hospitals.

Doctors who are qualified and experienced come to campus on a regular basis and are also accessible on call.





Sports-



Indoor and outdoor games such as chess, carrom, badminton, table tennis, tennis, cricket, basketball, football, volleyball, and others are available at the Indian Institute of Technology Dharwad.

The Sports Club of IIT Dharwad oversees all of the institute's sporting events.





Gym-



There is also a gym on site. As a result, the institute's best facilities are available.





Cafeteria-

The canteen is well-equipped and kept clean.



Auditorium-

Classrooms-



The classrooms at Indian Institute of Equipment Dharwad are equipped with cutting-edge technology that makes teaching and learning easier.





Extracurricular activity-

  

Codechef- 

They offer everyone a platform to learn and compete with other students at the national level through our tournaments. Students responded favorably to previous Avada Kedavra workshops on the fundamentals of competitive programming and competitions. AvadaCodavra, a group of students who are interested in coding, seeks to create a coding culture on campus by fostering competitive programming there.



Photography club-

Most of the members of the photography group are enthusiastic amateur photographers. The club documents all of the intriguing activities taking place on campus. Students have a number of options through the club to explore their passion in photography and learn the craft.

hostel_info:
Hostels offer spacious double shared rooms with attached toilets, as well as WiFi and recreational activities. There should also be a separate dining area with a mess area.

Each hostel features an entertainment area with a television, newspapers, and a selection of magazines. Visitors at the Institute, including academics, employees, and students, can stay in guest rooms.

Table tennis, volleyball, and ball-badminton courts are among the few sporting amenities available at the hostel.

There is also a medical facility.

Security guards are on duty 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and the building is monitored by CCTV cameras.

Students are covered by medical insurance, and there is a full-fledged hospital on campus to handle any medical emergencies.
campus_facilities:
Library:
The central library of IIT Palakkad is completely computerized.
The library has a collection of 2700 volumes, as well as reference materials. A range of audio-visual materials, such as CD-ROMs, scientific kits, journals, and newspapers, was also included.
Wi-Fi and LAN access are available at the library.
Users of the library are also enrolled with the MHRD-sponsored National Digital Library

Sports:
The support for sports facilities at IIT Palakkad has continued to grow. Cricket practice pitches have been built in addition to the existing facilities (football, volleyball, basketball, and table tennis).
For table tennis and basketball training, new equipment has been obtained. Students are also taught table tennis, physical fitness, and weight lifting by professional trainers.

Student Hostels-



Hostels offer spacious double shared rooms with attached toilets, as well as WiFi and recreational activities. There should also be a separate dining area with a mess area.



Each hostel features an entertainment area with a television, newspapers, and a selection of magazines. Visitors at the Institute, including academics, employees, and students, can stay in guest rooms.



Table tennis, volleyball, and ball-badminton courts are among the few sporting amenities available at the hostel.

There is also a medical facility.



Security guards are on duty 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and the building is monitored by CCTV cameras.

Students are covered by medical insurance, and there is a full-fledged hospital on campus to handle any medical emergencies.







Campus facilities-



Library-



The central library of IIT Palakkad is completely computerized.

The library has a collection of 2700 volumes, as well as reference materials. A range of audio-visual materials, such as CD-ROMs, scientific kits, journals, and newspapers, was also included.

Wi-Fi and LAN access are available at the library.

Users of the library are also enrolled with the MHRD-sponsored National Digital Library





Health Care-



For a little annual fee, students are insured by a comprehensive medical insurance system.

For cashless medical attention, IIT Palakkad has MOUs with Athani Hospital, Malabar Hospital, Ahalia Diabetes Hospital, and Thangam Hospital.. 





Sports-



The support for sports facilities at IIT Palakkad has continued to grow. Cricket practice pitches have been built in addition to the existing facilities (football, volleyball, basketball, and table tennis).

For table tennis and basketball training, new equipment has been obtained. Students are also taught table tennis, physical fitness, and weight lifting by professional trainers.









Sports facilities



Cricket practice pitch

Basketball coach

Cricket kits

Badminton rackets

Basket balls

Table tennis coach



Security-

                        

The institute has secured the safety and security of its campus and people.

A security agency has been tasked with the job of ensuring security to its staff and resources in order to do this.



Gym Room- 



Basic gym equipment is available in all hostels. If you're a member of the institute's hard core, the gym contains all you'll need.





Mess and canteen-



 Every hostel features a mess that serves three meals a day, as well as a canteen, which is a pay-as-you-eat facility.





Extracurricular activity-  



Vadya music club- 

It is the school's music club and was established by the first cohort of students in 2015. Being one of the oldest clubs, it has undergone significant change and consistently produces ground-breaking performances all year long. Open mic nights, band competitions, Sur Sangram, and other events are held by Vadya. Dr. Debarati Chatterjee is the mentor.





The dancing club- 

The move to light up any stage is called Sync to Beat. The club has planned events like Naatya, the dance combat of crews, and is always excited to take part. Dr. Lakshmi Sankar is the mentor.







YACC- 

You can succeed in Competitive Programming, Ethical Hacking, Full Stack Development, Game Development, and System Design at Yet Another Coding Club (YACC), which includes five independent and parallel paths. In order to make programming a rewarding experience for students with various skill sets, YACC works to establish a strong culture of problem solving and coding inside the institution. (Guide: Doctor Albert Sunny)







The Robotics Club (TRC)- 

It is a group of students that are passionate about building self-driving robots. Students from any discipline can participate in this multidisciplinary project and learn about and work on a variety of topics, including mechanical design, the kinematics and dynamics of the model, sensors, and the algorithms to be used.



Quiz Club- 

It is the newest of the cultural groups and serves as a symbol of how rapidly the number of quizzers at IIT Palakkad has increased. (Guardian: Dr. Sarath Sasi)







The goal of the Data Analysis Club (DAC)- 

It is to develop and fine-tune predictive models by gathering and analyzing data in order to address problems in the real world. The community actively participates in debating, studying, and working on ML and AI-related issues. The club works to create a multidisciplinary student body of data analysis experts. Dr. Sahely Bhadra is the mentor.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, BOMBAY                

                                                    



The Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay (IITB) is one of the Fifteen higher Institutes of Technology in the country set up to make available facilities for higher education, research and training in various fields of Science and Technology. 2021 NIRF ranking of IIT Bombay is 03.







Important information related to IIT Bombay-



Location -



Address- Main Gate Rd, IIT Area, Powai, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400076



Campus map link- https://maps.app.goo.gl/APA7og8RoinqWzpU9

    

        



   Campus size- 550 acres



    IIT Bombay is a small township in itself. Consciously developed, the campus has maintained and expanded its lush greenery.



                



Reaching IIT Bombay- The nearest railway station to IIT Bombay is KanjurMarg station and it is 2.6 km away and it is going to take about 15 to 20 minutes to cover this travel the distance by road and take an auto or a private taxi.

Students hostels-



There are a total of 18 hostels present in the campus out of these two hostels (Hostels 10 and 11) are newly constructed hostels, (Hostel 15) are for female students.



The hostels of IIT Bombay have an outdated infrastructure but are provided with all of the necessary amenities and features.



The new Hostels are extremely good in their infrastructure and facilities.

               

                

   

         Hostel Facilities:



24 hours water supply and electricity

Common room

Separate mess

24 hours security

Wi-Fi connectivity

Indoor games

Medical facility



 

Campus facilities -



 Library- 



The Central Library of IIT Bombay is one of India's largest libraries of Science, Engineering and Technology.

                



  Guesthouse-



There are 4 guesthouses on the IIT-B campus - Padmavihar, Vanvihar, Jalavihar and Gulmohar

Located just a short stroll away from the main gate. 

          

           



Sports Complex-



The institute has two swimming pools; football, hockey and cricket grounds; and tennis, basketball, squash and volleyball courts.

It also has a Students' Activity Center (SAC) for various cultural and other extracurricular activities

           

          



  Canteens- 



Their area  total 15-18 canteens inside the campus.





 

 OAT-

There is an Open Air Theater (OAT) in the SAC. This is used for open-air programmes

        

  Gym- 



The campus also has a  gym inside. Thus are the best facilities which are provided by the institute.

           



Security-



IIT Bombay has a huge and effective security force that covers all buildings, including residential areas, gates, and other areas.

Security is present at an accessible distance from all points on the campus.



Extra Curricular activities



The Trivia Club



The Quiz Club, one of IIT Bombay's oldest and most well-established groups, meets every Wednesday at 9:00 p.m. in the Hostel-8 lounge. Each meeting includes questions on a wide range of subjects, including love and marriage, sports, food, India, cartoons, music, and history. The revival of quizzing and other literary activities at IIT Bombay is partly attributable to the Quiz Club. It also helps that trivia is well known and that outside quizzes are recycled.

            

         

The Speaker’s Club



This group specializes in debate, extempore speaking, elocution, and jamming, among other speaking competitions. The latter was a competition that IITB used to win. In-house competitions, presentations, and fundaes sessions with skilled jammers are additional events planned.

            



The Hobbies Club



Electronics, astronomy, HAM radio, and aeromodelling are among the interests represented in this group. Students can design, experiment with, and build various electrical circuits in the electronics room at SAC, which is available at all times. Even the necessary components are offered without charge. In addition, every Tuesday and Thursday from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m., lectures are presented.



The Scrabble Club



This group plans to host workshops, financing sessions, Scrabble-based puzzles, competitions, tournaments, etc. despite not yet having solid grounds. As soon as it hit the notice boards, you could hear it.



The Mountaineering Club



In order to provide a break from the demands of IITian life and to keep people in touch with Nature, the group often arranges weekend treks in the Sahyadris. Last semester, locations including Rajmachi, Kabubai, Bhimashankar, and Harishchandragad were visited. After 25 arduous days in the Himalayas, ten club members obtained a Basic Mountaineering Course Certificate last summer. The group intends to enroll more students in the course this summer.

Hostel no. 12 and 13 are newly made.

Campus facilities-



IIT Kanpur offers a vibrant campus life. It is a safe and secure campus. Campus provides to students a beautiful and peaceful campus..

  

 Library- 



IITK has an excellent well-equipped air-conditioned library with a high bandwidth computer network-enabled for accessing library e-resources round the clock from anywhere. It has a collection of 274,362 books,1955 journals







Sports Facilities-

Badminton Court

Basketball Court

Cricket Ground

Football Ground

Squash Court

Swimming Pool

Tennis Court

Volleyball Court

Skating Rinks







Classroom-

IITK provides a world-class classroom with high internet speed.

Classrooms are fully air-conditioned.

           



Auditorium-

The campus also has wide and amazing auditoriums. 



Gym-

The campus has a well-equipped gym for students to stay fit. 



OAT- 

 Campus has an open Air Theater (OAT) with a huge capacity, nearly 1400 people.

Various Functions are organized here every year.





Canteens-.

Every hall/ Hostel on the campus has a canteen and mess. 





 Extracurricular activities-



Adventures Club



The IIT Kanpur Adventure Sports Club serves as a gathering place for outdoor lovers, hikers, and climbers.This club's members seek to foster an adventurous attitude among IIT students.Wall climbing, mountain riding, and trekking are all organized activities.



Joggers Club



This club's goal is to encourage members to adopt daily workout regimens outside of their rooms.This club's members are of the opinion that "Running makes every day a good day."

The organization arranges frequent bike rides and jogs around the neighborhood.



BRaIN (Biological Research and Innovation Network)



Molecular biology, genetics and evolution, botany, and other disciplines of biology are among the interests of members of the BRaIN (Biological Research and Innovation Network) club. With the assistance of IIT Kanpur staff and guest lecturers from throughout the nation, the club hosts a number of talks and seminars.



Commercial Club



Bill Gates once said, "It's not a mistake to be born poor, but it's a mistake to die poor." This club adheres to this maxim. The Business Club's members want to educate, motivate, and inspire kids about business and entrepreneurship.



Insight360



The Journalist Society's Insight360 organization specializes in video journalism. It is a socially engaged media organization of IIT Kanpur that covers the most recent events on and around the campus. It attempts to provide viewers with a behind-the-scenes look at the happenings, pursuits, and daily life at IIT Kanpur. In order for experts to refine their craft and beginners to understand the nuances, it also organizes courses for video making and video jousting throughout the year.



Aero-modeling club



The craft of designing, creating, and flying miniature airplanes is known as aeromodelling (powered or unpowered). Any material, including paper and balsa wood, can be used to make a model plane. Model airplane construction and assembly are part of the pastime; flying is part of the sport.



Society of Automotive Engineers



The Society of Automotive Engineers, IIT Kanpur, was founded in January 2011 by a group of students who felt compelled to complement the theoretical information taught in the classroom by placing a significant emphasis on the practical aspects of engineering.



Gliders Club



Everyone aspires to the stars. Those who want to fly can temporarily do so by joining the gliders club. The group arranges rides for both campus residents and students. In addition, they have plans for aerial photography.



Robotics Club



The undergraduate robotics club at IIT Kanpur gives students the tools they need to build robots. Along with essential advice, courses, and tutorials, the Club also provides tools, equipment, components, and workspace.



Electronics Club



The motto of the Electronics Club is Innovation, Imagination, and Application. The Electronics Club is a pastime group that seeks to educate and assist individuals in comprehending the modern world's seemingly complex electronic devices.



Astronomy Club



This society looks after the campus children's and students' cosmological curiosity. The club's primary goal is to popularize amateur astronomy within the institution and to promote it. The members frequently organize lectures and discussions for the university community from students, faculty, and other recognised organizations such as IUCAA, IIA, etc.

INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, KHARAGPUR

                                      



IIT Kharagpur is the oldest of the Indian Institutes of Technology, having been established in 1951. It is also the largest and most prestigious.



IIT Kharagpur is known for its academic and research achievements, as well as its world-class faculty.

 Campus size- 2100 acres





Reaching IIT Kharagpur-

The Institute is about 10 minutes away from Kharagpur railway station.



Kharagpur can be reached in about 2 hours by train or 3 hours by car from the Howrah railway station of Kolkata.



IIT Kharagpur has the largest campus in all the IITs. It is nearly 2100 acres.
