Student Hostels-



Students are housed in dorms at NIT Puducherry, which is a residential institute.

NITPY now has two boy's hostels and one girl's hostel, both of which offer pleasant lodging and are conveniently placed near the academic area.

Over 800 students are now residing in the hostels.



Hostel name-



Barani Hostel (Boys Hostel -1)





Bhavani Hostel (Girls Hostel)



Moyar Hostel (Boys Hostel -2)



Hostel facility- 



Wi-fi facility throughout the buildings

Uninterrupted water and electricity (with DG power backup) supply

Health Care facility

Outdoor games facility

Indoor sports/games facilities

Air conditioner facility

Water Coolers and Geyser are installed on each block

Laundry services on chargeable basis

Fire extinguishers and other safety measures are taken care of

Caretakers, housekeeping and security personnel services are provided



Campus facilities-



Central library-



The Central Library of NIT Puducherry began operations in 2010 and now offers an engaging learning experience with the best possible environment for study and learning on all working days.

It features a great reading space with enough natural light and appropriate ventilation.



The Central Library provides information and knowledge resources through a well-balanced collection of books, journals, magazines, and newspapers, among other things.



Wi-Fi and a LAN connection are available at the library, allowing users limitless high-speed internet access.



For the courses given, sufficient copies of textbooks and reference books are obtained. In addition, books of broad interest to students, faculty, and staff have been purchased.





Mess facilities-



Breakfast, lunch, and supper are served at a central mess facility at the hostels.

Caterers are chosen through an open tender process to run the mess.



The mess is outfitted with cutting-edge equipment for preparing and serving food under sanitary settings.



The Institute provides all infrastructural facilities for all messes, including space, water and electrical connections, culinary appliances, water purifiers, and so on.



Medical facilities-



Within the campus there is a Health Centre.

One Medical Officer, two Nurses, and two ambulance drivers work at the NITPY health centre.

All students, staff, and faculty members, as well as their families, receive primary health care from them.

They have two observation beds and provide ambulance services 24 hours a day, seven days a week for any medical situations.



Guest Room-



On the NIT Puducherry campus, a guest house is being built to accommodate key visitors, guest lecturers, visiting teachers, and parents.

The guest house is equipped with modern amenities and infrastructure, ensuring that guests have a pleasant stay while visiting NITPY.



Banks Facilities-



The State Bank of India has a branch on the NIT Puducherry campus that provides financial services to the university's students and faculty.

Students can use the ATM to withdraw money from their bank accounts anytime they need it.



Extracurricular Activities-



Sports and Games







Students that participate in sports develop their personalities via improved physical, emotional, and social wellbeing. A true sportsman will be able to quickly observe, think things over, and make a choice. Sports and games will instil the interpersonal skills, teamwork, and cooperative effort necessary for an engineer to function at a high level in both their personal and professional lives.



Blood Donation Camps



NIT Puducherry students' blood drive at General Hospital Karaikal:





Cultural Committee:



The cultural committee works in collaboration with NITPY and Student Welfare. The annual cultural festival is put on by the cultural committee every odd semester (September/October). The cultural festival began as an intra-college event called "Utkarsha" in 2016 and was a great success. The cultural festival then began to reach the sky and carried on as an inter-college cultural festival beginning in 2017 under the name "Le Ciel." Every year, the festival attracts a large number of attendees in and around Karaikal, becoming a great success.







Some clubs of NIT Puducherry:



Photography Club

Literary Committee

Coding Club

Dance Club

Music Club

Art & Craft Club

Design Club

full_content:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, ARUNACHAL PRADESH

                                                       

  

The National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh (also known as NITAP) is a public technical and research institute in Itanagar, Arunachal Pradesh's capital.







NIT Arunachal Pradesh is one of India's 31 National Institutes of Technology and is designated as a National Institute of Excellence.



Important information regarding NIT Arunachal Pradesh-



Location-



Address- 

Doimukh Road, YUPIA, Nirjuli, Arunachal Pradesh 791109



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/ZSMBzq4fEiR2wBrv6



Campus size-178 acres





Reaching NIT Arunachal Pradesh-

The nearest railway station is Naharlagun, about 3 kilometres from the current Yupia campus.

Guwahati is the next closest railway station. It takes roughly 10 hours to travel from Guwahati to Naharlagun.

Student Hostels-



The campus of NIT Arunachal Pradesh is entirely residential. The institute provides housing for its students and employees.



The campus has a total of seven hostels.



Hostel facilities-



24 hours water supply and electricity

Common room

Campus facilities-



Library-



Many books, volumes, and editions in print exist in the Central Library of NIT Arunachal Pradesh, and they are relevant to various specialisations such as engineering, technology, humanities, management, science, and so on.



Students are given their unique id and passcode at the time of admission, allowing them to download various study materials and research papers as needed.



They can use these credentials to access the digital library.

NIT Arunachal Pradesh's central library was built in 2010 and has been operational since then.



Sports-



The NIT Arunachal Pradesh places a high value on sports, and the institute's sports cell encourages students to engage in the institute's many sporting events.



It also hosts an annual sports festival that is held on an intercollegiate level and invites students from various universities and colleges to compete in various sports events.



Cafeteria-



NIT Arunachal Pradesh provides a canteen service to day scholars, hostellers, and NIT-AP academics and employees. Students and members of the institute are provided with healthy and delicious food and beverages at reasonable pricing.



Auditorium-



NIT Arunachal Pradesh's seminar hall is utilised for academic activities such as guest lectures, training sessions, workshops, and seminars pertaining to various specialisations.

The seminar hall also hosts the convocation ceremony for new NIT-AP graduates.

The seminar hall at NIT Arunachal Pradesh is air-conditioned and equipped with all of the necessary audio-visual equipment.



Medical facility-



The NIT Arunachal Pradesh Medical Unit is made up of paramedical staff, a doctor, and an ambulance service that is available 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

When contacted and in the event of an emergency, the paramedical staff and doctor, as well as the ambulance service, are available.

The medical unit also provides the tools and instruments needed to perform simple procedures such as stitches and stitch removal.





Extracurricular Activities-



Electrical Engineering Student Society







A society for engineering students is called the Electrical Engineering Student's Society (EESS). With the help of activities like workshops, contests, and quizzes, the EESS aims to promote knowledge exchange among peers and discipline pioneers. The society is made up of a group of people who actively and voluntarily participate in advancing the discipline.







Coding Club









We are a passionate bunch of programmers from Arunachal Pradesh's National Institute of Technology. Code fear needs to be replaced with inspiration and learning drive. We think that programming can be a lot of fun and that developing quantitative abilities is crucial for career advancement. No matter what your field or level of expertise, Coding Club is for you. Anyone can meet new individuals at a coding club, regardless of their gender or background. Everyone has the right to education, and we think that when we support one another, learning is more enjoyable and effective.



Developer Student Club









The Developer Student Club at NIT Arunachal Pradesh is one of the thousands of clubs that make up Developer Student Clubs. Our DSC focuses on creating useful goods that address pressing issues in and around our society. Solving these issues presents chances for the emerging developer community to demonstrate their skills, grow, and acquire new ones. The DSC NIT Arunachal Pradesh is not physically constrained by the NIT Arunachal Pradesh. We want to empower developers in North-East India and establish a presence there as well as internationally. To better promote the developer community to the aspiring developers nearby, we host workshops, hackathons, and competitions.

Student Hostels-



In its temporary campus at Govt. Polytechnic, Srinagar (Garhwal) Uttarakhand, the National Institute of Technology, Uttarakhand, established in 2010, currently has seven hostels for boys and girls.



The following are the details of the hostels, as well as the number of rooms available:





Each hostel is equipped with water purifiers, water coolers, hot water geysers, and telephones, among other things.



Campus facilities-



Library-



E-books, periodicals, books, and other materials are available in the library. The library also has research journals published by researchers from across the world available for students and staff to use.

The library is also equipped with Internet access and is entirely digitalized.



Sports- 



Students can improve their physical and mental health by exercising and participating in sports.

As a result, the institute provides sports and gym facilities to its students, and they are encouraged to use them to their best potential.



Auditorium- 



The conference hall holds 70 people and is utilised for seminars, conferences, and workshops. This is where important speakers and guest speakers have their seminars.



Medical/Hospital-



In the event of a medical emergency, the institute provides sufficient medical facilities to students and personnel. Medical officers and nurses are on call 24 hours a day, 7 days a week to attend to the institute's medical needs.

In the medical room, basic medical equipment is piled for usage.



Extracurricular Activities-



The co-curricular and extracurricular possibilities available to students at NIT Uttarakhand are numerous. NIT Uttarakhand has established a very active campus life, conducting everything from field trips to factories like power plants and auto manufacturers to encourage students to visit other IITs for competitions and cultural and technical festivals to hosting its own large-scale annual festival. Numerous student clubs that are entirely run by students and supervised by faculty members make sure that the entire academic year is filled with a variety of events, activities, workshops, festivals, etc.The Executive Body of each club, which consists of a Club Secretary, a Joint Secretary (two in the case of the Literary Society), a Treasurer, and two Executive Body Members, is in charge of running the club (three in case of the Literary Society).



     



The Institute has a number of well-known clubs, some of which are Klick—the Photography Club, Movie Club, Verbatim—the Speakers' Club, Literary Society—the hub club for all literary activities, Robotics Club, Rocktave—the Music Club, Dance Club, SAE BAJA Club, Programmers' Club, etc. The Institute fosters students' writing abilities and creativity through its wall magazine, "Think Tank," and publishes its monthly newsletter "Techno Savvy." For the purpose of instructing pupils in a variety of outdoor and indoor sports, such as cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, and table tennis, the Institute also hires competent coaches and trainers.

NIT Delhi's aim is to develop human resources who are creative, competitive, and inventive, with a high level of intellect and ethical ideals. The Institute provides a holistic education to its pupils, as well as instilling good moral values in them.

Student Hostels-



Many of NIT Delhi's outstation candidates have access to a well-equipped hostel.

The boy's hostels are on the Institute's main campus in Narela, whilst the girl's hotel is on the Inside campus.

The hostel rooms are clean, and each student is provided with a bed, study table, cupboard, stool, and chair.

The hostel wardens are helpful and pleasant.







Hostel facilities-



24 hours water supply and electricity

Common room

Separate mess

24 hours security

Wi-Fi connectivity

Indoor games

Medical facility



4. Campus facilities-



Central library-



The National Institute of Technology Delhi's central library, which opened on June 10, 2012, was built in Dwarka to provide students and faculty with easier access to information resources.

On February 18, 2014, the central library was relocated to the Narela main campus.









The central library's services and facilities are available to all NIT Delhi students and academic members.

The library has a large book collection that is continually expanding in terms of both volume and genre of books to suit the information needs of the students.

Students have access to a reading room at the central library.



Guest room-



Outsiders and visitors to the institute's campus can stay in a guest home on campus.











Sports facilities-



Outdoor sporting facilities at the National Institute of Technology Delhi include a basketball court with floodlights, a football court, and a cricket concrete pitch with nets.



Indoor sports facilities are also available at the Institute for activities such as chess, caroms, and table tennis.



Canteen-



The institute's canteen is provided with the following amenities: modern kitchen appliances, water coolers with RO, curtain provision, and stainless steel furnishings, including insect killer.



Gym-



Students at the National Institute of Technology Delhi have access to a gym where they can stay physically active and healthy.



5. Extracurricular Activities-



NIT Delhi - Google Developer Student Club







The Google Developer Student Club is a programme for college students that aims to assist them develop their technical knowledge and abilities before finally putting those knowledge and skills into practice to create solutions and address real-world issues for nearby businesses and communities. Any student is welcome to enrol, from beginning developers to experienced developers looking to increase their knowledge. It is a place where students may study and work together in a peer-to-peer learning setting. The GDSC will host conferences on topics such as machine learning, DSA, web development, app development, and more.



IEEE NIT Delhi



The IEEE NIT Delhi Student Branch was recently founded with the goal of providing students with a platform to develop their technical and managerial skills. It was founded as a nonprofit organisation with the goal of fostering an atmosphere where teamwork and mutual learning are valued. Numerous branches organise webinars, seminars, conferences, workshops, and tutorials for the benefit of students.

Campus facilities-



Library-

Health facilities-



One doctor, one nurse, and one FA are working at NIT Mizoram's Health Care Center.

The Health Care Centre is open Monday through Saturday from 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

There are two hospital beds available, as well as emergency treatment equipment and medications.

It's in Block 5 of the Academic building.

The institute has leased out two (two) emergency vans for 24 hours a day, seven days a week, which are permanently deputed in the hostels with a driver to give proper health care and in case of medical emergencies.







Transportation facilities-



Seven (seven) buses have been rented by the institute to transport students sleeping in the dormitories.

These buses have been assigned to transport students from the hostel to the institution and back to the hostel on a daily basis.

 







Extra Curricular Activities-



HackerEarth Club -





To assist programmers in honing their talents, HackerEarth is creating the world's greatest centre of programmers. Programmers use HackerEarth to compete in coding competitions and hackathons and to solve challenges in a variety of computer scientific fields, including algorithms, data structures, machine learning, the internet of things, and artificial intelligence.



As the official coding platform of NIT Mizoram, it will provide NIT MZ students with the best coding environment so they can participate in the global coding ecosystem. Passionate algorithmist, geek coder, or novice, HackerEarth group values all members. 



Electrical Engineering Society-







In order to help students keep up with the rapidly evolving technology, the Electrical Engineering Society at NIT Mizoram encourages them to participate in a variety of activities such as technical seminars and quizzes on various areas of electrical engineering. The society also aims to foster interaction between students and faculty by hosting interactive events.



Other Clubs & Societies at NIT Mizoram:



Photography Club: DRISHTI

Annual Newsletter: INFINITY

NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, NAGALAND

                                                      



The National Institute of Technology (NIT) is a public research university Nagaland is a higher education technical institute in Chumukedima (Dimapur), Nagaland. It is designated as a National Institute of National Importance.







It is one of the ten new NITs recognised by the Indian government in 2009 under the 11th Five-Year Plan, and it began operations in the academic year 2010. Initially,

Student Hostels-



NIT Nagaland has six (6) hostels, which serve as a "home away from home" for students.



Within the NIT Nagaland campus, there are now four (4) sixty-four sat boy's hostels, one (1) fifty-six seated ladies hostel, and a brand new thirty-two (32) seater PG boys hostel.









The hostels are named after mountains that are significant in the area.

Saramati A, B, C. Presently occupied by the 4th year students.

Japfu - Occupied by the 2nd & 3rd year students.

Tiyi - Also occupied by 2nd & 3rd year students.

Patkai A, B, C - Exclusively meant for the 1st year students

Dzukou- Ladies Hostel (Both UG and PG).

Zanibu - PG Men's Hostel.

Campus facilities-



Library-



NIT Nagaland Library serves the institute's teaching, learning, research, and creative initiatives.

There are almost 9500 books in the collection.



It also includes around 1400 e-books.

In addition, the library offers the most up-to-date collections of periodicals, magazines, and newspapers.

The library's services are completely automated.



Sports-



The school has adequate sports facilities for both indoor and outdoor games.

Badminton, chess, carrom, and table tennis are all available.

Stadiums for football and cricket are currently being built and are scheduled to be completed soon.

There is a plan to build a multi-gym complex.



Medical facility- 



In the event of any medical difficulties, the institute provides medical services with doctor visits to its students and personnel.

The institute also provides a 24-hour ambulance service for medical situations.



Extra Curricular Activities-







Student clubs and associations are frequently involved in planning various activities and upholding a green atmosphere both within the Institute and beyond society. The institution is actively working to form a few student clubs that cover various topics.

These are clubs which are currently active at NIT Nagaland:



Robotics Club



Literary Club 



Eco Club



Coding Club



Technical Club

overview:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, ROURKELA
The Government of India funds NIT Rourkela, which is one of the country's premier national level technical education institutions.
Under the name National Institute of Technology, Rourkela, the Government of India has elevated the Regional Engineering College, Rourkela to a deemed university.
The Institute's principal goal is to develop high-quality engineers and scientists in many fields of engineering and science at the graduate and postgraduate levels.

Student Hostels-



The Institute is entirely residential, with housing available to all students.

There are 11 hostels in all at the Institute, with 7 boys hostels, 2 girls hostels, and 2 married hostels (only for research scholars)

In the table, the names of the halls, the kind of lodging, and the number of students are listed.

Hostel Facilities-



Students can use a variety of common facilities throughout the halls.

Common areas, reading rooms, sports grounds, gyms, music rooms, courtyards, and gardens are among them.



Campus facilities-



Central Library-



Students that are enrolled in the institute have access to the institute's library. Biju Patnaik Central Library is the full name of the library (BPCL).

It serves as the institute's principal learning resource centre, with extensive collections of both print and electronic resources.

Faculty, students, and research researchers can use the central library to support their teaching and research activities.







Guest Houses-



Within the Institute's grounds, there are two guest houses.

The director's residence is next to the guest houses. South Block is the one in front of the bungalow, while North Guest House is the one behind it.



These guest houses have air conditioning in every room.



Sports and Fitness-



The Institute offers world-class sporting facilities to its students, including a football, volleyball, and hockey stadium.





It also has a gym with all of the latest equipment. Two instructors are on hand to assist the students in the gymnasium 







A cricket facility with floodlights is also available at the institute.





There are two tennis courts on campus, as well as a basketball court with distinct times for male and female students.





Canteens-



There are two canteens on campus: the calorie canteen and the SLR canteen, which are located between the Audio Visual Hall and the CSE department.

On all working days, including Saturdays, the Campus Cafe is open from 8 a.m. to 20 p.m.

There are several South Indian and North Indian meals to choose from. Meals are available for a very low price during lunchtime.







Places of Worship-



The Hanuman temple, Shiva Temple, and Sri Rama Temple near the institute substation, and Mangala temple near the South block guest house, are the three temples on campus.





Shopping Centre-



There are two shopping centres on campus that cater to the needs of students and locals. It consists of:

Book Store

Mini-Departmental / Xerox Shop

Stationary Shop

Amul Parlour

Dairy Parlour

Grocery Shop

Vegitable Shop

Hair Cutting Saloons

Travel Agency





Health Centre-



Students have access to health care on campus.

The medical facilities are open 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

Any emergency will be attended to by an ambulance.







Post Office-



There is a Post Office on.

The post office is located near the new substation and is open from 7:00 a.m. to 17:00 p.m. on weekdays and 9.00 a.m. to 14:00 p.m. on Saturdays.

 



Banks & ATMs-



State Bank of India, the institute's only bank, is located on campus.

The State Bank of India operates two ATMs, one at the Main Gate and the other near the hostel. Inside the Institute's academic area, there is one AXIS bank ATM.





Extracurricular activity-



Technical society-

One of the most active organisations at NIT Rourkela's Student Activity Center is the Technical Society, which also sponsors a number of satellite groups. This society's mission is to support and foster technological innovations through student-led entrepreneurial endeavours by hosting modelling workshops, technical lectures, and training sessions. The several clubs of the Technical Society are active in the following fields: astronomy, automobiles, aerospace, robotics, fluid dynamics, software, infrastructure, mathematics, business management, and entrepreneurship. Every year, the Technical Society also puts on a national-level Techno-management festival that draws representatives from numerous illustrious institutions and organisations across the country. Overall, fostering wise caution among aspiring engineers is essential for an advanced India.



SAC-

NIT Rourkela encourages extracurricular activities in addition to academic success to ensure the whole development of the personality. The main hub for student activities, student groups, programmes, and events, fitness and intramural sports, reaction and fun on the Institute campus is the Student Activity Center (SAC). Several significant clubs and four major societies are under the administration of the Student Activity Center. Additionally, each society has a few clubs that are run independently but are nonetheless overseen by the SAC. For the benefit of students, instructors, staff, alumni, and visitors, the facility is committed to student entertainment and interaction.



Literary and cultural society-

The performing arts have a long history in India. Numerous of our plays have always had as their theme the great Indian epics. The most concise definition of the performing arts includes dance, music, theatre, and literature. The Literary and Cultural Society and the Film and Music Society are the two most major performing arts societies at NIT Rourkela, both of which are under the auspices of the Student Activity Center.



Film and music society-

The importance of entertainment in reviving drained minds and rekindling enthusiasm is crucial. That is taken care of in this culture. Its duties include inviting professionals to train our students in a variety of music and dance styles, regularly showing films for staff and students in the Bhubaneswar Behera Auditorium, hosting an annual photography competition, hosting a celebrity night during the cultural festival, organising an annual music night, and facilitating practise sessions for the students. Numerous dancing clubs and music groups fall within its ambit, offering up-and-coming engineers a rare opportunity to experiment and amuse others.

full_content:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, SIKKIM

                                          



The National Institute of Technology Sikkim (NIT Sikkim or NIT SKM) is a public engineering and research institute in Sikkim, India, near Ravangla.

It is one of India's 31 National Institutes of Technology, and the Government of India has designated it as an Institute of National Importance. 



It is a self-governing institute that operates under the Ministry of Education (Shiksha Mantralaya) of the Indian government.

Student Hostels-



Despite the fact that the institute is operating on a temporary site, all students have access to residential facilities.

Separate housing facilities for males and girls have been established in distinct hostels.



BOYS HOSTEL:



NIT Sikkim has a total of ten (10) boys hostel structures. Six of the nine structures are located on campus, while the other four are located outside.



Prefab 1,2: Each of these hostels has two levels with 32 rooms (16 on each floor) and four washroom spaces (2 in each floor).

Each of these hostels houses a whole year's worth of students.

Each of the hostels also has a common room.

Prefab 1 also features a boy's gym so the boy's students and instructors may stay in shape.





Boys hostels 3,4,5,6: There are six rooms in each of these hostel structures, each with its own bathroom and toilet.

A single year has taken up residence in all four hostels.





Boys hostels 10,11,12,13: These dormitories are located outside of the College Campus and are home to first-year B-Tech and M-Tech students. There are various rooms with associated bathrooms and toilets in these hostels.





GIRLS HOSTEL:



All of the girl's hostels are similar to the boy's hostels 3,4,5,6 in that they have rooms with adjoining bathrooms and toilets.

The number of people who can stay in the rooms is determined by the size of the rooms and the hostel.





Campus facilities-



Library-



The institute is still in the process of establishing a well-equipped and digitised library. They do, however, have a regular library for students' academic learning and reference needs.







Sports-



Students have access to a variety of sports facilities to aid in their overall wellness.

The institute has both outdoor and indoor gaming equipment, which students are encouraged to use in order to stay active.



Medical facility-



The institute provides emergency and primary health care services to students, employees, and other on-campus residents in its medical unit, which is located within the campus, amidst the residential and academic zones, 24 hours a day, seven days a week to meet the needs of any health emergencies on campus.



Extra Curricular Activities-







Aayas  



The official community development organisation at NIT Sikkim is called Aayas (a prayas). It was started in 2014 by the class of 2012–2016. Under the auspices of this organisation, the students have since performed numerous acts of social service. The society constantly aspires to excellence in the growth of the neighbourhoods around and around the university. The focus of the students, with assistance from the professors, is on the socially underdeveloped neighbourhoods close to the university, which helps to advance the state's and the nation's overall development. 



ILLUZION DANCE CLUB



The fraternities on campus are the heart of college or university life. These organisations act as a student's second home, encouraging personal development and the discovery of hidden skills outside of the classroom. NIT Sikkim offers students the chance to discover and pursue their abilities and interests as well as being a haven for geeks and nerds. Similar clubs where students can practise, teach, and perform dance include Illuzion Dance Club. The club's major goal is to rock the crowd and light up the stage at all campus festivals and events.



The Photography Club



The majority of the club members are amateur photographers who gather together to support one another as they learn the craft and pursue photography as a fun pastime. The club works very hard to promote the success of emerging and talented photographers. The photography club is considering a vision that includes the expression of intense emotion in all activities that could aid the locals in pursuing their passion in photography. Our top priority would be igniting enthusiasm and interest within the entire NIT Sikkim family.



YANTRIKAA



The society is constantly working hard to extend and deepen its roots so that it will be stable as it advances farther. It consistently strives for the entire growth of the mechanical engineering department students. The club hosts a variety of events, including technical quizzes, debates, workshops, and games where all students are welcome to join and showcase their skills, enhancing their overall profiles.

NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, ANDHRAPRADESH

Campus facilities-



Central library-



Students and staff at the institute have access to a central library where they can get information for their projects.

Thousands of books and publications from around the world are housed in the library.

Students can also use the library to broaden their knowledge by browsing the books and publications available.



Sports-



The institute provides sports facilities, including indoor and outdoor game equipment, for student sports activities.

All of the necessary facilities are supplied because athletic activities are beneficial to a student's entire growth and mental health.

Students are strongly encouraged to use the athletic facilities.









Dispansary-



The Institute employs one full-time medical officer and five other paramedical personnel to care to the medical requirements of students, staff, and their families, and the health centre is located on campus. Individuals can be evacuated to the nearest State Government hospitals in the event of an emergency.





Extra Curricular Activities-



Entrepreneurship & Innovation Cell



Innovation and entrepreneurship, in the opinion of the E & I Cell of NIT Andhra Pradesh, are complementary. Our goal is to instil in the pupils an inventive and entrepreneurial mindset. The cell constantly offers assistance to aspiring businesspeople and creative minds. We begin by identifying concepts and work to develop them. The E&I-Cell at NIT Andhra Pradesh is committed to encouraging creative thinking and an entrepreneurial spirit among its students. We offer a platform for innovative and flamboyant minds to project their managerial, technological, and scientific abilities and assist them in shaping their ideas into reality.



Shrestha Club



Shrestha Club is a platform for students to harness their potential and energy toward becoming "Shrestha," or "A Leader," in society. Our goal is to provide the students with the principles and skills they need to succeed as individuals, contribute significantly to society, and help India become a global leader. For the purpose of enhancing leadership and personality abilities, Shrestha Club will host lectures and workshops. We try to teach the young people skills and strategies that will give them an advantage in the fierce competition of today.



PRAYATNAM 



PRAYATNAM makes an effort to support students' overall growth, together with the appropriate mindset supported by an analytical mind. Our main goal is to raise the standard of education, guarantee that all children receive an education, and give students career guidance on the professions they can pick so that their possibilities are not constrained.



GRAPHIC CAFE



The name itself is a story; "Graphic" stands for imagination, and "Cafe" represents bringing people together. This journey began for us in 2019. construction of a stage for presenting the abilities of our college. to create a conduit through which the world will hear our voice. The team has two wings (DESIGN, DEVELOPERS) dedicated to fostering innovative ideas and two wings (SOCIAL MEDIA, NETWORKING) devoted to fostering human connections. On YouTube, we have set up a Student Media channel. Our group works on preserving the flow of knowledge within the campus and building a connection to the outside world. Information about all the events and activities is available on all of our social media platforms.

overview:
National institutes of technology, WARANGAL
Regional Engineering College, presently known as National Institute of Technology, Warangal (Deemed University), was founded in 1959.
On October 10, 1959, Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru laid the foundation stone for this institute, the first of 30 NITs (previously known as RECs) across the country.
The Institute is well-known for its dedicated teachers and staff, as well as its cutting-edge infrastructure that promotes a healthy academic environment.
The Institute is always working to improve its technical capabilities.

full_content:
National institutes of technology, WARANGAL

                                               



Regional Engineering College, presently known as National Institute of Technology, Warangal (Deemed University), was founded in 1959.

On October 10, 1959, Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru laid the foundation stone for this institute, the first of 30 NITs (previously known as RECs) across the country.



The Institute is well-known for its dedicated teachers and staff, as well as its cutting-edge infrastructure that promotes a healthy academic environment.

The Institute is always working to improve its technical capabilities.



Important information regarding NIT Warangal-



Location-



Address-

National Institute of Technology Campus, fatima nagar, Telangana 506004



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/HVZmxb7e8Un1KbNG8



Campus size- 248 acres. 









Reaching NIT Warangal-

The railway station that is closest to NIT Warangal is Kazipet. The campus is 3 kilometres away.

The Institute has excellent residential facilities for all the students admitted to the various courses. 

There are a total of 22 hostel blocks in which 20 hostel blocks (halls of residence) for men and 2 for women.

Campus facilities-



Central Library-



The Central Library's services and operations are totally computerised. Koha software was used to establish a database of all Library acquisitions.

There are around 1,79,797 books, back volumes, technical papers, standards, CD-ROMs, Video Cassettes, e-books, and other items in the collection.











Sports facilities-



The Department of Physical Education provides training for all of the Institute's students and employees.

It offers fantastic infrastructure for both outdoor and indoor sports and games.



Outdoor games-

Volleyball

Football

Cricket

Tennis

Ball Badminton

Kabaddi

Kho-Kho

Handball

Hockey

Tennikoit

Basketball (concrete) and Tennis  

 

 







Indoor games-

Chess

Caroms

Gymnastics

Shuttle badminton with wooden flooring





Table Tennis

Weight Lifting and 16 & 12 stations multi-Gyms.





Canteens-



The Institute offers two canteens that serve high-quality meals.

The Staff Canteen at NIT is now open.







Bank & ATMs- 



The State Bank of India is located next to the main gate of the institute. The bank has a regular presence on campus and is housed in its own facility.



The State Bank of India has two ATMs, one within the bank and one outside the Sports Stadium.

ATM-I (In the Bank Premises) ATM-II (Outside the Bank Premises) (Inside the campus at the stadium).





Health care centre-



Within the site lies the Institute Health Centre.

The NITW Health Centre is a full-fledged facility with three full-time regular medical officers and one contract part-time doctor, as well as paramedical personnel (Staff Nurse, ANM, OPD Assistant, Dresser, Lab Technician).







Post Office-



There is a Post Office at the Institute. The NITW campus is a postal delivery office that is part of a separate postal zone.

The post office is located next to the Boys Hostels (12th Block Visvesvaraya Hall) and is open every day of the week.





Shopping complex-



The Institute includes a single shopping centre that serves the requirements of students and residents alike.

The following facilities are available at the Student Facility Complex, which is located next to the Transportation Section:

Laundry Shop

Hair Cutting Saloon

Uma Xerox Shop

Each of these remain open regularly from 9 to around 8pm.



Eateries have been established in strategic locations across the campus, including the hostel blocks. Coffee Day Express, Nescafe, Bru, and Amul are among the stores that stay open for more than 10 hours.



Extracurricular activity- 



 Music club-

A vibrant and exclusive group of musically talented students join together in the Music Club to compose captivating compositions. The club was founded with the intention of showcasing the musical talent of as many students as possible, and it has continued in this tradition over time.



 Dancing Club-

The DND club is an organisation made up of a committed group of people that are always eager to put on a well-choreographed show for the continuously interested crowd at our college and would try everything they can to make that happen. They excel in all dance genres and have a broad group of students that are passionate about dan from mass to modern to b boying. When it's time to turn up the heat and keep up with the rhythm, the only person you need to be is yourself.ce, 

Literary and Debating Club- The Literary and Debating Club has 65 members, making it the largest collegiate student organisation. It consists of three student organisations that coordinate events in Telugu, Hindi, and English while cooperating to help students in the areas of speaking and writing by organising fun and educational activities.



Quiz club-

Among the NIT Warangal student clubs, The Quiz Club takes pleasure in being one of the most active and well-liked groups.

The group, which has twenty members, is committed to creating and running top-notch quizzes for the campus's student body.

hostel_info:
There are 13 hostels in total, with 11 for men and two for women.

More than 6000 people can currently be accommodated in all hostels and flats.

For guest lodging, there is a separate hostel-international hostel. Another massive boys' hostel is almost set to open.
campus_facilities:
Library:
The campus features a big central library with over 100,000 books, making it one of India's largest technical libraries. It has almost 200 print journals on its subscription list.

Nalanda (Network of Automated Library and Archives), the institute's digital library, houses online materials.

Nalanda provides access to over 17,000 journals, conferences, databases, electronic theses, dissertations, and online courses to NITC and networked institutions.

Sports:
Outdoor activities like as tennis, football, volleyball, badminton, rollerblading, hockey, and basketball are all available at NITC, which also boasts a gymnasium, swimming pool, open-air theatre, and auditorium. It also features a cricket ground that has hosted Ranji Trophy matches.

National institutes of technology, CALICUT
The National Institute of Technology Calicut (NIT Calicut or NITC), originally known as Regional Engineering College Calicut, is a public technical university and a national institute governed by the NIT Act, which was established by India's Parliament.
It is one of the Government of India's National Institutes of Technology campuses, which provide high-quality technical education to students from all across the country.

National institutes of technology, CALICUT

                                               



The National Institute of Technology Calicut (NIT Calicut or NITC), originally known as Regional Engineering College Calicut, is a public technical university and a national institute governed by the NIT Act, which was established by India's Parliament.



It is one of the Government of India's National Institutes of Technology campuses, which provide high-quality technical education to students from all across the country.

Student Hostels-



There are 13 hostels in total, with 11 for men and two for women.

More than 6000 people can currently be accommodated in all hostels and flats.

For guest lodging, there is a separate hostel-international hostel. Another massive boys' hostel is almost set to open.











Hostel facilities-



Common facilities-



Every hostel has a common room and recreation centre with newspapers, magazines, TVs, and indoor sports/games equipment.

Almost all hostels offer free internet and computational resources 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.



Facilities in boys' hostel- 



Basketball court, volleyball court, table tennis court, shuttle court, mini football pitch, cricket net, open gym for first-year students, and an extension of the Co-operative store are all available in the Men's Hostel.

In the hostels, there are also two power laundries.



Facilities in girls' hostel- 



The female hostel has a ladies gym, a stitching and tailoring centre, a hostel library, a fitness parlour, an indoor sports centre, a DTP centre, an ice cream shop, and a co-operative store extension. In addition, the Ladies Hostel has a night canteen and an Amul kiosk.



Campus facilities-



Central Library-



The campus features a big central library with over 100,000 books, making it one of India's largest technical libraries. It has almost 200 print journals on its subscription list.

Nalanda (Network of Automated Library and Archives), the institute's digital library, houses online materials.

Nalanda provides access to over 17,000 journals, conferences, databases, electronic theses, dissertations, and online courses to NITC and networked institutions.











Guest house-



On a fee basis, relatives/guests of hostel inmates can stay in chargeable guest rooms in each hostel.





Mess-



There are 14 messes linked with men's dormitories supplying cuisine with a broad variety of menus, of which C mess is south Indian vegetarian and B mess is north Indian vegetarian, and 4 messes in ladies hostel catering to the needs of students from all over India.







In addition to the main messes, night messes are open until midnight, allowing students to eat tiffin and snacks while studying overnight.







Sports-



Outdoor activities like as tennis, football, volleyball, badminton, rollerblading, hockey, and basketball are all available at NITC, which also boasts a gymnasium, swimming pool, open-air theatre, and auditorium. It also features a cricket ground that has hosted Ranji Trophy matches.











Health care-



The Institute's own Health Centre, which has ten beds, provides medical services.

To look after the health needs of the campus inhabitants, there are two Medical Officers—a Lady Medical Officer and a Resident Medical Officer—and skilled nursing personnel.









Extracurricular activity-



Literary and Debate Club: 

This group hosts a debutante celebration for freshmen. More than your literary and debate abilities, the club depends on your level of street smarts. Most of the talks in this organisation centre on socially significant problems, and you'll meet some true campus gems with impeccable grammar and a wide vocabulary.



Indian Cultural Association-

There is no connection between this club and Indian culture. They hold an occasion named "Sangam," which features a lot of fascinating competitions. The title Mr. and Ms. Personality would be given to the winners. Shouting out "I am a sexy bitch" to demonstrate your confidence is one of the competition's challenges. ICA also hosts a kite-flying competition.



Dance & Theater Forum: 

This club has some serious talent, with dance and drama skills accounting for at least 75% of selection criteria. They host a week-long programme that concludes with the dance performances "Resplend" and the fashion display "Couture Boulevard." It serves mostly as a stage for senior year students to dance off their four-year-old angst. MBA and M.Tech students are also permitted to participate in the discussion.



ISTE (Indian Society for Technical Education): 

The event, named Spectrum, is really intriguing. They held the Drishti All India Meet in 2017. Despite its name, ISTE is not truly a technical organisation.

Campus facilities-
campus_facilities:
The campus community of NIT Durgapur is encouraged to live a healthy and active lifestyle, with an emphasis on both physical and psychological well-being.
By responding to the needs of its staff and students, the Medical Unit prioritises intensive care and sensitive treatment.

Library:
The Institute's study, teaching, research, and development programmes are all supported by the Library, which is one of the Institute's most important primary facilities.
It is housed in a three-story building with a plinth area of 1000 square metres.
The library has 1.7 lakh volumes of literature, including Text Books, Reference Books, and Bound Volumes of Journals, Standards, and other materials.

Sports:
Excellent facilities are available on campus, including well-equipped multi-gyms for boys and girls with different timetables.
This contains the magnificent football stadium as well.
The principal sports ground, known as the 'Oval,' hosts all of the major games throughout the year, including volleyball courts with floodlights.
Cricket practise pitch made of concrete
Basketball court made of concrete with floodlights
Tennis court made of concrete with floodlights
Badminton courts made of concrete with floodlights
Table tennis, chess, yoga, and other sports are available.
Aside from the central sporting facilities, most residence halls have volleyball and concrete badminton courts, as well as Table Tennis and Carrom board amenities.
Extracurricular activity-
Centre For Cognitive Activities-
The CCA, or Center for Cognitive Activities, is the hub where all of the students' technical and scientific endeavours come together to materialise. This club is a revolution that bridges the knowledge and application gaps as the technical gymkhana of the institute.

Dance Club-
The Nitdgp dance club is called Dance Club. Along with performing at college festivals like Independence Day, Republic Day, and Carpe Diem, we also compete in intercollegiate competitions, winning medals for our school. We support and perform all dance forms because we value diversity. We "work hard and party harder," as the saying goes.

Darpan-
They are the NIT Durgapur Hindi Society, actively working to promote "Hindi," the national language of India. Darpan is excited to see how participants in various programmes progress on a whole. In addition to medals, cash prizes, and citations to recognise their achievement, they offer students important and practical venues for the marketing of their skill.

Debating Society-
The official debate club at NIT Durgapur is the Debating Society. This club's main objective is to encourage university debate culture and dispel the fear of public speaking. It is essentially an ideological interest organisation. We strive to make young people articulate enough to express their opinions in a cogent and rational manner, as well as to inspire them to actively engage in topics of social interest and importance.

Enteract-
The official Drama Club of NIT Durgapur is Enterac

full_content:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, DURGAPUR

                                        



The National Institute of Technology, Durgapur (previously Regional Engineering College, Durgapur) was founded in 1960 by an Act of Parliament as one of eight such colleges with the goal of setting the standard for engineering education in the country and promoting national unification.







It is a Government of India-funded leading technological institution that is governed by an independent Board of Governors.



Important information regarding NIT Durgapur-



Location-



Address-

Mahatma Gandhi Rd, A-Zone, Durgapur, West Bengal 713209



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/UrHz7GZuDbkKqdWA7





Campus size- The Institute is set across 187 acres of land.







Reaching NIT Durgapur-



Durgapur Railway Station approximately 9 kilometres (5.6 miles) or 4.9 nautical miles from Nit Durgapur.

The journey from Durgapur Railway Station to Nit Durgapur takes 10 minutes.

The distance between Durgapur Railway Station and Nit Durgapur is around 9 kilometres (5.6 miles).

Darpan-

They are the NIT Durgapur Hindi Society, actively working to promote "Hindi," the national language of India. Darpan is excited to see how participants in various programmes progress on a whole. In addition to medals, cash prizes, and citations to recognise their achievement, they offer students important and practical venues for the marketing of their skill.



Debating Society-

The official debate club at NIT Durgapur is the Debating Society. This club's main objective is to encourage university debate culture and dispel the fear of public speaking. It is essentially an ideological interest organisation. We strive to make young people articulate enough to express their opinions in a cogent and rational manner, as well as to inspire them to actively engage in topics of social interest and importance.



Enteract-

The official Drama Club of NIT Durgapur is Enteract. It is a haven for everyone who enjoys theatre and nukkad. With arguably professional ability, Enteract delivers a wide range of performances, from street plays and stage plays to Monoacts and mimes.



Enterpreneurship Development Cell-

The NIT Durgapur Entrepreneurship Development Cell, formerly known as the NEN NIT Durgapur Students Chapter, is the institution's sole independent cell of significant national significance that fosters an entrepreneurial culture on campus.





GNU Linux Users' Group-

A group of GNU/Linux users who support the usage of Free and Open-Source Software is known as the GNU/Linux Users Group, NIT Durgapur. A group of FOSS supporters founded the Group in 2003 with the goal of promoting and advancing open source.



IEEE Student Branch NIT Durgapur-

The IEEE Student Branch, NIT Durgapur is a group of enthusiasts focused at fostering research related activities on the campus. They are an official partner with IEEE, a global organisation that annually gives countless young researchers the chance to present and publish their inventions.





Music Club-

They are NIT Durgapur's recognised music club. Through live concerts, we share our love of music with everyone at this campus. They also reach out to those beyond the college via our Facebook and YouTube channel. They play music in both Eastern and Western styles.

address: S Ambazari Rd, Ambazari, Nagpur, Maharashtra 440010

courses_offered.name: BTech
courses_offered.eligibility: Passed Class 12 from a recognised board with a minimum of 60% aggregate (55% for SC/ ST/ PwD candidates)

courses_offered.name: MTech
courses_offered.eligibility: A four-year bachelor’s degree with a CGPA of 8.0 (7.0 for ST/SC students)

courses_offered.name: MSc
courses_offered.eligibility: Passed Class 12 from a recognised board and a bachelor's degree of at least 3 years

courses_offered.name: PhD
courses_offered.eligibility: masters in any discipline with first-class or 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/PD)from a recognised university
courses_offered.entrance_exam: UGC NET/ CSIR NET/ JRF, written test and PI round

courses_offered.name: B. Arch
courses_offered.eligibility: Passed Class 12 from a recognised board with a minimum of 60% aggregate (55% for SC/ ST/ PwD candidates

hostel_info:
More than a thousand students are housed at V.N.I.T., which provides outstanding residential amenities.

Seven Boys Hostels and four Girls Hostels are available.

Each hostel is equipped with a telephone, television, sports equipment, water coolers, water heaters, and fully equipped mess facilities.

Every hostel has its own mess where vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals are served.
campus_facilities:
Cricket, football, basketball, kabaddi, lawn tennis, and badminton are some of the sports played at the VNIT Nagpur sports complex.
There is a yoga hall with a capacity of 100 people and a cardio fitness hall with a capacity of 100 people.
The students have access to a swimming pool as well.

Library:
The VNIT Library is a three-story, self-contained facility that dates back five decades.
The book and periodical collections have been systematically built up over time.

Sports:
Cricket, football, basketball, kabaddi, lawn tennis, and badminton are some of the sports played at the VNIT Nagpur sports complex.
There is a yoga hall with a capacity of 100 people and a cardio fitness hall with a capacity of 100 people.
The students have access to a swimming pool as well.

overview:
VISVESVARAYA NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, NAGPUR
Nagpur's Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology (VNIT) is a state-run technological institute.
The institute was founded in 1960 and is one of the country's 31 National Institutes of Technology (NITs).
The institute is named after prominent engineer, planner, and statesman Sir M. Visvesvaraya, and was previously known as Visvesvaraya Regional College of Engineering (VRCE).

full_content:
VISVESVARAYA NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, NAGPUR

                                           

Nagpur's Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology (VNIT) is a state-run technological institute.

The institute was founded in 1960 and is one of the country's 31 National Institutes of Technology (NITs).





The institute is named after prominent engineer, planner, and statesman Sir M. Visvesvaraya, and was previously known as Visvesvaraya Regional College of Engineering (VRCE).



Important information regarding NIT Nagpur-



Location-



Address-

S Ambazari Rd, Ambazari, Nagpur, Maharashtra 440010



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/bvD4RXfBxePhjkaa8



Campus size-225 acres.







Reaching VNIT Nagpur-

The nearest railway station to VNIT Nagpur is AJNI station. The campus is only 1.11 kilometres away. The campus is 1.52 kilometres from Nagpur intersection.



Courses offered by VNIT Nagpur-



Courses

Eligibility

Course fee

BTech



9 Streams-



Computer Science And Engineering



Mechanical Engineering



Electrical And Electronics Engineering



Metallurgical And Materials Engineering



Electronics & Communication Engineering



Civil Engineering



Mining Engineering

Student Hostels-



More than a thousand students are housed at V.N.I.T., which provides outstanding residential amenities.

Seven Boys Hostels and four Girls Hostels are available.

Each hostel is equipped with a telephone, television, sports equipment, water coolers, water heaters, and fully equipped mess facilities.

Every hostel has its own mess where vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals are served.











Campus facilities-



Library-



The VNIT Library is a three-story, self-contained facility that dates back five decades.

The book and periodical collections have been systematically built up over time.









Guest house-



Visitors or guests to the VNIT Nagpur campus can stay in a guest house.



Sports facilities-



Cricket, football, basketball, kabaddi, lawn tennis, and badminton are some of the sports played at the VNIT Nagpur sports complex.

There is a yoga hall with a capacity of 100 people and a cardio fitness hall with a capacity of 100 people.

The students have access to a swimming pool as well.









Gymnasium-



The sports centre complements the institute's VNIT gymnasium service, which is primarily for students interested in fitness activities.

It is available to students for a set length of time.







Health care-



The VNIT Nagpur Health Centre primarily emphasises holistic healthcare and preventative measures through education. The majority of activities such as OPD, yoga, meditation, and counselling are done on a regular basis.







Bank and ATM-



Within the campus, there are Canara and SBI bank branches.









Extracurricular activity-



A club at Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology is the Tesla Club of Innovation. A group of us are aspiring engineers. We strive to maximise our resources. Our club prefers to focus on fresh and unique initiatives.







Grooves Dance Society is the official dance club of VNIT which is a union of Team-911(Hip-Hop), Alaipayuthe(Classical), Versatile(Tollywood), Enigma(Bhangda) Dance Club of VNIT. The club conducts events all across the year and also represents the college in dance competitions outside of VNIT.







The social club at VNIT is called Prayaas. One cannot help everyone, but everyone can help someone, was our founding motto. We started out with 10 people in September 2006 and have since grown to hundreds who have gathered under one roof to help the less fortunate. Every ac MAG.COM. We're the official Magazine Committee (or since most of you recognize us by our swanky title: mag.com, we'd prefer to use that in substitution) of VNIT. ...

E-Cell. ...

IEEE. ...

Astronomy Club. ...

IV Labs. ...

Tesla Club of Innovations. 

We engage in fostering a sense of obligation to advance both our own personal growth and that of society. Teaching, blood donation drives, Joy of Giving Week, NGO visits, science exhibitions for Nagpur schoolchildren, and many other initiatives we are involved in are what motivates us to take the next step. You belong here if you believe that you need a platform to connect with others and make an impact.

overview:
MALVIYA NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, JAIPUR
Malaviya National Institute of Technology Jaipur (MNIT or NIT Jaipur) is an Indian public technical institution that focuses on science, engineering, and management.
It changed its name to Malaviya Regional Engineering College (MREC) Jaipur in 2002 and was designated as an Institute of National Importance in 2007.

full_content:
MALVIYA NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, JAIPUR

                                                    

  

Malaviya National Institute of Technology Jaipur (MNIT or NIT Jaipur) is an Indian public technical institution that focuses on science, engineering, and management.







It changed its name to Malaviya Regional Engineering College (MREC) Jaipur in 2002 and was designated as an Institute of National Importance in 2007. 



Important information regarding MNIT Jaipur-



Location-



Address-

Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/zi5wz4x2HLXmd99V8





Campus size- 317 acres







Reaching MNIT Jaipur-

The nearest railway station to MNIT Jaipur is Gandhinagar Jaipur station. The campus is 2 kilometres away.

Campus facilities-



Central Library-



The Institute features a large and well-equipped library that operates under an open access system.



It has a large collection of books, periodicals, reports, and reference materials, totaling over 1,33,600 volumes.



The library is undergoing a complete computerization project.. 









Guest house-



There are two guest houses on campus to accommodate visitors and students.







Canteen-



Students and workers can get refreshments at a canteen near the instructional zone, another near the dorms, and a Nescafe coffee shop during and after working hours.







Dispensary-



A full-fledged Dispensary is available in the campus. 

It is supervised by a residential Medical Officer. 

The services of a part-time Homoeopathy Doctor and a Lady Doctor are also available to students and to the family members of the staff.





Bank and post office-



On campus, there is a full-fledged dispensary.

A resident Medical Officer oversees the programme.

Students and family members of the faculty can also use the services of a part-time Homoeopathic Doctor and a Lady Doctor.







Sports complex-



Students at MNIT have access to a variety of sporting facilities.

The following are the details:



Outdoor Games-



Basketball (Two Courts with flood lights)







Volleyball (Three Courts with flood lights)

Tennis (Three Courts with flood lights)

Cricket (Two cemented pitch and one cricket pitch)



Football (Two Fields)

Athletic

 

Indoor Games-



Badminton (One Court)

Billiard (One Table)

T. T.

Chess

Gym



Extra Curricular Activities -



The MNIT CACS (Creative Arts & Cultural Society) provides a thorough platform that is user-friendly for students for the exploration of talent, development of leadership abilities, and teamwork. It encourages the students to demonstrate their abilities outside of the classroom by taking part in numerous activities that are planned during the semesters.The top entries from each competition are displayed in the annual technical (SPHINX) & cultural fest (BLITZSCHLAG).



The following is a list of the numerous societies that make up CACS:



Music and Spic Macay - The musicians, singers, and dancers want to come here. This group offers a clear forum for people to demonstrate their enthusiasm for performing arts that interest them. The club also houses Spic Macay, a group dedicated to preserving the history and culture of the nation. For the revival of our nation's cultural history and pride, renowned celebrities are also frequently invited to school.





Dramatics Society - Dramatics Society is another vibrant group at MNIT Jaipur that attracts a lot of members. For students interested in acting, directing, stage management, writing, and designing or creating sets, lights, sound, and costumes, it provides a wealth of opportunities. Drama Is Life, the club's name, organises creative events all year round.



Fine Arts - This club is the epitome of originality and ingenuity. It attempts to enhance the inventiveness and creative aspects of aspiring engineers. Over the course of the year, many activities like designing and crafting are introduced.







Technical - As technical college students, the most useful skill we learn is how to muck around with the int, float, and char to become proficient coders. Geek culture supports the geeks by introducing them to coding, ethical hacking, and other technical pursuits. Additionally, participation and mutual support among the registered students are encouraged.



Film and Photography Club - A portion of the universe is saved into our sight when the constantly changing, unending globe is captured in a frame in the space of a few nanoseconds. Thus, photographic talents play a significant role in this to capture some breathtaking aspects of nature, art, love, compassion, environment, and many more. For the students to experiment with camera lenses, this group sets a minimum standard.







Literary Society - Until they are given a correct meaning and the proper tone, words are only a collection of letters. Students have the chance to develop their interests through the Literary Society, where poets, artists, and other creative people demonstrate their talents through a variety of activities like debates and impromptu performances. The techniques for overcoming stage fright and becoming confident public speakers are taught to students.



Student Mentoring Program (SMP) - Through the student mentoring programme, senior students mentor and guide junior students in a constructive and productive manner. Since the mentor has more experience than the mentee, they work together to hasten the mentee's overall development. Through mentoring, senior undergraduates who have walked in their footsteps before are matched with first-year students.



NSS - Members of the National Service Scheme work with slums, communities, and nonprofit organisations to complete 120 hours of regular tasks over the course of an academic year. A volunteer maintains continual contact with society as part of the service programme. The participants choose a specific village or slum and engage with the locals and their way of life there.





Electoral Literary Club - Understanding Indian politics and attitude is essential in this busy period for democracy. The Electoral Literacy Club educates kids about democracy and facilitates debates about voting. It prepares pupils to become the kind of responsible citizens that the nation requires.



Web Portal Club - The Welfare of Students The student welfare officer and students are connected through a web portal. The club's members will work on the website platform, which offers frequent updates on the progression of events and activities. Students will feel more connected and knowledgeable about events on campus as a result.

full_content:
MOTILAL NEHRU NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, ALLAHABAD

                                                  



MNNIT (Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology Allahabad, Prayagraj) is an institute dedicated to academic excellence and quality.







It was founded in 1961 as a joint venture of the Government of India and the Government of Uttar Pradesh as one of India's seventeen Regional Engineering Colleges, and it was an associated institution of the University of Allahabad, India's third oldest university.



Important information regarding MNNIT Allahabad-



Location-



Address- 

Barrister Mullah Colony, MNNIT Allahabad Campus, Teliarganj, Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh 211004



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/zVHBRsMTf2AaBuVD7









Campus size- 222 acres



Reaching MNIT Allahabad-

The MNNIT Allahabad campus is located 8 kilometres from Prayagraj intersection (Allahabad junction). The train station provides excellent connections to metro cities including as Delhi, Kolkata (Howrah/Sealdah), Mumbai, and others.

Student Hostels-



The institute has a total of 9 hostels, with 7 hostels for boys and two hostels for girls.

The boys' hostel has a capacity of 1800 (1044 single seated rooms and 252 triple seated rooms), while the girls' hostel has a capacity of 145.

A chair, a study table, a tea table, a cot, a fan, a bulb connection, two socket outlets, and two shelves are provided in each hostel room.













Boys' hostel facilities- 



Every floor has geysers and water coolers with the aqua guard.

For STD/Local calls, use PCO.

Shop for books and stationery.

Internet access is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week over a 100 Mbps LAN.

Room for visitors.

Generator, which provides electricity to the hostels in the event of a power outage.

Canteen open 24 hours a day.

Newspapers and magazines are available.

Students' organisation.



Girls' hostel facilities-



Machine for washing clothes.

PCO is located within the hostel.

In the event of a power outage, a generator supplies electricity to the mess and common area.

In the hostel's grounds, there is a provisional goods store.

There are four computers.

Line for intercom.

Internet access is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week over a 100 Mbps LAN.

Geysers and a water cooler

Newspapers and magazines are available.

There were two motor pumps installed.

Refrigerator and air cooler





Campus facilities-



Library-



MNNIT Allahabad's Central Library has 1,22,670 books and bound periodical subscripts, including 14526 International e-Journals, 7998 €, and 29508 IEEE e-Conference Proceeding.

Within its restricted size, the library strives to provide the finest possible academic community.









Health centre-



All students, institute staff, and their dependents have access to the following services through the Institute Health Centre.

OPD

Pathology

Physiotherapy

Dental

ENT

Psychiatry

Homoeopathy

Ayurvedic 







Banking-



All students and employees have access to a good banking facility on campus, which includes an ATM.

The following banks are located on our campus:



VIJAY BANK ( MNNIT Branch ) Location Code IFSC SWIFT CODE Phone No. : Beside CAD / CAM LAB: 7184 : VIJB0007184 : VIRGIN ABRSM : 0532-2271137 









State Bank of India ( MNNIT Branch ) Location: Near Yamuna Gate



Mess and Canteen-



The Institute's campus offers outstanding eating establishments that offer affordable food and cafeteria products.

On campus, there are two cafeterias: Bikanervala Food and Amul India.

Under the overall supervision of wardens, the messes are overseen by nominated student representatives.

The international students have their own mess called an international mess. Non-vegetarian cuisine is also provided at this dinner.











Sports facilities-



Indoor table tennis, television, music room, robotics room, carrom board, and newspaper reading room are all available at the student activity centre (SAC).









A basketball court, two indoor badminton courts, lawn tennis courts, a football/cricket ground, a volleyball ground, and a gym are all available.









Post office-



The site also offers a post office service. It's close to the colony's entrance.







Extra Curricular Activities-



The extracurricular programmes at MNNIT are fantastic. Here, you can take part in a wide range of activities, some of which are as follows.



When you begin your college career at MNNIT, this is the very first event.



It's an occasion that gives freshly enrolled pupils a chance to showcase their extracurricular skills. Additionally, it promotes improved collaboration and communication between junior and senior students.



Since it is solely a student-focused event, second-year students serving as a committee of student coordinators are responsible for everything from choosing the events to running the event.







Secondly, The annual AVISHKAR technical event takes place after Swagat.

Among all inter-nit sporting events, MNNIT is the champion.







The most thrilling event of the entire Mnnit CULRAV. It is the MNNIT's yearly cultural festival, which is jam-packed with events.







In addition to these events, there are other other occasions like debate, essay writing, quiz competitions, handwriting competition, TED talks, Scout NCC. riddle-puzzle solving competitions, orations and many more.

The institute provides hostel accommodation for girls. Girls' hostels hired by Engineering College Ajmer Society  ensure privacy and homely atmosphere. 

The  rooms are neat and clean, spacious, airy and well ventilated with a sufficient number of toilets and bathrooms. 

The well balanced nutritious vegetarian food prepared in the kitchen is served in hygienic conditions. 

The hostels ensure congenial atmosphere for academic development of the students.









Campus facilities-



Swami Vivekanand Central library-



The college has Digital Library: 20 Computers available for the user to access e-journals and study material in the library and 07 for office use. 

Library Software: e-Granthalaya 3.0 library management software with OPAC facility.







Sports facilities-



The college has sports facilities for all students.







Mess-



The well balanced nutritious vegetarian food prepared in the kitchen is served in hygienic conditions. 

The hostels ensure congenial atmosphere for academic development of the students.







Security-



The campus have full security.





Guest house-



The college has guest house/waiting room facilities for visiting faculty and student family members.





Canteen-



College has a spacious canteen facility in the college premises and is run by college management. 

A variety of south indian food and other items are provided to the students and staff of the college at highly subsidised rates with highest quality.







Extracurricular activity-



The Debaters Club or The Sophists Club-

Through debates, declamations, extempores, presentations, and other activities, the group aims to help students improve their oratory talents. It facilitates the use of logical and analytical reasoning, which improves the rhetorical fluency required for public speaking.



The Dramatic Club-

Under the auspices of "William Shakespeare," "Kalidasa," and "Pearl," his group holds theatre contests that offer a stimulating environment for examining and expressing human emotions. Dramatic talents like acting, scripting, writing, directing, etc. become more sensitive as a result of the therapeutic experience drama provides.



The photography club-

A powerful form of expression and communication, photography allows for an endless variety of perception, interpretation, and execution. Our students came together to form this group after the Photorazz event, and it has since developed into a vital interdisciplinary endeavour for the university.



The unicorn club-

Under teacher supervision, students oversee the clubs' and societies' operations.

This Tech Club fosters inventiveness that benefits society over the long term. It assists in forming new partnerships that support entrepreneurship and innovation. Here, students learn the finer points of effective project management and cooperative teamwork. In order to put their knowledge to use and long-term society sustainability, they receive practical experience with innovative open-source technology.











Placements- 



About 30% of the students get placed from college. 

The highest salary package offered was 8 LPA, and the lowest salary package offered was 2.5 LPA. 

Top recruiters of our college are IBM, TCS, Ericsson, Collebra, Infosys, Capgemini, Mahindra and Mahindra. Almost 80% of the students got internships.

overview:
DELHI TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
Delhi Technological University (DTU), formerly known as the Delhi College of Engineering (DCE) is a state university in New Delhi, India.
It was established in 1941 as Delhi Polytechnic.
In 1952, it started giving degrees after being affiliated with the University of Delhi.
The institute has been under the Government of Delhi since 1963 and was affiliated with the University of Delhi from 1952 to 2009.

full_content:
DELHI TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY 

                                  



Delhi Technological University (DTU), formerly known as the Delhi College of Engineering (DCE) is a state university in New Delhi, India. 

It was established in 1941 as Delhi Polytechnic.





In 1952, it started giving degrees after being affiliated with the University of Delhi. 

The institute has been under the Government of Delhi since 1963 and was affiliated with the University of Delhi from 1952 to 2009. 

In 2009, the college was given university status, thus changing its name to Delhi Technological University.
