Every hostel has its own common room and gymnasium. 

Additionally, every hostel subscribes to the latest magazines and newspapers for the residents.



Campus facilities-



Central Library-



DTU(Formerly DCE) was established in 1941 and is a premier technical institute ranked 7 in India, its Library System consists of a Central Library and 15 departmental libraries which collectively support the teaching, research and extension programmes of the Institute. 







The Library, besides having a huge collection of books on engineering, science and humanities offers library services through its various divisions.



Guest house -



The guest house within the campus is meant for the staying purpose of individuals visiting DTU. 



The rooms are comfortable with all modern facilities available within.



Gymanism-



DTU has an ultra-modern gymnasium with high-tech equipment. 

The gymnasium has been refurbished recently and new equipment like digital electronic treadmills have been added to the impressive setup.



Health centre-



DTU has a full-fledged 20 bed hospital block. 

Services of two medical practitioners are available to the students throughout the day and evening. 





The college invites specialised medical practitioners for ENT, eye, dental care, nutrition experts once a week.



Bank & ATM-



The State Bank of India (SBI) has a branch within the college campus to cater to banking requirements of the students. 

All the banking transactions are carried out within the branch itself.

The bank has also provided Automatic Teller Machine (ATM) service within the campus for the benefit of the DTU community.





Post offices-



The bank has also provided Automatic Teller Machine (ATM) service within the campus for the benefit of the DTU community.



The Indian Post has its office within the campus to cater to the mail related needs of the DTU community.



Sport Complex-



Sports and games the students of Delhi DTU have excellent facilities for sports such as 450 metre track for running, hockey and football grounds, cricket ground, volleyball courts, basketball courts and tennis courts. 







There are also indoor games rooms such as chess,carrom, rules table tennis with  rules and regulations in each hostel of the college campus. 



Extracurricular activity-

DTU Times - 

The best and most important of all, without a question. Excellent chance to learn and grow as a person. Moreover, some of the most intelligent individuals you will encounter in college.





IEEE -

large society a good introduction to management and collaboration. Many people have accomplished tremendous things by leveraging the platform in the right way. Many people complain about politics before quitting.





E-Cell - 

One of the best societies to experience if you're resourceful and willing to take risks. Similar to DTU Times, E Cell has access to practically all entrepreneurs, startup events, startups, and other organisations in the nation.





Drama Society - 

Recently, there have been a handful of them. The word is Pratibimb. This can develop into one of the top clubs in DTU if it is led by a sane individual who is also interested in acting and isn't just doing it for the credentials.



 UAS/UAV and the Car teams - 

Pure gold is UAS/UAV. It is more difficult to decide among the car teams. They make an effort to set themselves apart from one another, yet they are all essentially the same. Solaris, Defianz, SMV, and Mini Baja are all noteworthy.

Then there are dance organisations, photo and film clubs, music organisations, and many more.









Placements statics-

overview:
NETAJI SUBHAS UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
Netaji Subhas University of Technology (NSUT) is a seat of higher technical education in India. Prof. Jai Prakash Saini is the Founder Vice-Chancellor of Netaji Subhas University of Technology (NSUT), New Delhi.
Previously NSUT was an Autonomous Institution under Govt. of NCT of Delhi and affiliated to University of Delhi, Netaji Subhas Institute of Technology is a seat of higher technical education in India.
It was established in 1983 as Delhi Institute of Technology with the objective of meeting the growing demands of manpower in the emerging fields of engineering and technology with a close social and industrial interface.
Over a period of time, the institute has carved a niche for itself, both nationally and internationally, for excellence in technical education and research.

full_content:
NETAJI SUBHAS UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY 

                                            



Netaji Subhas University of Technology (NSUT) is a seat of higher technical education in India. Prof. Jai Prakash Saini is the Founder Vice-Chancellor of Netaji Subhas University of Technology (NSUT), New Delhi.

Previously NSUT was an Autonomous Institution under Govt. of NCT of Delhi and affiliated to University of Delhi, Netaji Subhas Institute of Technology is a seat of higher technical education in India. 



It was established in 1983 as Delhi Institute of Technology with the objective of meeting the growing demands of manpower in the emerging fields of engineering and technology with a close social and industrial interface. 

Over a period of time, the institute has carved a niche for itself, both nationally and internationally, for excellence in technical education and research.



Important information regarding NSUT-



Location-



Address-

Dwarka Sector-3, Dwarka, Delhi, 110078

Student Hostels-



NSUT's hostels provide a wide range of facilities to ensure that students enjoy a vibrant campus life, while simultaneously accommodating a large number of students. 



A total of four hostel buildings (BH1, BH2, BH3, BH4) are present for male students, one for each year. 









There are two girls’ hostels (GH). 





Each hostel is well maintained with staff working overtime to ensure that students live comfortably inside the building premises. 



Each hostel has its own in-premises mess. 



The hostels have 24 hours water supply & power backup available. 

A parking lot is located outside each hostel for the convenience of students.



Campus facilities-



Library-



NSUT Central Library is well equipped with 20 National English and Hindi newspapers accompanied by 60 magazines that are procured on a regular basis. 

In order to fully utilise the available facilities, it is essential that one is aware of the process of membership.









Other facilities:



The collection of books is categorised in five sections:



Book bank cum textbook section

General section

Reference section

Periodical section

Audio Visual cum Digital Library section



Sports-



NSUT offers state of art facilities in the domain of physical education, providing the following facilities:



Gymnasium with latest fitness equipments

Two basketball courts

Two volleyball courts

Cricket ground

Football ground with a seating arrangement of approximately 5000



Two tennis courts



Running Tracks (400m)





Shopping-



NSUT has a substantial number of shops located all around campus. 

This makes shopping for basic amenities, groceries & stationery items convenient for campus residents. 

The campus is self-sufficient & houses a large shopping complex which caters to most of the students’ needs.



Canteen-



In addition to the regular mess facilities in the hostels, the NSUT campus boasts of four affordable canteens spread throughout the campus. 

Two of these are in close proximity to the boys’ hostels and provide an easily accessible & inexpensive alternative to mess food. 





All canteens provide delicious vegetarian and non-vegetarian food at reasonable prices.





Bank-



NSUT has two branches of National Banks located in the basement of the main administrative block.

Andhra Bank

State Bank of India

ATMs for easy money withdrawal are located at the two college gates as well as the basement of the administrative block.



Medical facility-



NSUT has a round-the-clock medical facility located near the Type 5 faculty housing which provides immediate medical aid to all residential staff & students. 

The facility is staffed by full time & part time doctors as well as visiting consultants & specialists to render 24 hours medical service to college residents. 





Treatment, consultation & medicines are provided free of cost to all students, staff & staff dependents.



Extracurricular activity-



Literary club-

This e-newspaper is an effort to maintain NSIT competitive with other schools across the world. It was designed with a vision to weave the various spheres of college life together and inform everyone on all they need know while in NSIT. The Alliance team does everything possible to give every student access to the most recent NSIT updates. Anything deemed pertinent for an NSITian is aired, whether through our editions, published twice a semester, or merely through social media. This includes college fests, internship possibilities, faculty interviews, and placement statistics. The NSIT's sole newspaper society,‘



Debsoc-

In order to gather and talk about life, the universe, and everything, a dozen or so mildly effusive aspiring engineers created the Debating Society of NSIT, which was officially founded in 2010.



After a number of years, DebSoc no longer represents the value the name carries, exposure to the national debating circuit, or the chance to connect with some of the many high achievers in our campus.





IEEE-

The world's top professional association for technological advancement is EEE, a non-profit organisation. The IEEE is a world leader in many fields, including consumer electronics, biomedical engineering, electric power, computers, and telecommunications, thanks to its global membership. The American Institute of Electrical Engineers (AIEE), founded in 1884, and the Institute of Radio Engineers (IRE), became the IEEE in 1963.





CSI NSIT-

Established in 1965, the Computer Society of India (C.S.I.) is a non-profit organisation made up of IT professionals, students, academics, scientists, businesses, and other organisations. This diverse group of individuals is dedicated to advancing the theory and practice of computer science, engineering, information processing, and other computer-related CSI. - On November 1st, 2004, the NSIT students' branch was established under the direction of Dr. (Ms.) Ritu Sibal and Dr. M.P.S. Bhatia (DSW, NSIT).

hostel_info:
Students who prefer to live in the hostels at IIIT-Delhi will be accommodated.

There are currently four hostels: three for boys with 508, 394, and 280 beds, and one for girls with 380 seats.

There are also 22 married hostels accessible for Ph.D. students who are married.

Every room in the hostels is equipped with the equipment that a student requires, such as a bed, table, chair, bookshelf, and cabinet.

Students can unwind in the evenings or on vacations by spending time in the Common Room, which has a TV, newspapers, and periodicals.

On the upper levels of the dining structure, there are sports and other recreational amenities.
campus_facilities:
Library:
The Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology's library and information centre is a multi-story building with a student study centre on the first floor.

On the second level, there is a full-fledged library with thousands of books, journals, and periodicals.

Students can use several labs on the higher floors of the building.

Sports:
Several outdoor and indoor sports facilities are also available at the Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology.

Table tennis tables, badminton courts, squash courts, pool tables, and a swimming pool are among the amenities available at the institute's new sports complex.

Carrom and chess boards are also provided in the common areas of the hostel.

A multi-purpose sports field is also located on campus, featuring two tennis courts, a basketball court, a football field, and a volleyball field, as well as suitable lighting.

   2.   M.Tech 

         Total fees- ₹ 2.77 Lakhs











Student Hostels-



Students who prefer to live in the hostels at IIIT-Delhi will be accommodated.

There are currently four hostels: three for boys with 508, 394, and 280 beds, and one for girls with 380 seats.







There are also 22 married hostels accessible for Ph.D. students who are married.

Every room in the hostels is equipped with the equipment that a student requires, such as a bed, table, chair, bookshelf, and cabinet.

Students can unwind in the evenings or on vacations by spending time in the Common Room, which has a TV, newspapers, and periodicals.

On the upper levels of the dining structure, there are sports and other recreational amenities.







Campus facilities-



Library-



The Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology's library and information centre is a multi-story building with a student study centre on the first floor.







On the second level, there is a full-fledged library with thousands of books, journals, and periodicals.

Students can use several labs on the higher floors of the building.



Sports-



Several outdoor and indoor sports facilities are also available at the Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology.

Table tennis tables, badminton courts, squash courts, pool tables, and a swimming pool are among the amenities available at the institute's new sports complex.

Carrom and chess boards are also provided in the common areas of the hostel.









A multi-purpose sports field is also located on campus, featuring two tennis courts, a basketball court, a football field, and a volleyball field, as well as suitable lighting.



Cafeteria-



The college has a canteen facility for staff and students.





Auditorium-



IIIT, Delhi has a dedicated auditorium, which can seat up to 500 people. 



There are 25 lecture halls present at the campus, which can hold 60-300 people.



Medical-



Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology provides 24/7 medical facility for all students, faculty members, staff. 





Bank facility-



The IIIT Delhi campus has a stationery and photocopy shop to cater to the stationery requirements of students.



Extracurricular activity-  



EVARISTE-

The IIITD's Math Club is called Évariste. The club hosts events including Theory Talks, Speed Proving Tournaments, and Zero Prerequisite Contests. The club's primary goals are to increase student interest in theoretical computer science and pure mathematics while also having fun.



FOODBAR-

The purpose of FooBar is to promote competitive programming at our campus and create a vibrant CP culture where everyone can benefit from one another's knowledge. We often hold activities like CodeClasses and ProSorts to do this.





LITSOC-

LitSoc, the college's Literary, Debating, and Anime Society, is one of IIIT-biggest Delhi's clubs. Debating, book clubs, MUNs, poetry, anime, public speaking, writing, and a lot more are just a few of the events we host.





BYLD-

The software development club at IIIT Delhi is called BYLD. We are a bunch of college students who enjoy creating new things. By bringing together motivated individuals that want to work, innovate, create, and byld, we hope to establish and continuously enhance the development culture at IIITD.





AUDIOBYTES-

The IIIT Delhi's music society is called Audiobytes. By planning vocal and instrumental performances and encouraging music lovers to compete in inter-college competitions, we hope to promote a greater appreciation of music among the students at our college.

No. 09, 1st floor (Rooms) Toilet and bathroom are separate.

One for journals, one for faculty, one for librarians, and six for a store and photocopier on the second floor (two rooms and one large hall) (Capacity 150) Toilet and bathroom are separate.

One room for the Professor in charge of the library and one room for the PG, each with its own toilet and bathroom.





























Canteen-





The college canteen also provides nice cuisine and a clean environment for students. Food, as we all know, is a basic requirement of every human being. As a result, they provide tasty and nutritious cuisine to our children.

























Health care-



With a total bed capacity of 500, MC Hospital is well equipped to admit patients. Department of Outpatient Care (O P D).

Medicine, Surgery, Eye, ENT, Obstetrics & Gynecology, Paediatrics, Orthopedics, Psychiatry, and a Dental OPD are all available at the facility.

In the emergency block, there is also a 24-hour Emergency Lab for round-the-clock investigation and reporting. The department of Pathology & Microbiology, located on Kankarbagh Main Road, performs specialised testing such as histopathology, FNAC, bone marrow examination, and special microbiological examinations.





Security-



As like every college this college also maintains security policy. There are gate security guards , hostel security guards and also female security guards for girls' hostels.





Bank-



They have an SBI branch in their college building. They also have an atm facility.

NETAJI SUBHASH MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL

                                                



Medical College and Hospital of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Patna is one of the best private medical colleges in Bihar; it is also known as NSMCH Bihar and is located in Patna, Bihar.







It was founded in the year 2020 and offers high-quality medical education. 

Bihar's Netaji Subhas Medical College is one of the state's most prestigious and well-known institutions.

The hostels at Netaji Subhash Medical College are developed and designed keeping the comfort and safety of students in mind.

Hostels are equipped with all comforts and amenities. The hostels are air-conditioned .

Central Library – 



The central library at the college is excellent. It has a total floor area of 3000 square metres, with a separate teaching reading room and large reading halls that can accommodate over 200 students.

The library's entire collection exceeds 24,80 volumes, and it subscribes to most of the national and international journals required for undergraduate studies.



Canteen- 



The Institute has a well established Cafeteria and Mess. 

The cafeteria is a creative outpost for the students where they discuss their illuminations over a cup of coffee, rest apart to feed the empty stomach. There is an ergonomically designed mess where food is served to the students which they take from home.

 The cafeteria and mess is carried on by staff.





Health care- 



The college boasts a dedicated and highly trained faculty in all specialties with extensive teaching experience at both the undergraduate and graduate levels, as well as ongoing, relevant, and innovative research programmes.

 Senior members of the teaching faculty are also invited as paper setters, examiners, and moderators by several universities across the country.

 



Security-



Like every college, this college also maintains a security policy. There are gate security guards , hostel security guards and also female security guards for girls' hostels.         

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    

Infrastructure– 



Netaji Subhash Medical College is located in Bihta, Patna's capital city. The college is set on a five-acre tract of land that is surrounded by lush vegetation. 

Students benefit from a superb learning atmosphere in which they can study, observe, practise, and develop the necessary skills.

The campus has cutting-edge infrastructure, including advanced labs and clinical departments, as well as other necessary amenities.



Physical education-



"A Healthy Mind Resides in a Healthy Body" is a saying that goes like this: "A Healthy Mind Resides in a Healthy Body." This is a well-known adage, and we at Netaji Subhas Medical College & Hospital add to it by providing excellent athletic facilities.

Our college features a well-stocked gym and swimming pool, as well as a lavish playground with assistance for outdoor games and a great setup for inside facilities.

ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, PATNA

                            



The All India Institute of Medical Sciences in Patna is ideally situated to address regional disparities in healthcare services, research, and training.

AIIMS Patna was one of six institutions in the country to improve healthcare infrastructure, provide quality medical education in super-specialty disciplines, and promote healthcare inclusion in neglected areas, along with Raipur, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Jodhpur, and Rishikesh.

Reaching Amity University-

Get on a Metro from Airport Express Metro line at IGI Airport to New Delhi Metro station. Change the metro to Yellow line towards Huda city centre from New Delhi Metro station.



2. Get off at the next station, Rajiv Chowk Metro station and change here from Yellow line to blue line towards Noida City Centre.



3. Get off at Botanical Garden Metro Station.



4. From Botanical Garden Metro station take a 3-wheeler vehicle called auto rickshaw [Approx Fare INR 30 to 50 on shared basis] which would take you to Amity University sector 125.

The Department of Physical Education's goal at SRC is to attain excellence in sports. Lady Shri Ram College of Commerce

Hostel facilities 

The goal of the girls hostel is to provide a convenient and comfortable place for outstation female students to stay. It also assures the female residents' safety. The hostel's affairs are managed by the resident Warden, who provides leadership and oversight. 

About 300 students can stay in the hostel's 200 rooms. It gives you access to campus wifi 24 hours a day, seven days a week. In the hostel, there is a common area with a television and sound system. For the use of the students, the hostel also has an infirmary with basic medications.

            











Facilities 





Library:- 

The college's library is well-stocked, with over 8,000 books and frequent subscriptions to international and national journals, magazines, and newspapers. It meets the requirements of undergraduate and postgraduate students. The library features reading rooms on both the ground and first floors, which can seat over 300 students

 .





         

Sports facility- 

The Department of Physical Education's goal at SRC is to attain excellence in sports. The department's focus is on gender parity and diversity in sports, as well as creating a welcoming and fearless environment and instilling in pupils the ideals of fair play and sportsmanship. The department encourages and facilitates student participation in college and university sports tournaments and championships in a variety of sports such as Aquatics, Archery, Athletics, Badminton, Ball Badminton, Baseball, Basketball, Cross Country, Chess, Cricket, Football, Hockey, Handball, Judo, Korfball, Netball, Softball, Table Tennis, Lawn Tennis, Taekwondo, Yoga, and others.



                           

































































Canteen- 

 SRCC also has a canteen which provides vegetarian food to candidates at nominal rates. 







Auditorium- 

The Lady Shriram College for Women's auditorium has a seating capacity of 700 people with a cutting-edge lighting and sound system. The auditorium's audio equipment consists of a 12-channel mixer with a studio master. The institute hosts cultural events and other activities at the auditorium. Additionally, LSR features three conference rooms with air conditioning and enough for 100 people. Along with individual microphones, they have audio-video capabilities.











Healthcare- 

There are two beds in the medical room at Lady Shriram College. A wheelchair, a blood pressure monitor, and first aid kits are also available in the emergency department. To help sick students, a nurse is on duty on campus.

The residence halls are separated into six blocks situated throughout the university. Form (named after Revd. Samuel Scott Allnutt, Founder Principal, 1881-1899), Rudra north and south (named after Mr. Sushil Kumar Rudra, Fourth Principal, 1906-1923), and Mukarji east and west (named after Mr. Sushil Kumar Rudra, Fourth Principal, 1906-1923). (named after Mukarji and Satya , sixth principal 1926-1945). In July 1997, the Allnutt South Block became the first ladies block. What began in a modest room with one book cabinet and a few books in 1881 has evolved into a fully automated and air-conditioned library with two fully fledged wings known as the old wing and the new wing, with a collection of over one lakh books. Today, the college library has a total covered space of 993.10 square meters. Large reading rooms, stack spaces, and space for a variety of other activities abound throughout the library. 

                                                 
Sports:-  Apart from competitive games for the benefit of the junior members of the college as intra murals, the college's department of Physical Education has been organizing sports activities for improving the health and wellness of the students, as well as inter college invitational competitions for the college teams, as per the traditions of St. Stephens College.       

                      
                                      
Medical facility:- The institution provides medical care to all resident junior members 24 hours a day, seven days a week. On weekdays from 9 a.m. to 11 a.m. and 1 p.m. to 3 p.m., the nurse is at the campus dispensary. On Tuesdays and Fridays, a doctor from St. Stephens Hospital pays a visit. In the event of an emergency at any other time, the nurse in her college house can be consulted.
 
 
 Bank:- Within the grounds of St. Stephen's College is a full-fledged branch of the State Bank of India. The SBI oversees all of the college's financial dealings. This includes everything from fee payments to society account administration.

It was founded on 1 february 1881, St, stephen’s is the oldest college in Delhi.It was originally associated with Calcutta University, then Punjab University. Finally, when Delhi University was founded in 1922, it became one of the three initial constituent institutions.

St. STEPHEN’S COLLEGE DELHI UNIVERSITY

St. Stephen's college is a religious foundation drawing inspiration from the life and teaching of Jesus christ.Its goal is to assist its members in realizing spiritual, moral, intellectual, and artistic ideals.

It was founded on 1 february 1881, St, stephen’s is the oldest college in Delhi.It was originally associated with Calcutta University, then Punjab University. Finally, when Delhi University was founded in 1922, it became one of the three initial constituent institutions.

Students, both male and female, can stay in safe environments on university campuses. Separate housing facilities for girls and boys are available on the Central Campus. Security guards are on duty 24 hours a day, 7 days a week at each facility's entrance, and CCTV surveillance is installed in common areas. 

The hostel wardens' rules about residence and politeness are carefully enforced here. The number of students accepted each year is limited, thus parents and students are encouraged to choose the hostel option during the admissions process. Outside the campus, there are safe lodging options within walking distance of the institution.

campus_facilities:
Transport : Day scholars can take advantage of the University's bus service. Intercampus visits, industrial visits, and academic field trips are all possible uses for the facility. In addition, transportation is given for resource persons who are invited to the campus.

Library :-  There are multi-disciplinary libraries throughout campus that are well-stocked with books and journals. Students can utilize the internet to look for information about any book or journal article on any school. For this purpose, each student is assigned a unique identification number. The library is open from 8:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. every day.

		

 Play ground :  Christ is a great believer in the adage that "all work and no play makes jack a dull boy." Extracurricular activities are just as vital as academic activities because the university aspires for full person development. All schools provide facilities for both indoor and outdoor sports. On campus, league competitions and tournaments occur throughout the year.

Health and awareness:  All campuses include medical centers with qualified doctors and nurses on hand, as well as an ambulance service on standby. On campus, students' emotional and physical well-being is prioritized, and students can seek help and comfort from counselors for mental health issues. Wellbeing specialists are sufficiently staffed on all campuses. In transactions between students and wellbeing consultants, strict professional confidentiality is maintained.

Labs: On each CHRIST campus, there are several labs associated with different schools. To strengthen the research culture, there are scientific labs, computer labs, engineering labs, language labs, performance studios, Media labs, incubation and innovation centers.
overview: Christ University, Bangalore is a premier higher education institution in India.
description: Christ University, Bangalore is a premier higher education institution in India.
full_content:
Christ was born out of the educational vision of St Kuriakose Elias Chavara, an educationist and social reformer of the nineteenth century in South India. He founded the first Catholic indigenous congregation, Carmelites of Mary Immaculate (CMI).



Christ (Deemed to be University) was one of the first institutions in India to be certified by the NAAC in 1998, and it received the highest grade of 'A' on a 4-point scale in 2004 and 2016.

Students Hostel:  

 

Students, both male and female, can stay in safe environments on university campuses. Separate housing facilities for girls and boys are available on the Central Campus. Security guards are on duty 24 hours a day, 7 days a week at each facility's entrance, and CCTV surveillance is installed in common areas. 

The hostel wardens' rules about residence and politeness are carefully enforced here. The number of students accepted each year is limited, thus parents and students are encouraged to choose the hostel option during the admissions process. Outside the campus, there are safe lodging options within walking distance of the institution.

Campus Facilities–



Transport : Day scholars can take advantage of the University's bus service. Intercampus visits, industrial visits, and academic field trips are all possible uses for the facility. In addition, transportation is given for resource persons who are invited to the campus.







Library :-  There are multi-disciplinary libraries throughout campus that are well-stocked with books and journals. Students can utilize the internet to look for information about any book or journal article on any school. For this purpose, each student is assigned a unique identification number. The library is open from 8:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. every day.

		



 Play ground :  Christ is a great believer in the adage that "all work and no play makes jack a dull boy." Extracurricular activities are just as vital as academic activities because the university aspires for full person development. All schools provide facilities for both indoor and outdoor sports. On campus, league competitions and tournaments occur throughout the year.



Health and awareness:  All campuses include medical centers with qualified doctors and nurses on hand, as well as an ambulance service on standby. On campus, students' emotional and physical well-being is prioritized, and students can seek help and comfort from counselors for mental health issues. Wellbeing specialists are sufficiently staffed on all campuses. In transactions between students and wellbeing consultants, strict professional confidentiality is maintained.



Labs: On each CHRIST campus, there are several labs associated with different schools. To strengthen the research culture, there are scientific labs, computer labs, engineering labs, language labs, performance studios, Media labs, incubation and innovation centers.





























Extra Curricular Activities:

CHRIST (Deemed to be University) Commerce Association (CUCA) – Inter class activities conducted throughout the semester during activities hours on Mondays Wednesdays and Fridays. [Business Quiz, Product Launch, Mock court, Mock Stock, HR, Adventure, Venture Capital, Ad Analysis, Logo-Slogan, Best Manager etc]. Membership and participation is compulsory for all B.Com students.

Commerce Club – Monthly/Fortnightly programs such as guest lectures, seminars, and Panel discussions of invited experts from the industry. Membership is limited to interested students who pass the qualifying written essay.



Commerce Chronicle / Altheia – Daily news letter carrying info on current affairs relating to Business and finance. A group of students from 2nd year are entrusted with the responsibility. Hand written and displayed on the department notice board.



Prayas – Intercollegiate national level fest hosted by CHRIST (Deemed to be University). About 15 – 20 colleges participate. At Least 60 students were involved in organizing the event. (2 days)

Mitige – Inter – class competition (for all sections of B.Com A,B,C,D,E,T,&P) in various business related events . The best class trophy awarded to the class with maximum points. Atteast 40 volunteers are involved in organizing this event. (two days)



Cultural Day – Inter Class cultural competitions, where students exhibit skills in dance, singing and others. (one day)



Sports – Inter class sports, Football, Volley ball, Basket ball, throw ball, Chess, Carom, Sudoku. This takes place during lunch hour or after 4. pm. During the semester

hostel_info:
The hostel, constructed by famous architect Walter George and dedicated by Lady Edwina Mountbatten, adds historical appeal and a feeling of legacy to the campus. It's set up in an aesthetically pleasing quadrangle with well-kept gardens and a grand bottle palm-lined pathway running through it. Its appeal is enhanced by long corridors, a dining hall with a high arched ceiling, and stonework balconies. 

To preserve its red brick front and unique interiors, substantial repair and refurbishing work has been carried out in recent years. In 2012, a new hostel block was built. It has the same opulence as the old Walter George structure. It sits smugly on the outskirts of the ancient hostel, blending in perfectly and adding equal beauty.

campus_facilities:
Library: Miranda House has six levels of library space. I Textbook; (ii) General Books; (iii) Reference Tools; (iv) Reserves; (v) Periodicals; and (vi) Braille Books are the six specific divisions. With the purchase of the LIBSYS LSEase LMS package in 2001, it provided cutting-edge service automation. It has followed technical advancement as a continual process since then. A Web-Centric OPAC was just deployed. Six terminals and three touch Information Kiosks provide access to the OPAC.

Auditorium: The Main Hall of Miranda House contains an Auditorium with seating for 450 people. It is part of the Walter George-designed college complex, and is one of the college's oldest structures. The building's design is distinctive. The Miranda House Auditorium and its stage evoke fond recollections and are historically significant.

Sports :- Archery, athletics, badminton, basketball, chess, cross-country, netball, powerlifting, table tennis, taekwondo, tennis, weight lifting, volleyball, and yoga are among the college's 14 sports teams. Miranda House is the first college with an outdoor basketball court with a bounce back shock proof surface. The Delhi Kalyan Samiti provided funding for this project in 2009. The Tennis Court has been resurfaced every five years or so by the college to ensure its care. A synthetic surface has taken the place of the conventional clay surface.

                                                         

                                                                                                                       

Cafeteria : The Cafeteria has been remodeled and is now furnished with custom-designed furniture. It has a well-ventilated open kitchen with chimney hoods over burner stoves for the preparation of specific foods with utmost cleanliness.

Miranda House, which began offering a B.Sc. Honors (Botany) course at the University of Delhi in 1948, was a forerunner in science education for women. Science was taught at the university in 41963–64, then in 1971, B.Sc. General and B. Sc.
Miranda House's founding Principal: She was the first woman to earn an MA in Mathematics from Punjab University in 1930, as well as a Tripos from Cambridge. The college gained an international reputation as a top institution of higher education for women under her active leadership.

full_content:
MIRANDA HOUSE DELHI University



Miranda House, which began offering a B.Sc. Honors (Botany) course at the University of Delhi in 1948, was a forerunner in science education for women. Science was taught at the university in 41963–64, then in 1971, B.Sc. General and B. Sc.

Miranda House's founding Principal: She was the first woman to earn an MA in Mathematics from Punjab University in 1930, as well as a Tripos from Cambridge. The college gained an international reputation as a top institution of higher education for women under her active leadership.

3. Hostel: 

The hostel, constructed by famous architect Walter George and dedicated by Lady Edwina Mountbatten, adds historical appeal and a feeling of legacy to the campus. It's set up in an aesthetically pleasing quadrangle with well-kept gardens and a grand bottle palm-lined pathway running through it. Its appeal is enhanced by long corridors, a dining hall with a high arched ceiling, and stonework balconies. 

To preserve its red brick front and unique interiors, substantial repair and refurbishing work has been carried out in recent years. In 2012, a new hostel block was built. It has the same opulence as the old Walter George structure. It sits smugly on the outskirts of the ancient hostel, blending in perfectly and adding equal beauty.

4. FACILITIES:-



Library: Miranda House has six levels of library space. I Textbook; (ii) General Books; (iii) Reference Tools; (iv) Reserves; (v) Periodicals; and (vi) Braille Books are the six specific divisions. With the purchase of the LIBSYS LSEase LMS package in 2001, it provided cutting-edge service automation. It has followed technical advancement as a continual process since then. A Web-Centric OPAC was just deployed. Six terminals and three touch Information Kiosks provide access to the OPAC.



Auditorium: The Main Hall of Miranda House contains an Auditorium with seating for 450 people. It is part of the Walter George-designed college complex, and is one of the college's oldest structures. The building's design is distinctive. The Miranda House Auditorium and its stage evoke fond recollections and are historically significant.





Sports :- Archery, athletics, badminton, basketball, chess, cross-country, netball, powerlifting, table tennis, taekwondo, tennis, weight lifting, volleyball, and yoga are among the college's 14 sports teams. Miranda House is the first college with an outdoor basketball court with a bounce back shock proof surface. The Delhi Kalyan Samiti provided funding for this project in 2009. The Tennis Court has been resurfaced every five years or so by the college to ensure its care. A synthetic surface has taken the place of the conventional clay surface.

                                                         

                                                                                                                       

Cafeteria : The Cafeteria has been remodeled and is now furnished with custom-designed furniture. It has a well-ventilated open kitchen with chimney hoods over burner stoves for the preparation of specific foods with utmost cleanliness.





                                                        

Medical Unit:- 

 In January 2014, Miranda House, which is part of the hostel, launched a Medical Support Unit. It's a joint facility with the college. It complements the current setup, which requires all students in residence to be members of the World University Service (WUS) Health Center, University of Delhi, which is located across the street from Miranda House at V. P. Patel Chest Institute. Students at WUS have access to basic medical services. 

The in-house medical unit is well-equipped, and Nu Life Hospital in the area provides competent help. From Monday to Saturday, from 9.00 a.m. to 4.30 p.m., a nurse is on duty. Three days a week, from a.m. to p.m., the doctor is accessible for consultation.





5. Extra Curricular Activities: 

Sports: Miranda House has excellent sports facilities with a dedicated sports club that organizes various sports events, including basketball, volleyball, football, badminton, and more.

Cultural events: The college has a vibrant cultural scene with a range of events, including dance, music, drama, and literary events. The college's cultural society organizes various events and competitions, including the annual cultural fest, to showcase students' talents.

Technical events: Miranda House has a strong focus on technical education and offers various technical events, including coding competitions, hackathons, and workshops, to provide students with opportunities to develop their technical skills.

Social activities: The college encourages its students to engage in various social activities and contribute to society. The college's social service league organizes various activities, including blood donation drives, cleanliness drives, and visits to orphanages and old age homes.



Entrepreneurship activities: Miranda House has an entrepreneurship cell that provides students with opportunities to develop their entrepreneurial skills and turn their ideas into successful businesses. The entrepreneurship cell organizes various events, including business plan competitions, startup boot camps, and mentoring sessions.

full_content:
Hansraj College , New Delhi 



It is a government college situated  in Malka Ganj , Delhi is one of the largest constituent colleges of the University of delhi. It was founded in 1948 and offers liberal arts courses

Science and business go hand in hand.

The college provides higher education to over 5000 students with its qualified faculty team.

Location 



Address: UNIVERSITY OF DELHI, Mahatma Hans Raj Marg, Hansraj College, Malka Ganj, Delhi, 110007



Campus map link : https://maps.app.goo.gl/4yiGW5j2dpuHpEW19

Reaching the Hansraj college- Nearest metro station is the vishwavidyalaya station which is 12.5 km away from the campus. Nearest railway station is Sabzi Mandi station  which is very close to the main campus.

Hostel 



The hostel has 344 rooms. Out of this the few rooms are allocated for exchange students miscellaneous categories. The hostel fee is up to 8000.

The college's hostel can house 200 male undergraduate students. It offers both indoor and outdoor game areas as well as a multipurpose gym. A mess committee made up of resident students oversees the dining facilities cooperatively. Today, power backup is provided around-the-clock. The adjacent World University Service Health Center offers medical assistance. Each and every student has access to Wi-Fi. 

Immediately following the conclusion of their admission to the college, students who want hostel accommodations should submit a separate application on a required form (available from the College Hostel Office). Admission to the Hostel is not a requirement for admission to the College.

Facilities



Canteen : The college also has a canteen that serves vegetarian food to students at  nominal rates.

             





Sports facility:- The college organizes many inter and intra school basketball , football , etc.  

                            In which students participate with full enthusiasm. 



                                                                        





 

 









 

Library: Among all the institutions on the University of Delhi's north campus, Hansraj College boasts the largest, fully air-conditioned, and well-equipped library. The College library, which is located in the heart of campus, uses RFID technology in conjunction with KOHA, an Integrated Multi User Library Management Software. It has Wi-Fi connectivity and is monitored by CCTV cameras. 





Yogashala: The institution has built a Vishwa Bandhu Center of Indian Culture (Delhi Unit), which is associated with Vishveshvaranand Vedic Research Institute (VVRI), Hoshiarpur, Panjab, and follows Mahatma Hansraj's theory and DAV philosophy. 



Laboratories: A range of science courses are offered at Hansraj College. All of the laboratories are outfitted with the most up-to-date technology and apparatus, allowing students to conduct experiments with extreme precision and accuracy.Every department offers top-notch laboratory facilities, and students are educated in them to improve their practical skills and knowledge. Labs with the most up-to-date technology are routinely upgraded with new and updated equipment, giving students an advantage.





Extra curricular Activities

Hansraj dramatics societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing theatrical skills among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to acting, directing, and producing plays.



Swaranjali, the music societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing musical skills among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to singing, playing musical instruments, and composing music



Hansraj debating societyThis society focuses on developing the skills of critical thinking, public speaking, and knowledge-sharing among students. The society conducts various debates, Model United Nations (MUNs), and other activities related to current affairs and global issues



Terpsichorean, the choreography societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing dance skills among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to dance styles such as contemporary, jazz, and hip-hop.

Oorja, the western dance societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing western dance skills among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to dance styles such as salsa, tango, and ballroom.



Kavya Kriti, the Indian dance societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing traditional Indian dance forms among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to dance styles such as Bharatanatyam, Kathak, and Manipuri.

Ostraca, the creative writing societyThis society focuses on promoting and enhancing creative writing skills among students. The society conducts various activities, events, and workshops related to writing genres such as fiction, poetry, and screenplays.

hostel_info:
The hostel is semi private and has a very good infrastructure. Every room is equipped with air conditioning, the hostel has a badminton court , and common rooms on every floor. The cuisine is very good, with snacks and meals offered three to four times a day. The hostel corridors and washrooms are quite clean and tidy.

To make room for distant female students, Girls' dorms are located separately at Hindu College. The hostel for girls provides basic amenities like a separate TV area, chess, table tennis, and more. Both undergraduate and graduate students can stay in dormitories.

Particulars

Fees

Boys

5000 monthly

Girls

6500 monthly

Facilities 

Library- Among the Delhi university college libraries, the Hindu library is one of the oldest. In 1899, it was founded in conjunction with the establishment of the institution.The library of Hindu College is entirely air-conditioned and computerized. The library is open to students from all classes. All major textbooks are placed in the reserve section to allow students to compose their own lessons. A well-equipped reading area houses a huge collection of daily, weeklies, periodicals, and journals covering a wide range of topics.

 

Sports-The college features a full-fledged physical education department with two teaching members, as well as all of the necessary equipment for all major games and an exercise multi-gym. The following infrastructure facilities are offered in the college for various sporting activities:

Athletics standard 6 lane 400m track , Long jump pit , high jump pit 

Football - standard 80m x 60m field with permanent goal post 

Cricket- standard size field and pitch 

Basketball - concrete court 

Lawn tennis - Regular size clay court 

Shuttle badminton- indoor court 

Handball - standard size court with portable goal post

Ball badminton - regular court 

Bank: The college has a state bank of India which is located within the campus.

                                                 

Counseling cell : Students are counseled by the college on a variety of professional and personal matters.

Health Care : The College features a well-equipped medical room with first-aid facilities and a full-time nurse to deal with the students' routine health concerns. In the event of an emergency, the college also offers a doctor-on-call service. The WUS Health Centre, which is located on the University Campus, provides health services to the dormitory inhabitants. In the event of an emergency, the student is sent to the Hindu Rao Hospital, St. Stephen's Hospital, Tirth Ram Hospital, and Sant Parmanand Hospital, all of which are within walking distance of the college.

Hindu College ,New Delhi 





The college was founded by the late Shri Krishna Dassji Gurwale in the historic kinari bazar(Chandni Chowk) with prominent Delhi citizens as trustees. 



                                            

















This college is renowned for its highly inclusive and positive political atmosphere and the least DUSU interference in college elections while comparing to various other Delhi university colleges .



                                                              

























Information regarding the institute:-

Hostel:- 

The hostel is semi private and has a very good infrastructure. Every room is equipped with air conditioning, the hostel has a badminton court , and common rooms on every floor. The cuisine is very good, with snacks and meals offered three to four times a day. The hostel corridors and washrooms are quite clean and tidy.

To make room for distant female students, Girls' dorms are located separately at Hindu College. The hostel for girls provides basic amenities like a separate TV area, chess, table tennis, and more. Both undergraduate and graduate students can stay in dormitories.





Particulars

Fees

Boys

5000 monthly

Girls

6500 monthly



Facilities 



Library- Among the Delhi university college libraries, the Hindu library is one of the oldest. In 1899, it was founded in conjunction with the establishment of the institution.The library of Hindu College is entirely air-conditioned and computerized. The library is open to students from all classes. All major textbooks are placed in the reserve section to allow students to compose their own lessons. A well-equipped reading area houses a huge collection of daily, weeklies, periodicals, and journals covering a wide range of topics.



 







Sports-The college features a full-fledged physical education department with two teaching members, as well as all of the necessary equipment for all major games and an exercise multi-gym. The following infrastructure facilities are offered in the college for various sporting activities:

Athletics standard 6 lane 400m track , Long jump pit , high jump pit 

Football - standard 80m x 60m field with permanent goal post 

Cricket- standard size field and pitch 

Basketball - concrete court 

Lawn tennis - Regular size clay court 

Shuttle badminton- indoor court 

Handball - standard size court with portable goal post

Ball badminton - regular court 











Bank: The college has a state bank of India which is located within the campus.

                                                 



Counseling cell : Students are counseled by the college on a variety of professional and personal matters.



Health Care : The College features a well-equipped medical room with first-aid facilities and a full-time nurse to deal with the students' routine health concerns. In the event of an emergency, the college also offers a doctor-on-call service. The WUS Health Centre, which is located on the University Campus, provides health services to the dormitory inhabitants. In the event of an emergency, the student is sent to the Hindu Rao Hospital, St. Stephen's Hospital, Tirth Ram Hospital, and Sant Parmanand Hospital, all of which are within walking distance of the college.







Extra Curricular Activities:

 Cultural Societies: The college has several cultural societies, including dance, music, and theater groups, that organize various events throughout the year. Students can join these societies to showcase their talents and participate in inter-college competitions.



Sports: Hindu College has excellent sports facilities and offers a variety of sports, including football, cricket, basketball, volleyball, and athletics. The college has several sports teams that participate in inter-college tournaments and matches.



Debating Society: The college has a debating society that organizes regular debates and discussions on a range of topics. Students can participate in these events to improve their public speaking skills and engage in intellectual discussions.

Photography Society: The college has a photography society that offers students the opportunity to develop their photography skills and showcase their work. They organize workshops, photo-walks, and exhibitions.

Social Service League: The Social Service League encourages students to engage in social service activities and contribute to the community. They organize various campaigns and initiatives to promote social awareness and service



Entrepreneurship Cell: The college has an entrepreneurship cell that aims to foster an entrepreneurial mindset among students. They organize events like guest lectures, startup competitions, and workshops.



Literary Societies: The college has several literary societies that organize events like poetry recitations, story-writing competitions, and book clubs. Students can participate in these societies to improve their writing skills and engage in literary discussions.

overview:
RAMJAS COLLEGE DELHI UNIVERSITY
Ramjas College is one of Delhi's oldest colleges, having been founded in 1917 by Rai Kedar Nath, a prominent educationist and philanthropist. The College, which began in the humble surroundings of Darya Ganj in Old Delhi, today has a sprawling campus, cutting-edge facilities, and an illustrious faculty of academics educated at major Indian and foreign institutions.
Ramjas College is a University of Delhi college located on the university's North Campus in New Delhi, India. Under the auspices of the University of Delhi, the college admits both undergraduates and postgraduates and confers degrees.

RAMJAS COLLEGE DELHI UNIVERSITY



Ramjas College is one of Delhi's oldest colleges, having been founded in 1917 by Rai Kedar Nath, a prominent educationist and philanthropist. The College, which began in the humble surroundings of Darya Ganj in Old Delhi, today has a sprawling campus, cutting-edge facilities, and an illustrious faculty of academics educated at major Indian and foreign institutions.

Ramjas College is a University of Delhi college located on the university's North Campus in New Delhi, India. Under the auspices of the University of Delhi, the college admits both undergraduates and postgraduates and confers degrees.

The hostel has been actively involved in cultural events such as film screenings, seminars, talks, and field trips.

Library :- One of the most important assets is the College Library, which features an online cataloging system that allows access to library resources. All of the books are coded using special software and are available via computers in the library and departments. Students and professors have had free and easy access to the Internet, allowing the college to achieve higher and more global levels of education and research.

 	





Sports:- 

Archery, Athletics, Basketball, Volleyball, Chess, Football, Judo, Korfball, Netball, Powerlifting, Taekwondo, and Bodybuilding are just a few of the sports offered by the Department. Furthermore, the students have consistently participated in Inter-University and Inter-College sporting competitions, demonstrating their talent by winning a number of awards for their alma mater over the years, the details of which are given in this report. 

Students are also encouraged to compete at the state, national, and international levels in order to follow their aspirations. Ramjas College has also been selected as one of the practice venues for teams competing in the 2010 Delhi Commonwealth Games. Extra Curricular Activities:

Cultural Festivals: Ramjas College hosts various cultural festivals throughout the year, including the annual college fest 'Mosaic'. Students can participate in dance, music, drama, and other cultural activities during these festivals.



Sports: The college has excellent sports facilities and encourages students to participate in various sports activities, including cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, table tennis, and more. The college also has a well-equipped gymnasium for fitness enthusiasts.



Societies and Clubs: Ramjas College has a diverse range of societies and clubs, including the Debating Society, Quiz Club, Dramatics Society, Fine Arts Society, Film and Photography Club, and more. These societies organize various events and competitions throughout the year to promote the development of different skills and interests.



Community Service: The National Service Scheme (NSS) and the National Cadet Corps (NCC) are two popular community service programs offered by Ramjas College. Students can participate in various social service activities, including blood donation drives, cleanliness drives, and more.



Entrepreneurship Development Cell: Ramjas College has an Entrepreneurship Development Cell that provides guidance and support to students who are interested in starting their own ventures. The cell organizes various workshops, seminars, and events to promote entrepreneurship among students.

COLLEGE OF TECHNOLOGY AND ENGINEERING, UDAIPUR (CTAE)

                                                 



The Maharana Pratap University of Agriculture and Technology, Udaipur's College of Technology and Engineering (CTAE) is a constituent college of the Maharana Pratap University of Agriculture and Technology, Udaipur.

The college first opened its doors in 1964.









The college has a well-equipped infrastructure with sophisticated state-of-the-art instruments/equipment in each discipline to provide students with hands-on instruction.

Sports-

Laboratory-



For the advantage of students, the college provides departmental laboratories.





Banks Facilities-



For the convenience of lecturers and students, the university has two State Bank of India and Indian Bank ATMs.





Cafeteria-



CTAE Udaipur has a cafeteria where students may get healthy and sanitary cuisine.





Extra Curricular Activities-



PROGRAMMING CLUB



Students with a variety of interests, including coding, app development, machine learning, and web design, can gather at the Programming Club. The club is in charge of organising programming activities for CTAE Udaipur students.



Weekly workshops are organised by the club and focus on a variety of topics, including fundamental programming, web design languages, machine learning methods, and mobile application development. The institute will hold programming contests to foster a culture of coding.



NSS



The NSS strives to help students realise their inner potential and change their minds so they can integrate into the society to which they belong.



With regards to the NSS's goals, it aims to help students and young people develop their interdisciplinary personalities so they can, in addition to their academic pursuits, build their ability for dealing with catastrophes and natural disasters, leadership abilities, and democratic views.



By promoting and assisting youth in taking an active part in socially beneficial and nation-building activities, the NSS Units of CTAE have been achieving the goals of NSS and have also been crucial in the personality development of young people.

Narsee Monjee College of  Commerce  and Economics

It is one of the greatest commerce colleges, and because it is located in Mumbai, placement prospects are plentiful.

overview:
DELHI TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
Delhi Technological University (DTU), formerly known as the Delhi College of Engineering (DCE) is a state university in New Delhi, India.
It was established in 1941 as Delhi Polytechnic.
In 1952, it started giving degrees after being affiliated with the University of Delhi.
The institute has been under the Government of Delhi since 1963 and was affiliated with the University of Delhi from 1952 to 2009.
In 2009, the college was given university status, thus changing its name to Delhi Technological University.

full_content:
DELHI TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY 

                                  



Delhi Technological University (DTU), formerly known as the Delhi College of Engineering (DCE) is a state university in New Delhi, India. 

It was established in 1941 as Delhi Polytechnic.





In 1952, it started giving degrees after being affiliated with the University of Delhi. 

The institute has been under the Government of Delhi since 1963 and was affiliated with the University of Delhi from 1952 to 2009. 

In 2009, the college was given university status, thus changing its name to Delhi Technological University.



Important Information regarding DTU-



Location-



Address- Bawana Rd, Delhi Technological University, Shahbad Daulatpur Village, Rohini, New Delhi, Delhi 110042



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/JZKjBuFdn34BrKa6A

Student Hostels-



The DTU campus is fully residential.



Boys Hostel-



Delhi Technological University has 9 Boys hostels in the campus. 

These hostels are:



Bhaskaracharya Hostel

Sir C. V. Raman Hostel

Sir J.C. Bose Hostel

Varahmihir Hostel

Sir Vishveshwarya Hostel

Homi Jehangir Bhabha Hostel

Aryabhatt Hostel-

Ramanujan Hostel-











Type – II B-5 Boys Hostel



Every hostel has its own common room and gymnasium. 

Additionally, every hostel subscribes to the latest magazines and newspapers for the residents.



Girls hostel-



Delhi Technological University has 6 Girls hostels in the campus. These hostels are-



Sister Nivedita Hostel

Kalpana Chawla Hostel

Type – III Block 1 Girls Hostel

Type – III Block 2 Girls Hostel

Type – II Block – 1 Girls Hostel

Type – II Block – 2,3,4 Girls Hostel











Every hostel has its own common room and gymnasium. 

Additionally, every hostel subscribes to the latest magazines and newspapers for the residents.



Campus facilities-



Central Library-



DTU(Formerly DCE) was established in 1941 and is a premier technical institute ranked 7 in India, its Library System consists of a Central Library and 15 departmental libraries which collectively support the teaching, research and extension programmes of the Institute. 







The Library, besides having a huge collection of books on engineering, science and humanities offers library services through its various divisions.



Guest house -



The guest house within the campus is meant for the staying purpose of individuals visiting DTU. 





The rooms are comfortable with all modern facilities available within.



Gymanism-



DTU has an ultra-modern gymnasium with high-tech equipment. 

The gymnasium has been refurbished recently and new equipment like digital electronic treadmills have been added to the impressive setup.



Health centre-



DTU has a full-fledged 20 bed hospital block. 

Services of two medical practitioners are available to the students throughout the day and evening. 





The college invites specialised medical practitioners for ENT, eye, dental care, nutrition experts once a week.



Bank & ATM-



The State Bank of India (SBI) has a branch within the college campus to cater to banking requirements of the students. 

All the banking transactions are carried out within the branch itself.

The bank has also provided Automatic Teller Machine (ATM) service within the campus for the benefit of the DTU community.





Post offices-



The bank has also provided Automatic Teller Machine (ATM) service within the campus for the benefit of the DTU community.



The Indian Post has its office within the campus to cater to the mail related needs of the DTU community.



Sport Complex-



Sports and games the students of Delhi DTU have excellent facilities for sports such as 450 metre track for running, hockey and football grounds, cricket ground, volleyball courts, basketball courts and tennis courts. 







There are also indoor games rooms such as chess,carrom, rules table tennis with  rules and regulations in each hostel of the college campus. 



Extracurricular activity-

DTU Times - 

The best and most important of all, without a question. Excellent chance to learn and grow as a person. Moreover, some of the most intelligent individuals you will encounter in college.





IEEE -

large society a good introduction to management and collaboration. Many people have accomplished tremendous things by leveraging the platform in the right way. Many people complain about politics before quitting.





E-Cell - 

One of the best societies to experience if you're resourceful and willing to take risks. Similar to DTU Times, E Cell has access to practically all entrepreneurs, startup events, startups, and other organisations in the nation.





Drama Society - 

Recently, there have been a handful of them. The word is Pratibimb. This can develop into one of the top clubs in DTU if it is led by a sane individual who is also interested in acting and isn't just doing it for the credentials.





 UAS/UAV and the Car teams - 

Pure gold is UAS/UAV. It is more difficult to decide among the car teams. They make an effort to set themselves apart from one another, yet they are all essentially the same. Solaris, Defianz, SMV, and Mini Baja are all noteworthy.

Then there are dance organisations, photo and film clubs, music organisations, and many more.







Placements statistics-



Placement record 2016



PAST RECRUITERS -

overview:
K L UNIVERSITY GUNTUR
KL Deemed to be University began as KL College of Engineering in 1980-81, was upgraded to KL College of Engineering Autonomous by UGC in 2006, and was declared Deemed to be University by UGC, MHRD Govt. of India in 2009.
The university was first accredited by NAAC with an A grade in 2012 as a Deemed to be University, and was later re-accredited with an A++ mark in 2018. This institution was designated as a Category I institution by the UGC in 2019.

full_content:
K L UNIVERSITY GUNTUR 

                                               

     



KL Deemed to be University began as KL College of Engineering in 1980-81, was upgraded to KL College of Engineering Autonomous by UGC in 2006, and was declared Deemed to be University by UGC, MHRD Govt. of India in 2009.







The university was first accredited by NAAC with an A grade in 2012 as a Deemed to be University, and was later re-accredited with an A++ mark in 2018. This institution was designated as a Category I institution by the UGC in 2019.

Reaching Institute-

Campus facilities-



Library-



K L Deemed to be University features a large canteen with modern equipment and a clean environment that serves high-quality cuisine and provides prompt service to all students and staff.

On campus, there is a 1500-square-meter central cafeteria. Mini cafes and fast-food outlets can be found around the city.









Canteen-



K L Deemed to be University features a large canteen with modern equipment and a clean environment that serves high-quality cuisine and provides prompt service to all students and staff.







On campus, there is a 1500-square-meter central cafeteria. Mini cafes and fast-food outlets can be found around the city.



Open Air Theatre-



The college has an open-air auditorium, with a seating capacity of 4500 for conducting various cultural activities throughout the year.



Health Centre-



A full-fledged health centre with all the facilities is established to cater to the needs of the students, staff & faculty, and to the general public in the adopted villages.





Extra Curricular Activities-



Aarohana Club(Trekking)



Through the organisation of a large-scale trekking event, we hope to turn Aarohana into a brand associated with KL Deemed University. We encounter numerous situations in life, some of which lift our spirits and others which depress us. By forcing children to participate in tasks that cause them to rise and fall, we help them manage the ups and downs of life. Nature has a built-in ability to relieve the developed soreness. We create scenarios that teach the pupils the value of supporting one another in challenging circumstances.









Abhinaya Club (Dramatics)







The club dedicates its efforts to preserving cultural treasures. In the past, when abhinayam was the most important form of expression and the language of communication, Abhinaya strives to return the world to a new and improved cultural society.



Natrana Club (Dance)







K LUSO's dance club, Narthana, is a venue that fuses the complex motions of classical dance with the dramatic elements of theatre. It is a performance venue where people can hone their dancing abilities. It allows people to showcase their ability on a large stage and promotes culture through entertainment.



Varna (Painting)







The club focuses to foster an active group of young artists who like and participate in the visual arts. By doing this, we strive to give students the knowledge and resources they need to enhance their artistic experiences.



Swara (Music)







In KLUSO, swara represents the global octet "Sa Re Ga Ma Pa Da Ni Sa," which inspires each and every sound resolutely. Every miraculous voice that is born to sing with vitality that revivifies every ear that hears and every heart that feels can be created and conquered by this auteur.

Student Hostels-



GIET has separate hostel facilities for boys and girls on its campus. 

The two hostels on the campus are well-managed and maintained in a very well organised manner with dedicated staff and close surveillance.



Spacious living rooms with attached bathrooms and neatly laid out sparkling tiled floors make sure on-campus stay truly comfortable



Campus facilities-



Godavari Institute of Engineering & Technology (GIET) has the facilities of a food court, gym, sports, transportation, library and hostel. 

The college gives importance to sports and promotes a healthy lifestyle through various sports and gym.



Library-



A well-stocked, media-rich library is yet another remarkable feature of GIET. 

Substantial time, efforts and financial resources have been spent to develop the library so that it leaves nothing to be desired. 

Apart from housing a large collection of volumes related to diverse streams of engineering education, the Library subscribes to over 400 prestigious journals of national and international repute. 







The library has an exclusive space reading room and an audio-visual section.



Food Court-



A full-fledged cafeteria is functioning within the campus for the convenience of the staff and students. 

It has a variety of food which cater to the tastes of each individual. 



Gym-



GIET has the facility of a full-fledged gym on the campus. 

It also has the facility of the professional trainer who guides the physical work-outs. 







Sports-



At GIET, sports facilities are provided to the students. 

The college has indoor and outdoor sports facilities. Indoor games like carrom, table tennis, chess are available in the hostel. 

The college has a well-furnished football field and cricket fields. It also has a hockey field, handball, volleyball and badminton court.









Bank and ATM-



GIET campus has the facility of bank and ATM of PNB. This helps students for financial transactions.





Clinical Facility: 



The GIET campus has the facility of a Healthcare Centre which is accessible to the students and staff. 

The medical professionals are available at the campus. 

The Centre also organises wellness awareness programmes for the students and staff. The centre also runs a fitness club, which is managed by trained fitness professionals.



Extra Curricular Activities-



GIETR is full of extra curricular activities which includes the cultural events, technical events, a lot of opportunities for students to participate in these and enhance themselves and utmost unlock their potential. Some of the glimpses of the activities are demonstrated in the images below:







At GIET, we think that life should be just as full and fulfilling outside of the classroom. We value physical health on par with academic performance. For the students, we have created a multitude of sporting options.











Placement Statics-



In this college approximately 80% percent of students are placed every year.

The package offered by the companies is the highest package is approximately 12 LPA and the lowest package is approximately 3 LPA.

And companies recruiting are TCS, Infosys, Wipro etc. and nearly 70% of companies give internships from top MNCs.

Central Library-



The university's library covers a total space of 3000 square metres.

It has a big collection of books, periodicals, and reference books, among other things.

At the library, there is also a digital section with e-books and e-journals.











Sports-



Cricket, football, volleyball, basketball, and badminton are all played on the grounds of Vignan University.

On campus, there is also an athletic track and a walking track.

At the varsity, a variety of athletic activities are held under the supervision of experienced coaches.





Guest Room-



A guest house is available at Vignan University for visiting academics and students' parents.

The guest home is completely equipped with 40 air-conditioned rooms, wireless internet, and a dining hall.





Banks Facilities-



On the campus, there is a branch of UCO Bank.

There are also two ATMs, one each from UCO Bank and Punjab National Bank.



Extra Curricular Activities-



The institution offers a wide range of opportunities and amenities for various clubs, including literary, cultural, athletic, and fine arts clubs. Students can showcase their intrinsic and latent talents in the fields of their interests through these clubs. The facility features a well-planned infrastructure that enables students to conduct and take part in a wide range of leisure activities.







Open-air theatres: Two large open-air theatres with a combined seating capacity of 1500 to 2000 pupils are available for use in a variety of co-curricular and extracurricular activities as well as to host large-scale events like Vignan Mahostav (A National Level Youth Festival).



Seminar Rooms: The Visweswaraiah and Aryabhatta blocks each have state-of-the-art seminar and gallery halls that can be used for intra-departmental literary and cultural activities.







NCC: The goal of the National Cadet Corps is to cultivate leadership skills, camaraderie, and moral integrity in young people throughout India.



The VFSTR NCC Company inspires students with top-notch instruction and the principles of selfless service.



The training fosters teamwork, a sense of nationalism, and it also aids in the development of a person's complete personality, preparing them to take on civic responsibilities in their country.









Placement Statics-



Vigan Foundation Of Science, Technology & Research has released its placement data for the year 2020. 400 Students got placements in the top IT firms like TCS and Cognizant. More than 60 firms are yet to visit the campus. Most of the students got placements before the final semester.

JNTUCEK KAKINADA- JAWAHARLAL NEHRU TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING

Student Hostels-



The institute provides accommodation to their students.

The Hostels are well equipped with 209 rooms for Girls and 205 rooms for Boys and a Dining Hall, a Common Room, Kitchen and RO plant, Gym and arrangements for Indoor games in every Hostel. 





Campus hostels provide accommodation to about 650 Girls and 750 Boys from UG & PG courses.



HOSTEL AMENITIES-



Each Hostel is a self-contained unit with amenities such as a TV room, reading area/room, an indoor games, shuttle court, gym, a dining hall with mess. 

Dining Hall is equipped with Proper sitting arrangement for 150 students to be accommodated at a time. Mess supervisor available during the mess timings. 

UV/RO purified Drinking Water unit. 

Hygienic conditions in the entire mess area should be ensured with installed stainless steel equipment and fixtures. 

Adequate number of toilets and bathrooms with their regular maintenance. 

Hot water provided in hostels.
