Display Boards giving the information about the hostel staff, hostel rules, 

precautions and suggestive measures, hostels bills etc. 

Provision of conduction of regular health check up camps.



Campus facilities-



Library-



The JNTUCEK Kakinada library is a two-story structure that was built in 1946.

The college's primary library serves the needs of students and faculty members.





The library has a significant quantity of books, magazines, journals, and other materials. The library has a wide variety of books for students to choose from.



Sports-



The Department of Physical Education is quite active in organising sporting activities on campus.

Both indoor and outdoor sports are available at the college.

 









Students are encouraged to participate in the tournaments.



Cafeteria-



A cafeteria is also available at JNTUCEK Kakinada.

It caters to students and faculty members with delectable cuisine and beverages.

The cuisine comes at a reasonable price.



Auditorium-



A modern auditorium is available for academic and cultural events at the college.



Wifi-



Wifi is available throughout the entire campus of Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University College of Engineering for students and teachers.

They have full access to the entire campus.





Banks Facilities-



For the convenience of students, a State Bank of India branch is located on college grounds.





Extra Curricular Activities-



VYKYA CLUB





Students studying ECE manage the club Vykya. Its primary goal is to help pupils communicate more effectively. Students are expected to speak about current hot topics. Students meet once a month to debate a variety of subjects regarding current events.







CONSTELLE



CSE students are in charge of the club CONSTELLE. Its goal is to carry out a variety of activities, including Drop Everything and Code (DEC), paper and poster presentations, weekend speeches, and magazine production.





Computer Society of India (CSI):



JNTUK UCEV in Vizianagaram has been a member of CSI for 10 and a half years, and the CSI-JNTUKUCEV-Student Branch was founded in 2014 with 247 student members. Dr. A S N Chakravarthy of C S E has been proposed as the coordinator of the student branch.



The JNTUKUCEV-Student Branch's mission is to inspire, support, and nurture new industry entrants while facilitating research, knowledge exchange, learning, and career advancement for all categories of IT professionals. It also works to help these individuals integrate into the IT community. Technical seminars, workshops, lectures, and industrial visits are all actively conducted by it. Prominent professors, IT executives, working professionals, and other distinguished guests are among those invited.



IEEE:



The biggest professional organisation in the world, IEEE promotes technological innovation and excellence for the good of humanity. Through IEEE's frequently recognised publications, conferences, technology standards, professional and educational activities, and membership, a global community is inspired.

Through its various bodies, IEEE hosts a wide variety of conferences and meetings on a variety of international subjects. IEEE encourages collaborations between students and other IEEE organisations through the student activities committee.







Placement Statics-

VRSEC VIJAYAWADA- VELAGAPUDI RAMAKRISHNA SIDDHARTHA ENGINEERING 



VRSEC stands for Velagapudi Ramakrishna Siddhartha Engineering Institution, a private engineering college in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India.





This institute offers undergraduate education and postgraduate education in engineering.

Student Hostels-



The VRSEC hostels are more than simply a place to sleep; they're a home away from home.

They were designed to provide a welcoming, safe, inclusive, and secure living environment for kids, allowing them to form lifetime friendships and ease the transition from high school to college.







VRSEC Hostel Facilities and amenities include-



There are single and sharing bed choices available.

Rooms feature a cot, chair, study table, study lamp, bookcase, and cabinet, all of which are ergonomically constructed.

First-aid station, pharmacy, photocopier service, food kiosks, gymnasium (air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned), sport amenities, laundry service, Wi-Fi service, utility shop, and visiting rooms are all available.

Residential Counsellors, Wardens, Supervisors, maintenance workers, and security guards are on call 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

For late night / early morning transportation, a security guard will supervise and safely transport students from the campus to the nearest railway station and bus terminals.

Water and power are available 24 hours a day, thanks to stand-by captive generators.



Campus facilities



Library- 



Velagapudi Ramakrishna Siddhartha Engineering College has a total of 74,753 volumes, including 18,445 book titles and approximately 1061 back volumes of scientific and technical magazines.







The VRSEC library operates on an open access model, inviting users to peruse at their leisure.



Bank Facilities- 



To make transactions easier, the VRSEC branch of Canara Bank is located on campus.

Canara Bank has set up an ONLINE banking facility for students and staff, giving them access to banking services 24 hours a day, seven days a week.







There is no need for students to return to town to conduct their financial transactions because they can do it at this extension centre.



Sports Facilities- 



Football, cricket, basketball, volleyball, shuttle service, and entertainment are just a few of the sports that JNTU Kakinada offers.

A gym is also available.



Canteen- 



The Food Court is a restaurant that serves both non-vegetarian and vegetarian cuisine at cheap costs.

There are also fast food establishments that offer quick meal options for individuals who want to taste a variety of cuisines.

The institution offers a number of sanitary dining options.





Extra Curricular Activities-



IEEE:



The biggest technical professional association in the world, IEEE works to advance technology for the good of all. Through its frequently recognised publications, conferences, technology standards, professional and educational events, and membership, IEEE encourages a global community to innovate for a better tomorrow. IEEE fosters a community where members work together on innovations that are transforming the world, including computing and renewable energy systems as well as aerospace, communications, robotics, healthcare, and other fields. Worldwide, IEEE is recognised as the "voice" of engineering, computer, and technology knowledge. A diversified group of elected and appointed volunteer members serve as IEEE's leadership.



Vignatha Student Club:



A group of students who share a common interest are organised into the Vignatha Student Club, which was founded at the department of Electronics and Communications Engineering. The club may be organised for social, literary, athletic, political, or other purposes. Students from this club have the option to join this club and do so for a variety of reasons, such as the exploration of personal interests, networking opportunities for the workplace, opportunities for social interaction, social opportunities for professional experience, personal growth, leadership development, and organisational and management skills. Students will be able to participate in a variety of technical and non-technical club activities and get the necessary experience to succeed in the demanding outside world.



IETE:



The Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (IETE) is India's top-ranking, internationally recognised professional society dedicated to the promotion of electronics, telecommunications, and information technology science and technology. The Institution, which was established in 1953, offers leadership in scientific and technical fields that directly affect the growth and economics of the country. The Indian government has designated IETE as a Scientific and Industrial Research Organization (SIRO) and as a nationally renowned educational institution. The advancement of electrotechnology is a key component of IETE's goals. The IETE organises and sponsors technical conferences, symposiums, exhibitions, seminars, and other events all throughout India. It also publishes technical publications and offers its members chances for continuing education and professional promotion.





Athisaya:



The ECE department's students founded the Athisaya Student Cultural Association in 2014, with the department head's support and under the direction of professor Dr. M Padmaja. This group provides a venue for students to showcase their talent in extracurricular pursuits like literary events, dance, music, and instrument playing, among other things.



The club offers members a learning opportunity because, under the guidance of the faculty Coordinator, students are in charge of planning, raising money for, managing, and scheduling activities for these clubs. Their talents are improved, and their knowledge and learning graph are highlighted.

 The centre is open for all students from 9 AM to 5 PM. G Pulla Reddy Engineering College medical centre has a qualified team of doctors and nurses.



Extra Curricular Activities-



For the benefit of the students' entire development, the college offers a variety of co-curricular and extracurricular programmes, including social service. The GPREC campus is home to a large number of organisations and forums that keep students entertained after class hours. These clubs and forums were started by enthusiastic students who had a passion for the arts, culture, or hobbies. 



Each department's IEI student chapter hosts lectures, exams, and seminars on technical subjects. 





The literary and cultural organisation, Bhuvana Vijayam, gives students a stage on which to showcase their abilities in the areas of literature and culture.







Through group discussions and quizzes, the Center for Awareness & Discussion aims to familiarise students with current events.



Working for the goals of global brotherhood, selfless service to society, and instilling virtuous qualities in young people is the Vivekananda Study Circle. 



The Art of Living Forum was established to benefit college staff and students by promoting happiness, a stress-free mind, a healthy body, and a flourishing existence. 



T&P club was established to educate students about available career paths, educational requirements, and industry standards.





Placement Statics-



There will be an ITCA program to train for placements. About 70% of the previous batch students got placed at the college.

The highest salary package offered is 7 LPA, and the lowest salary package offered is 4 LPA. 

Top recruiting companies like Infosys, TCS, and Cognizant visit our college for placements. 

Most of the students got internships in good companies.

It is affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Kakinada

The campus is about 100 kms north-west of Visakhapatnam. Extremely well-connected to Visakhapatnam, Vizianagaram and Srikakulam, the Institute is just 18 kms from the nearest railway station at Chipurupalle on the Chennai-Howrah line.

Student Hostels-



GMRIT hostels were built in 1998. Providing separate accommodation for men and women, the present strength of the residential community is 1000 +. 



Women's Hostel-



The women's hostel has two blocks "Nagavali" and "Vamsadhara" with a capacity to accommodate 444 students.









Men's Hostel-



The men's hostel has five blocks "Vindhya", "Satpura" "Aravali" "Nilgiri" "Himalaya" with a capacity to accommodate 1263 students











Campus facilities-



Library-



The Library has a mixed collection of both print and electronic formats, which include books, journals, databases, CDs/DVDs, e-journals, reports, etc.





The Knowledge Resource Centre has subscription to online journals of IEEE(ASPP), ASME, ASCE, SPRINGER, ELSEVIER (Science Direct), MCGRAW HILL (Access Engineering Library) , ASTM DIGITAL LIBRARY, J- GATE(Engineering and Technology) and DELNET. 



Sports-



GMRIT provides sports facilities and encourages them to participate in indoor and outdoor games for their physical and mental well being.







Medical Facility-



The college provides medical facilities to the students and faculty for any emergency or injuries. First aid kit with all the necessary equipment is also available.



Canteen-



GMRIT has a  canteen that provides nutritious, healthy and hygienic meals and snacks to the students at a reasonable price.





Auditorium-



The Institute has a fully air-conditioned and well-equipped auditorium for technical events, management events, activities, etc.





Extra Curricular Activities-



Robotics Club:



The goal of the GMRIT Robotics Club is to develop students who are enthusiastic about learning about robotics and working with robots. Kids that collaborate and encourage other students to work together make up the Robotic Club. The group puts on activities and encourages students to develop their skills in robot creation. For the benefit of the students, each student coordinator is assigned a certain set of responsibilities. Regardless of one's department, the Robotic Club makes sure that every person has the chance to learn about robot technology. Every year, the robotics club puts on a number of activities, including seminars, workshops, and interactive sessions.





Music Club



One of the most famous and sought-after clubs at the institute, the music club is charged with cultivating awe for music and culture. The club's members participate in the activity all year long while also holding a concert series over the course of the semesters. The club's members put on outstanding and charismatic performances at the many events they host. The club's signature event, the ROCK BAND, is where its members share their love of music via their heartfelt compositions and performances.







Dance Club



Students' self-confidence should be raised through dancing club, which also highlights artistic talent and originality. They can explore their creativity on a credible platform. This club contributes significantly to intra-institute cultural events including Independence Day festivities, Republic Day celebrations, and other significant milestone occasions. The club sponsors events where the students put on fantastic and captivating performances.







GDSC: For students that are interested in Google Developer Technologies, there is GDSC, GMRIT. We give you the chance to demonstrate your abilities and advance your knowledge in a better learning setting. We operate as a catalyst to bring about the necessary change in the society with the Perfect Blend of Professionalism.







We also have several professional bodies, which includes,



Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE)	



Computer Society of India (CSI)	



The Institution of Engineers India (IEI)	



Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (IETE)	



Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)



Indian Institute Of Chemical Engineers (IICHE)	



Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE)	



Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Student Chapter



Placement Statics-



The college is offering a lot of placement opportunities for the students. GMRIT is providing 75% placements for the students. 

There are placement offers from MNCs like Wipro, TCS, Tech mahindra and so on the packages for placements is about 3 to 7 lakh per annum. Placements are more for software students.

The college has a well-equipped library with all the modern facilities

It has state of art audio-visual technology consisting of CD/ DVD ROM and Video Cassettes.

Canteen-

Extra Curricular Activities-







NSS(National Service Scheme)

The motto of the National Service Scheme (NSS), "NOT ME BUT YOU," is to develop students' whole personalities.



ROTARACT CLUB (RAC GVP)

ROTARACT CLUB'S ("FELLOWSHIP THROUGH SERVICE") mission statement

Rotaract's goal is to help students develop their leadership abilities.





WE R 4 HELP

WeR4Help's goal is to provide essential necessities to underprivileged families in the college area.



YES (Youth Enlighting the Society)

People in rural areas are made aware of the many government and outside agency programmes via YES.



HOH - (Hearts of Humanity )

The only goal of HoH (Hearts of Humanity) is to cheer up the orphans.



VIDYADAAN



VIDYADAAN, which seeks to assist underprivileged children with their primary education.





YFS (Youth For Sustainability)

"To work for sustainable living in order to incite a cultural and behavioral change in every individual," says YFS (Youth For Sustainability).





Placement Statics-



About 50% of students got placed in different MNCs. Some top recruiting companies like Tech Mahindra, TCS, and Wipro visit our college to hire students. 

About 30% of students got an internship in our college. 

The highest salary package offered in our course is 30 LPA, and the lowest salary package offered is 3.35 LPA.

Library:
The Institute's Library is a knowledge centre. In August 2010, NIT Arunachal Pradesh opened its Central Library. 
The library has a large quantity of books and journals, which are regularly replenished. It has a large library of information resources, mostly in the fields of engineering, science, and management.
 Thirty pupils will be able to use the reading room. On a fee basis, students can use the Xerox and printing facilities. The Central Library also offers an Online Public Access Catalogue (OPAC) service through Libsys, as well as access to E-Books and E-Journals. Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities.

The hostel mess cuisine is of decent quality. The hostel wardens are helpful and cooperative. The hostel staff is courteous and helpful.

Campus facilities-



Campus life-



The atmosphere on campus is pleasant. The weather on campus is always pleasant.

 On the 20th of February, there is an annual festival. It is Arunachal Pradesh's most popular function, with numerous additional annual festivals to commemorate it.



Library-

The Institute's Library is a knowledge centre. In August 2010, NIT Arunachal Pradesh opened its Central Library. 

The library has a large quantity of books and journals, which are regularly replenished. It has a large library of information resources, mostly in the fields of engineering, science, and management.

 Thirty pupils will be able to use the reading room. On a fee basis, students can use the Xerox and printing facilities. The Central Library also offers an Online Public Access Catalogue (OPAC) service through Libsys, as well as access to E-Books and E-Journals. Currently, the Central Library houses around 34000 volumes, 4584 titles, 35 print journals, and more than 26 print periodicals.



Mess and canteen-

Food cost is student friendly.

Lab facilities are poor on the old campus, but expect good lab facilities on the new campus.

Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the library and hostel is available at high speed.

Sports-

4. Campus facilities-



Lecture room-



The lecture halls of NEFTU are particularly built to provide a world-class learning experience, taking into account each and every unique requirement of the detailed course materials of several faculties.

 They leave no stone unturned in our efforts to improve the lecture room design, which is an essential component of any university's infrastructure. 

Library-

 The library of NEFTU is rich in information ranging from science to technology, management to the humanities, emphasising its importance.  All of these things will be accomplished in this computer lab. 

Wi-fi-

 They provide a well-equipped workshop and laboratory to cater to all technical and scientific aids of the vast variety of courses offered.

HIMALAYAN UNIVERSITY ITANAGAR
Himalayan University is a non-profit university in Itanagar, Arunachal Pradesh, India, that is accredited by the University Grant Commission of India under section 2f of the UGC Act 1956.
The institution was founded to grow and uplift the economy through higher education in order to raise the literacy rate in the North Eastern region of India, as well as the rest of the country.

full_content:
HIMALAYAN UNIVERSITY ITANAGAR

	 

 Himalayan University is a non-profit university in Itanagar, Arunachal Pradesh, India, that is accredited by the University Grant Commission of India under section 2f of the UGC Act 1956.

 The institution was founded to grow and uplift the economy through higher education in order to raise the literacy rate in the North Eastern region of India, as well as the rest of the country.



Important information regarding HU-

Location: 



Address-

Campus map link-



https://goo.gl/maps/1HcttXQR7DHbKt4D8



Campus size: 200 acres





Reaching HU-



The closest railway stations to Itanagar are Harmuti in Assam and Naharlagun in Arunachal Pradesh, both of which are 34 kilometres and 15 kilometres away, respectively. It takes 20 minutes and 42 minutes to reach.

Students Hostel-  



The Himalayan University offers students housing on a first-come, first-served basis. The hostels provide a warm and welcoming environment rich in chances for personal development and holistic development. 

The college provides a secure, comfortable, sanitary, and healthy environment for the hostellers, keeping in mind comfort and excellent hospitality.

To meet the needs of the students, the hostel also provides good lodging, boarding, and laundry services.

Every hostel complex has a designated mess with a well-equipped kitchen and eating area where students may get clean, healthy, and healthful meals.







Campus facilities-



Campus life-  

This provides a university for my sports and extracurricular activities from the campus.

  To take education from the Nainital and other place their social groups in class one grouping students in a mean they are conducted or monthly.

their conducted from the books from the institute and they are a meeting in classrooms in their social group from the social set Facebook Instagram in Google Yahoo .



Library-

Himalayan University's library has over 1.43 lakh volumes, research journals, reports, multi-volume reference works, dictionaries, encyclopaedias, and handbooks to help students and staff members in their academic and research endeavours.

 The library is re-equipped with all written and electronic resources from many streams on a regular basis to provide the most up-to-date tools to assist teaching, learning, and research activities.



Mess and canteen-  

Whole Indian meals are being cooked here with even vegetarian or non-vegetarian, and special dishes are also preferred.

It is open all day.





Labs- 

The infrastructure of the institute is quite good and the best part is the surroundings. They are providing proper labs and classrooms.

Classrooms are spacious and well-equipped with projectors and good quality audio-visual systems. 



Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi with high speed present in libraries, tech laboratories and hostels is available.

Sports-

Outdoor playgrounds, a swimming pool, lawn tennis, badminton, volleyball, and basketball courts, a gymnasium, and a variety of recreational activities are available at Himalayan University to assist students stay active and develop a sense of sportsmanship. 

Our college's sports centre also offers a variety of options for students to participate in intra-college, inter-college, and university-level sports contests.

Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities.

NORTH EASTERN REGIONAL INSTITUTE OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
The North Eastern Regional Institute of Scientific and Technology (NERIST) is a science and technology-focused higher education institute located in Nirjuli, Itanagar, Papum Pare district, Arunachal Pradesh, India.
It is an independent, fully funded, and controlled deemed university established in 1984 by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Department of Education, Government of India.

NORTH EASTERN REGIONAL INSTITUTE OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY  



The North Eastern Regional Institute of Scientific and Technology (NERIST) is a science and technology-focused higher education institute located in Nirjuli, Itanagar, Papum Pare district, Arunachal Pradesh, India. 

It is an independent, fully funded, and controlled deemed university established in 1984 by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Department of Education, Government of India.

Students Hostel-  



NERIST offers a total of ten hostels, with eight for boys and two for females. PG boys and PG girls each have their own hostel. 

The ladies' hostels are safely housed within the faculty and officer's residential complex.

 The academic buildings are within walking distance of the hostels.

Hostel expenses per month 24k rupee.





Campus facilities-

Library-  

The institute has a library system which consists of a Central library and 11 departmental libraries supporting the teaching, research and extension programme throughout the academic year.

 The students, faculties, employees are issued library cards for issuing books from the central library. 

The library has a good collection of books on every engineering subject. Besides that, the library has a journal section from where one can issue the latest version of most popular and useful journals. The library also has a collection of novels.







Mess and canteen- 

The hostels will have a high-quality canteen that will be professionally managed.

The SURAKSHA Company is in charge of the hostel security, and is led by an officer on deputation from the Arunachal Pradesh Police.



Auditorium-  

The Auditorium is the college's principal location for cultural events, departmental gatherings, technical festivals, and the performing arts

. The auditorium, which can seat 500 people, is well equipped with LCD projectors and soundproof audio-video equipment. 

The auditorium is also utilised for annual functions by the various schools on campus.  



Guest House-   

With a spectacular view of the hills of Arunachal Pradesh, the NERIST guest house offers comfortable, exquisite decor and modern facilities. 

The 14 newly furnished fully air conditioned rooms (8 no.) and suites (6 no.) offer all of the everyday amenities as well as a wonderful living experience. 

The experienced chefs prepare a variety of Indian dishes, ranging from informal all-day dining to formal fine dining. The guest house also has a meeting/drawing room that can be used for a variety of functions.

Room per day 1000 rupee excluding food.



Wi-fi-  

Facilities like Wi-Fi in the library, health unit, Classroom, Academic building are available.

Sports and club-  

Club Information-



The faculty and officers' club provides the ideal venue for members and their families to explore, bond, laugh, feast, and create lifelong memories. The club's main goal is to bring people together. Members of the club come from many religions and castes. This creates a foundation for all members to stay connected.

The club includes the majority of the officers and academic members. For a year, the members elect the President, Vice–President, General Secretary, and other executive members.

overview:
Nalanda College of Engineering – (NCE), Chandi
Nalanda College of Engineering (NCE), Chandi is a governmental engineering college established by Government of Bihar and its foundation laid in 2008.
It is affiliated to Aryabhatta Knowledge University.
The college offers programs in four courses, namely, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering.
Admission in Nalanda College of Engineering - Admission to NCE Chandi is entrance-based. Earlier Undergraduate admissions are through the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination, but from 2019 and onward admission will be taken only by national level exam Joint Entrance Examination – Main merit list. For Admission to postgraduate programs is based upon score and rank in (GATE) conducted by IITs/IISCs.
How to apply at Nalanda College of Engineering Chandi?
Admission is through an online application process candidates can apply online through the official website of the college. After filling out the application form, candidates need to wait for the publication of the first, second and third round of the counsel process. If the candidate manages to acquire a position in any of the lists, then he/she will have to report physically with all the documents at the college on the pre-specified date announced by the college.

full_content:
Nalanda College of Engineering – (NCE), Chandi

                           

Nalanda College of Engineering (NCE), Chandi is a governmental engineering college established by Government of Bihar and its foundation laid in 2008.



It is affiliated to Aryabhatta Knowledge University.

The college offers programs in four courses, namely, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering.



Admission in Nalanda College of Engineering - Admission to NCE Chandi is entrance-based. Earlier Undergraduate admissions are through the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination, but from 2019 and onward admission will be taken only by national level exam Joint Entrance Examination – Main merit list. For Admission to postgraduate programs is based upon score and rank in (GATE) conducted by IITs/IISCs.

How to apply at Nalanda College of Engineering Chandi?



Admission is through an online application process candidates can apply online through the official website of the college. After filling out the application form, candidates need to wait for the publication of the first, second and third round of the counsel process. If the candidate manages to acquire a position in any of the lists, then he/she will have to report physically with all the documents at the college on the pre-specified date announced by the college.

             Chandi-Jalalpur Rd, Bihar Sharif, Bihar 803108

Campus size- 51.64 acres

full_content:
Maulana Azad College of Engineering and Technology, Patna



       



Maulana Azad College of Engineering and Technology, is one of the most prestigious Minority Engineering Colleges of Bihar. 





The college was established by Millat Education Society, Patna in 1987. 

It is the oldest private Engineering College of Bihar.



Important information regarding the institute - 



Location-



Address- Gorhna, Neora, Bihar 801113



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/4DpZipH3BD4mzcgZ7







Campus size-

It has a sprawling campus which is spread over an area of 18 acres of Land.



The campus is 20 km (12 mi) from Patna Junction and 16 km (9.9 mi) from Patna Airport, Patna, Neora, on an 18-acre (7.3 ha) campus.



Courses offered by NIT Patna-



Courses

Civil Engineering



Computer Science and Engineering



Electrical And Electronics Engineering



Electronics & Communication Engineering



Mechanical Engineering

Exams Accepted : BPTPIA CET 



Eligibility: Pass in 10+2 + BPTPIA CET

Electronics & Communication Engineering



Mechanical Engineering

Student Hostels-



There is no hostel available in Maulana Azad College of Engineering and Technology.

PGs are available outside the campus.



Campus facilities-



Library-



The Library stocks books, print periodicals, newspapers, Handbooks, and non-book materials, including CDs, received with books.





Canteen-



Canteen is located inside the college and provides fresh and hygienic food to students.

Almost all types of food is available at the college canteen.





Sports-



Sports activities help the students to keep them fit both mentally and physically. 

The Institution provides abundant facilities for the students to get accustomed to the varied sports activities like BasketBall, Football, Cricket, Table Tennis, etc.



Guest house-



The campus also provides guest house facilities for their visiting faculty or student family members.



Extra Curricular Activities-



The college maintains a programme of events each year to give students a structured setting where they can express all of their creative, emotional, technical, and even unheard chords. The participants value the originality and inventiveness that is supported by organised and cogent teamwork. Different competitions in the areas of music, dance, and drama bring the kids' innate abilities to the fore and help them get recognition. Students take part in activities outside of the college as well and win awards. The students themselves plan and run events like Techno Fest and Alumni Meet.







The campus has a wide range of amenities that make living there enjoyable. Campus life is exciting and thriving. Additionally, the campus offers a number of student festivals and activities. Each department of the festival or event is identified, and it is once more entirely student run.





Placement statistics-



The college coordinates with the companies to provide good placement to its students in the top companies.

Almost 75% of the students were placed from our course. 

The highest salary package offered is 5 LPA, and the lowest salary package offered is 1.2 LPA. 

The average salary package offered is 2 LPA.

overview:
Muzaffarpur Institute Of Technology, Muzaffarpur
Muzaffarpur Institute of Technology (also known as MIT Muzaffarpur) is a coeducational public engineering college in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, India.
It is managed by the Bihar Department of Science and Technology and funded by the Bihar Government.
It was created in 1954, shortly after India gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1947.
The foundation stone was laid by Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister.
It is one of Bihar's best government engineering institutes, with AICTE accreditation.

full_content:
Muzaffarpur Institute Of Technology, Muzaffarpur

                

Muzaffarpur Institute of Technology (also known as MIT Muzaffarpur) is a coeducational public engineering college in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, India.

It is managed by the Bihar Department of Science and Technology and funded by the Bihar Government.

It was created in 1954, shortly after India gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1947.

The foundation stone was laid by Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister.

It is one of Bihar's best government engineering institutes, with AICTE accreditation.





Important information regarding MIT MUZAFFARPUR-



Location-

Address- Laxmi Chowk, Brahmapur, Bihar

Campus map link- https://maps.app.google/mitmuzaffarour.org



Campus size- 250 Acre



Muzaffarpur Railway Station is the nearest railway station to MIT, MUZAFFARPUR.

It is 3.5KM km away from the campus.



Courses offered by MIT Muzaffarnagar-



Courses

Eligibility

BTech



8 Streams-



Leather Engineering



Information technology Engineering



Mechanical Engineering



Civil Engineering



Electrical Engineering



Electronics & Communication Engineering



Computer Science And Engineering



Electrical And Electronics Engineering

Entrance exam: JEE MAINS and B.C.E.C.E Eligibility: Passed Class 12 from a recognised board.





MTech



6 Streams-

    

Civil engineering



Mechanical engineering



Electrical engineering



Electronics engineering



Information technology



Machine design



Thermal engineering



Entrance exam: GATE

Student Hostels-



Seven boys' hostels are having a capacity to accommodate 1400 students and one girl hostel (the Golden Jubilee Girls Hostel) with a capacity to accommodate 200 students. 

Hostel -01(Damodar hostel) was constructed in 1952 before the college's main building was constructed. 

Guest Houses for parents and others are also located on the campus.

Hostel Fees-

Hostel facilities-

a mess

canteen

and laundry facilities.

Campus facilities-



Central library -



MIT has a rich, established library y.. a thousand books of different engineering stream occupied library well... Every semester related books anyone can easily get here.



It has an uninterrupted power supply. Number of magazines and newspapers provided with curriculum books



It has some foreign and Indian journals in various branches of engineering and has more than 10000 texts and reference books.  









Canteen -   

Mit Canteen, Mit Muzaffarpur, M.I.T Information Technology



Health care-



Like every top college, this college also has one hospital. It is always open 24 hours for students. The general check-up is done here.







Security-



The Security system is good at MIT. There is present College gate security, hostel gate security and for girls, there is also a present female security guard.



Bank-



The institute has a State Bank of India Branch that is now operating in Muzaffarnagar.

State Bank of India IFSC Code – SBIN0004603







Post office-



The Indian post office is located in Muzaffarpur, Mushahari, and Muzaffarpur.

The Post office is providing all needed facilities like General posts, Registered posts, Speed posts, Postal savings schemes and many more.



Physical education-



The Outdoor Games Facilities:



Basketball

Cricket 

Football

Intercollegiate badminton is held every year.

Chess

Volleyball

Lawn Tennis 

Badminton 



The Indoor Games Complex (IGC) provides the following facilities



Table tennis

Chess





Extra Curricular Activities-



MOXIE





In order to help students develop practical skills and complete projects, MOXIE strives to offer them a platform and tools. In a time when technology is rapidly advancing, we work to teach and assist students in learning new skills. We are committed to developing initiatives that are both inventive and practical. To help students complete their projects, MOXIE offers a variety of lectures, workshops, and mentorship programmes. The club's goal is to expose students to difficulties and cutting-edge technology across various fields in order to foster and expand their skill sets in the IT sector.





MITRA





The Bihar State Disaster Management Authority's interest led to the choice of the "Earthquake Safety Clinic" and Center for MIT Muzaffarpur (BSDMA).







Since young people's minds are most receptive to new ideas, it is important to teach and make them aware of some fundamental truths. They will play a significant part in their nation's growth as future citizens. It is essential to address the MITigation Resilience and Awareness (MITRA) club for disaster mitigation, preparedness, and response through events on campus and in the neighbourhood in order to create a "culture of safety & readiness" among the students.



Other Clubs:









Placement statistics-



Many students, about 50 per cent, from Mit got internships. Mostly got it from Wipro, tata, Indian oil, and prism cement.

At MIT only 10 to 20 percent of students are placed.

hostel_info:
For students living on site, the institute has five hostels: two boys hostels with a capacity of 100 students each, two ladies hostels with capacities of 65 and 100 students each, and a separate first-year hostel for males with a capacity of 36 students. 

The hostels are surrounded by lovely green gardens and a children's play area. 

Each dormitory has its own reading room, Gymnasium, television room, and dining hall with mess. The warden is the administrative leader of each hostel and is a college faculty member.

 In addition, each hostel has a caretaker/matron who manages the hostel's day-to-day operations.
campus_facilities:
The college also has its own dispensary, which employs one MBBS medical practitioner and a support team, as well as first-aid kits in each department.
 If a student has a medical condition, he or she can consult the institute doctor directly. Patients are referred to local hospitals such as the Chhattisgarh Institute of Medical Sciences, Government District Hospital, Apollo Hospital, KIMS, Govt. Mental Hospital, and Sanjivani Clinic (at Koni) for treatment in the event of an emergency.

Library:
The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. 
As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.
Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopaedic reference. 
They subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks.

Sports:
Cricket, volleyball, badminton, and football are all played on the campus's sports grounds. Students are given a variety of sports uniforms and equipment.
 Various athletic competitions, such as volleyball, cricket, chess, and carom, are held during Sports Week. Students are encouraged to compete in zonal and inter-zonal events. All sports-related programmes in the institute are managed by a sports committee led by a college faculty member.
Extracurricular Activity- 
Developer student club- 
Student communities at universities for those interested in Google developer tools include Developer Student Clubs. students interested in growth from any undergraduate or graduate programme

GDSC- 
Developer Student Clubs Government Engineering College Bilaspur successfully hosted an information session on the application.

Placement statics of college-
Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 
Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.

overview:
GOVERNMENT ENGINEERING COLLEGE BILASPUR
GEC Bilaspur (Government Engineering Institution, Bilaspur) is a government engineering college in Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, India.
It is connected with Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University, Bhilai, and was founded in 1964.
Along with Government Engineering College Raipur and Government Engineering College, Jagdalpur Government Engineering College, Ambikapur, it is one of four Government Engineering Colleges in Chhattisgarh.

full_content:
GOVERNMENT ENGINEERING COLLEGE BILASPUR



GEC Bilaspur (Government Engineering Institution, Bilaspur) is a government engineering college in Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, India.

 It is connected with Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University, Bhilai, and was founded in 1964.

 Along with Government Engineering College Raipur and Government Engineering College, Jagdalpur Government Engineering College, Ambikapur, it is one of four Government Engineering Colleges in Chhattisgarh.



Important information regarding by this college-

Location: 



Address- 44PJ+M2Q, Koni, Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh 495009



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/PDsYr9Rr8m6Jg1nG6



Campus size: 179 acres









Reaching GEC BILASPUR-



The college is only about 6.06 kilometres from the GTK GUTKU railway station and can be reached in about 8 minutes.



Engineering Courses offered by this college-



Courses

Eligibility

Streams offered

Bachelor of technology (B.Tech)

10+2 =12 passed with 75 percent marks 



Entrance exam- JEE MAIN

6 Stream: 

Computer Science and Engineering

Electronics & Communication Engineering

Electrical Engineering

Chemical Engineering

Mechanical Engineering

Students Hostel-  



For students living on site, the institute has five hostels: two boys hostels with a capacity of 100 students each, two ladies hostels with capacities of 65 and 100 students each, and a separate first-year hostel for males with a capacity of 36 students. 

The hostels are surrounded by lovely green gardens and a children's play area. 

Each dormitory has its own reading room, Gymnasium, television room, and dining hall with mess. The warden is the administrative leader of each hostel and is a college faculty member.

 In addition, each hostel has a caretaker/matron who manages the hostel's day-to-day operations. 







Campus facilities-



Library-

The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. 

As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.

Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopaedic reference. 

They subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks. 



ATM Facility-  

The neighbouring GGDU central University campus has ATMs from SBI and PNB.

Computer Labs-  

Aside from distinct computer laboratories in each department, the institute features a 100-capacity centralised computer centre with internet access. 

For GEC Bilaspur students, the computer centre provides online assessments and in-house software instruction.



Medical facilities-  

The college also has its own dispensary, which employs one MBBS medical practitioner and a support team, as well as first-aid kits in each department.

 If a student has a medical condition, he or she can consult the institute doctor directly. Patients are referred to local hospitals such as the Chhattisgarh Institute of Medical Sciences, Government District Hospital, Apollo Hospital, KIMS, Govt. Mental Hospital, and Sanjivani Clinic (at Koni) for treatment in the event of an emergency.

Sports-

Cricket, volleyball, badminton, and football are all played on the campus's sports grounds. Students are given a variety of sports uniforms and equipment.

 Various athletic competitions, such as volleyball, cricket, chess, and carom, are held during Sports Week. Students are encouraged to compete in zonal and inter-zonal events. All sports-related programmes in the institute are managed by a sports committee led by a college faculty member.





Extracurricular Activity- 



Developer student club- 

Student communities at universities for those interested in Google developer tools include Developer Student Clubs. students interested in growth from any undergraduate or graduate programme



GDSC- 

Developer Student Clubs Government Engineering College Bilaspur successfully hosted an information session on the application.





Placement statics of college-



Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 

Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.

hostel_info:
475+300 boys and 502 girls are housed in two hostels, one for boys and the other for girls. Each dormitory is overseen by a Vishwavidyalaya faculty member who serves as Warden.

Students interested in staying in a hostel at UTD must fill out an application form.

The Vishwavidyalaya's criteria for allocating hostel space are as follows: First priority is given to individuals who were admitted during the previous academic session and have yet to complete their studies. Second priority is given to students who have been admitted to a full-time programme and are not from Bilaspur.

Because of the limited hostel facilities available, candidates should be aware that admission to a programme of study at UTD does not automatically entitle them to a hostel room.
campus_facilities:
Library:
The Central Library was founded as a part of the University on October 26, 1984, to suit the academic demands and requirements of students, teachers, and research scientists. 
It is housed in a large structure with a seating capacity of more than 500 people at any given moment. It has 1,74,315 books, 9740 e-books, 4567 back volumes of Indian/Foreign intellectual journals, and 1566 Ph.D. theses in its library. 
For automation and to give RFID-based surveillance systems with circulation to its users, the library employs SOUL 2.0 software. To access e-resources, users inside the Library and on campus have been given free Internet and Wi-Fi access. On-demand services such as reprography, current awareness service (CAS), and selective dissemination of information (SDI) were also accessible.

Sports:
The university includes a well-equipped physical education centre with fields for volleyball, khokho, basketball, and lawn tennis, among other activities. A single sports complex exists to help young people enhance their athletic abilities.

Extracurricular Activity-
 
The Dean of Student Welfare's office offers plenty of room, opportunity, and resources for students to fully develop their personalities and leadership skills. Students actively assist in running the hostels, transportation, amenities centre, culinary services, and literary, cultural, and artistic events.
 It organises debates, discussions, essay competitions, scientific exhibitions, Tech-fest, and writing competitions under the literary activities, and the cultural activities are designed to give the students of this University a stage on which to showcase their acting, singing, dancing, and musical talents.
For the staging of various debates, music, dance, folk songs, and drama events in addition to other personality development & professional competitions and contests, a good infrastructure is available, including a 650-seat university auditorium, double film projectors, and audio-visual aids.

Placement statics of college-
Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 
Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.
Placement brochures ( 2019- 2021)-
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1319IpFKtdH3WqZ20Hh6GzFn0pJ7yqxT1/view?usp=sharing
overview:
GURU GHASIDAS VISHWAVIDYALAYA
In Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, India, Guru Ghasidas Vishwavidyalaya is a central university. It is one of Chhattisgarh's major and oldest higher education institutions. The Central Universities Act of 2009, No. 25 of 2009, established the university.
Formerly known as Guru Ghasidas University (GGU), it was initially launched on June 16, 1983, after being foundedhe Association of Comm by an Act of the State Legislative Assembly. The Association of Indian Universities and tonwealth Universities both have GGV as a member.
The University has received a B+ rating from the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC).
The institution is named after Guru Ghasidas, a prominent Satnami Saint (born in the 17th century) who championed the cause of the oppressed and fought against all types of social evils and injustice that existed at the time.

To access e-resources, users inside the Library and on campus have been given free Internet and Wi-Fi access. On-demand services such as reprography, current awareness service (CAS), and selective dissemination of information (SDI) were also accessible.



Mess and canteen-  

On campus, near the administrative building, there is a cafeteria. During office hours, the canteen provides snacks, tea, coffee, cold drinks, and other refreshments. 

It is open Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. The quality, pricing, and standard are all monitored by a university-appointed canteen committee.

Computer Labs-  

Since its foundation in 1990, the G G University Computer Center has been a cutting-edge state-of-the-art facility. It is outfitted with the most up-to-date technology and software. 

The Computer Center serves as a hub for the growth and development of the University's teaching, research, administration, examinations, finance, and other essential cells.

 The Compute Centre is equipped with a variety of Unix and Windows operating systems. The Computer Centre, which is surrounded by lush green trees, contains over 80 terminals (i-5) with Windows and Linux operating systems. 

Users' PCs are supported by a suitable number of peripherals, such as printers, switches, multimedia devices, and equipment. Standard office automation, programming, and computation packages are implemented to assist students, instructors, and researchers.



Bank- 

The following banks are located on campus and offer ATM services. In the interest of students, faculty, and university employees who live on campus:

I Punjab National Bank (07752-260034); (ii) Punjab National Bank (07752-260034); (iii) Punjab

(ii) Bank of India - +91-07752-260073

(iii) Post Office (07752-260032)

In addition, a single post office is accessible to serve all of the above-mentioned users.

A State Bank of India ATM machine is also available at the University's main gate.



Health centre-  

Students, residents on the campus, and University workers are all served by the Health Center. At the Centre, one Medical Officer and assisting personnel are stationed. Specialist doctors from the Chhattisgarh Institute of Medical Sciences (CIMS) pay visits to the Health Centre on a regular basis.

 In the near future, it is proposed that the Health Centre be relocated to a separate building. During the 2010-11 fiscal year, the Health Centre assessed and treated over 7174 individuals. For various blood testing, the Health Centre features an Electrocardiography (ECG) machine and a Chemical Blood Analyzer. 

The Health Centre also offers twenty-four-hour ambulance service, as well as emergency services and doctors on call 24 hours a day, seven days a week. In addition, the Centre has a limited number of in-patient beds. 



Sports-

The university includes a well-equipped physical education centre with fields for volleyball, khokho, basketball, and lawn tennis, among other activities. A single sports complex exists to help young people enhance their athletic abilities.





Extracurricular Activity-

 

The Dean of Student Welfare's office offers plenty of room, opportunity, and resources for students to fully develop their personalities and leadership skills. Students actively assist in running the hostels, transportation, amenities centre, culinary services, and literary, cultural, and artistic events.

 It organises debates, discussions, essay competitions, scientific exhibitions, Tech-fest, and writing competitions under the literary activities, and the cultural activities are designed to give the students of this University a stage on which to showcase their acting, singing, dancing, and musical talents.

For the staging of various debates, music, dance, folk songs, and drama events in addition to other personality development & professional competitions and contests, a good infrastructure is available, including a 650-seat university auditorium, double film projectors, and audio-visual aids.



Placement statics of college-



Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 

Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.

overview:
CHHATRAPATI SHIVAJI INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY
For the past two decades, Chhatrapati Shivaji Institute of Technology has been a leading technical college in the state of Chhattisgarh, with a strong track record in engineering education. CSIT is an AICTE-accredited, ISO 9001:2015-certified, NAAC-accredited, and NBA-accredited institute.
It is associated with the Bhilai-based Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University. From time to time, CSIT has attained impressive national rankings.
It is well-known for its academic brilliance, research and development activities, and commitment to the total grooming of its students.

Campus facilities-



Central workshop-



The Central WorkShop is the Institute's centre facility, and it is one of the facilities that serves, supports, and enhances academic activities. The CSIT Central Workshop is always working to find practical solutions to complicated engineering problems, which may entail in-house design and development. 

It also aids researchers in the creation of their own experimental setups, as well as the modification of current ones if necessary. Machine shop, Welding shop, Fitting shop, Foundry shop, Carpentry shop, and Black Smithy shop are all located in the Central Workshop, which covers an area of 588.30 square metres. 

For proper workplace management, the central workshop follows the 5S standard practice. The Central facility's assets are its well-trained and experienced personnel.



Library-

Tagore Library is the institute's well-equipped, state-of-the-art Central Library. This four-story behemoth, which houses over 64,000 books, 150 journal subscriptions, and other reading resources, is a magnet not only for students but also for the state's academic community.

The library is fully air-conditioned and well-equipped to make learning easier for students, faculty, and staff. 



Mess and canteen-  

The institute site is equipped with a pleasant and comfortable canteen to fulfil students' appetites and provide them with a rest after a day of hard work.

 The canteen is operated in collaboration with "Jalaram." The multi-cuisine canteen delivers delectable vegetarian dishes at extremely reasonable costs.



Labs-  

Over a 700 sq. ft. area, a computer centre with advanced software and facilities was established.

With a total area under the roof of 3000 sq. ft., there are 10+ computer labs with advanced software and technologies.



Wi-fi-

campus_facilities:
Library:
The Chouksey Group of Colleges features a central library that is well-managed, well-stocked, and fully computerised, with over 37177 volumes of text and reference books. 
The library is constantly updated with new papers in the fields of engineering, MBA, economics, commerce, pharmacy, mathematics, physics, chemistry, botany, zoology, biology, statistics, and so on.
 Aside from books, the Library has a large number of magazines and research publications related to the many subjects taught in the institutions.
Through Delnet and J Gate, the library has access to a variety of online e-resources.
Library's users have access to over 5,500 online e-journals and over 33000 e-books covering a wide range of disciplines in Engineering, Management, Pharmacy, Medical, Social Science, Humanity, and other fields. There is a collection at the central library.

Sports:
By following a planned and targeted course of action, the Chouksey Group of Colleges believes in empowering students via comprehensive education. 
The college has provided a stage for many of our students' abilities. The college boasts excellent infrastructure for sports and related extracurricular activities, which have been developed and flourished throughout time. 
There have been expansions and renovations to these facilities for indoor and outdoor games, as well as traditional Indian games, from time to time. Every year, the students compete in intercollegiate sports such as Athletics, Volleyball, Baseball, Cricket, Badminton, Table Tennis, and Chess.
 Chouksey Group of Colleges has made a name for itself in the field of sports and games at the university, state, and national levels.

Smart Club- 
The club wants to help students develop their intellectual interests. The SMART Club's mission and goal is to inspire students to learn about the world by fostering an understanding of applied research techniques, assisting involved students through mentoring in proposal writing, communication skills, innovative projects, research writing, and qualitative research guidance, and supporting them through other means as well.

overview:
CHOUKSEY ENGINEERING COLLEGE
The Chouksey Engineering College (CEC) in Bilaspur was established in 2001. The CEC is a member of the Chouksey Group of Colleges. The institute offers undergraduate and graduate programmes in a variety of disciplines, including engineering, management, and pharmacy.
Admission to the CEC Bilaspur is based on JEE Main, CGPPHT, CAT, MAT, MAT-A, MAT-C, and GPAT scores.
The institute also has a training and placement division that aids in 100% placement for its students.

The temple is quite gorgeous, featuring Lord Shiva's Big Shiva Linga and Ram Sita in the temple's central hall. On all significant occasions, the temple is decked with shringar of all Gods. Pandit ji performs arti twice a day, while students, faculty, and devotees perform puja-parikrama throughout the day.

The temple, which is made of red sandstone and white marble, is a stunning sight to behold. Many deities, a garden, and a hall for religious speeches are housed on more than half an acre of ground. A visit to the temple will allow you to appreciate the building's architecture.



Library-

The Chouksey Group of Colleges features a central library that is well-managed, well-stocked, and fully computerised, with over 37177 volumes of text and reference books. 

The library is constantly updated with new papers in the fields of engineering, MBA, economics, commerce, pharmacy, mathematics, physics, chemistry, botany, zoology, biology, statistics, and so on.

 Aside from books, the Library has a large number of magazines and research publications related to the many subjects taught in the institutions.

Through Delnet and J Gate, the library has access to a variety of online e-resources.

Library's users have access to over 5,500 online e-journals and over 33000 e-books covering a wide range of disciplines in Engineering, Management, Pharmacy, Medical, Social Science, Humanity, and other fields. There is a collection at the central library.



Mess and canteen-  

The food served in the college setting has a significant impact on the long-term development of students' eating habits, food preferences, and attitudes regarding food.

 The college cafeteria plays a critical role in promoting nutritious foods and fostering a healthy eating culture on campus. This can extend beyond the collegiate setting, influencing food choices in the home and community, as well as enhancing the social and multicultural aspects of food and eating.

The Chouksey Group of Colleges' cafeteria is known for all of the delights produced there.

 It is hygienic, nutritive, and well-designed. In the wide, clean, and comfortable Cafeteria, students rest and socialise. 

The cafeteria staff treats both the staff and the students with respect. There is a place where you may get fresh and healthy food.





Labs-  

Labs don't have the new and latest practical apparatus and gadgets, especially Chemistry and Physics labs. The Mechanical department had labs wherein the equipment wasn't functional.

Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at high speed.



ATM-

The Chouksey Group of Colleges campus has an ICICI Bank ATM that is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week. It provides on-campus banking services 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

 The ATM is a lifesaver for the Hostellers and faculty. This ATM serves the needs of students and staff, and because it is located on campus, it saves them valuable time and effort.



Sports-

 By following a planned and targeted course of action, the Chouksey Group of Colleges believes in empowering students via comprehensive education. 

The college has provided a stage for many of our students' abilities. The college boasts excellent infrastructure for sports and related extracurricular activities, which have been developed and flourished throughout time. 

There have been expansions and renovations to these facilities for indoor and outdoor games, as well as traditional Indian games, from time to time. Every year, the students compete in intercollegiate sports such as Athletics, Volleyball, Baseball, Cricket, Badminton, Table Tennis, and Chess.

 Chouksey Group of Colleges has made a name for itself in the field of sports and games at the university, state, and national levels.





Extracurricular activity-



Smart Club- 

The club wants to help students develop their intellectual interests. The SMART Club's mission and goal is to inspire students to learn about the world by fostering an understanding of applied research techniques, assisting involved students through mentoring in proposal writing, communication skills, innovative projects, research writing, and qualitative research guidance, and supporting them through other means as well.

full_content:
M.M COLLEGE OF TECHNOLOGY RAIPUR



M.M. College of Technology is located in Raipur, India, in the state of Chhattisgarh. MMCT is a private institution that was founded in 2007. The AICTE has granted the college accreditation. M.M. College of Technology offers 11 courses in Engineering in one stream. B.Tech, M.Tech, and Diploma are some of the most popular degrees provided at M.M. College of Technology.

 M.M. College of Technology is a leader in research and innovation, in addition to having a strong teaching pedagogy. M.M. The College of Technology places a strong emphasis on activities outside of the classroom, as seen by its infrastructure, extracurricular activities, and national and international connections.

 M.M. College of Technology offers a wide range of job opportunities, including corporate and public sector jobs, as well as entrepreneurship.

Hotel expenses per month 6k.



   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



With well-designed and adequately ventilated classrooms, well-equipped and cutting-edge technology laboratories, computer labs, and a library, the Institute's building, with a built-up area of approximately 15000 sq.m., is complete in all respects, catering to the needs of Academic as well as Administrative needs.



Library-

The Institute's Library, which also serves as an information centre, is not only a wealth of information, but also a knowledge laboratory and the hub of intellectual activity in our institution. Currently, the library houses around 19754 volumes of technical and non-technical books, 55 technical and non-technical journals and magazines, and ten local and national newspapers.

This Institution uses DELNET connectivity to provide inter-institute lending among 7443 member libraries, as well as online access to thousands of e-books and e-journals of national and international level video lectures by NPTEL for the benefit of students, professors, and staff.

The Book Bank programme, which began in 2010-11, has gotten such a positive reaction from kids that it is currently in its third year.



Mess and canteen-  

The food served in the mess is also good. Students get food 4-times, and night mess is also available. 

Sometimes students can get Manchurian sandwiches, and most of the time pav bhaji. It provides awesome and tasty food. 

Labs-  

For the benefit of students, the institution built excellent laboratories with sophisticated technology and state-of-the-art equipment to meet the needs of the curriculum, including application-based training.



Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at 20Mbs high speed.

Computer facilities-

The institution built a completely air-conditioned Computer Centre and five computer laboratories with the most advanced system configurations, all connected via LAN with 300 mbps bandwidth and with access to the internet 24 hours a day, seven days a week.





Extracurricular activity-  



NDLI Club-



NDLI Clubs are established at institutions and nodal organisations, and these Clubs hold activities to assist students in developing knowledge, skills, and characteristics outside of the scope of the regular curriculum that are crucial for their advancement in the specific professional field. Events could take place physically or virtually. The NDLI Club portal is used to host virtual events, which are web-based. Events are planned at the institute level, the city level, the district level, the state level, the regional level, or the all-India level.







Existing Departmental Professional Bodies



1. Association of Civil Engineers (MMACE)

2. Network of Computer Science Engineers (MMNCSE)

3. Spark of Professional Electrical & Electronics Engineers (MMSPEEE)

4. Voltage of Electronics & Telecommunication Engineers (MMVETE)

5. Associated Council of Mechanical Engineers (MMACME)

6. Well organized Mining Engineers (MMWOMIN)

Placement statics of college-



Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 

Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.

hostel_info:
The following amenities are available in the room: 1. Wardrobe 2. A shelf for storing personal items. 3. Bedspreads (paid ) 4. Bucket and Mug (paid) Etc And the room quality is excellent, with daily cleaning performed by floor employees.

 Toilets and bathrooms are also excellent, with both western and Indian toilets. 

The meal is ideal because it includes both vegetarian and non-vegetarian options. There will be a proper breakfast with milk, egg, toast, omlette, and one more dish, followed by a proper lunch and dinner according to the schedule. 

Only the hostel in-charge will register you; you will not be required to do anything. There is no option to select PG. Those who apply for admission must live in the hostel.
campus_facilities:
Library:
The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than we could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.
Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopaedic reference. We subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks.

Sports:
The campus has medical facilities. Sports facilities are so good, and the campus has a big cricket ground, football ground, nice badminton court, gym, basketball court, squash room and pool room. Table tennis tables are also available in every hostel and hall.
Extra Curricular Activities -
AeroD: The Aerodynamics Club (AeroD) holds workshops and also constructs miniature aircraft.
Celestia: Astronomy is a topic that interests Celestia. They go out and observe the night sky.
LDC: The Literary and Debating Club (LDC) hosts literary events like jam sessions, debates, and literary quizzes, as the name implies.
Drama Club: It presents street plays and dramas in Hindi in addition to English.
Dance Club: It plays at its own events as well as ones that are hosted by it.
Mime Club : It typically performs at festival openings, its orientation, and at the end of the festival.
EPAC: The Environmental Protection and Awareness Club (EPAC) is dedicated to preserving the environment and raising environmental awareness. They coordinate drives to clean up the beaches and certain bird-watching activities.
Kala: The club Kala is for creative individuals who occasionally produce items for festivals and occasionally perform as part of the festival.
In addition to hosting regular discussions, the quiz club hosts the BITS Quizzing League (BQL) in the second semester and also plans quizzes for the festival.
Matrix: Aside from the GOT quiz held in the previous semester, I am unaware of any of its events.

Wallstreet Club: Regular classes on finance and related topics are held at the Wallstreet Club. Additionally, it plans financial-related activities for Quark (a tech festival) and Waves (cultural fest)
Music club: As the name suggests, the Music Club hosts music nights and is a club for musicians.
App Development Club: Some members of the app development group are passionate about creating apps for phones. They currently concentrate on Android, but they plan to switch to iOS soon.
Robotics and Electronics Club (ERC): As the name suggests, this club is for robotics. That is all I can recall at the moment.

CG Club: The Computer Graphics Club (CG Club) is constructing a 3D model of our campus at the moment.

overview:
BIRLA INSTITUTE OF PILANI, GOA
The Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Goa Campus (abbreviated as BITS Pilani, Goa Campus) is a deemed university campus in Goa, India. It is one of the four campuses of BITS Pilani, which was founded in 2004. The Aditya Birla Group contributes money to the institute's residential facilities.
The campus was renamed Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani K. K. In 19 April Birla Goa Campus after the death of the Chancellor Krishna Kumar Birla, in August 2008.

Students Hostel-  



The following amenities are available in the room: 1. Wardrobe 2. A shelf for storing personal items. 3. Bedspreads (paid ) 4. Bucket and Mug (paid) Etc And the room quality is excellent, with daily cleaning performed by floor employees.

 Toilets and bathrooms are also excellent, with both western and Indian toilets. 

The meal is ideal because it includes both vegetarian and non-vegetarian options. There will be a proper breakfast with milk, egg, toast, omlette, and one more dish, followed by a proper lunch and dinner according to the schedule. 

Only the hostel in-charge will register you; you will not be required to do anything. There is no option to select PG. Those who apply for admission must live in the hostel.

   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



The atmosphere on campus is pleasant. The weather on campus is always pleasant.

 On the 20th of February, there is an annual festival. It is Arunachal Pradesh's most popular function, with numerous additional annual festivals to commemorate it.



Library-

The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than we could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.

Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopaedic reference. We subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks. 



Mess and canteen-  

the food served in the mess is also good. Students get food 4-times, and night mess is also available. Sometimes students can get Manchurian and sandwich, and most of time pavbhaji. It provide awesome and tasty food. 

Labs-  

Projectors are used while teaching. Laboratories are well updated and safe. 



Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at 20Mbs high speed.

Sports-

The campus has medical facilities. Sports facilities are so good, and the campus has a big cricket ground, football ground, nice badminton court, gym, basketball court, squash room and pool room. Table tennis tables are also available in every hostel and hall.





Extra Curricular Activities -



AeroD: The Aerodynamics Club (AeroD) holds workshops and also constructs miniature aircraft.



Celestia: Astronomy is a topic that interests Celestia. They go out and observe the night sky.



LDC: The Literary and Debating Club (LDC) hosts literary events like jam sessions, debates, and literary quizzes, as the name implies.



Drama Club: It presents street plays and dramas in Hindi in addition to English.



Dance Club: It plays at its own events as well as ones that are hosted by it.



Mime Club : It typically performs at festival openings, its orientation, and at the end of the festival.



EPAC: The Environmental Protection and Awareness Club (EPAC) is dedicated to preserving the environment and raising environmental awareness. They coordinate drives to clean up the beaches and certain bird-watching activities.



Kala: The club Kala is for creative individuals who occasionally produce items for festivals and occasionally perform as part of the festival.

In addition to hosting regular discussions, the quiz club hosts the BITS Quizzing League (BQL) in the second semester and also plans quizzes for the festival.



Matrix: Aside from the GOT quiz held in the previous semester, I am unaware of any of its events.





Wallstreet Club: Regular classes on finance and related topics are held at the Wallstreet Club. Additionally, it plans financial-related activities for Quark (a tech festival) and Waves (cultural fest)



Music club: As the name suggests, the Music Club hosts music nights and is a club for musicians.



App Development Club: Some members of the app development group are passionate about creating apps for phones. They currently concentrate on Android, but they plan to switch to iOS soon.



Robotics and Electronics Club (ERC): As the name suggests, this club is for robotics. That is all I can recall at the moment.







CG Club: The Computer Graphics Club (CG Club) is constructing a 3D model of our campus at the moment.



CFC: At Waves, BITS Pilani Goa's yearly cultural festival, the campus fashion club, or FashP, puts on a 30-minute display.



CEL: The Coalescence mini-fest, which draws a sizable number of entrepreneurs from across the nation, is organised by CEL, the Centre for Entrepreneurship and Leadership, which also fosters entrepreneur culture on campus.







Many more groups exist, including Enactus, the Gym Club, the TedX team for the Ted Event, the BITSMUN team for the MUN during Quark, and the BITSAAD, which is the alumni affairs division.

Placement statics of college-



Students have consistently excelled not only in their academic subjects but also in extracurricular activities. 

Training and Placement cell assists undergraduate students in completing an eight-week training/internship in a reputable firm over the summer break to complete the requirements of their B.Tech. programme.



Placement brochures ( 2019- 2020)-



https://drive.google.com/file/d/1H3oeRTuCVDp5CP67qVTw6iAyOYhAbDys/view?usp=sharing
