PADRE CONCEICAO COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING



Established in 1997, Padre Conceiço Institution of Engineering (PCCE) is a private engineering college in Verna, Goa, India. The college is connected with Goa University in Taleigao, Goa, and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) in New Delhi has authorised the programmes. 

The college site was designed by civil engineer Olavo Carvalho and is part of the Agnel Technical Education Complex in Verna, Goa.

 PCCE was the state's first private engineering college. Pacers are the name given to PCCE students.

Students Hostel-  



PCCE provides hostel for girl students only. But there are PG's nearby suitable that available for boys. The average cost per month at PG is 5000. 

The mess food that is offered by PCCE is good, friendly price and hygienic. Hostel expenses per month 10k-15k.

   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



College have annual events. Students have our technical event called tachyon in September that held of each year and a cultural festival in January –February that also held of each year. 

The library is great we have a lot of books available. The classrooms are so much not updated means average they are not that well equipped. 

They also have a sports event in March. College have E- cell, music club, sports club in this college.





Library-

The library is the beating heart of any educational establishment. The same may be said for our Library. The Central Library is an amazing state-of-the-art facility with a unique worldwide resource collection. 

The Library now has a collection of 25728 books, including textbooks and reference books. It has a stacking space, a reference section, a primary reading area, and a secondary reference reading area. 

The area of engineering is rapidly evolving; in order to keep students and faculty up to date, the Library subscribes to IEEE 187+ e-journals and 1400+ international proceedings, as well as 44 print journals and publications. 

This is the digital library era. Central Library's services are delivered via an integrated web-based Library Management system.



Mess and canteen-  

Complete Indian meals are cooked with even vegetarian or non-vegetarian, and special dishes which are also preferred.

Food cost is student friendly.





Labs-  

Spacious classrooms and laboratories are available for every course for the respective department.





Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the library, labs and hostel is available at high speed.

Faculty-

college is under Goa University as all engineering colleges in Goa are affiliated with Goa University. So, the syllabus of Goa University is not much average. 

Teachers here are helpful apart from others. 





Extra Curricular Activities -



Without games and sports, a student's life would not be complete. To perform well on competitive exams, a student needs to put in a lot of study time. To enjoy the health and vitality of life, he should also engage in sports and activities.







Through involvement in various sports and games, Padre Conceicao College of Engineering encourages the complete growth of its students. In addition to having indoor facilities for games like Badminton, Table Tennis, Carrom, and Chess, the college boasts amenities including a Lawn Football Ground, a Volleyball Court, Cricket Nets, and two Basketball Courts.







Every year, the college has a sports festival in which more than half of the students and teachers actively take part. The college also employs skilled trainers to work with the many teams competing in the intercollegiate competitions.







Placement statics of college -



Placement offered by this college was quite decent. Its suggesting a bit better than it's counterparts in Goa. Most deserving students do get placed. 

The minimum CTC offered is about 3.5 LPA and the highest goes upto 13 LPA. Many internships are offered by this college but very rarely were they ever paid.

full_content:
GOA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING



Goa Engineering College, often known as Goa College of Engineering or simply GEC, is a public college associated with Goa University that offers courses in engineering subjects. 

With more than 2,200 students, it is the oldest engineering college in Goa and is located on the Farmagudi plateau in Ponda. It was established in 1967.



Important information regarding this college-

Location: 



Address-Bhausaheb Bandodkar Technical Education Complex Farmagudi, Ponda, Goa 403401



Campus map link- https://goo.gl/maps/X3UMGkE52c9wsR5NA

Campus size: 300 acres

 

Reaching GOA ENGINEERING COLLEGE-



The college is only about 25 kilometres from the Margao railway station and can be reached in about 42 minutes.



Engineering Courses offered by this college-

Computer Science and Engineering

Electronics & Communication Engineering

Civil Engineering

Electrical Engineering

Chemical Engineering

Mining Engineering

Mechanical Engineering

Hostel Fees Per Year

Hostel rooms can accommodate three students sharing one room. The typical monthly rent is 1900 rupees. 

Hostels are not well maintained. All 4 hostels provide a meal plan. However, the food is not up to par, however there are nearby hotels where you may find quality food.  

There isn't a specific hostel run by the college, although there are numerous, very reasonably priced hostels all across the area. 

It has all the bare necessities, including a washing facility, adjacent bathrooms, WiFi on every floor, and three meals every day. 





Campus facilities-



Campus life-

Campus life is unforgettable. Campus of GEC is about 250 acres. This is even bigger than Vatican City, which is the smallest country in the world. GEC has a lot of things to offer. Gender ratio is about 3:1, 3 boys to 1 girl. Students from all races were studying in this institute. Even students from Northeast are allotted 1 student seat in every branch of this college.



Library-

The library is organised into several sections, each with its own sitting arrangement. Reference, circulation, and periodical sections are included. Every working day, the library is open. Over the years, our college has improved and expanded its collection, and it is continually updating its services and collection. 

Textbooks, reference books, national and international journals, e-journals, and e-books are all available. There are over 12502 volumes and 1856 titles in the library. 

Elsevier Science Direct is one of the electronic databases that the library subscribes to. Faculty, students, and other users can access 46 Indian and international journals and magazines. Our pupils have access to a book library at our institute. Project reports for B.E. are also accessible.



Mess and canteen-  

Complete Indian meals are cooked with even vegetarian or non-vegetarian, and special dishes which are also preferred.

Food cost is student friendly.

Labs-  

Labs are very good with well-maintained equipment, and computer labs are fully air-conditioned. 



Wi-fi-  

The college has its on-campus Wi-Fi provision. Other facilities that you could find in our college include Computer labs ( with completely functioning AC's), a library, (with almost all the books prescribed by the Goa university and more)

Sports-

a sports ground (including courts for Basketball and Volleyball, grounds for Football and Futsal, etc.), FIRE ( Forum for Innovation and Incubation Research and Entrepreneurship), Electrical and Electronics lab, Workshop's (for Welding, Carpentry, Fitting, etc. ), labs ( for Physics and Chemistry), Seminar halls, auditoriums, a chapel, a photocopying area, washrooms, Separate common rooms for girls and boys and a canteen with good food at reasonable rates.



Extracurricular activity-



FOSS Club

We are a group of individuals who adore free and open source software here at the FOSS Community GEC. The club was founded to foster relationships with other FOSS supporters within and outside of our campus. We welcome everyone to attend our activities, including working adults and first-year college students. In our college, we work hard to promote the open source and collaborative software development cultures, and we actively recruit students from all across Goa to participate in open source software projects.







Coders Club

A group of Goa College of Engineering students who are interested in technology is called GEC Coders Club. This organisation was founded with the goal of bridging the gap between academic requirements and market demands. It also holds numerous events to raise the standard of programmers graduating from our college. To inspire students and give them the confidence to take part in such events on a bigger scale, the club holds numerous workshops, practical sessions, competitive coding tournaments, etc. 







Music Club

At the music club, we work to make sure that everyone—including those without musical training—can come together to study and appreciate a variety of musical styles.

To make sure that everyone is up to date with the most recent developments in the music industry, we regularly hold jam sessions, open mic nights, and more recently, online catch-up gatherings.





As far as our club's accomplishments go, we have competed in and won numerous intercollege contests at the state and national levels.

The music club today boasts a fully furnished music room with a variety of instruments, including a drum set, a keyboard, and guitars, in contrast to its humble beginnings as a small group of music enthusiasts who would jam wherever in the college.



Art & Literary Club

The group on campus exists to give students the chance to explore their creative and talent potential. Every activity children participate in gives them the opportunity to interact with classmates who have a variety of interests and backgrounds, and it also broadens their perspective on the world. They can develop their social and leadership abilities while also learning to value different points of view. Being a part of the club's events is enjoyable and relatively stress-free. It also attempts to foster reading habits and participation in formal activities like debate and essay writing as well as informal talks. The blossoming of the student's whole personality development is at the heart of the club's interest.







ACHIEVEMENTS  -

WINNERS AT TECHNOVA CULTURAL EVENT 2018, DBCE

WINNERS AT RENAISSANCE 2016,

WINNERS AT TANDAV 2017

Campus facilities-

Library –

The collection in the library encompasses 90520 books of 33726 titles varying in subjects like Engineering, Management, Architecture & Design, Basic Science & Humanities and also includes reference books, number of CDs/DVDs and subscribed to 53 print journals and magazines in print and 1326 e-books and other e-resources. 

Major e-resources such as IEEE Journals, Science Direct, IEEE Proceedings, ASME, ASCE and Springer which are accessible to the faculties and students’ community from anywhere in the campus. 

 Remains closed on Sunday & Public Holidays







Cafeteria-

Canteen facility is available in the campus for the students and staff members. An entrée of delectable cuisines is provided at pocket-friendly prices.





 



Sports-



The institute team has constantly dominated the inter-college sports meet and is a force to reckon with. The inter-year and L.D Cup tournaments are also fiercely contested and often, produce new talents for the university team.





 





Extra Curricular Activities -

Utilizing free time in college life is important because it can reap many benefits. At L.D. College of Engineering, students get enough time to develop their co-curricular skills. There are many clubs in the college which help students to do that.

overview:
INSTITUTE OF INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNOLOGY RESEARCH AND MANAGEMENT (IITRAM)
It was established in the year 2013.
It is ranked at #201 in India and #2 in Ahmedabad for B Tech by NIRF ranking 2020. The institute offers B Tech at the UG level, M Tech at the PG level, and Ph.D. at the Research level.
More than 35 reputed organizations participated in the last placement session and recruited a good number of students.

full_content:
INSTITUTE OF INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNOLOGY RESEARCH AND MANAGEMENT (IITRAM)                         

It was established in the year 2013.

It is ranked at #201 in India and #2 in Ahmedabad for B Tech by NIRF ranking 2020. The institute offers B Tech at the UG level, M Tech at the PG level, and Ph.D. at the Research level.



More than 35 reputed organizations participated in the last placement session and recruited a good number of students.

Important information about this institute-

Location-



Address-

Institute Of Infrastructure Technology Research And Management, Khokhar Rd, Near Khokhar Circle, Maninagar East, Khokhar, Ahmedabad, Gujarat 380026



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/uFtyWYwtxPiu7Pjv7

Campus size- 

It has a small campus of 10 acres.

How to reach IITRAM?

The closest stations to IITRAM in Maninagar East are:

Bihari Mills is 102 meters away, 2 min walk.

Khokhra Circle is 187 meters away, 3 min walk.

Rabari Colony is 3609 meters away, 46 min walk.

Student hostel-



The hostel provides airy, spacious and furnished rooms. 

It also has facility of a study room. 

The hostel also provides indoor and outdoor games facility. IITRAM has a cafeteria with Mess facilities where flavours of healthy food mingle with the happy faces of students.

Boys Hostel Off-Campus Hostel, Girls Hostel Off-Campus Hostel

Hostel facilities-

Rooms of the hostel are airy, spacious, and furnished with basic necessities which include bed with mattress and pillow, writing table, chair and cupboard. Housekeeping facility is also provided by the institute.

IITRAM Hostel comprises of an airy and spacious study room where students can sit, learn and plan their study time according to their convenience. It remains open for 24 X 7 and strictly remains as `silence zone`.

After busy and hectic schedules, the Entertainment Area provides refreshing time to the students. Here they are provided LCD TV with HD Tata sky facilities.

Sports plays an important role in the all-round development of students. Focusing on this aspect, the hostel provides facilities of the following indoor & outdoor games.

Hostel images-



Campus facilities-

Library-

The library has a collection of over 4700+ books, 7,000+ e-books, 6,000+ e-journals, a fine collection of books on literature and English language and a select collection of biographies, and general interest titles. 

The library is fully automated with SOUL 2.0 integrated library management software and also implemented integrated RFID technology, smart card, auto check in and check out systems etc. 

The library provides online access to many resources like ASCE, IEEE Xplore, IEEE-Wiley, ASME, JSTOR, etc.





Sports – 

All the required sports facilities are available in the campus such as Badminton Court Volleyball Court Table tennis etc. 





Labs-

The college supports more practical and hands on learning with the most advanced lab facilities.



Available Facilities- Chemistry Lab Computer Lab Electronics Lab Language Lab Mechanical Lab Physics Lab Control system lab|E-Yantra lab Geoscience lab





Gym – 

Gym includes all the facilities and they are regularly maintained. 



Auditorium -

Campus also have auditoriums. They are large and have good sitting capacity. 



Open air theatre - 

The Open Air Theatre serves as a platform for the students to express their creativity by rejuvenating their hidden talents. 

The campus has a cafeteria that serves healthy and hygienic meals. State-of-art classrooms with audio-visual aids enhance the teaching-learning process. The library houses a good collection of books, journals, CDs, DVDs and provides online access to world-class journals and periodicals.

Other Facilities includes:

Mechanical Workshop, Open Air Theatre, Conference Hall, Seminar Hall, cafeteria, medical and health facilities.

Mess

IITRAM has a cafeteria with mess facilities where flavours of healthy food mingle with the happy faces of students.



Extra Curricular Activities -

The campus life at the Institute of Infrastructure, Technology, Research and Management is active and energetic, and the students have access to all the amenities they need for their leisure activities. Since its foundation, IITRAM has encouraged young people to grow as individuals by being involved with groups and events hosted both within and by other esteemed institutions.



The Open Air Theater gives the pupils a stage on which to showcase their latent skills and engage in creative expression. A cafeteria on campus offers hygienic and nutritious food. The teaching-learning process is improved by using modern classrooms with audiovisual equipment. In addition to offering online access to prestigious journals and publications, the library has a good variety of books, journals, CDs, and DVDs.



IITRAM has athletic facilities, a gym, and field trainers. For the institute, student fitness is always a top priority. Leading universities encourage their students to compete in sports events. 

Wi-Fi is available on the campus. In order to develop as an aspiring engineer with moral and ethical integrity, IITRAM students participate in internship programmes, research, and extracurricular activities.

Apart from these, the following clubs are associated with the institute: -

NEST Club



Robotics and Aeromodelling Club



Robotics Club



INK – Innovation n Kreation, SҜI – Sports Ҝlub of IITRAM, Paintography Club, Movie Club and Theatre Club.



Placement statistics –



More than 35 companies of various industries including Engineering and Technology, Consulting and IT have visited campus for hiring students. 

IITRAM Recruitment Highlights 2019-20 (B Tech)

Total No. of Students Placed

108

Highest Package Offered

Rs. 9 Lakh pa

Average Salary Offered

Rs. 4-5 Lakh pa

Lowest Package Offered

Rs. 1.5 Lakh pa

hostel_info: The institute also provides hostel facilities for girl’s students. Basic furniture, modern appliances, water, electricity, etc are available in the hostels. Students can also access free internet from anywhere inside the campus hostel.
campus_facilities:
Separate fields are maintained especially for outdoor games.
The institute has a huge cafeteria and can accommodate a large number of students.
The IT infrastructure of the institute is well-maintained. 
Other AD Patel Institute of Technology facilities like transport, cafeteria is available on chargeable basis.

Library:
ADIT Anand library facilities include textbooks, reference books, national and international journals, periodicals, newspapers, magazines, e-books, e-journals, online databases, CDs, DVDs, floppies and many more. The library facility is available for both the students and faculty members and provides numerous resources for learning and research work.
The College has a well-stocked library with 22,739 books
IEEE online subscription is also available in library round the clock
It is subscribed 76 journals and 48 international journals and 4 magazines for students to help their study and research
It is well equipped with modem facilities and resources in the forms of CDROMs, DVD-ROM, online databases, micro-documents, video cassettes, books, journals, theses, reports, etc.

Sports:
Indoor and outdoor sports like chess, carrom, badminton, table tennis, cricket, basketball, football, hockey, volleyball, etc are provided by the AD Patel Institute of Technology sports department. Students are also encouraged to participate in various sports for physical and mental well-being.
overview:
A D PATEL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY PARTNA, GUJRAT
A.D Patel Institute of Technology is one of the technical institutions in Gujarat.
The institute is affiliated to the Gujarat Technological University (GTU). The college provides both undergraduate and postgraduate courses in the engineering field.
The institution at present offers 8 B.E courses and 3 M.E. courses.

A D PATEL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY PARTNA, GUJRAT

full_content:
BIRLA VISHVAKARMA MAHAVIDYALAYA, ANAND, GUJRAT                                  

Birla Vishvakarma Mahavidyalaya was established in 1948. 

The institute has been announced as an autonomous college since 2015. It is located in Vallabh Vidyanagar (V.V.Nagar), Gujarat. 

The institution is affiliated with Gujarat Technological University from June 2008 onwards and is approved by AICTE. 



The institution has been NAAC and NBA-accredited. 

This engineering college offers Graduation, Post-graduation, PhD, PDDC and D2D courses from in streams like Civil, Structural, Computer, Electronics, Electrical, Mechanical, Production, Electronics and Communication, IT engineering. 

The total number of courses offered is 20.

CHAROTAR UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY(CHARUSAT), ANAND
Charotar University of Science and Technology (CHARUSAT) is a State Private University established under the Government of Gujarat.
The University is recognized by University Grants Commission (UGC) under Section 22 of UGC Act 1956 and accredited with grade A by NAAC in 2016.
CHARUSAT offers 72 Programs through 9 different institutes, 6 faculties, 4 major centres/ cells at the Undergraduate, Postgraduate, Research level Ph.D. programs in numerous disciplines, and their specializations.

CHAROTAR UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY(CHARUSAT), ANAND                                    

Charotar University of Science and Technology (CHARUSAT) is a State Private University established under the Government of Gujarat.

The University is recognized by University Grants Commission (UGC) under Section 22 of UGC Act 1956 and accredited with grade A by NAAC in 2016. 



CHARUSAT offers 72 Programs through 9 different institutes, 6 faculties, 4 major centres/ cells at the Undergraduate, Postgraduate, Research level Ph.D. programs in numerous disciplines, and their specializations.

Some of the top listed recruiters of the University are Adani Group, Alstom, Asian Paints, Berger Paints, CADENCE, Ford Motors and ICICI

5. Extra Curricular Activities -

Students can participate in extracurricular activities like singing, dancing, drama performances, elocution contests, debates, instrument playing, and more thanks to CHARUSAT. In the same vein, SPOURAL, a university-level competition programme, is organised by CHARUSAT every academic year.



Each year, faculty members of CHARUSAT compose the central council at the university level. Students and instructors from CHAUSAT make up the central council. All extracurricular activities are carried out by the Central Council with the aid of volunteers.



The first round of auditions for spoural (cultural festival) are held at the appropriate departments. Finalized departmental students must proceed through the final audition round. Students are chosen to perform in the Spoural after two rounds of auditions.



Spoural events encompass both on- and off-stage activities. All competitors must perform in accordance with the event's rules, and they are evaluated by judges from several departments. Judges may be members of the CHARUSAT faculty or professionals from the outside world.



Further, there are various cells in the institute for the student to enhance him/her. For your convenience, we’ve listen them below: -

full_content:
G H PATEL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERNG AND TECHNOLOGY, GUJRAT                                              

G H Patel College of Engineering and Technology (GCET) is an engineering college in Vallabh Vidyanagar, Gujarat, India. 

GCET is named after Shri Gordhan bhai Hathi bhai Patel, a philanthropist of Gujarat.



Established in 1996 to provide undergraduate level technical education, GCET is also involved in industrial consultancy.

GCET is affiliated with the CVM University.

In 2013, GCET was ranked at number 53, at number 42 in 2014 and at number 28 in 2015 among the Top 100 Engineering Colleges of India by Dataquest

G.H. Patel College of Engineering and Technology Hostel Details:

G.H. Patel College of Engineering and Technology has two hostels, one for boys and one for girls.

The hostel rooms are spacious with natural lights and ventilation; each student is provided with a study table & chair. 

Each hostel has 24 hrs electricity, water supply, and internet connection. 

Other facilities include close by shopping complex, transportation, and medical facility.

Campus facility –

Library-

Central Library serves the academic needs of about 1879 UG students and nearly 114 faculty members and about 56 technical, administrative staff of our institute.

Besides its services are extended to a number of other organizations also.

The library resources stand at about 34,032 volumes of books, and back- volumes of periodicals, CD, audiocassettes, thesis and standards. 

The journal subscription goes beyond 125. 

While all journal subscriptions are available in the present form, some are available in CD ROM form also.

The library collection also includes a large section of expensive reference books, many text book collection meant to cater for the courses running in the institute and a collection of books ear-marked for needy students (under fund available through GAA Book Bank).



Wi-Fi-

GCET campus is covered with WIFI technology. Keeping in mind the technological needs of all students and staff members, 7 wireless routers have been used for extensive network coverage. Authenticated students and staff members can access the high speed internet service 24/7 round the year from GCET campus.

Sports Facilities-

Available Facilities includes-Badminton Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground





Labs-

Available Facilities includes: Chemistry Lab Computer Lab Electrical Lab Electronics Lab Mechanical Lab Communication System Lab Microprocessor Lab



Cafeteria-

G H Patel College of Engineering and Technology (GCET of Engineering and Technology has a cafeteria that services the food requirements of students, staff, and faculty.



Auditorium-

G H Patel College of Engineering and Technology (GCETof Engineering and Technology has a spacious auditorium with a seating capacity of 500 people. All the major events of the institution are organised in the auditorium. 



Extra Curricular Activities -

ISTE - Under the Societies Registration Act of 1860, the Indian Society for Technical Education was established as a professional, national, non-profit organisation. In order to expand its activities and further the cause of technological education, the Association of Principals of Technical Institutions (APTI), which was founded in 1941, changed its name to "Indian Society for Technical Education" in 1968.



IEEE -On February 28, 2006, the GCET IEEE branch received approval from IEEE Headquarters in New York. The original branch included two executive members and a total of 29 student members. The Asia Pacific Region of IEEE includes Gujarat Section's 7th IEEE Student Branch, GCET IEEEStudent Branch (IEEE Region 10).



CSI - A small group of IT enthusiasts who were a computer user club and felt the need to systematise their activities planted the first seeds for the Computer Society of India (CSI) in the year 1965. Additionally, they desired to share ideas and knowledge in what they perceived to be a rapidly developing industry.



IICE - Just before Indian Independence, in a time when nationalistic ideals were stoking, the Indian Institute of Chemical Engineers was founded. Dr. Hira Lal Roy, a brilliant visionary and the father of chemical engineering education in India, felt that the country needed a venue like this to nurture the fledgling initiatives for the expansion of chemical engineering education and to pique interest in the field.



ASME - Undergraduate students can access improved learning options through the ASME Students Section of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers. On the ASME website, students must sign up for membership. All interested students are eligible for a free first-year membership.

IGBC - In 2001, the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) created the Indian Green Building Council (IGBC). "To enable a sustainable built environment for everybody and facilitate India to be one of the global leaders in the sustainable built environment by 2025," reads the council's mission statement. The council provides a wide range of services, including the creation of new green building rating systems, certification services, and training programmes in green construction. The council also puts on the Green Building Congress, which is its premier yearly green building event.



NPTEL - The Indian government's Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) has funded the National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL), which is being carried out at the Indian Institute of Science and seven other Indian Institutes of Technology. For more than ten years, NPTEL has provided self-study courses in engineering, the humanities, and science.



PRAPAMBH - The GCET Project Club, PRAPAMBH, was established on July 18th, 2007, to offer workshops-based events focusing on the wide engineering culture. By pushing them to put their talents to use in the real world through teamwork, the group hopes to engage young engineers in the difficulties and enjoyment of science, technology, and engineering. The students competing in various technical events receive technical support from PRAPAMBH.



SPE - The Central Board of Irrigation and Power (CBIP, INDIA), Government of India, established the Society of Power Engineers (INDIA) in 1947. It is a top organisation working to advance the technological knowledge of power engineers by making the most recent advancements available globally. The society frequently holds group discussions, workshops, and journal publications.

ED Cell - Students can learn about entrepreneurship and related topics on the ED Cell's platform. A faculty coordinator and a faculty representative from each department make up the ED Cell. The cell is connected to the student coordinators. The cell regularly plans events that benefit the students of the institute and nearby institutes.



GCET Language Club - The goal of the language club is to create a platform for the students' overall development as well as their writing abilities. The modern language lab at GCET is well-maintained and features Orell software for language learning. Technology and communication are combined in Orell DLL. They learn not only how to become clearly defined professionals but also how to become clearly defined human beings. They are transitioning from being to becoming, and in this manner, our exercises act as a kind of lamp.



ISA - The International Society of Automation (www.isa.org) is a nonprofit professional organisation that establishes standards for those who use engineering and technology to enhance the administration, security, and privacy of contemporary automation and control systems used in critical infrastructure and in industry. The 1945-founded ISA creates widely used international standards, certifies professionals in the industry, offers education and training, publishes books and technical articles, organises conferences and exhibits, and offers networking and career development programmes for its 40,000 members and 400,000 clients worldwide.

full_content:
PANDIT DEENDAYAL ENERGY UNIVERSITY- GANDHINAGAR, GUJARAT                                                      



Pandit Deendayal Energy University (PDEU) is a private university located in Gandhi Nagar, Gujarat, India. It was founded on April 4, 2007.



In 2021, it obtained the 65th NIRF rank.

The university is a member of AIU and is NAAC accredited with an ‘A’ grade & CGPA of 3.39 out of 4.0.

The university has 4 schools, namely School of Petroleum Technology, School of Technology, School of Petroleum Management, and School of Liberal Studies.



Important information about this institute-



Location-



Address-

Knowledge Corridor, Raisan Village, PANDIT DEENDAYAL ENERGY UNIVERSITY Rd, Gandhinagar, Gujarat 382007



Campus map link-

https://g.page/pdeuofficial?share



Campus size- 100 ACRES



 



 









HOW TO REACH PANDIT DEENDAYAL ENERGY UNIVERSITY?



Bhaijipura near koba circle is the best landmark. Cab ride from Ahmedabad station is nearly 45 mins and from airport its just 20 mins.



Nearest railway station are kalupur and Sabarmati, then you can come by brts or daily bus services which have stop near the college cross road, nearest airport is like 14 km away it is well connected.

Student hostel –



Each Hostel of the University offers a variety of Social and Academic oriented activities that make it possible to learn more not only about you but also about those living in hostel.

 Our in-campus residential facilities offer furnished hostel rooms on three seat sharing basis subject to its availability. 

There are Wi-Fi enabled hostel blocks which are well-designed to meet general needs of the students.

These include emergency medical facilities, a doctor on call and a well-equipped and comfortable lounge to facilitate group activities.

Hostel facilities-

Security:Access into the Hostel areas is controlled 24 hours Securities. For Girls rooms and their privacy are being taken care of specially

.

Wired Rooms:Each room in all of our halls has two Ethernet ports connected to the campus network and the internet. Students need to bring their own computer of course.



Laundry:The laundry service is arranged at Hostel. Students can manage with their time schedule. They have to pay as per pricelists.



Fitness Facility:University has its Wellness Centre Facility available on charge basis. When living on campus you are more likely to visit these facilities during specified hours.



Images of the hostel –

Particulars

85,000



Mess Charges (FY 2020-21):



a) Rs. 44,000/- per year for breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks as per canteen schedule. This is compulsory for candidates opting for hostel facility of the University.

b) Rs. 29,000/- per year for breakfast, lunch, and snacks for day scholars as per the canteen schedule. (Monday to Friday)



Campus facilities-



Library-



The Library Resource Centre at the university has been automated using Koha- an open-source international user- friendly library package and stocked with 64800 books, 231 print journals, 14 databases, 14010 E-Books, 8424 E-Journals, 3676 CDs/DVDs. 

Services such as OPAC for checking online availability and reserving online, biometric reader, remote access to e-resources and database are available at the library. 

Library also subscribes to online databases like EBSCO, Infraline, American Chemical Society, ASCE, ASME, IEEE Explore, J-Store etc. School of Petroleum Management (SPM) Library is on the ground floor and centrally located at the SPM building with open access to the collection of Books.

The Faculty of Engineering and Technology library is situated on the second floor at B block of PDEU, Small signboards FOET Library consists of two library SPM and SOT open access to the collection of books. School of Liberal Studies (SL 

Wellness Centre-

PDEU has a well-established wellness centre present on its campus which can accommodate up to 50 students at once. The centre is open throughout the day. 



Sports Facilities-

The university provides various facilities for outdoor games which include open ground for football and cricket, practice court for basketball, volleyball, badminton.







Laboratories-

Well-equipped laboratories are present within each department of the university. Some of the laboratories include Physics Laboratory, Chemistry Laboratory, Thermodynamics Laboratory, Radiation Measurement Laboratory, Network Theory Lab, Power Electronics Lab.







Cafeteria & Food Court-

Food court in the university provides healthy food which is chartered by the dietitians and refreshments throughout the day. 



Arts Room-

This room provides space for the students to practice music, dance, drama and organises various cultural activities of the university. 

Language Lab-

This lab in the university helps the students to comprehend and use different languages effectively.

Auditorium-

Air-conditioned lecture halls of the university give the comfortable ambiance to the students to learn and develop more. 

All the Lecture Halls are equipped with Projectors and PC.

They have comfortable seating of rotatable chairs. There are around 10 Lecture halls for School of Liberal studies.

 





Extra Curricular Activities -



AJIA BUNKER



Ajia Bunker is a club for Korean and Japanese culture. As Korean and Japanese pop culture, which includes K-dramas (Korean television series), K-pop, and Japanese pop music, affect young people around the world (Korean pop music). This club captures the spirit of these vivacious and distinctive cultures. We intend to expose the participants to Korean and Japanese pop music, animation, food, literature, art, and gaming throughout the course of the year.







DAASTAN



All theatre enthusiasts are given a platform through Daastan, the PDEU Dramatics Club, to express their craft, through emotions, without restraint. The main goal of this club is to develop aspiring artists through a variety of activities such stage acting, street acting, stand-up comedy, mime, anchoring, and hosting workshops to develop various abilities. The group welcomes everyone who is keen to learn and have fun together, not simply those who have experience in a certain profession. Everyone has the chance to reinvent their talent on stage thanks to Daastan.

DEBSOC



The PDEU DebSoc group was established to discuss, consider, and debate a variety of topics, including global agendas and even locally relevant issues, all while attempting to foster critical thinking. The group hosts discussions on a variety of topics, including ethics, public policy, and possibly even existence in general.

SPIC MACAY



The Society for the Promotion of Indian Classical Music and Culture Amongst Youth (SPIC MACAY) is a non-profit youth organisation that promotes Indian classical music, classical dance, folk music, yoga, crafts, and other aspects of Indian culture in order to preserve and honour the heritage of these untouchable facets of the country's cultural heritage. The SPIC MACAY- PDEU branch holds a number of events where we examine the various facets of our Indian culture! Together, we learn from this and share our experiences.

AATMANN



The goal of Pdpu's psychological club, Aatman, is to protect the wellbeing of every student on campus. The club's sole focus is on various mental health issues and an individual's mental well-being ( topics often untouched otherwise). Aatman also want to make psychology accessible to everyone by introducing it to them. He also wants to give people the confidence to seek assistance when they do. It intends to achieve its goal by planning various events and hosting various performances to increase awareness among the attendees of the same.

ALCHEMY



The mission of our group is to advance deep chemical knowledge that is applied in daily tasks and activities. Through engaging and fascinating seminars, webinars, and events, we also assist students in learning and utilising various chemistry topics as well as the most recent advancements in the chemistry domains. The club also makes an effort on a monthly basis to assist and educate people about the value of chemistry and how it is so closely tied to our lives that it may be considered the crown jewel of life's difficulties.

ANIRVEDA



Anirveda, the TechnoEconomics Club of PDEU, is enthusiastic about a wide range of subjects since they genuinely believe in breaking down barriers. The club juggles with technology, economics, and everything in between. The club's mission is to raise public understanding of current events and how they impact the global economy. Anirveda wants to unite all students and make difficult knowledge more approachable and available to everyone.

AVIONICS



The PDEU's aero-modeling club is called Avionics. It covers a variety of aviation-related topics, and the staff is dedicated to educating people about the industry. The goal of the club is to provide students with the ideal environment for learning and knowledge sharing. The club places a strong focus on the value of innovation. This club's mission of supporting innovation in the aeronautics area is made possible by the contributions of several professionals, educators, aviation enthusiasts, and students.



Placement statistics-



During the 2020 placement drive, the overall highest salary stood at INR. 19.20 LPA which was offered by ONGC to the students from the School of Technology. 11, B. Tech students from PDEU got the offer of the mentioned highest package.

Under the discipline of Management, the highest CTC suffered a decline as the amount offered was INR. 14 LPA in comparison to INR. 18.4 LPA in 2019.

DHIRUBHAI AMBANI INSTITUTE OF INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY GANDHINAGAR  (DA-IICT)                                                                







(DA-IICT), is a private Self-financed University/Institute located in Gandhinagar, Gujarat, India.







It offers courses in Engineering, Science, and Design departments. The courses offered by DA-IICT are B Tech, M Tech, M Des, MSc, and Ph.D. 



The top recruiters of DA-IICT are Microsoft, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs.

Important information regarding institute-



Location-



Address- Daiict college, Reliance Cross Rd, Gandhinagar, Gujarat 382007



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/eE8X2H4obrfs5gBa9





Campus size- 50 acres



How to reach DAIICT?

By Air- Ahmedabad airport boasts of a good taxi service where cabs can be hired to take you to DA-IICT. Regular buses are also available at a short distance from the airport which would drop you off at either Gh-0 or Ch-0 from where you can hire an auto to take you to the college campus. Directions to the campus would be placed at strategic points as well as our help desk will be stationed at the airport.

By Rail- Government buses, shuttling between Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar, run on a regular basis, and one of their various stops is just a stone's throw from the Ahmedabad railway station. Also, Gujaratis are very amiable people, and will direct you to the proper bus if asked. The stop at Gandhinagar where you need to disembark is Gh-0 or Ch-0, both of which are less than 1 Km from the DA-IICT campus. From here, autos could be easily hired to the campus. we will also be placed at these points to guide you. Taxi services consist of mostly Ambassador Cabs and taxis could be hired for a one-way trip to DA-IICT.

By Road- Regular buses are available from the Gita Mandir Bus terminal at Ahmedabad, apart from the various bus-stops across the city to take you to Gh-0 or Ch-0 near DA-IICT.

Student hostel -



Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology has two halls of residence, one for men and the other for women. 

For students using their own computers in the room, internet facility is provided at free of cost.

Boys Hostel -

Number of beds – 900, Shared Rooms, In-Campus Hostel, 

Girls Hostel-

Number of beds – 195, Shared Room, In-Campus Hostel

Hostel Facilities-

Hot water (using geysers), Laundry and TV Rooms with D2H are available at all halls of residence.



A very nice food cafeteria is available on campus for food of hostel residents where variety of food is available at nominal cost.



There is on campus medical centre where two well experienced doctors visit for treating the hostel residents on a stipulated time on all official days. 



Institute vehicle with driver is available for 24 hours for any medical emergency.

Hostel image-





Hostel fees-

Hostel Rent: Rs. 20,000 to Rs. 22,000 per semester depending upon the hostel block in which the room is allotted 

There are multiple food options available in the campus (The expense will be approximately Rs. 5,500 per month)month



Campus facilities-

Campus library (resource centre)-

The library is located in two wi-fi-enabled buildings with an area of approximately 16,000 square feet, which can host approximately 300 people at a time. The library has a stock of 11230 e-books, 78 print journals, 31020 books, 5674 e-journals, 10 databases, 663 theses and 3913 audio visual materials in ICT, arts and social sciences.



The library has resource sharing a collaboration with Information and Library Network (INFLIBNET) and National Digital Library of India (NDLI).



Cafeteria-

DA-IICT campus has 7 food courts offering a wide variety of food covering almost all the Indian cuisine.



Two food courts serve South Indian food, one special breakfast, snacks and pastries and one fruit and fruit juices.



There is a special arrangement with Amul to make Amul products available at the food court and a separate food court which serves non-vegetarian food including egg dishes.



Sport complex-

Badminton Court, Cricket Ground, Basketball courts -02, Athletic track(400mtrs) -01, Indoor Badminton courts -03, Gymnasium with all modern facilities-01, Football ground-01,Volleyball Courts -02, Table Tennis Hall equipped with six tables - 01, Yoga and meditation zone.



Labs-

 Computer Lab| Language Lab| Multimedia Lab| Grid Lab| Networks Lab| VLSI Lab| RF Lab |Computational Science and HPC Lab







Security - 

The college has necessary security around the clock. The security is conducted across an authorised private security company employing security supervisors and security personnel. Their work is tracked by the management of the college and also by the centrally controlled establishment of the Reliance ADA Team.

Gym –

Well-equipped Gymnasium and Yoga Hall on the first floor of the sports complex.



Hospital / Medical Facilities- 

The Institute has a Medical Centre. There are two doctors visiting the centre at specified hours and the students can consult them without any charge. DA-IICT has arrangements with the Apollo and SAL Hospitals which allows the students to be admitted on concessional rates without advanced deposit.

Other facilities-

Extra Curricular Activities -

Sports and games have a significant and distinctive role in the daily curriculum at DAIICT. It supports not just the general educational objectives but also the growth and maintenance of fitness, motor skills, social skills, and health. Our institute's sports department has consistently worked to fulfil the fundamental goals of games and sports by giving aspirant students the proper infrastructure and inspiration. Numerous talented athletes who were created by our school have won medals for our school in intramural and extramural events. Athletics, Table Tennis, Badminton, Basket Ball, Tennis, Cricket, Volleyball, Chess, and Football are just a few of the sports in which DAIICT has competed at the District, State, and National levels.

overview:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, VADODARA
Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Vadodara was established by the Ministry of Human Resource Development under Public-Private Partnership in 2013.
IIIT Vadodara is funded by the Government of India, Government of Gujarat, Gujarat Energy Research and Management Institute, Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals and Tata Consultancy Services.
The college offers B tech in CSE and IT, M Tech in CSE and PhD in Engineering and Science.
The B.Tech. program is offered under two specializations for a duration of four years. To be eligible for admission, the candidates must possess a 10+2 qualification in PCM with a minimum of 50% marks. Admission to the program is done on the basis of a valid score in JEE Main.
At the PG level, the institute offers M Tech. Computer Science Engineering under four specializations. To be eligible for admission, the candidates must have a B.Tech. degree with a minimum of 60% marks in aggregate. Selection to the program will be based on scores in GATE.
Candidates seeking admission to the Ph.D. program should have a Master’s degree in Engineering or equivalent or an M Phil degree with Mathematics as a major subject.
Ph.D. admission is based on a written exam conducted by the institute followed by an interview.
Candidates who have qualified GATE/CSIR UGC NET/UGC NET/UGC-JRF will be given preference during admission..

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, VADODARA   

   

Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Vadodara was established by the Ministry of Human Resource Development under Public-Private Partnership in 2013. 







IIIT Vadodara is funded by the Government of India, Government of Gujarat, Gujarat Energy Research and Management Institute, Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals and Tata Consultancy Services.

The college offers B tech in CSE and IT, M Tech in CSE and PhD in Engineering and Science. 

The B.Tech. program is offered under two specializations for a duration of four years. To be eligible for admission, the candidates must possess a 10+2 qualification in PCM with a minimum of 50% marks. Admission to the program is done on the basis of a valid score in JEE Main.

At the PG level, the institute offers M Tech. Computer Science Engineering under four specializations. To be eligible for admission, the candidates must have a B.Tech. degree with a minimum of 60% marks in aggregate. Selection to the program will be based on scores in GATE.

Candidates seeking admission to the Ph.D. program should have a Master’s degree in Engineering or equivalent or an M Phil degree with Mathematics as a major subject.

Ph.D. admission is based on a written exam conducted by the institute followed by an interview.

Candidates who have qualified GATE/CSIR UGC NET/UGC NET/UGC-JRF will be given preference during admission..

https://maps.app.goo.gl/trLMQs8CPgncj9oKA







Campus size- 50 acre





How to reach IIIT Vadodara?



The nearest Bus stop is called the Pathikashram Central Bus stop (6.5 Kms), Nearest major Railway station is at Ahmedabad (32 Kms), and the Airport is the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (24 Kms).  

 

Gandhinagar is 26 Kms from Ahmedabad, 134 Kms from Vadodara, 150 Kms from Godhra, 289 Kms from Surat, 240 Kms from Rajkot and 553 Kms from Mumbai. 



Students/ Parents can book cabs like Ola, Uber. Other conventional vehicle is also  available like autos and taxis with negotiable rates.

full_content:
THE MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA    

   

Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda [MSU] is a private university founded in the year of 1949, at Vadodara, Gujarat.

It offers a huge number of courses, namely UG, PG, and Doctoral courses in various faculties that include Engineering & Technology, Science, Arts, Commerce, Fine Arts, Law, Family & Community Services, Education & Psychology, Pharmacy, Journalism, Social Work, Management Studies, & Performing Arts.                                                                                                                                     

Admissions to the courses at Maharaja Sayajirao University [MSU] of Baroda are either based on Entrance Tests, counselling rounds, or based on Merit obtained in the previous qualifying examination.

Some of the top recruiters at MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA includes Accenture, Amazon, Microsoft, L&T Technology Services, Cognizant, Reliance Industries Ltd, Tata Motors, Dabur India Ltd and much more.

Important information regarding the institute –

Location-



Address- The M. S. University of Baroda, Pratapgunj,Vadodara, Gujarat-390002



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/aN4ptjEkxuEkrabd8

Campus size- 275 acres                                  



How to reach the institute-

MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA. is situated at the Prime Location of Vadodara. There are several ways to reach MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA. 

The very 1st way is by Rail transport; by train. we can reach M.S. university by train as it's opposite to the Vadodara railway station; 100-200mts around. 

Secondly, we can reach MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA by bus transport as well; as bus services are available directly to Vadodara and guess what bus depot is even 100-200mts around the university.

The University has 12 boys and 4 girl hostels.

 All the hostels are equipped with automatic gas fired hot water boilers facility, which provides hot water during the winter season. 

Hostels are also equipped with a dining hall and a common hall.

Campus Facilities-



Library-



Shrimati Hansa Mehta Library was established on 1st May 1950.

It has a single largest Reading Room of 10,600 sq. ft. which can accommodate about 1100 readers at a time.

Library subscribes to more than 512 journals.

The library has over 8 Lakh books/ periodicals and above 1300 dissertations/thesis.  It has 10 Kindle e-book readers.













Canteen-



The cafeteria of the university provides meals to the students, faculty and staff at a discounted rate.

Canteen remains open for all days 24 hours (24×7), Quality of food is decent.











Sports facilities –



Sports Facilities includes the like of cricket, kho-kho, hockey, football, volleyball, basketball, handball court, Tennis etc.

  





 





 









Labs-



There is various LAB at MAHARAJA SAYAJIRAO UNIVERSITY OF BARODA, some of them are Civil Engineering Lab, Computer Lab, Electrical Lab, Electronics Lab, Mechanical Lab.

 









Auditorium-

The university auditorium is used by the students and faculty to conduct various activities and functions.

C.C Mehta Auditorium has been equipped with advanced audio and visual facilities.

 

Medical/Hospital-

The health centre was established in 1964 and has trained medical officers and nurses.

It caters to all the medical needs of the students and staff of the university. 

Other Facilities include Music room, dance room, guest room.

Extra Curricular Activities -

There are several organizations to join at MSU which provide you with a variety of extra curricular activities. The popular student organisations in MSU are listed below:

Political

VVS (Vidyarthi Vikas Sangh) affiliated with ABVP (now ABVP).

ABVP (Akhil Bhartiya Vidyarthi Parishad) affiliated BJP

NSUI ( National Students Union of India) affiliated congress.

Hostel committee.

Training & placement cell.

The student union of the university (i.e UGS, VP, FR)

Social



Vivekanand Kendra ( not a students organization as such)

Prerna ( event)

Entertainment

Paramarsh

BBA baazigar

One of the famous clubs there is AISA(All India Student Association). There are several other organizations for instance for technology faculty the students get involved in the events.



Some of the reasons you should join the organizations and clubs are given below:

Building your capacity is the main benefit you receive.

With the organised system you have in place, you can enhance your abilities.

Enduring friendships.

Learning.

Recollections of the work that was done there.

These organisations can help you become aware of qualities you didn't know you have.

full_content:
NIRMA UNIVERSITY, AHMEDABAD                                                                                                   

Nirma University, Ahmedabad is a private university established in the year 2003.



Nirma University was ranked between 101-150 by NIRF 2020 and was ranked 4 by Outlook Magazine. The university has been accredited by NAAC, NBA, and SAQS. It offers 5 UG, 8 PG, 4 integrated, and Ph.D. (Full time & Part-Time) courses like B Tech, MBA, M Tech, BCom LLB(Hons.), etc.

Its top recruiters are Deloitte, TATA, NVidia, Reckitt Benckiser, HUL, L&T, and Nestle, etc. 



Location-



Address- Sarkhej - Gandhinagar Hwy, Gota, Ahmedabad, Gujarat 382481

Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/LXUHet5kHygjmvh77





Campus size -115 acre



How to reach Nirma university?

Brts corridor is an option, nearest stop is sola Bhagwat on sg highway. From there one can reach Nirma by taking shuttle auto.

SGVP gurukul, Hiramani school(hostel) are opposite to Nirma on S.G. Highway.

Student hostel-



The campus of the university has separate hostels for boys and girls’ students. All the basic facilities are available at both boys and girl’s hostels.

There are three hostel buildings in all, two hostels are for the boys and one for the girls. The hostels have a total capacity of around 800 students.

Hostel facilities-

The hostels have sports and other recreational facilities such as cable TV, common room for interaction, etc.

All hostel rooms have internet connectivity round the clock.

All the hostels have basic amenities like water purifiers, common room, recreation room, mess, TV, laundry services, security and surveillance system etc.

Boys Hostel In-Campus Hostel, Girls Hostel In-Campus Hostel

Campus facilities –

Library-



Nirma University library acts as a key resource and learning centre of the University to meet the information needs of more than 6,000 users. 

It has adopted open-source software KOHA for integrated library management.

 Each constituent institute of the University has a separate library which is located in their respective buildings. To facilitate users with reading and brain storming, separate reading rooms have been provided along with the libraries. 

It has a collection of 1,07,288 books and 13,661 online periodicals. 

Library has subscribed to several Institutional Memberships like INFLIBNET Centre, The American Resource Library centre-Mumbai, The British Library – Ahmedabad, Institute of Engineers – Kolkata, Indian Society for Earthquake Technology (ISET), Developing Library Network (DELNET), Indian Geotechnical Society, The Computer Society of India, Vikram Sarabhai Library - IIM.



Gym-



The university has a well-equipped modern gymnasium.





Sports-



Nirma University has both indoor and outdoor sports facilities for the students. Students can play badminton, cricket, table tennis, football etc. during free time.



Labs-



Civil Engineering Lab| Computer Lab| Electrical Lab| Electronics Lab| Mechanical Lab| Instrumentation and Control Lab



Research lab-





Computer lab-



Mechanical lab-



Specialised lab(civil)-



Cafeteria-

The university has a cafeteria that provide hygienic and healthy vegetarian food. For hostel students, an individual mess is also present at the campus. 



Auditorium-

To host academic and non-academic events of the university, a well-furnished auditorium equipped with the latest technology is there in Nirma University campus. 



Medical/Hospital-

A medical centre is present within university premises. 

Classrooms-

All the classrooms of Nirma University are well furnished and equipped with latest technology. Smart classrooms at the campus makes teaching and learning an easy process. 

Transport-

The Institute owns a fleet of forty-five buses that ply in almost all areas in Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar. The transportation facility is available for students, faculty members and staff.



Alumni Associations-

Nirma University are proud of our Alumni and the difference they have made to the world around them. With more than 2000 members joining the group every year, the alumni network with about 15000 members currently is growing stronger year on year.

Banks Facilities-

A branch of the Kalupur Commercial Co-operative Bank Limited along with ATM is there in the university for the convenience of students. 



Other Facilities:



Moot Court (Law), Design Studio, A/C Classrooms, Seminar Hall and Air-conditioned Amphitheatre



Extra Curricular Activities -

Nirma University is not only a fantastic setting for intellectual pursuits and intellectual growth, but it is also a vivid and vibrant setting for personal and social growth. Numerous extracurricular, co-curricular, and curricular events in the areas of sports, adventure, and culture are taking place all throughout the campus. On campus, there are also a number of literary events.



Each institute offers a full schedule of athletic, adventurous, literary, and cultural events. 

Additionally, the university plans yearly athletic and cultural events for all of the centrally located institutes. All students have enough possibilities for their whole development, which is ensured by the university and its component institutes.



One of the distinctive aspects of the university is the presence of student organisations and clubs. The various institute organisations and clubs engage in a wide range of academic, social, cultural, and sporting activities. Students assume responsibility for the planning, organising, carrying out, and reviewing of events that occasionally take place at their particular institutes.



The kids' leadership and collaborative skills are fostered by this culture. The satisfaction that comes from a job well done motivates the kids to participate in these activities in addition to learning. During the process, the students become aware of their gifts and abilities and are motivated to hone them.

ER (Enchanted Rhythms), Infocrates , iL2 (Invincible Leaders' League), CSI (Computer Society of India), and OrCES (Organisation of Civil Engineering Students) are just a few of the organisations and groups that the Institute of Technology oversees.



Placement Statistics -

DHARMSINH DESAI UNIVERSITY-[DDU], NAIDAD



                                       



The University was set up by Shri Dharmsinh Dadubhai Desai in the year 1968.

DDU is also very popular for the campus placements of students by most of the prestigious organizations such as Amazon, Infosys, TCS, Reliance industries, ISRO, Tech-Mahindra, BYJU’s and many more.

Campus facilities -

Library-

The college library is providing separate reading room for students and faculties.

The library is totally computerised and it has SOUL [Software for University Libraries] which provides the database of books available in the library.

The library has 43017 books, 6413 journals, 12 international and 45 national journals.

The library opens at 8:30 AM to 8:30 PM on weekdays and 8:30 AM to 1:00 PM on Saturdays.



 

Classrooms-

classrooms in the college are spacious and are having multi-media facility.

 

Laboratory-

Labs are lined with modern instruments.

It has laboratories in each department. 



Workshop-

The college has Industrial Technology Machine Rooms.

 

Gym-

Dharmsinh Desai University gym facilities include the latest equipment for workouts. The gym also provides a trainer to guide the students.

Sports-

Chess, carrom, table tennis, badminton, football, tennis, cricket, basketball, volleyball, etc are the sports provided by the university sports department. The college has indoor facility of squash, badminton, table tennis, carom, chess and gymnasium and archery as an outdoor. Student at DDU Nadiad are always encouraged to participate in various sports to be physically and  

Cafeteria-

The university cafeteria facility serves hygienically prepared nutritious food to the students and staff members.



Auditorium

The university auditorium is very spacious and is used for organising events, seminars and conferences. The auditorium can accommodate a large number of audience.

Medical/Hospital

The university has a medical clinic, which provides first-aid facility to the students and staff members.

Wi fi

The entire campus is enabled with wi-fi. Dharmsinh Desai University wi-fi facility can be availed by the students and faculty members for free.

Banking-

The College has Oriental Bank of Commerce (OBC) Branch located inside the main building within the college premises. Additionally, there is an ATM with a passbook printing facility located near the college canteen.



Extra Curricular Activities - 



There are many clubs / groups for different activities. The major ones are listed below:

D. Coders:- This group consists of coders who code, participate in coding events, host coding events, and more. Check the Facebook page for other updates. 

Spandan :- This is a social welfare organization. Hold blood donation camps twice  a year, visit orphanages to  make  children smile, teach, visit elderly housing for at least a day to live a happy life, give comfort, and more. We are doing activities. They also recall home visits where child criminals have been imprisoned. 



CSI (Computer Society of India) :- This is primarily for CE and IT students. They arrange workshops (related to ethical hacking, HTML, web technology, etc.). They host technical events at Felicific, an absolutely creative techno culture festival. 

Shutterbugs :- They visit historic sites and archaeological sites and convey their knowledge of sculpture, infrastructure and history in the form of photographs. They are also exhibiting the best photos at the university. We also hold a photo contest. 

DDU Connect :- The ingenious idea of ​​a college newsletter  gave birth to this club. They provide students with insights into the events taking place at DDU, along with articles on the latest technology, poetry (submitted by the author or  student), and more. 



NCC (National Military Academy) :- This is not a club. But this is a group of students who want to learn about military training. Army officers will teach you different types of parades, weapons training (eventually), theory lectures, and more. I don't want to join the army, but to join, please visit the camp (ATC, CATC, etc.). Test certificates  help you in both public services and government work, as well as in the military. Learn how soldiers are trained thoroughly, how difficult it is  to become a soldier, and how to protect the people from terrorist attacks. But sadly, I have to go to Vidyanagar every Saturday for this. Nevertheless, you will learn a lot.

campus_facilities:
Library:
Parul University has 10 libraries with more than 24000 books, 15825 online journals/magazines, 1315 print journals/magazines, 30 news dailies, reference section contains encyclopaedias, books on general knowledge and competitive exams, dictionaries, previous volumes of journals/magazines and remote access to thousands of libraries through DELNET and also to various reference and plagiarism software. 
All libraries are linked to the central library “Gyanoday Bhawan” and are fully computerized with a barcode system for issue and return of books. 
More than 20000 students avail book bank facility every year from these libraries at the campus.

Sports:
Parul University also has the indoor sports facility like chess, carom, etc available for the students on campus. 

The University's students have brought laurels by winning many inter-college sports tournaments under the guidance of a dedicated team of Physical Instructors. 

Annual indoor and outdoor activities/competitions within the university are also organized to provide a strong platform to our students to showcase their sporting abilities.

Campus facilities -

Library-

Parul University has 10 libraries with more than 24000 books, 15825 online journals/magazines, 1315 print journals/magazines, 30 news dailies, reference section contains encyclopaedias, books on general knowledge and competitive exams, dictionaries, previous volumes of journals/magazines and remote access to thousands of libraries through DELNET and also to various reference and plagiarism software. 



All libraries are linked to the central library “Gyanoday Bhawan” and are fully computerized with a barcode system for issue and return of books. 



More than 20000 students avail book bank facility every year from these libraries at the campus.





Cafeteria-



Parul University serves hygienic, homely and healthy food for all our students and scholars is the top most priority for the management of Parul University. 

All the hostels of the university have spacious dining halls and messes which serve breakfast, lunch, and dinner as per the daily menu with special diet on Sundays. 

Moreover, there are food courts and college canteens where the students can go for tea, coffee, cold drinks, ice cream, snacks and different varieties of food throughout the day. The university also has Nescafe outlets on campus for students and staff.

                  



Sports complex

Parul University also has the indoor sports facility like chess, carom, etc available for the students on campus. 

The University's students have brought laurels by winning many inter-college sports tournaments under the guidance of a dedicated team of Physical Instructors. 

Annual indoor and outdoor activities/competitions within the university are also organized to provide a strong platform to our students to showcase their sporting abilities.

Available Facilities: 

Badminton, Court Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground ,Volleyball Court ,HandBall Court ,Table Tennis.



Sports Facilities





Sports Facilities





Soccer Field



Soccer Field



Gym

The fitness centre of Parul University has a modern gymnasium and aerobics arena spread over an area of more than 5000 square feet with sophisticated types of equipment, steam bath, sauna bath, hot showers and changing area.







Shuttle Service

Parul University has 63 buses and 20 passenger vans.

Daily Bus facilities from cities nearby Vadodara such as Anand, Nadiad, Ahmedabad, Karjan and Bharuch are also available to students and staff members. 

The university has drivers which are highly experienced and are licensed by the RTO to drive college buses to ensure utmost safety of both the students and staff members.



Labs



Computer Lab



Computer Lab



Computer Lab



Other facilities include –

Moot Court (Law)

Design Studio

Hospital / Medical Facilities

Wi-Fi Campus

Auditorium

Music Room

Dance Room

A/C Classrooms

Convenience Store



Extra Curricular Activities- 

Projections-2020

Projections is an international technical festival that Parul University is hosting with more than 45 different seminars, guest lectures, special events, and technical and non-technical presentations. Projections is one of Gujarat's largest technical festivals, with more than 7000 competitors and 15,000 spectators in 2016.



Innovation Day Celebration

Parul Institute of Technology had observed Innovation Day on the auspicious occasion of Dr. Abdul Kalam's 88th birthday anniversary. Through the use of audiovisual presentations, PowerPoint presentations, and poster presentations, students and faculty members actively engaged in the event and expressed their creative ideas.

“The Gandhi Global Solar Yatra” program

The Gandhi Global Solar Yatra programme was held at PU in collaboration with IIT, Bombay with the goal of conceptualising the concept of "Energy Swaraj." A "Student Solar Ambassador Workshop" was held at the Faculty of Engineering & Technology as a part of the programme. More than 100 children received hands-on instruction in building their own solar study lighting. The workshop's major goal was to make young people more aware of solar energy and provide them with the skills necessary to cope with the effects of climate change.

full_content:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, KURUKSHETRA

                                                        



The National Institute of Technology Kurukshetra (NITK) has a reputation for providing high-quality technical and management education, research, and training.



It has been designated as a National Institution of Great Importance.







Since its inception in 1963, NITK has made remarkable progress toward excellence. A wide lush green campus, excellent infrastructure, state-of-the-art support system, current curriculum, and dedicated faculty create an ideal atmosphere for great teaching, learning, and research.

There are nine boys' hostels and three girls' hostels at the Institute.



GIRLS HOSTEL



The Institute's girls hostel consists of two buildings: 1. Alaknanda Bhawan and 2. Bhagirathi Bhawan.

3. There are 610 seats available for B. Tech and postgraduate students at Cauvery Bhavan.



All of the hostels are well-maintained and well-equipped.



BOYS HOSTEL



There are nine boys hostels at NIT Kurukshetra. Three of the boys' hostels have triple beds, while the others have single beds.

The single-bed rooms in the Post-graduate hostel are all available.

From A to I, all of the hostels are named alphabetically. All of the names are connected to Mahabharta's characters.

In keeping with the custom, the new Mega Boys' Hostel (Hostel number. 10) is likewise titled alphabetically in the same series as 'Jagdishwar Bhawan', beginning with the letter 'J.'

Campus facilities-



Library Facility-



The library is located in a separate structure with a 3600 square metre covered space. The library effectively and efficiently meets the demands of academics, research scholars, and students with its extensive resources, space, and services.







There are 100000 books in the library, 6028 back volumes, and 3659 e-books. In the domains of science, management, and technology, the library subscribes to 158 print periodicals and roughly 2800 online articles.

The library is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week to its users.



Sports Complex- 



Basketball, volleyball, lawn tennis, badminton, and racquetball courts, as well as cricket and football grounds, are all part of the complex's lush green playground.



It also contains a 400-metre sports track and a mini-gym.

Students can participate in a number of recreational activities as a result of this.













Health Centre-



The residents and students of NIT Kurukshetra have access to a health centre for their medical requirements.

A full-time resident medical practitioner works at the health centre.

For specialised care and critical cases, the health centre directs patients to empanelled doctors. The health centre has a laboratory, pharmacy, radiology, ECG service, casualty, and dental treatment.

In the event of an emergency, an ambulance service is available.







Security-



For the safety and protection of students and staff, a security force of over 130 people is on hand.

The security personnel are well-trained and come from the armed services. To protect the safety of students, staff, and property, 67 CCTV cameras have been deployed throughout the campus.



Guest Room-



National Institute of Technology Kurukshetra guest homes are developed as an added advantage for students who require additional housing and are located near the university.

All students who are in need of a place to stay are given a place to stay in a hostel. Normally, a student whose domicile is within 30 kilometres of the University Campus, particularly male students, will not be supplied with hostel accommodations. 

In each hostel, there is a mess/kitchen unit. Under the direction of the particular Warden, the Mess is operated by a student body on a cooperative basis. The affairs of hostels are handled by a council of Wardens led by the Chief Warden, with participation from the particular hostel's student committees.

Every hostel features a large, comfortable common area with a television and a dish antenna hookup. A billiards facility is also available on the premises of the boys dormitory. The Hostels are now being networked with the rest of the campus.

 The Reference Section, Circulation Section, Audio-Visual Section, Periodical Section, Book Bank, and Digital Library make up the Library and Information Centre. 

The University library has used Library Software to automate all of its library activities in order to deliver a more effective and diverse selection of academic resources, such as books, journals, online databases, DVDs, and other valuable items.

 With its current collection of knowledge resources and innovative information services, the GDGU Library & Information Resource Center fills an important gap.

GD GOENKA UNIVERSITY, GURUGRAM



The G D Goenka University, recognized by UGC, is the top ranking private university in Delhi, NCR and the best University in Gurgaon. 

The highly acclaimed University is the Higher Education venture of G D Goenka group with the path-breaking legacy of 28 years in Education.



Important information regarding by this college-

Location: 



Address- Gate No 3: G D Goenka education cityG D Goenka education city, Sohna - Gurgaon Rd, Sohna, Haryana 122103



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/qDppwSELEyeu8bgv5



Campus size: 60 acres







Reaching gd GOENKA UNIVERSITY-



The college is only about 6.42 kilometres from the Cansaulim railway station and can be reached in about 18 minutes.

In each hostel, there is a mess/kitchen unit. Under the direction of the particular Warden, the Mess is operated by a student body on a cooperative basis. Every hostel features a large, comfortable common area with a television and a dish antenna hookup. A billiards facility is also available on the premises of the boys dormitory. The Hostels are now being networked with the rest of the campus.
