Campus facilities-



Campus life-



The atmosphere on campus is pleasant. The weather on campus is always pleasant.

 On the 20th of February, there is an annual festival. It is Arunachal Pradesh's most popular function, with numerous additional annual festivals to commemorate it.



Library-

The library at G D Goenka University is the heart of the institution, with a large collection of books in Engineering, Management, Architecture & Planning, Communication, Fashion & Design, Hospitality, Humanities & Social Sciences, Medical and Allied Sciences, Law and Education, as well as a large reference collection to meet the academic and research needs of students and faculty.

 The Reference Section, Circulation Section, Audio-Visual Section, Periodical Section, Book Bank, and Digital Library make up the Library and Information Centre. 

The University library has used Library Software to automate all of its library activities in order to deliver a more effective and diverse selection of academic resources, such as books, journals, online databases, DVDs, and other valuable items.

 With its current collection of knowledge resources and innovative information services, the GDGU Library & Information Resource Center fills an important gap.



Mess and canteen-  

The food served in the mess is also good. Students have been provided food 4-times, and night mess is also available. It provides awesome and tasty food. 

Labs-  

Projectors are used while teaching. Laboratories are well updated and safe. 



Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi Is available on the whole campus.

Sports-

With seven football and hockey fields, a cricket pitch, a 400-meter athletics track, various basketball, tennis, and volleyball courts, and a Fitness Center that is certainly the crown jewel of the GDGWS infrastructure, GDGWS is a national leader in sports infrastructure.





Extra Curricular Activities-



Swimming Club

 

Board of Student Activities (BSA) - Swimming Club was a member of the organising team for the 2019 Inter-university Annual Sports Meet, Sportopia, which organised a swimming competition in which students from various schools and universities actively competed.



Music Club



The music club "REVERB" hosted a music competition for the university's aspiring singers on September 26, 2018. At 11:20 a.m., the event began in the B-block auditorium. The event lasted for approximately 50 minutes. The judges for this competition were Mr. Saurabh Ambasth and Dr. Tithi Bhatnagar. Mansi Kapoor served as the ceremony's master. The first competitor entered the stage after the competition's regulations were declared. After eleven flawless performances in a row, the judges had some time to calculate the scores as Ekta Sehrawat, Kumar Gandharv, and Ketan gave a lovely medley. After that, the results were made public, with Shreya Sharma being named the first runner-up and Shwas Vipul Gossain being named the winner.







Literary Club



On November 17, 2018, GDGU's Board of Student Activities (BSA) Literary Club hosted a poem recitation contest. Students from the University's various schools participated actively in the competition. The topics covered everything from love to life, patriotism, and women's empowerment. Dr. Anindita Roy Choudhary, Assistant Professor, School of Engineering, Dr. Ramandeep Kaur, Assistant Professor, and Dr. Reva Raina, Professor & Dean School of Education, judged the competition. For their recitations, three students—Yash Aggarwal from the School of Communication, Govind Bali from the School of Law, and Poornima Singh Pawar from the School of Law—were recognised as winners.





Dance Club



On April 10, 2019, G D Goenka University hosted the solo division of the Reverie's Dance Competition, which was organised by the Board of Student Activities (BSA). The contest got underway with a lot of fervour and energy. The competition was attended by students from several schools, and it was judged by Drs. Shewta Mongia and Abhilasha Vyas, assistant professors at the school of engineering. The contestants showcased a variety of dance genres, including western, modern, hip-hop, and classical. All of the participants' choreography, rhythm, coordination, and presentation were flawless. Rahul Yadav from the School of Architecture & Planning received the Second spot, while Shruti Chauhan from the School of Agriculture Sciences received the First position.







Cricket Club



On November 14, 2018, the GD Goenka University students participated in an intra-university cricket league hosted by the Cricket Club Board of Student Activities (BSA). The gathering was mesmerised by the enthusiastic performances of our students, with Milan Bedi, SoE, and his team coming in second, and Naman Malik, SoE, winning. It also put great effort and focus into organising Sportopia 2019, an annual inter-university sports meet, in which students from various colleges and institutions actively participated.

Library:
Dr. Kannan was born on September 15, 1967, and graduated from Bishop Heber College of Bharathidasan University in Tiruchirappalli, Tamil Nadu, with a degree in Library and Information Science. He earned his master's degree in library and information science from Madras University and his Ph.D. from Alagappa University.
 He earned his Ph.D. in Library and Information Science from Gauhati University in Guwahati (Assam). Public Library System in Bhutan: A Systematic Approach for Future Developments was the title of his study. He also holds a master's degree in English literature and a postgraduate certificate in computer science and applications.
Dr. Kannan has more than 20 years of expertise in the field of library and information science, including teaching LIS and working as a librarian at academic libraries.
 He has worked at Bishop Heber College and Arunai Engineering College (AEC) in Tamilnadu, as well as Sherubtse College at the Royal University of Bhutan.

overview:
SRM UNIVERSITY DELHI-NCR, SONEPAT
Sonepat, Haryana, India's SRM Institution, Haryana (SRMH), sometimes referred to as SRM University, Delhi-NCR, Sonepat, is a private university.
The Rajiv Gandhi Education City is where it is situated. The SRM Institute of Science & Technology Trust (SRM IST Trust), Chennai, passed the Haryana Private Universities (Amendment) Act, 2013, which also founded Shree Guru Gobind Singh Tricentenary University, Jagan Nath University, NCR, GD Goenka University, and K.R. Mangalam University.
The school is a member of the SRM Educational Group, which also includes the parent deemed university, the SRM Institute of Science and Technology in Chennai, as well as the private SRM University in Sikkim.

DRONACHARYA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING



Dronacharya College of Engineering, Gurugram (Estd: 1998) is one of the most well-known technical colleges in the country, having been authorised by the AICTE in New Delhi. 

The college was founded in 1998 and has since grown to become a must-attend institution for engineering students interested in pursuing a brilliant career in the field of technology.

 Dronacharya College of Engineering is associated with MDU, Rohtak, which is ranked 90th among Indian institutions by the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) in 2019. It offers bachelor's and master's degrees in engineering.

On campus, the College offers a stylishly constructed Boys' Hostel with one, two, and three seaters. The hostel is a home away from home, with hygienically prepared healthy cuisine, a leisure room with a television, indoor games and internet access, and exceptional discipline combined with desired freedom. 

The Girls? Hostel in Sector-5, Gurgaon, is modelled after an ideal academic environment where rules are followed to the letter.

 We understand parents' concerns about providing a safe and secure environment for their children. The wardens in both hostels serve as protectors for the residents.







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



Every festival is celebrated here College level , university level, and national level camps are also created NSS, NCC is compulsory for all students mainly in the first year Nehru library is the largest library in Asia placed here Dance classes, sports classes, music, and other cultural activity classes are also for all students of the university.





Library-

These aren't books, lumps of lifeless paper on the shelves, but living minds." The Gilbert Highet Library is an educational institution's heart, intellect, and soul, as well as a source of creativity, inspiration, and insight for both students and faculty. Learning and libraries are like the light and the lamp to each other.

The Information Resource Centre (Library), which is aesthetically designed and state-of-the-art, is located on the third level of the College building and is completely air-conditioned. It has been progressively and methodically bolstered.

The IRC's operations and services are fully automated, thanks to the National Informatics Centre's e-Granthalaya multi-user Integrated Library Automation Software and a Bar Code-based circulation system. The IRC has a number of features.





Mess and canteen-  

The Canteen is an excellent location for scholarly and personal interactions.

 The college features a well-designed canteen that serves sanitary cuisine, snacks, juices, soft drinks, and other beverages at reasonable costs.



Auditorium-  

The College has an air-conditioned, well-furnished Auditorium with an LCD projector and sound-proof audio-video equipment. 

It can accommodate approximately 450 people and is where all lectures, group debates, and other practical plans are held.

Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at high speed.

Robotics Automation lab-

The College has an air-conditioned, well-furnished Auditorium with an LCD projector and sound-proof audio-video equipment. It can accommodate approximately 450 people and is where all lectures, group debates, and other practical plans are held.



Extra Curricular Activities-



Technical Language Society



The club provides students who are eager for information with a competitive platform. It promises to foster in the students a love of quizzing, debating, creative writing, reading, elocution, etc. A literary club would improve general knowledge and assist students gain confidence and self-efficacy. The Technical Language Society's mission is to give all students a learning environment and a stage on which to showcase their skills. Both organisational and persuasive skills will be developed in the students.







Coding Club



The Club's mission is to find exceptional programmers who can write, design, and build code that adheres to acceptable programming standards. By offering courses that advance programming knowledge and expertise, it seeks to advance and develop the programming culture.







All B.Tech. and M.Tech. students from all academic fields are eligible to compete in this challenge competition. Each participant will be given a challenge, and each participant will have a certain amount of time to complete the assignment. Winners will be selected among the participants who develop the best code (one that satisfies the challenge's requirements).



Robotics Club



Even though the idea and development of autonomous machines are not new, it took until the 20th century for reliable study into the practicality and applications of robots to expand significantly. And today, the area of robotics is expanding quickly as we continue to research, design, and create new robots for a variety of useful applications, including household, commercial, and military ones. Having said that, the DRONACHARYA Robotics Club works to pique the curiosity of the school's students about robotics. Along with making a genuine effort to promote information about robotics and its many uses, TEAM stands for "Together Everyone Achieves More." This excellent quality is what has kept the club together and allowed it to develop to its present potential.

campus_facilities:
Library:
The Digital Library of India (DLI) is a free-to-access digital collection of rare books gathered from India's libraries. 
The DLI project began in early 2000 with the goal of archiving all of humanity's significant literary, artistic, and scientific works, preserving them digitally, and making them freely available through the Internet for education, study, and appreciation for future generations.
 The creation of a Digital Library with a free-to-read, searchable collection of one million books, primarily in Indian languages, is proposed as a first step in realising this objective.
 The project was started by the Office of the Principal Scientific Advisor to the Government of India and then taken over by the Ministry of Communications' Department of Electronics and Information Technology (DeitY).

overview:
GEC PANIPAT- GEETA ENGINEERING COLLEGE
K.R Education Society established the Geeta Group of Institutions in 1985 with the goal of promoting excellence in higher education. The institute was founded in response to Haryana's serious dearth of high-quality higher education.
GGI has established itself as one of the premier engineering institutions in Haryana, Delhi NCR, and Panipat in all of its areas of Engineering, Law, Hotel Management, Education, Polytechnic, and Management.
The Geeta Group of Institutions' prestige has been further boosted by the attainment of the Academic Excellence Award in the field of technical education.
GGI's mission is to help students improve their technical, conceptual, and overall growth. GGI aspires to nurture students by instilling professional ethics in them and preparing them to be responsible citizens.

full_content:
THE NORTHCAP UNIVERSITY

The NorthCap University (NCU), formerly ITM University Which is a private university found in Sector 23-A, Gurgaon, Haryana, India. 

It was established in 1996 by Educate India Society, registered under the Registration of Societies Act of 1860 as an Engineering Institute, to give education in Technical and Management. 

Formerly it was affiliated to Maharshi Dayanand University, RohtaK.

Important information regarding this college-

Location: 



Address- Near Rotary Public School Cartarpuri Alias, Huda, Sector 23A, Gurugram, Haryana 122017



Campus map link-  

https://goo.gl/maps/x5NoQeE5qij97jUcA





Campus size- 10 acres







Reaching Northcap University-

The college is only about 3 kilometres from the Cyber city metro station and can be reached in about 5 minutes.



Engineering Courses offered by this college-



Courses

Eligibility

Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning

Cyber Security & Forensics

Cloud Computing

Robotics and Automation

Data Sciences

Augmented Reality & Virtual Reality

Computer Science and Engineering

Internet of Things

VLSI & Embedded System

Blockchain

Full Stack Web Development

10+2 =12 passed with 75 percent marks

Students Hostel-  

The hostel facilities are good and average. The food is bad and the fees are also high. 

The hostel seats are not much, it is limited  and the rooms are generally 4 sharing.

 Hostel students have to get their own AC/cooler on rent and pay their bills. 

Most of the students do stay in PG's. The rentals are around rs. 7000 to 12,000 per month.   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



Campus life has an excellent gender ratio.

 They have students from Delhi so there is quite a good diversity. They have proper and beautiful labs with proper infrastructure. 

Colleges have a cricket league, fests, robot wars etc. These events are happening regularly by the college society. Colleges have societies named ACM, GSC , SAE working regularly.



Infrastructure-

The campus is completely equipped with Wi-Fi and an air-conditioner. The laboratories are well maintained and of good quality. 

Classrooms have projectors, and the library has all the required study material. It also provides 2 cafeterias. 

A medical room is there and an ambulance is also available. In case of emergency, they also go to Columbia Asia hospital.

The facilities in mechanical engineering are really helpful, experienced and have up to date knowledge of the course. 

The library has a large variety of books.



Mess and canteen-  

The food at the canteen is tasty, but the menu could use a few more choices. The canteen is organised effectively. Both indoor and outdoor games are entertaining and well-planned.

      Labs-  

Spacious classrooms and laboratories are available for every course for the respective department.





Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the full campus is available with high speed.

Faculty-

If students have any questions, they can contact the faculty at any time, and they will never reject.

They are willing to provide more classes if you request them.

You can call them at any time, or you can even contact them over WhatsApp in the evening to receive advice and support.



Extra Curricular Activities -



NCU is full of clubs, societies and committees. Here, you’ll have a wide range of clubs to enhance yourself to your full potential. Given below is the list of the clubs at NCU: -

Engineers Without Borders

The Awakening Club



 Apart from the clubs, NCU also has a wide range of Professional Societies which are very essential for student’s professional development. We’ve listed all the professional societies with respective images below, so you can have an idea, what the institute actually offers.

Students Hostel-  



The hostel has 4 students sharing a room for girls & 3 for boys but where girls of all years are provided accommodation, boys are given only for 1 st year since the building is under construction. 

Hostel food is of average quality but is edible to satiate the appetite. Hostel registrations complete along with the college admission forms submission. 

PG's are common a dozen in the locality, you may easily find them on sec 11, sec 10, sec 8, and sec 7 which are near to the college location and are rented at an avg of 7000/- month.   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



Life at the campus is very joyful. Hostel time is very awesome. There are about 16 clubs with so many facilities and all seniors are very helpful in making a good environment for study, singing and dancing.

There are extracurricular activities on campus like parade and fest , induction programme.

Library-

The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions.

 We have far more space than we could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. 

As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.

 



Classroom -  

Infrastructure is average. Classrooms are very decent, and you would be given Wi-Fi. Physical and e-library, labs and workshops are up to the mark, and the environment is also very good. Girls hostels are very good and big, and staff members are so cooperative.

Labs-  

Projectors are used while teaching. Laboratories are well updated and safe. 



Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at 20Mbs high speed.

Faculty-

Good and helpful faculty members include teachers. Teachers are highly educated, and some have earned PhDs from prestigious universities like IIT, NIT, etc.

Extra Curricular Activities -



SRIJAN



The only fine arts organisation at JC Bose University is the YMCA,  Srijan. The club was founded in 2003 and has continued to function honestly over time. Every year in the months of September or October, the club holds three-day tryouts to choose new members. These auditions are followed by a final round, which if you pass would grant you membership in the Srijan club. Everyone in the club is treated equally, and Srijan is like a family to all of its members. Dr. Preeti Sethi is the club's faculty mentor and coordinator, and she oversees all club-related events. The joint and secretaries of the club oversee the club's activities, which are also coordinated by the club's members.



   



SAMARPAN



A group of individuals with a shared interest in igniting and showing the tech-fire in them founded SAMARPAN, an association of aspiring electronics and electrical engineers at YMCAUST. As its name implies, the group is committed to teaching and promoting new technology, and all of its members share this commitment. Instead of relying solely on tried-and-true concepts and methods, we support innovation.





Pragyanam — A Physics Society



The goal of this club is to promote physics education for the benefit of society and the larger physics community.



MechNext Club



The mechanical engineering students of the YMCA University of Science & Technology in Faridabad have a technical activities group called MechNext. In January 2008, the club was founded. Through the planning of several technical and nontechnical activities, this group seeks to promote the overall development of engineering students.



SAE INDIA YMCA Collegiate Club



An outgrowth of SAE INDIA, one of India's top sources for mobility technology, is SAE INDIA YMCA COLLEGIATE CLUB. The ownership of SAE INDIA rests with its members, who are Individuals from the mobility community, including Engineers, Executives from Industry, Government Officials, Academics, and Students. SAE INDIA is an Individual Member Driven Society of Mobility Practitioners.

overview:
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF FOOD TECHNOLOGY ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND MANAGEMENT
The Ministry of Food Processing Industries oversees the higher education institution known as the National Institute of Food Technology Entrepreneurship and Management (NIFTEM), which is recognised to be a university (MOFPI).
It offers a food technology and supply academic programme that leads to bachelor's, master's, and doctoral degrees in technology.
It is situated in Sonipat, Haryana, India's Kundli Industrial Area. In 2021, it received the designation of Institute of National Importance.

 Furthermore, one hostel (Working Women Hostel) is being created specifically for Ph.D. Research Scholars and Working Women at the University.

overview:
GURU JAMBHESHWAR UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
In 1995, the Guru Jambheshwar University of Science and Technology in Hisar was established. The university's authority includes science, technology, engineering, pharmacy, and management studies.
An Act of the Haryana Legislature created Guru Jambheshwar University of Science and Technology in Hisar on October 20, 1995. On November 1, 1995, it was solemnly inaugurated. It is named after Guru Jambheshwar Ji Maharaj, a 15th-century saint and environmentalist.

 On the 20th of February, there is an annual festival. 



Library-

The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.

Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar (Deemed to be University) is a deemed university in Mullana, Haryana, India, near Ambala. It was formed in the name of Maharishi Markandeshwar Ji in the year 1993.

, two chairs, and two almirahs for two students. Every room has a bathroom attached to it. Hoster is a really tranquil place. There are 14 hostels at this college.

• If you wish to stay at a hostel. You can visit the campus hostel and complete a registration form for hostel allotment.Hostel expenses per month 7k.   







Campus facilities-



Campus life-



This college conducts a Universum. Every Universum comes with different types of actresses. For example in 2019 Darshan Raval Singer had come. 

This college provides the central library and also has every department its own library. This college also plauys sports and has players of all types of game.

Library-

Because youth can grow for the future with their cultural roots, the library periodically organizes various programmes such as seminars/workshops not only related to the library but also societal issues.

 NPTEL, Ovid Collection, Wolters Kluwer's UpToDate, ASME, ASCE, IEEE, SCC Online, Lippincott William and Wilkins, EBSCO (Management, Hospitality and Tourism Complete and Dentistry), Bentham Science, Criminal Law Database, URKUND Plagiarism software, K-hub, Animal Simulator, AIR Supreme Court Database, and other memberships ensure that the library stays ahead of the curve in the digital era of research and education.



Mess and other facilities-  

The medical facilities are good and free of cost for MMU students. The facilities for swimming pool, basketball court, badminton court, cricket and the football field are there too. 

The quality of the food served in the hostel, and the canteen is good, and my hostel rooms are not much different from my home.



Labs-  

Labs are equipped with AC. Every necessary thing is available in the lab, and the lab attendant is also very helpful. The library plays a main role. 

Wi-fi-  

Students greatly benefit from the fast Wi-Fi throughout the entire campus when taking tests.

Faculty-

 The teachers were highly qualified and were very helpful and the teaching quality was very professional. 

The course curriculum was very relevant and the semester exams were held at an average rate. They were neither difficult nor easy and the students who worked hard had a good pass percentage overall.





Extra Curricular Activities -



Students have the opportunity to develop and discover their potential through MM(DU) Clubs in an outstanding learning environment, which is always changing to meet the needs of industry and the wider world. This supports the students' development into a workforce that is full of creativity, innovative, and easily employable.

There are more than 50 student organisations and clubs at MM(DU), however a few of the most noteworthy ones are as follows:







Expressions: The Literary Club



The Literary Club aims to improve students' linguistic and creative expression abilities so they may participate in India's expanding corporate culture.



This club holds the following activities to help students improve their speaking, writing, listening, reading, and vocabulary:



University E-Magazine MMaverick (The core team edited and created this.)

Association of Speakers Weekly Saturday sessions

Discussion Group Debate Declamation

Spell-B Role Play

Writing Poetry and Essays



Eco Club- The Environmental Club



The purpose of starting an environmental club is to educate students about eco-friendly practices and inspire them to work toward a balance between the economy and the environment. Two to four times a month, the club meets.



MMDU NE Clava



The MMDU NE Clava: A Student Organization was founded by students from the eight north-eastern states of India with the goal of promoting their local culture there. Over 70 people from the eight North East states are currently a part of the group.



Drishti: The In House Media



The Department of Student Welfare at MMDU's "Drishti" In-House Media has a staff of photographers and videographers, script writers, content producers, and graphic designers. They are currently focused on covering various events taking place on campus.



Srijan: The Creative Arts Society



Srijan: The Fine Arts Society has been working as the ideators and producers of decor for all high-status events inside the campus. The Fine Arts Society has a team of artists who specialise in painting, sketching, making, designing, installation arts, etc.



CodeX



A technological group focused on fostering an atmosphere where students can enhance their technical and logical thinking skills is called CODEx, also known as the community of developers. The Computer Science & Engineering Department's CODEx club is a project that arranges numerous technical and programming competitions that provide students a chance to work with one another and compete against one another. This teaches the pupils how to improve both their interpersonal and academic skills.



Technocrats Club: Inspiring Innovation and Discovery



With the goal of inspiring innovation and discovery, MMICT&BM (MCA) established the group "Technocrats." The goal of this club is to expose students to computer-related difficulties and cutting-edge technologies in order to nurture and improve their skill sets in this field. The Technocrats Club serves as a sort of launchpad for promoting highly qualified technocrats who are adept at technical tasks, creative endeavours, and innovation.



MMIM: SAC 



Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar Institute of Management's SAC was established to help students develop their natural talents. The club encourages students to participate actively in extracurricular activities including CrossFit, Debate, Rangoli, Poster Making, Best out of Waste, and many other social activities.



Skyscrapers (The society of Civil Engineering Students)



Skyscrapers offer students the ability to participate in a variety of activities, from sports, personal interests, and hobbies, to technical ones, for their all-around growth. Students are urged to join these professional organisations in order to develop their abilities and widen their horizons.



Events and Fests



“Respecting Diversity, Celebrating multicultural”



The MMDU's Events & Fests are well-known both locally and nationally. The MMDU has been planning well-planned cultural and extracurricular events both on and off the university campus, and it encourages students to take part in both on- and off-campus cultural events.







Cultural Facilities



The Centralized Cultural Block's four primary training wings are for modelling, dance, theatre, and music. Our students are trained at the academy by qualified instructors from the entertainment industry.



Placement statics of college-



Nearly every student has the opportunity to participate in an interview during the placements, which feature more than 250 companies.

There are additional classes available, or perhaps we should say that students can prepare for interviews with the help of their lecturers. More than 100 students were placed, and they receive pay ranging from 3.5 LPA at the lowest end to 8 LPA at the highest.

Tech Mahindra, Infosys, the Indian Navy, as well as numerous other businesses, have relocated here.

overview:
NATIONAL DAIRY RESEARCH INSTITUTE
The National Dairy Research Institute (NDRI) in Karnal is India's leading dairy research institute.  In 1989, the institute was granted the title of Deemed University.
The Imperial Institute of Animal Husbandry and Dairying was founded in Bangalore in 1923 as the National Dairy Research Institute. It was renamed Imperial Dairy Institute in 1936. In 1947, after India's independence, it was renamed to its current name. The institute's headquarters were relocated to Karnal in 1955.

full_content:
NATIONAL DAIRY RESEARCH INSTITUTE



The National Dairy Research Institute (NDRI) in Karnal is India's leading dairy research institute.  In 1989, the institute was granted the title of Deemed University.

The Imperial Institute of Animal Husbandry and Dairying was founded in Bangalore in 1923 as the National Dairy Research Institute. It was renamed Imperial Dairy Institute in 1936. In 1947, after India's independence, it was renamed to its current name. The institute's headquarters were relocated to Karnal in 1955.

overview:
CHAUDHARY CHARAN SINGH HARYANA AGRICULTURAL UNIVERSITY
Charan Singh Chaudhary Haryana Agricultural Institution is a public agricultural university in the Indian state of Haryana, headquartered in Hisar. It is Asia's largest agricultural university. The institution has a total of 8,645 acres (3,499 ha) of land (about 7,219 acres (2,921 ha) on main campus and 1,426 acres (577 ha) at outstations). It is named after Chaudhary Charan Singh, India's sixth Prime Minister.
It began as a Punjab Agricultural University satellite campus in Hisar. Haryana Agricultural University was created as a university by the Haryana and Punjab Agricultural Universities Act, which was signed on February 2, 1970. As a result, it is regarded as the state of Haryana's first university.

hostel_info:
All students who are in need of a place to stay are given a place to stay in a hostel. Normally, a student whose domicile is within 30 kilometres of the University Campus, particularly male students, will not be supplied with hostel accommodations. 

In each hostel, there is a mess/kitchen unit. Under the direction of the particular Warden, the Mess is operated by a student body on a cooperative basis.

 The affairs of hostels are handled by a council of Wardens led by the Chief Warden, with participation from the particular hostel's student committees.

Every hostel features a large, comfortable common area with a television and a dish antenna hookup. A billiards facility is also available on the premises of the boys dormitory. The Hostels are now being networked with the rest of the campus.
campus_facilities:
There is a wi-fi facility at the institution, however the classroom isn't in the best of shape. The dorms at this university are great, and the rooms are spacious and clean. If I'm talking about dorms, the restrooms are also tidy and clean, and the mess food is, in my opinion, good.
Most sporting facilities are provided, and there is a shopping centre where students can get the stuff they need.

Library:
The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than we could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.
Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopedic reference. 
They subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks.

Sports:
The campus has medical facilities. Sports facilities are so good, and the campus has a big cricket ground, football ground, nice badminton court, gym, basketball court, squash room and pool room. Table tennis tables are also available in every hostel and hall.
Extracurricular Activity
Literary Club 
This club focuses on enhancing the literary skills of students through various activities like debates, poetry recitation, writing competitions, and discussions.
Music and Dance Club  
This club promotes music and dance culture among students and provides a platform for students to showcase their talents.

Drama Club 
This club organizes various theatrical performances and workshops to hone the acting skills of students.
Sports Club 
This club promotes sports culture among students and organizes various sports events and competitions.

Photography Club 
This club provides a platform for students to explore their photography skills and organize photography competitions and workshops.

Entrepreneurship Club 
This club aims to encourage entrepreneurial skills among students and provides them with the necessary resources to start their own ventures.

Robotics Club 
This club focuses on building and programming robots and participates in various robotics competitions.

Social Service Club 
This club encourages students to participate in various social activities like blood donation camps, awareness campaigns, and environmental cleanups.

overview:
DEENBANDHU CHHOTU RAM UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
Chhotu Ram State College of Engineering, Murthal (CRSCE), originally Deenbandhu Chhotu Ram Institution of Science and Technology (DCRUST), is a state university in Murthal, Sonipat, Haryana, India.
It was formed in 2006 by an act of the Haryana government, which upgraded a college that had been established in 1986.
The Haryana government established it in 1986 to honour Sir Chhotu Ram (1881–1945). It was then known as the Chhotu Ram State College of Engineering, Murthal, until the Haryana government raised it to university status on November 6, 2006, through Act 29 of the Haryana Legislature.

It was formed in 2006 by an act of the Haryana government, which upgraded a college that had been established in 1986. 

The Haryana government established it in 1986 to honour Sir Chhotu Ram (1881–1945). It was then known as the Chhotu Ram State College of Engineering, Murthal, until the Haryana government raised it to university status on November 6, 2006, through Act 29 of the Haryana Legislature.

Every hostel features a large, comfortable common area with a television and a dish antenna hookup. A billiards facility is also available on the premises of the boys dormitory. The Hostels are now being networked with the rest of the campus.   

Hostel Fees

Occupancy

Fees			

Single

Rs.6000/-		

Double

Rs.4000/-



Campus facilities-



Infrastructure-



There is a wi-fi facility at the institution, however the classroom isn't in the best of shape. The dorms at this university are great, and the rooms are spacious and clean. If I'm talking about dorms, the restrooms are also tidy and clean, and the mess food is, in my opinion, good.

Most sporting facilities are provided, and there is a shopping centre where students can get the stuff they need.



Library-

The Campus Library is a two-story structure with various asymmetric portions. We have far more space than we could possibly collect in a decade's time, and the Campus community has already realised the value of this tranquil, cool location surrounded by lovely foliage. As a result, it is mostly used as a reading area as well as an online search and collaboration centre.

Apart from the bread and butter textbooks, we have a considerable collection of over 40,000 solid reference volumes, accounting for 85 percent of the overall collection and primarily valuable for research and encyclopedic reference. 

They subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks. 



Faculty-  

In the ECE department there are 18 main faculty consisting of professors and assistant professors, each of them expert in their own subjects and 15 non teaching staff which includes lab assistants and technicians. 

All of them are very helpful and I am satisfied with their teaching method. The course is relevant. 

Semester exams are not at all tough. All you need to do is pay attention to the teacher's lectures and you are like half way done with your preparation pass percentage is quite very high, almost 95%. 



Labs-  

Projectors are used while teaching. Laboratories are well updated and safe. 

Classroom-

Campus is spread over 273 acres which is too much for students to do all activities. All the facilities are there in campus like library, Wi-Fi, shopping complex, and also medical facilities.

 There are a total of 7 hostels , 4 for boys and 3 for girls. Each hostel has their own mess. Mess-food is good. Hostel rooms are quite little but sufficient for a single student.

 

Wi-fi-  

Wi-fi in the hostel is available at high speed.

Sports-

The campus has medical facilities. Sports facilities are so good, and the campus has a big cricket ground, football ground, nice badminton court, gym, basketball court, squash room and pool room. Table tennis tables are also available in every hostel and hall.





Extracurricular Activity

Literary Club This club focuses on enhancing the literary skills of students through various activities like debates, poetry recitation, writing competitions, and discussions.



Music and Dance Club  This club promotes music and dance culture among students and provides a platform for students to showcase their talents.



Drama Club This club organizes various theatrical performances and workshops to hone the acting skills of students.



Sports Club This club promotes sports culture among students and organizes various sports events and competitions.



Photography Club This club provides a platform for students to explore their photography skills and organize photography competitions and workshops.



Entrepreneurship Club This club aims to encourage entrepreneurial skills among students and provides them with the necessary resources to start their own ventures.



Robotics Club This club focuses on building and programming robots and participates in various robotics competitions.



Social Service Club This club encourages students to participate in various social activities like blood donation camps, awareness campaigns, and environmental cleanups.

full_content:
INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, MANDI

                     



IIT Mandi is one of the eight new Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) created by the Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD). It is a well-known major Engineering and Technology institute, with UGC and AICTE approval. 







Important information regarding IIT Mandi-



Location-



Address- Parashar Road, Tehsil Sadar, Near Kataula, Kamand, Himachal Pradesh 175005



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/saKEMXVsxpheZ5QYA





Campus size- 538 acre





Reaching IIT Mangi-



By broad gauge train, the nearest railway stations are Joginder Nagar, Chandigarh, and Kalka, which are all connected by regular bus services.



Shimla, Chandigarh, Pathankot, and Delhi are all accessible by road from Mandi.



It is connected to nearby towns such as Manali, Palampur, and Dharamshala by regular bus services.

Student Hostel-



IIT Mandi features a fully residential campus, which houses around 1800 students.

Six boys' hostels, two girls' hostels, and one married scholars' flat are available.





Boys hostel- 

Suvalsar

Parashar

 Nako in South Campus

and Beas Kund in North Campus. 



Girls Hostel-

 Chandratal in South Campus and 

 Gauri Kund in North Campus.



     

     



Campus facilities-



Library-



The Central Library at IIT Mandi has over 41,868 books and gives access to over 50000 e-journals.

A good variety of audiovisual items, including as CDs and DVDs, is also available at the library.

         





Banks and ATM-



In the main campus, there are two banks with ATMs (State Bank of India and Punjab National Bank).



Stores: 



South Campus has a provision, grocery, and stationary store, while North Campus has a provision and grocery store.



Sports: 



Basketball, cricket, volleyball, soccer, hockey, football, badminton, tennis, table tennis, and cycling are just a handful of the sports that are played on campus. Hiking and trekking are two activities that are encouraged with supervision. 

.







HealthCare facilities- 



On both campuses, IIT Mandi offers a functioning medical center that is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week, with doctors, nurses, and all essential medications. In the event of an emergency, they also have an ambulance on standby.





Cafeteria: 



The institute has two canteens serving many good quality Indian dishes along with a bakery shop. 

They also have dining halls in both the camses.



                

            

Gym: 



The institute provides students with access to a gymnasium that is well-equipped with a variety of training apparatus. Inside the gymnasium, students can also participate in yoga and aerobics.





Auditorium-



For many of the college's events and services, students have access to an auditorium.





A/C Classrooms: 



IIT Mandi's classrooms are nicely furnished and have some air conditioning.

There are four virtual classes in all..

Reaching MITH-



Una (Himachal Pradesh), about 80 kilometres from Hamirpur, is the nearest wide gauge railway connection.

Una is well connected to the rest of the country, and the travel from Old Delhi Railway Station takes nine hours. From Hamirpur to Una, taxi and bus services are available.

Student Hostels-



The National Institute of Technology (NIT) is a public research university Hamirpur is a residential university, and all students are expected to live in the hostels.

For males and girls, separate hostel facilities are available.

There are currently eight hostels for boys and four hostels for girls.

Campus facilities-



Central library-



NIT Hamirpur's Central Library is divided into two buildings. It includes a number of unique e-journals and e-books.

They can be accessible through the Digital Knowledge Centre on the library's grounds.

All of these resources are open access, which means that any student can use them without restriction.







Bank & post office-



On campus, there is only one abi bank.

It also functions as a post office.





Health centre-



The institute's health centre is its beating heart, providing treatment for a variety of disorders as well as first help to the injured.

In the event of a medical emergency, the ambulance van is available 24 hours a day to transport patients to another hospital for treatment.









Extra Curricular Activities -



With the ever-changing trends and paradigm shifts in academia, students are always looking for new ways to grow as individuals. Cultural activities and numerous student clubs are only a few examples of possibilities that help students acquire technical, managerial, and conceptual skills. Furthermore, these activities allow students to connect into the institute community, particularly among the faculty, and to form a mentor and buddy network. 





NIT Hamirpur provides a number of such forums in the form of clubs for its students to choose from, allowing them to channel their energies into bringing their ideas to life. 





The following are some of the important clubs offered by NIT Hamirpur:



1. Organisation Club



2. Music Club



3. PR Club



4. Dance Club

6. Discipline Club

9. English Club



10. Decoration Club



11. Hindi Club



12. Informal Club



13. Pixonoids



14. Web Team



15. Dramatics Club



16. SPIC MACAY NIT Hamirpur Student Chapter

campus_facilities:
Library:
The NMAMIT Central Library is located in a main wing of the college academic building and is open to students, staff, and research scholars from 8 a.m. to 11 p.m. The library is completely self-contained. All of the library's functions are easily handled with the help of 'KOHA,' an open source library automation software. Books and other documents are issued and returned using a barcode system. 

The library has 82317 volumes of text and reference books (as of March 31, 2018), 1300+ e-journals, and 242 national and international technical journals in print. The library has a total area of 31000 square feet and can accommodate 750 pupils at a time.

Sports:
Throughout, the Department's principal goal has been to provide play opportunities to all college students who are serious about participating in sports. Encourage the gifted to make a real attempt to achieve higher distinction in sports at the V.T.U., State, Inter-University, and National/International levels by providing them with the best possible equipment, coaching, training, nutrition, kitting, and so on.

Infrastructure for College Sports 1. Access to indoor facilities Indoor stadium with three courts for badminton, one court for basketball, four courts for table tennis, and other facilities such as carom, four courts for chess, and so on. Conditioning Hall with multi-gym and individual exercise stations for weightlifting, powerlifting, and best physique. A separate fitness center is available for athletes and sportspeople. 1. Facilities for use outside A 400-meter standard athletic track with facilities for all forms of throws and jumps, as well as a pavilion with a fitness facility.
overview:
NMAM Institute Of technology Karkala Taluk
The Visvesvaraya Technological University, Belagavi, is associated with the NMAM Institute of Technology, which was founded in 1986. The All India Council for Technical Education in New Delhi has recognised the institute, which is accredited by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council [NAAC] with a 'A' grade and a CGPA of 3.11 out of 4 till October 20, 2022. Ten Undergraduate Programs
From 2007 to 2008, the institution was awarded Academic Autonomy by the Visvesvaraya Technological University, and NVT Quality Certifications certified it to the ISO 9001:2015 standards for quality education (ANAB accredited).

Student Hostel 



NMAMIT features distinct boys' hostel buildings that are shared to create a good setting for students to pursue their studies in a healthy and caring environment. Hostels are home to about half of the students. 

On the NMAMIT campus, there are seven boys hostel blocks. Each hostel block is overseen by a Manager and coworkers from the hostel. All of the hostels have cots, tables, chairs, a wardrobe, and a ceiling fan in each room.

To satisfy the needs of students from all over the country, the hostel operates a vegetarian and non-vegetarian mess featuring North Indian and South Indian cuisine. On a regular basis, cultural events, self-development programmes, and academic support activities are held exclusively for hostel students. 

The 310mbps Campus Wi-Fi network connects all of the hostel blocks. •Health Care•R.O. Processed Drinking Water•Cold & Hot Water Supply•Diesel generators to provide backup power in all hostel blocks•Television Hall•Laundry •Round the clock security•Ambulance Service for emergency and referral services.









Campus facilities- 



Library- 



The NMAMIT Central Library is located in a main wing of the college academic building and is open to students, staff, and research scholars from 8 a.m. to 11 p.m. The library is completely self-contained. All of the library's functions are easily handled with the help of 'KOHA,' an open source library automation software. Books and other documents are issued and returned using a barcode system. 

The library has 82317 volumes of text and reference books (as of March 31, 2018), 1300+ e-journals, and 242 national and international technical journals in print. The library has a total area of 31000 square feet and can accommodate 750 pupils at a time.







Sports- 



Throughout, the Department's principal goal has been to provide play opportunities to all college students who are serious about participating in sports. Encourage the gifted to make a real attempt to achieve higher distinction in sports at the V.T.U., State, Inter-University, and National/International levels by providing them with the best possible equipment, coaching, training, nutrition, kitting, and so on.

Infrastructure for College Sports 1. Access to indoor facilities Indoor stadium with three courts for badminton, one court for basketball, four courts for table tennis, and other facilities such as carom, four courts for chess, and so on. Conditioning Hall with multi-gym and individual exercise stations for weightlifting, powerlifting, and best physique. A separate fitness center is available for athletes and sportspeople. 1. Facilities for use outside A 400-meter standard athletic track with facilities for all forms of throws and jumps, as well as a pavilion with a fitness facility.









Canteen-



There are three primary cafeterias on the NMAMIT campus where students can eat and socialize. The Sri Durga Cafeteria serves delicious south Indian cuisine. It offers a wide range of hygienically cooked South Indian, Chinese, vegetarian, non-vegetarian, chats, fresh fruit juices, and ice cream flavors. Kitchen Bells is another appealing and sanitary dining option for students, with pizzas, burgers, noodles, and cakes, among other items.





Transport- 



For students and employees from Karkala, Mangaluru, Udupi, Kundapur, Bantwal, and Belthangady, the College maintains a fleet of 32 buses. Approximately 900 students from all across the region use these bus services to get to and from the College.







Guest room– 



When parents and guardians of students visit the college and wish to remain during their trips, a Guest House service is offered for a modest fee. The student must make their reservation at least three days before their arrival. Each room in the guest home is equipped with air conditioning, a cot, a wardrobe, a telephone, a television, and an Internet connection. This guest home accommodates VIPs, Institute guests, and invited guests.







Extracurricular activity-



Shipwright club-

On April 22, 2022, as a part of the mechanical engineering festival "Overhaulin 2022," the Shipwright Club of NMAMIT sponsored an activity called "Aqua Race." During the session, club members' designed and constructed radio-controlled ships were on display.



Sentia club-

On April 15 and 16, 2022, MITE Moodabidri hosted the State Level Cultural, Technical, and Management Fest known as "Sentia 2022," where our students took first place overall.



Spardha-

The Vaikunth Mehta National Institute of Cooperative Management (VAMNICOM), Pune, hosted the National Business Quiz "SPARDHA 2022," and ONMAM Institute of Technology congratulates Shrikrishna and Swasthika, the winners.





Placement statistics- 



In 1990, Abhyuday, the Department of Counseling, Welfare, Training, and Placement, began providing Student Counseling services. After a five-year investigation into the causes of student issues, NMAMIT set out to create a healthy environment on campus through a series of ground-breaking programmes aimed at their well-being and advancement. Several training programmes to improve student employability have also arisen.

full_content:
MANIPAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)

           

The Manipal Institute of Technology (MIT), Manipal was established in 1957 as one of the first self-financing engineering colleges in the country

In 2000, it became a constituent institute of the deemed university - Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE), which is known today as Manipal Academy of Higher Education

MIT offers a bachelor’s and master’s program in engineering streams. It also gives students the option to carry out full-time or part-time research. The minimum duration for postgraduate qualified PhD students is three years for full time, and four years for part-time. There is a one year relaxation for applicants with an MPhil degree



























The institute undertakes sponsored research programmes supported by funding agencies such as DST, CSIR, AICTE and the Ministry of Environmental Sciences. It has collaborative research programs in association with premier research laboratories and institutes in India and abroad.

Manipal Institutions have long been distinguished for excellence in professional education. The University is home to some of the top ranking institutions across diverse disciplines."

Cafeteria-

Here is no place like Manipal for Chinese, Mexican, Italian, Malaysian, Indian, Arabian and other kinds of food. The restaurant dishes offers different kinds of foods; China Valley with its delicious Chinese Manchurian and rice, and Snack Shack with its selection of American, Italian and Asian food platters, Danish with its awesome shawarma  and Hangyo Saiba with its finger-lickin’ Indian gravies and breads.



The best place to get everything is the KMC Food Court;  safe, tasty and hygienic food; the only place with a students’ mess, and outlets for Chinese, Indian, Malaysian, and Thai cuisine, at student-friendly prices, like Kamaths Upahar, where the best south Indian dosas are available at reasonable prices.

Sports-

Wifi-

The entire campus is Wi-Fi enabled and 24x7 Wi-Fi connectivity is provided to students and faculty members in the institute.

Auditorium-

Manipal Institute of Technology has a well-maintained auditorium with the necessary facilities to conduct various events and activities.





Extra Curricular Activities-

Manipal Academy of Higher Education has a long history of student organisations and clubs that draw people from different academic fields, institutions, programmes, and countries. It's one of the characteristics that gives Manipal a special charm and a distinct advantage over other college campuses.

There are academic-focused technical groups as well as cultural clubs that allow students to explore the performing arts, fine arts, music, dance, and other activities that are uplifting to the soul and mind.

Students have a variety of clubs they can join and participate in to become a part of the university's cultural scene.



Developer Student Clubs

Student communities at universities for those interested in Google developer tools include Developer Student Clubs. They serve as spaces where students can interact: Meet other university students who are enthusiastic about developer technologies. Everyone is welcome, regardless of their academic background or major; learn: Gain new skills through practical seminars, in-person training, and project-building activities; and grow: Learn about a wide range of technical topics. Use your newly acquired knowledge and connections to create excellent local problem-solving strategies. Improve your network, career, and abilities. Helping others learn will allow you to give back to your community.

International Society of Automation (ISA), Manipal

By uniting the automation community to achieve industrial excellence, ISA increases technological competence. The company creates broadly accepted international standards, accredits professionals in the industry, offers education and training, publishes books and technical articles, organises conferences and exhibits, and offers networking and career development programmes for its members and clients globally.





By enabling student members to use the wealth of ISA resources to broaden their understanding and develop themselves into competent, future-ready engineers who will lead the industry of tomorrow, ISA Manipal, as a student section, shares this global vision with the students and seeks to close the learning-to-practice gap.

Placement statics- 

Manipal Institute of Technology (MIT) Manipal has always been very proactive in placing the students into their preference profiles. The institute has a well-organised placement team headed by the associate director, three assistant directors, and faculty and student coordinators from each department. 300+ recruiting teams, including 50+ Fortune 500 companies, visit MIT Manipal for UG and PG campus placements and internships recruitment every year.

full_content:
Pilot



Overview:

Pilots are highly trained professionals who learn in depth about flying different kinds of aircrafts. These trained professionals are also responsible for maintaining the internal mechanisms of an aircraft. The courses for becoming a pilot are according to the specialization in aircraft. There are various types of different aircrafts, and so are their pilots.

Goods pilots, fighter plane pilots, passenger pilots, sports pilots and private pilots are some of the different kinds of pilot. The specialization of a pilot completely depends on the stream and subjects they choose. If aircrafts and the sky mesmerizes you like anything, then maybe being a pilot is the best suited career profile for you! Read more to know everything about this Career profile. 

Benefits Of Choosing Pilot As A Career Option:

Flexible work schedule: Pilots do not have to work 9 to 5 everyday; instead they have a well-defined flexible working schedule. They can choose to travel according to their own requirements and schedule. 

Non-monetary benefits: along with a good pay scale, a pilot gets benefits of medical insurance, free meals, complimentary lodging and other allowances. 

Good pay scale: pilots are one of the highest paid professionals in India as well as abroad. The pay scale of a pilot is handsome and enables them to live a comfortable life.

Aviation has great scope: be it government or private, aviation has great scope in coming years. The aviation industry faces constant changes, ultimately providing greater job opportunities. 

Job security: as the aviation industry is constantly changing, there is a huge demand for pilots.

How To Become A Pilot?

The step-by-step procedure given below will guide you through the complete process of becoming a pilot right after class 12th. 



Eligibilities and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

Candidates must be at least 17 years old to apply for the pilot courses.

The maximum age to become a pilot completely depends on the type of entrance exam or institute.

Educational qualifications:

A candidate applying for the pilot admission must have passed 10+2 with physics, chemistry and mathematics as compulsory subjects.

A candidate must have studied English at an Intermediate level till 10+2.

Candidates are required to have passed the qualifying exam with at least 50% aggregate.

If a candidate from the commerce background wants to pursue pilot courses then he/she can enroll with PCM subjects from an open state board.

Medical requirements:

All the candidates must have a medical certificate issued by the authorities. 

Skill Requirement To Become A Pilot:

Clear communication

Environmental awareness

Team working skills

Aircraft awareness

Quick decision making

Leadership skills

Mathematics and technical skills

Entrance Exams To Become A Pilot

If you wish to build your career as a pilot in the aviation industry then you need to qualify some entrance exams. The main aim of these entrance exams is to test the aptitude, communication, and decision making skills of a candidate. To know more about these entrances, have a quick look below.



Indira Gandhi Rashtriya Uran Academy (IGRUA) entrance test for pilots.

Air Force Common Admission Test (AFCAT).

Aircraft Maintenance Engineering Common Entrance Test (AME CET).

Courses Available To Become A Pilot

You can pursue pilot courses right after completing your 10+2. There are various institutes and universities offering admission to different pilot courses. Have a look at the table below to know what all are the UG and PG courses to become a pilot.

Courses Available At UG Level:

Top Institutes offering Pilot Courses:

There are various colleges and institutes offering Pilot courses right after class 12th. If you want to make your future in the aviation industry as a pilot then you must enroll in any of the given universities for the pilot courses.

Types of pilot specializations:

There are more than seven specializations of pilots. The job profile and the specialization are completely based on the course and subjects chosen by the candidate at the time of certification. The table below will explain in detail about various specializations of pilots.

Specialization

Description

Commercial pilots

Commercial pilots are responsible for managing the cargo and passenger flights. They are also trained to fly the fire fighter plans.

Cargo pilots

Cargo pilots manage the commercial flights carrying shipments and couriers. They use advanced technologies for loading and unloading the shipment to the correct locations.

Charter pilots

Charter pilots are responsible for flying the charter planes closely related to an individual or any business. They are directly related to the customers.

Military pilots

Military pilots are trained to defend the country from foreign attacks. They are trained for air to air combat.

Fire fighter pilots

These pilots are responsible for flying the fire fighter aircrafts. These aircrafts are generally used in wildlife fires, forest fires and during any natural calamity. 

Test pilots

Test pilots have the responsibility of checking the newly produced or modified aircrafts. They do so to check if the aircraft is fine for the operations.

Helicopter pilots

Helicopter pilots are responsible for flying helicopters. They are licensed and certified by the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA).



Pay Scale Of Pilots:

Pilots are one of the highest paid professionals in the world. We would rather say that sky's the limit for pilots! The pay scale of a pilot completely depends on the experience they have gained and the specialization they choose. To know more about the pay scale of pilots, refer to the information given below.

Specialization-Wise Salary Of A Pilot:

Top Recruiting Agencies:

If you are looking for top recruiting agencies for pilots, then there is a long list for the same. The best part of being a pilot is that you can have enormous opportunities in government as well as the private sector. Below given are some of the top recruiting bodies for pilots.







Demands And Future Scope:

Demand and scope in government sector:

The government has recently said that there will be a requirement of 1000 fresh commercial pilots every year.

According to a survey on commercial pilots, about 862 commercial pilot licenses were issued in the year 2021, 578 in 2020 and 744 in 2019. 

The data shows that there is an increasing scope for the pilots in the coming years. 

Aviation being a constantly changing industry, requires new concepts and skills every second, thus making the scope even larger.

Demand and scope in private sector:

The private sector has numerous opportunities for the fresh pilots.

Vistara, SpiceJet, Air India and AirAsia are some of the top recruiting domains for pilots.

With a handsome pay scale and rewarding job profile, this profession has no limits for the scope in the private sector.

Abhinandan Vardhman

Man Mohan Singh

full_content:
BIRLA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY AND SCIENCE, PILANI

                                               

BITS Pilani, or Birla Institute of Technology and Science, is an all-India higher education institute.



The objective of BITS is to develop knowledge and educate students in the field of science, technology, and other.



Important information regarding BITS Pilani-



Location-



Address-

Vidya Vihar, Pilani, Rajasthan 333031



Campus map link-

Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani

https://maps.app.goo.gl/WnoR32gyDL7NePUt9







Campus size-about 328 acres



Reaching BITS Pilani-

Pilani is accessible by both road and rail.

The nearest railway stations are Chirawa (16 km) on the Western line and Loharu (16 km) on the Northern railway (24 Km).



Courses offered by BITS Pilani-



Courses

Eligibility

B. E. ( Bachelor of engineering)



7 Streams : 



Computer Science Engineering



Chemical Engineering



Mechanical Engineering



Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering



Electrical & Electronics Engineering

Computer Science And Engineering



Software Engineering

Year

Student Hostels & Mess-



The Institute is entirely residential, and all students are housed in hostels.

Students whose parents or close relatives live in Pilani may be granted permission to become day-scholars only in extraordinary situations.

There are eleven boys' hostels and one girls' hostel complex.











The hostel rooms are completely furnished and equipped with internet access as well as water purifiers and solar water heaters.

In each hostel, there is a common area with cable TV and a telephone.

Each set of two boys' hostels has a kitchen-cum-mess facility. The girls' hostel, on the other hand, has its own mess, which is located within the hostel's perimeter.

Students staying in the hostel are required to eat their meals in the hostel messes.

The students run the mess halls which are attached to the hostels.



Campus facilities-



Library-



The library can accommodate up to 8,000 students and features 738 open access study spaces as well as 246 open-access IT workstations.

It houses student computing facilities in addition to the books and periodicals you'd anticipate.







There are almost half a million artefacts in the collections, including:

There are around 3,000 active journal titles.

Databases, journals, and ebooks are among the more than 20,000 electronic resources available.

A large media collection (CDs & DVDs).



Sports-



The Sports Club is in charge of organising games and sports.

Physical Director, Senior Coach, Life Saver (both male and female), Martial Art Instructor, and various support staff make up the Institute's team.

Football, hockey, cricket, basketball, volleyball, tennis, badminton, table tennis, athletics, gymnastics, health club, swimming, water polo, weightlifting, and more games and sports are available.











Aside from organising regular events in the aforementioned sports, the Sports Club also hosts the BITS Open Sports Meet (BOSM) every year in the First Semester, which attracts several outstation teams.



Guest Room-



For visiting staff and student family members, the college includes a guest house/waiting room.









Swimming Pool-



A 25 by 12.5 metre swimming pool is available for learning to swim, water polo, and racing.

Students can join the Swimming Club/Health Club by paying the required fees.



Extra Curricular Activities-



The Executive Committee of the Department of Management at BITS Pilani is in charge of organising a number of student-focused activities that aim to close the knowledge gap between the academy and the business world. The ILC and Sponsorship Club Head, Vice President, Secretary, Joint Secretary, Treasurer, and the other five team members make up the six-person committee that is led by the President. This committee consists of ten clubs, and the Executive Committee's main responsibility is to coordinate with these clubs to organise and carry out various activities all year long. Additionally, the Annual Department Fest - Interface must be organised by ExCom.







Vishwast - The Marketing Club



Our goal is to encourage active learning while assisting management students in exploring career options in the wide field of marketing without sacrificing originality or performance.



Business Analytics Club 



Business Analytics Club  The club's primary activities include planning tests, workshops, and seminars on various business analytics-related themes.



Finomaniac - The Finance Club



A platform for interaction between students, faculty, alumni, business executives, and researchers will be offered by Finomaniac - The Finance Club. Gaining knowledge about the financial sector and how it intersects with other industries including investment banking, corporate banking, and financial services is our main goal.



Manav - The HR Club



An organisation's workforce is its most significant asset. Manav - The HR Club seeks to raise awareness of the value of human resource management across a range of industries and organisations.





Vishwakarma - The Operations Club



By holding various case study competitions, quizzes, guest lectures, and enhancing the industry interactions, Vishwakarma - The Operations Club aims to facilitate knowledge sharing on topics related to Operations Management, Supply Chain Management, and Inventory Management, among others. This helps to develop professionals who are prepared for the workplace.



Industrial Linkage Club 



The Industrial Linkage Club facilitates group contact with eminent figures in the business and academia and hosts guest lectures, workshops, case studies, and other events with the goal of bridging the gap between the industry and academia.



Other Clubs: -







You may let your creative side out at the Creative Activities Club (CrAC). The BOSM fun-game events, the OASIS seminars and competitions, the APOGEE technical creative puzzle activities, and the BOSM Galleria art gallery. Additionally, the club regularly contributes to campus beautification.



Photographic workshops and training sessions are held by the photography club known as Photog. The club presents two well-liked exhibitions: "Exposure" for OASIS and "Panorama" for APOGEE.



The BITS Radio-Control (RC) Club is a collection of RC fans, model aeroplane creators, and hackers. carries out workshops, air displays, and aerial photography sessions.



The 2012-founded IEEE BITS Pilani chapter supports the presentation of papers, workshops, quizzes, and lectures.



The largest Model United Nations event in India is organised annually by the BITSMUN Society.



The largest ACM chapter in the world, the Association for Computing Machinery (BITS-ACM), is dedicated to expanding the body of knowledge in advanced computing and robotics.



A student organisation at BITS Pilani called the Center for Entrepreneurial Leadership (CEL) promotes entrepreneurship on campus.



The literature club at the Pilani and Goa campuses, Mind And Thought For Radical Information EXchange (MATRIX), hosts book discussions, reading sessions, and film festivals.



The Embryo Club is an intellectual organisation present on all BITS campuses with the mission of promoting interaction amongst BITS students, professors, and alumni. More than 200 lectures have been given since 2006.



A venue for car enthusiasts to gain practical expertise with vehicles is the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) and Inspired Karters Racing.



Nirmaan plans social events on and near the campus.



At the semester and during student festivals, the Hindi Drama Club (HDC) and the English Drama Club (EDC) put on stage and street plays.



Both the Hindi Press Club (HPC) and the English Press Club (EPC) produce a yearly college magazine that details events on campus.





The Debating Society organises participation in regional and national debate competitions. Since 2011, it has hosted QED, one of India's biggest debates.



Quizzes and other literary events are held by the Hindi Activities Society (HAS) and the English Language Activities Society (ELAS).



ABU Robocon is attended by a team of robotics engineers called Team Robocon.



The LAN Radio Club of BITS Pilani, Radioaktiv, creates and transmits musical programmes.



The Wall Street Organization (WSC), the country's first undergraduate investment club, aims to help engineering students learn about business.



Hindustani classical music and Carnatic music programmes are organised by the classical dance and music club Ragamalika.



The Mime Club hosts competitions when the festivals are taking place and performs sketch performances twice a year (during Oasis and APOGEE).



The Music Club performs at events and during "music evenings."



The Dance Club sponsors dance classes and gives performances at cultural gatherings.







Enthusiasts of astronomy can join the Astro Club. The club has two telescopes: a 12.5-inch Cassegrainian telescope and a Galilean telescope. The group provides workshops and hosts night sky viewing events.



The Bitsaa Cycle Club strives to offer bikes at reasonable prices to BITS Pilani students.



The school's short films are produced by the Filmmaking Club, which also holds workshops.



The Renewable Energy Club conducts research on renewable energy sources and raises awareness of them.



The DC++ CLUB is anticipated to launch soon with the goal of encouraging LAN sharing among college students.





Placements-

 Placement Statistics

The Mahima Shiksha Samiti sponsors Jaipur National University, which is one of Rajasthan's best self-financed private universities.



The University has set many benchmarks in higher education, technical education, and medical education. 

It is one of the top most universities in Jaipur Rajasthan that provides exceptional academic excellence through a plethora of courses.

Student Hostels-



There are separate hostels for boys, girls, and international students.

JNU is a top private university in Rajasthan that offers over 2500 students a home-like residential experience.

Hostelers have the option to study in quiet while also socialising with other like-minded students from around the world.



Campus facilities-



Library-



This Library, with its 1304 square metres of space, is the ideal location for a tranquil learning environment.

The Library is well-lit and air-conditioned, and it is well-located to give a pleasant ambience free of noise.









Sports & gymnasium-



Cricket, basketball, football, indoor badminton, volleyball, table tennis, pool/snooker, chess, and carrom are among the sports accessible at JNU.







Classroom-



JNU's 245 lecture theatres and lecture rooms provide a pleasant, comfortable, and airy environment for learning activities.



These rooms were designed with sound acoustic principles in mind to ensure that every student can participate in the learning process.



Laboratories-



Students can investigate and hone their talents in one of sixty-three laboratories dedicated to various engineering fields.



Auditorium-



Jaipur National University, one of Rajasthan's leading private universities, believes that a world-class university would be incomplete without a state-of-the-art auditorium.



Each JNU campus has a high-quality auditorium where noteworthy events are held on a regular basis.



Extracurricular activity-



National Youth day-

On August 12, 2020, the School of Education at Jaipur National University in Jaipur conducted an online International Youth Day (IYD) under the auspices of NSS. Every year on August 12, people worldwide celebrate International Youth Day. The UN Inter-Agency Network in Youth Development members and the Focal Point on Youth choose the day's theme, frequently with input from youth organisations. Their participation was guaranteed by more than 59 participants. The United Nations has recognised June 20 as International Youth Day (IYD), a day of awareness. A specific set of cultural and legal concerns that youngsters are confronting are the focus of the day. On August 12, 2000, the first IYD was observed. Every participant shared their unique perspectives on the International Youth Day.



Tap on beat-

Dance is the manifestation of emotions and the unspoken language of the spirit. To express these feelings, first-year B.Sc. nursing students from Seedling School of Nursing competed in the Tap On Beat dance competition with the subject "Tribute to Indian Army" and received rousing applause from the audience for their mesmerising performance and riveting scenarios.



Gravity sports-

On March 19 and 20, 2021, SIILAS Campus, Jaipur National University, organised the Sports Fia. One of the largest co-curricular activity programmes, it gives all students a wide range of opportunities. This project gives the kids and staff a chance to have fun, learn new sports, improve social interaction, and put their bodies to the test.
