Student hostels-



The campus of the All India Institute of Medical Sciences in Bibinagar, Hyderabad, has offered housing for MBBS students.

Currently, the campus has three boys hostel wings and two girls hostel wings that house 50 second-year students (35 males and 15 girls) and 62 first-year MBBS students (33 boys and 29 girls).

The hostels for First MBBS students are on the second level of the building, while the hostels for Second MBBS students are on the first.



            

         

   Room facility - 



Some rooms have a student strength of two, while others have a student strength of three.

Every student has his or her own bed, closet, table, and chair.





Campus facilities-



Food services - 



The canteen on the hostel grounds serves breakfast, lunch, evening snacks, and dinner to the students, with eating arrangements on two different floors. Canteen also features a vending machine for tea and coffee, as well as snacks and an ice cream bar for when you become hungry.



Library-



The books and general reading part is on the lower floor, the reading section is on the middle floor, and the state-of-the-art e-library and journal section is on the top floor.



The library has around 8000 medical and nursing books as well as about 900 general topic books.



There is internet access via Wi-Fi.







Sports and gym facility - 



Students have access to a well-equipped gym that includes a treadmill, an aerofit 8806, and push-up bars, among other things.

Tennis courts, football fields, badminton courts, volleyball courts, and cricket grounds are all available on the premises of hostels.

Students' common areas also have indoor games like table tennis, chess, and carrom boards.

Students take evening walks while listening to instrumental music on the walking track in the rainbow park encircling the colourful fountain.



outdoor games-





Indoor games-







Security system- 



Guards are stationed at each hostel wing's access and exit points as part of the hostel security system. The hostel's in-and-out entries are meticulously recorded in entry-exit registers.

Visitors are not permitted inside the hostel without the approval of the hostel warden.

All entry and exit points are captured by CCTV cameras, which are constantly monitored at the security office on the ground level of the hostel building to ensure the safety of the students.



Medical services-



The hostel keeps first-aid kits on hand at all times. Near the hostel, there are OPD and ICU services.

On-call doctors are available 24 hours a day & 7 days a week. Students do not have to pay anything to participate in lab investigations.



Internship-



As per the Government of India rules, the interns of MBBS are entitled to a stipend of Rs 17, 900- ₹ 23,500 per month. 

All AIIMS do not provide the same stipend. It varies according to their respective states/Union Territory's rules.

For both undergraduate and postgraduates students, AIIMS Bhopal has separate dorms for men and women.



Outstation students will find the hostel rooms to be quite pleasant, since they are furnished with all of the required amenities.



The AIIMS Bhopal campus has a total of four hostels, two of which are for males and four of which are for girls.

    4. Campus facilities-



Guest Rooms-



A guest house with double-bedded rooms and internet access is available at the campus. Family members of students and visiting faculty members can stay in AIIMS Bhopal guest rooms for a reasonable fee.









Central Library-



A reading room is available at the library, which is also equipped with a Radiofrequency Identification (RFID) system.



Print and online resources are available in AIIMS Bhopal's library.



It houses a large library of medical science books, as well as national and international journals, biomedical databases, periodicals, newspapers, and magazines.



The library has online resource subscriptions.











Wi-Fi-



Students and faculty members have free access to the internet for academic and research purposes.

They can access the facility at any time and from any location on campus.



Gym-



The gym of the All India Institute of Medical Sciences in Bhopal is open to both boys and females. A gym trainer is also available at the campus to assist students.





Sports-



The college sports department offers indoor and outdoor games such as chess, carrom, badminton, table tennis, tennis, cricket, basketball, volleyball, football, handball, and others.



Fields and courts, particularly for outdoor games, are maintained on a daily basis.









Students are also encouraged to play a variety of games to improve their physical and mental fitness.



Auditorium and open air theatre-



The auditorium is very large and can accommodate a huge number of people.

There is also an open-air amphitheatre at the college.









Medical/Hospital-



The college offers a medical clinic on campus that provides free first-aid care to students and staff.



There are also qualified medical professionals in the medical clinic.



   5.   Internship-



As per the Government of India rules, the interns of MBBS are entitled to a stipend of Rs 17, 900- ₹ 23,500 per month. 

All AIIMS do not provide the same stipend. It varies according to their respective states/Union Territory's rules.

There are three boys' hostels and two ladies' hostels for MBBS students.

Nursing and PhD students are housed in separate buildings.



The rooms are big and come equipped with a bed, a table, a chair, and a closet.



During the first year, accommodations must be shared, but starting in the second year, solitary rooms are available.

The hostels have eight floors and working elevators.











Campus facilities-



Central Library-



The college features a large central library, which opened on October 3, 2012, and has seen tremendous growth in the past nine years.



The academic block's library is located on the ground floor and serves all students, from B.Sc. to MBBS to nursing, as well as staff and research members.



 







Mess and canteen-



Students have access to eateries on campus that are open 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

Students can eat meals and snacks whenever they like.

The cuisine is tasty, hygienic, and reasonably priced.

On its campus, there are two messes and two canteens.









Sports facilities-



A cricket field, volleyball court, badminton courts, and other sporting facilities are available on campus.

Students have access to a gym on campus as well.











Security-



The campus is secure thanks to the presence of SIS security guards.

With these guards on the premises, no outsiders will be able to get access.



Medical/hospital-



A specialized hospital area on campus houses 43 departments, including pre-clinical, para-clinical, specialty, and super-specialty departments.



In the event of an emergency, students can also seek medical assistance from the hospital.







Internship-



As per the Government of India rules, the interns of MBBS are entitled to a stipend of Rs 17, 900- ₹ 23,500 per month. 

All AIIMS do not provide the same stipend. It varies according to their respective states/Union Territory's rules.

overview:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, JODHPUR
The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India, established AIIMS Jodhpur as one of the SIX NEW AIIMS under the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna (PMSSY) to correct regional imbalances in quality tertiary level healthcare in the country and achieve self-sufficiency in graduate and postgraduate medical education.
These institutions will be established by an Act of Parliament, similar to the original All India Institute of Medical Sciences in New Delhi, which provides undergraduate and postgraduate medical education in all branches and related fields, as well as nursing and paramedical training, in order to bring together educational facilities of the highest order for the training of personnel in all branches of health care activity in one location.

ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, JODHPUR

                            



The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India, established AIIMS Jodhpur as one of the SIX NEW AIIMS under the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna (PMSSY) to correct regional imbalances in quality tertiary level healthcare in the country and achieve self-sufficiency in graduate and postgraduate medical education. 







These institutions will be established by an Act of Parliament, similar to the original All India Institute of Medical Sciences in New Delhi, which provides undergraduate and postgraduate medical education in all branches and related fields, as well as nursing and paramedical training, in order to bring together educational facilities of the highest order for the training of personnel in all branches of health care activity in one location.

hostel_info:
Students are also housed on the AIIMS Rishikesh campus.
The dorms of AIIMS Rishikesh are designed to meet all of the students' daily needs.
In the gardens and surrounding areas of the hostels, there are many trees and plants.

The hostel rooms are brand new, well-ventilated, and equipped with furnishings such as a bed, table, chairs, cabinets, and bookcase.
All of the hostels include common rooms with TV and cable hookups for the students' benefit.

There are geysers in all washrooms and water coolers (with ROs) on each floor of the hostels.
In addition, all of the hostels have security guards on duty 24 hours a day and seven days a week.
campus_facilities:
Library:
In AIIMS Rishikesh, there is a well-equipped library containing the most recent editions of medical books and journals. If needed, students can use computers in the library.

In addition, hostels include small libraries with non-academic literature and novels in practically every genre (mystery, fantasy, romance, philosophy, autobiographies, etc.) to read in spare time.

overview:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, RISHIKESH
The All India Institute of Medical Sciences Rishikesh was strategically positioned in natural beauty, near to the sacred Ganges, to solve regional imbalances in healthcare services, research, and training.
It was founded in the first phase of the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna and given autonomy by the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (Amendment) Bill, 2012.
description:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, RISHIKESH
The All India Institute of Medical Sciences Rishikesh was strategically positioned in natural beauty, near to the sacred Ganges, to solve regional imbalances in healthcare services, research, and training.
It was founded in the first phase of the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna and given autonomy by the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (Amendment) Bill, 2012.
full_content:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, RISHIKESH

            



The All India Institute of Medical Sciences Rishikesh was strategically positioned in natural beauty, near to the sacred Ganges, to solve regional imbalances in healthcare services, research, and training.







It was founded in the first phase of the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna and given autonomy by the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (Amendment) Bill, 2012.

3. Student hostels-



Students are also housed on the AIIMS Rishikesh campus.

The dorms of AIIMS Rishikesh are designed to meet all of the students' daily needs.

In the gardens and surrounding areas of the hostels, there are many trees and plants.





The hostel rooms are brand new, well-ventilated, and equipped with furnishings such as a bed, table, chairs, cabinets, and bookcase.

All of the hostels include common rooms with TV and cable hookups for the students' benefit.







There are geysers in all washrooms and water coolers (with ROs) on each floor of the hostels.

In addition, all of the hostels have security guards on duty 24 hours a day and seven days a week.



4. Campus facilities-



Central Library-



In AIIMS Rishikesh, there is a well-equipped library containing the most recent editions of medical books and journals. If needed, students can use computers in the library.







In addition, hostels include small libraries with non-academic literature and novels in practically every genre (mystery, fantasy, romance, philosophy, autobiographies, etc.) to read in spare time.



Mess and Canteens- 



The site also provides a dining facility for students.

It serves a range of dishes throughout the week to please the palate, as well as EOF health.

There is a meal schedule for the week that changes at regular intervals so that children are not bored with the same type of food.









There is also a well-functioning canteen that serves tea, coffee, Maggi, Maggiettes, and snacks until late at night, which is extremely useful for students during examinations! Students can also buy milk, ice cream, chips, and other packaged foods from them.



Sports-



The following are the sports facilities available on the AIIMS Rishikesh campus:



Badminton courts

Lawn Tennis court

Volleyball court

Basketball court

Cricket Field

Football field

Table Tennis

Carrom

Chess

Ludo





 5. Internship- 



As per the Government of India rules, the interns of MBBS are entitled to a stipend of Rs 17, 900- ₹ 23,500 per month. 

All AIIMS do not provide the same stipend. It varies according to their respective states/ Union Territory's rules.

full_content:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, RAEBARELI





The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (Amendment) Act, approved in 2013, created the All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Raebareli as an autonomous institution and a National Institute of Medical Sciences.



Mission & Vision-To create a centre of excellence in medical education, training, health care, and research that is infused with scientific culture, compassion for the sick, and a desire to help the disadvantaged.







Important information regarding AIIMS Raebareli-



Location-



Address-

Dalmau Rd, Munshiganj, Uttar Pradesh 229405



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/P6HQmdphRnT124DU8









Campus size-



In July 2013, the Uttar Pradesh government gave 97 acres out of a total of 148 acres to the Central Government for the construction of AIIMS Raebareli.





Reaching AIIMS Raebareli-

It takes 22 minutes to travel from Raebareli Station to Aiims. Approximate driving distance between Raebareli Station and Aiims is 19 kms.

overview:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, MANGALGIRI
Under the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna, the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare of the Government of India is establishing AIIMS Mangalagiri as one of the AIIMS healthcare institutes (PMSSY).
The PMSSY planned to establish new AIIMS-like institutions in underserved areas of the country in order to redress regional imbalances in quality tertiary level healthcare and achieve self-sufficiency in graduate and postgraduate medical education and training.

full_content:
ALL INDIA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES, MANGALGIRI

                              



Under the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojna, the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare of the Government of India is establishing AIIMS Mangalagiri as one of the AIIMS healthcare institutes (PMSSY).



The PMSSY planned to establish new AIIMS-like institutions in underserved areas of the country in order to redress regional imbalances in quality tertiary level healthcare and achieve self-sufficiency in graduate and postgraduate medical education and training.







Important information regarding AIIMS-Mangalagiri-



Location-



Address-

Mangalagiri, Andhra Pradesh 522503

Student hostels- 



Students are well-served by the Institute's accommodations. The students are given all of the necessary amenities to make their stay here enjoyable.

On campus, there are a total of 6 hostels, with two each for undergraduate male and female students. Two for male and female postgraduate students, and two for male and female nursing students.



Hostel facilities-



Items provided in the room: Cot, Mattress, Side Table, Study Table, Study Chairs, Curtains, Cupboards, Dustbin.

24*7 electricity and water supply in all hostel

Caretakers and housekeeping services are provided in the hostel during the day.

Cold and normal drinking water supply in the hostel

Geysers in each bathroom

Separate Sickroom

Lifts facility

Purified Drinking water (RO) on each floor

24*7 Security service









4. Campus facilities-



Mess-



Breakfast, lunch, evening snacks, and dinner are all provided at the mess. The service provider was chosen through a tender process. The Mess Committee keeps an eye on everything.



Their students have access to good, nutritional, and sanitary meals, including both vegetarian and non-vegetarian options, as well as South and North Indian cuisine.









Library-



Print and online resources are available in the AIIMS Mangalagiri library.

It houses a large library of medical science books, as well as national and international journals, biomedical databases, periodicals, newspapers, and magazines.

The library has online resource subscriptions.





Guest Room-



For visiting staff and student family members, the college includes a guest house/waiting room.



Wi-Fi-



Students and faculty members have free access to the internet for academic and research purposes.



They are welcome to utilise the facility at any time and from any location on campus.



Gym and sports complex-



The college sports department offers indoor and outdoor games such as chess, carrom, badminton, table tennis, tennis, cricket, basketball, volleyball, football, handball, and others.

Fields and courts are kept in good condition on a daily basis, especially for outdoor games.

Students are also encouraged to play a variety of games to improve their physical and mental fitness.

Both boys and girls students can use the gym at the All India Institute of Medical Sciences Mangalagiri.



Internship-



As per the Government of India rules, the interns of MBBS are entitled to a stipend of Rs 17, 900- ₹ 23,500 per month. 

All AIIMS do not provide the same stipend. It varies according to their respective states/Union Territory's rules.

campus_facilities:
This hospital was first established as a private facility with Queen Victoria as its name. In 1894, the late Maharajah Sri G.N. Gajapathi Rao, KCIE, made a donation to this hospital. 
In 1949, this Hospital's management was then turned over to the Government of Madras. 
It was recently refurbished with new blocks and contains three units with 147 bedrooms.

Library:
The Central Library of this Medical College is about 3400 square metres in size and also has air conditioning. There are reading rooms open 24 hours a day, and this central library's outdoor reading room can accommodate 150 pupils. 
There are 240 seats available inside the reading room. Staff rooms and reading areas are separate and available. 
There is also a reading space for PG students. There are 36,184 books available in all. 
There are 34 foreign journals and 72 Indian publications available. This library offers an internet facility with 25 nodes, a librarian room, a journal room, and a completely air-conditioned computer room for E-Library use.

Sports:
For outdoor sports like cricket, football, volleyball, tennis, badminton, and others, there are excellent playgrounds available. There are also indoor game rooms equipped with everything needed.
 Both employees and students have access to a variety of equipment in the gym. For managing the sports activity and necessary upkeep, there is a full-time licensed Physical Education instructor accessible. 
This college has triumphed in numerous state and national tournaments, winning championship shields in the process.

overview:
ANDHRA MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL
Andhra Medical College is a part of NTR University of Health Sciences and is located in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India.
It is the sixth-oldest medical college in India and the oldest in Andhra Pradesh. The Indian Medical Council has approved of it.
Currently serving as vice chancellor is Dr. P. Shyam Prasad.

full_content:
ANDHRA MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL



Andhra Medical College is a part of NTR University of Health Sciences and is located in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India. 

It is the sixth-oldest medical college in India and the oldest in Andhra Pradesh. The Indian Medical Council has approved of it. 

Currently serving as vice chancellor is Dr. P. Shyam Prasad.

Campus size: 18 acres









Reaching AMC VISAKHAPATNAM-

Students Hostel-  



Two people will share a single room that will be provided. A single room for one may be obtained during the last year. There is no bathroom nearby. without a cooling system. 

All three meals will be provided by the hostel canteen. Aside from the hostel, there are a lot of single rooms near the hospital.

Hostel expenses per month: 4k.





Campus facilities-



Library-

The Central Library of this Medical College is about 3400 square metres in size and also has air conditioning. There are reading rooms open 24 hours a day, and this central library's outdoor reading room can accommodate 150 pupils. 

There are 240 seats available inside the reading room. Staff rooms and reading areas are separate and available. 

There is also a reading space for PG students. There are 36,184 books available in all. 

There are 34 foreign journals and 72 Indian publications available. This library offers an internet facility with 25 nodes, a librarian room, a journal room, and a completely air-conditioned computer room for E-Library use.



Lecture Facility-  

The air-conditioned facility has six lecture halls available. There are five lecture halls in a college. Each of these has 250 seats and is equipped with audio and video.

The hospital has one lecture hall with a seating capacity of 500 with audiovisual equipment. These lecture halls have a gallery design. There are two exam rooms with a combined seating capacity of 500 and 250 students. 

The 2000 square metre Medical College Auditorium was constructed with all amenities.



Computer Labs-  

Aside from distinct computer laboratories in each department, the institute features a 100-capacity centralised computer centre with internet access. 

For students, the computer centre provides online assessments and in-house software instruction.



Medical facilities-  

This hospital was first established as a private facility with Queen Victoria as its name. In 1894, the late Maharajah Sri G.N. Gajapathi Rao, KCIE, made a donation to this hospital. 

In 1949, this Hospital's management was then turned over to the Government of Madras. 

It was recently refurbished with new blocks and contains three units with 147 bedrooms.

campus_facilities:
Siddhartha College of Medicine Government General Hospital in Vijayawada, which has a 43,287 sqm building, serves as the city's teaching hospital. With multi-department treatment facilities, it has 707 teaching beds available.
 Acute medical care unit, paediatrics, neonatal intensive care unit, obstetrics and gynaecology, internal medicine, general surgery, nephrology, gastroenterology, cardiology, neurology, endocrinology, and diabetes care are among the specialisations. 
The Directorate of Medical Education oversees the operation of this teaching hospital on behalf of the State of Andhra Pradesh.

Library:
The 1800 square metre, two-block Siddhartha Medical College Library is fully air conditioned. A total of 19331 books and 106 periodicals, including 70 Indian and 36 foreign publications, are available.
 There are 40 computers and internet capabilities available at the SMC digital library. A reference section, a section for textbooks, a back volume area, a stock area, a section for periodicals, the librarian's office, a section for audio and video, a section for reprographics, a computer workstation with internet access and access to e-resources, a technical section, and a round-the-clock reading room are also included.
 Both inside and outside, there are two reading rooms available. There are 200 seats available inside, and 100 seats are available outside. 
There is a separate staff reading room with 30 seats that is available. It also offers a PG reading room.

Sports:
Indoor games are accessible, and there is an entertainment area with TV and music, as well as an air-conditioned study room with laptops and the internet. 
There are recreational facilities such as playgrounds and outdoor games like basketball, volleyball, badminton, and throwball. Indoor sports and a gym with all the amenities are offered.

The Dr. Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital in New Delhi is home to the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Medical Sciences (ABVIMAS), formerly known as the Post Graduate Institute of Medical Education and Research (PGIMER). It is connected with New Delhi's Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University.
As a Post Graduate and Super-Specialty Research and Training Institute, it was founded in 2009.

full_content:
ATAL BIHARI VAJPAYEE INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES



The Dr. Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital in New Delhi is home to the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Medical Sciences (ABVIMAS), formerly known as the Post Graduate Institute of Medical Education and Research (PGIMER). It is connected with New Delhi's Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University.

As a Post Graduate and Super-Specialty Research and Training Institute, it was founded in 2009.



Important information regarding this college-

Location: 



Address- Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital, Type III, beside President's Estate, New Delhi, Delhi 110001



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/AXCwemYRrguJEEm96



Campus size: 179 acres





Reaching ABVIMS-



The college is only about 2 kilometres from the Patal chowk railway station and can be reached in about 4 minutes.

Students Hostel-  



There are single, double, and triple sharing rooms available at the hostel. Standard and hygienic meal quality is provided.

 The food is high quality . Monthly mess fees are 3000. After being accepted into college, students can register for hostels. 

The area is among India's greatest due to the close proximity of the Rashtrapati Bhavan and Connaught Place, as well as the excellent metro and transportation connectivity. 





Campus facilities-



Infrastructure-

College has good infrastructure. A new building that will have 19 floors and be equipped with the newest features is now being constructed. Additionally, it boasts a brand-new cafeteria where students can have their lunches in a spacious, well-ventilated seating area.



Faculty-  

Teachers in college are good, helpful, and they conduct SGDs. They are highly qualified. They have completed their PGs from reputed colleges like AIIMS BHU, MAMC, LHMC, UCMS, KGMC, etc. Semester exams are easy, but professionals are tough, which are taken once a year, and based on it, they pass to next year. The pass percentage is 100% after supplementary exams.



Sports-

The college provides a table tennis court in a doctors hostel, a cricket ground in Talkatora Garden and a gym.

 Talkatora Garden is nearby, 2 minutes' walking distance. It has an attached hospital named Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital as it is a medical course.

overview:
NORTH DMC MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HINDU RAO HOSPITAL
The Medical College is located on the Hindu Rao Hospital's campus.
It's in the Civil Line neighborhood of North Delhi. The North Campus of Delhi University is to the north of the hospital, Civil Lines is to the east, Old Subzi Mandi is to the south, and Malka Ganj is to the west.
Surrounded by a number of significant landmarks, the most notable of which are the RAJ NIWAS – Office and the official house of the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi.

overview:
MAULANA AZAD MEDICAL COLLEGE
MAMC is a medical college situated in New Delhi, India, that is connected with the University of Delhi and managed by the Delhi government.
It has given the same name to Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, an Indian freedom warrior and the country's first education minister.
It was founded in 1959 on Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, close to the Delhi Gate.

full_content:
MAULANA AZAD MEDICAL COLLEGE



MAMC is a medical college situated in New Delhi, India, that is connected with the University of Delhi and managed by the Delhi government.

It has given the same name to Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, an Indian freedom warrior and the country's first education minister. 



It was founded in 1959 on Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, close to the Delhi Gate.



Important information regarding this college- 



Location-



Address-  Bahadurpur Shah Zafar Marg, near Delhi gate, Maulana Azad College Campus, Balmiki Basti, New Delhi.



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/kyQiZajnER9VhrS19













Campus size- 30 Acres





Reaching MAMC-

The distance between MAMC campus and the Delhi metro station is 1.3 km. It is the nearest station and it takes 6 minutes.If travel via Jawarharlal Nehru Marg it takes 17 minutes as walking distance.

The library collection comprises o books, journals, theses and e-journals.

In the library Current journals and back volumes related to journals are arranged on shelves in alphabetical order by title.

 The library has so much resources and attached study rooms for students and calm study environment, separately for boys and girls, with reading rooms.







Health care -



The Lok Nayak Jaiprakash Hospital, the GB Pant Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research, the Maulana Azad Institute of Dental Sciences, and the Guru Nanak Eye Center are all located on the college site.

 These facilities provide 2800 beds, 7200 daily outpatient visits, and 47 operating rooms for patients and medical students to use. 

It serves 290 undergraduate students, 245 postgraduate and postdoctoral students each year, with 426 academic members and 810 resident doctors providing instruction.



Labs and theatres - 



The lecture theatres are exceptionally spacious, with room for 250 students to sit comfortably. 

Centrally air-conditioned lecture theatres and well-equipped labs are provided to provide students with an unrivalled learning experience.



Wi-fi and Classroom -



Students have to login free Wi-Fi across the campus. With E-learning, the classrooms were light, spacious, and spotless.

VARDHMAN MAHAVIR MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL         



New Delhi's Vardhman Mahavir Medical College (VMMC) is a medical Collag that is affiliated with the popular Safdarjung Hospital

Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University is the umbrella organization for the college.



Important information regarding this college- 



Location-



Address- Ring Road New Delhi -110029 Delhi South West Delhi India.



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/FgS7ihVoP2hxL4MN6















Campus size- 47 Acres





Reaching VMMC-

The distance between VMMC and South extension metro station is 1km and it is the nearest distance. It takes 3 min by walk.

In the case of the metro YELLOW LINE is a transit line that has routes that pass near VMMC and Safdarjang Hospital.

Students Hostel -



There are two hostels for males and one for girls.

There is a scarcity of places in dormitories, which is why all-India quota students are given priority over state quota students.

The hostel is nice, and the rooms are air conditioned. However, there are no attached restrooms. The ground floor is a shambles. Cleaners are assigned on a regular basis to maintain the hostel clean.

Hostel expenses per month Rs.4000 - Rs.7000

Cultural events -



Annual feast celebrated in VMMC in the month of August/September that known as Nirvana. 

There is VPL is a sports feast/meet with many sports thats includes cricket, football, TT, badminton, Kho-Kho, tug of war, UNO, Call of Duty, and many more. 





Central library- 



The library is excellent, with a variety of materials and ample space in the reading area.

The college library has all of the literature relating to our course by authors such as BD CHAURASIA, AK Dutta, and AK Jain, among others. 

The library is large and well-lit, as well as air-conditioned.









Infrastructure-



The college has a fantastic structure. The greatest place to go is the library.

 The anatomy museum, dissection hall, and haematology labs are all well-organized.

 The projectors are available in all of the classes. 

The instruments in the laboratories are well-equipped. The classrooms are well-equipped.





Health care –



Safdarjung Hospital is home to Vardhman Mahavir Medical College (VMCC).

Patients at Safdarjung are charged almost nothing. 

Their drugs are also provided free of charge. 

You must pay for your own equipment if you are not exceedingly poor.



Cafeteria- 



Our college has four canteens: a Boys Mess, a Girls Mess, a Sports Injury Canteen, and a PG Mess, all of which serve practically everything, including thali, dosa, and chow mein.

 The cuisine is likewise of excellent quality and comes at a very affordable price.





Physical education-

sports injury centre available for national and state level athletes, and free treatment is provided to them. Burn, causality, and emergency centre are also available in the college.

Jamia Hamdard is an institute of higher education in New Delhi, India, that is regarded as a university.

 The Ministry of Human Resource Development recognised it as an Institute of Eminence in 2019.

It has an open playground, cricket and football pitches, a modern gymnasium, indoor game facilities and badminton courts.





Central library-



The university's library system is made up of a central library and six academic libraries: science, medicine, pharmacy, nursing, Islamic studies, management studies, and information technology.

 After the founder's younger brother, Hakim Mohammed Said, the central library is named 'Hakim Mohammed Said Central Library.





Computer center -



The university has a computer center that serves as a lab for the Department of Computer Science, as well as computing and system analysis units, as well as all required peripherals and software.

 The computer facility has five laboratories, each with its own set of tools for research and development.



Health care -

Cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular illnesses are also studied at the institute. 

In conjunction with the All India Heart Foundation and the National Heart Institute, which is present in New Delhi, the Institute announced a one-year postgraduate diploma in preventive cardiology. 

The Hamdard Imaging Center was founded to bring medical imaging services under one roof. 

University students, teaching and non-teaching personnel, their families, and the poor are all supplied with free OPD services.



Infrastructure- 



The hostel's infrastructure is solid, with adequate facilities for everything.

Wi-Fi is available, and the library and labs include the necessary instruments and tools.

All of the labs and lecture halls are fully air-conditioned, and the lecture halls have recently been updated.

overview:
GOVERNMENT MEDICAL COLLEGE AMRITSAR
The Government Medical College in Amritsar was founded in 1864 as a medical school in Lahore. In 1920, it was again located in Amritsar.
The college has a rich history and has gained a reputation for excellence in medical education and patient care in the region. Over the years, the school has established itself as a breeding ground for medical educators and trained physicians who have fanned out across the country to serve as vital players in both private and public healthcare to fulfil the country's rising requirements.

GOVERNMENT MEDICAL COLLEGE AMRITSAR



The Government Medical College in Amritsar was founded in 1864 as a medical school in Lahore. In 1920, it was again located in Amritsar.

The college has a rich history and has gained a reputation for excellence in medical education and patient care in the region. Over the years, the school has established itself as a breeding ground for medical educators and trained physicians who have fanned out across the country to serve as vital players in both private and public healthcare to fulfil the country's rising requirements.

      3. Students Hostel- 



Punjab medical college hostels are located on campus in Jalandhar. Both hostels of Punjab Medical College Jalandhar provide students with cabinets, separate study rooms with laptops and internet access, comfortable beds, bathrooms, and adjacent toilet facilities.

Hostel accommodations at PIMS Jalandhar are available on a shared basis. • There are 175 rooms available for boys and 40 rooms available for girls separately. Nearly 580 students can be accommodated in the hostel. The male and female wardens patrol both dorms separately.

Both the boys' and girls' hostel rooms are equipped with a variety of amenities. For the benefit of the students, canteens/mess with dining halls, separate guest rooms, R.O pure drinking water, 2 wheeler parking, and other facilities have been given. It offers indoor services.







4. Campus facilities-



Library-

The Punjab Institute of Medical Sciences features a 2579-square-meter air-conditioned library facility. For students and employees, the PIMS library has 11,042 books and 71 Indian publications and 33 foreign journals.

 The library has four independent reading rooms with seating capacity of 480 people for library users, both staff and students. 

Online users can utilise computers at the PIMS Jalandhar library that are connected to the internet. 

On all college working days, this library is open from 8 a.m. to 10 p.m.



Outdoor games-

Type of games Football, Volleyball, Badminton, Kho-Kho



Gymnasium- 

Gym is also available in college.



Hospital- 

For clinical purposes, the Punjab Institute of Medical Sciences affiliated hospital has a 650-bed capacity. On an average day, the PIMS Jalandhar associated hospital sees 1,500 outpatients.

The PIMS hospital in Jalandhar has roughly 7 operating rooms, including 13 major operating rooms and 5 minor operating rooms.

According to data from the previous year, the PIMS associated hospital performs 36 major and 03 minor surgeries every day.

overview:
ADESH INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES
Adesh Institute of Medical Science and Research (AIMSR) is a private medical college with a 750-bed teaching hospital. The college was founded by Adesh Institutions in 2006. The Medical Council of India has authorised AIMSR, and also the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has granted permission.
From the 2006 to 2011 MBBS Admissions Batch, it was connected with Baba Farid University of Health Sciences, Faridkot, and since 2012, it has been affiliated with Adesh University, Bathinda.

full_content:
ADESH INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES





Adesh Institute of Medical Science and Research (AIMSR) is a private medical college with a 750-bed teaching hospital. The college was founded by Adesh Institutions in 2006. The Medical Council of India has authorised AIMSR, and also the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has granted permission. 

From the 2006 to 2011 MBBS Admissions Batch, it was connected with Baba Farid University of Health Sciences, Faridkot, and since 2012, it has been affiliated with Adesh University, Bathinda.

Adesh Institute of Medical Sciences includes a library that is 2408 square metres in size. The college campus has a large centralised air-conditioned library with four ventilated and lighting reading rooms that can accommodate 480 people. 

There are 13,502 books in the library, as well as more than 200 national and international jjournals.LThe library also purchased 1,349 e-journal subscriptions so that it can provide e-journal services through 44 internet nodes connected to a digital consortium.

Both students and teaching staff have access to computers with internet access. 

On all college working days, the central college library is open from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.

Cricket, badminton, volleyball, basketball, and football are some of the outdoor sporting activities accessible at Adesh medical college Bathinda. Indoor activities such as carrom, chess, and table tennis, as well as gymnasium facilities, are available at the campus.



Canteen-  

For the use of students, employees, and patients, Adesh Medical College Bathinda provides a canteen/meals facility.

 On the AIMSR Bathinda campus, three canteens and one mess are provided, as well as a separate dining hall for employees, students, and visitors. 

Students can eat both vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals at the AIMSR Bathinda hostel mess.

hostel_info:
The dorms at the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences are located on the college grounds. On the college campus, separate dorm rooms are offered for boys and girls.

 Nearly 700 students can stay in those hostels. In the hostel, single and double rooms are available on a shared basis; 2 or 3 people are permitted per room. 

The SGRDIMSR Amritsar hostel has a dedicated study space with computers and internet access for the use of the guests.

The dormitory rooms for both boys and girls are equipped with all the amenities that students may possibly need. 

The dormitories at the SGRD Institute of Medical Sciences offer amenities including closets, cosy mattresses, bathrooms, and adjacent toilets. Separate male and female wardens are in charge of guarding these dormitories.
campus_facilities:
The Sri Guru Ram Das charitable hospital Vallah - Amritsar is connected to the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences. An average of 1,400 outpatients are treated daily at the SGRDIMSR Amritsar associated hospital. 
A cutting-edge medical facility with 992 beds for both teaching and clinical purposes and a variety of super specialties. 
The philanthropic Sri Guru Ram Das hospital has roughly 16 operating rooms, including 14 large operating rooms and 2 small operating rooms.
 The SGRDIMSR Amritsar Affiliated Hospital does 21 minor operations daily in addition to 34 major operations.

Library:
The 2,440 square metre air-conditioned library facility is part of the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences.
 There are 15,230 books in this collection, including 73 Indian publications and 62 foreign journals. 
The library has three different reading rooms with seating for 350 people that are available to employees and students.
 40 computers are available for online users at the SGRDIMSR Amritsar library. On all college working days, the central library is open from 8 am to 9 pm.

Sports:
To boost the students' physical health, SGRDIMSR Amritsar offers a variety of sporting activities. Indoor sports like table tennis, carrom, chess, and gym facilities are offered at the Sri Guru Ram Das Medical College. 
This college has playgrounds for outdoor sports including football, volleyball, badminton, and cricket for the advantage of the students.

overview:
SRI GURU RAM DAS INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES AND RESEARCH
The Institute of Medical Sciences & Research was established in 1997 and is named after Sri Guru Ram Das Ji, the fourth Sikh Guru and the man who founded the sacred city of Amritsar. In December 2002, the first class graduated. Up until the admissions batch of 2016, the Institute was connected with Baba Farid University of Health Sciences in Faridkot. Currently, it is affiliated with Sri Guru Ram Das University of Health Sciences in Sri Amritsar (from Admission Batch 2017 onwards).
The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India, formally recognises the MBBS degree in Letter No. U.12012/224/2015-ME.1 [FTS] .130652] Dated 28-03-2018. At the Sri Guru Ram Das Charitable Hospital in Vallah, medical students receive clinical instruction.

Students Hostel-  



The dorms at the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences are located on the college grounds. On the college campus, separate dorm rooms are offered for boys and girls.

 Nearly 700 students can stay in those hostels. In the hostel, single and double rooms are available on a shared basis; 2 or 3 people are permitted per room. 

The SGRDIMSR Amritsar hostel has a dedicated study space with computers and internet access for the use of the guests.

The dormitory rooms for both boys and girls are equipped with all the amenities that students may possibly need. 

The dormitories at the SGRD Institute of Medical Sciences offer amenities including closets, cosy mattresses, bathrooms, and adjacent toilets. Separate male and female wardens are in charge of guarding these dormitories.







Campus facilities-



Library-

The 2,440 square metre air-conditioned library facility is part of the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences.

 There are 15,230 books in this collection, including 73 Indian publications and 62 foreign journals. 

The library has three different reading rooms with seating for 350 people that are available to employees and students.

 40 computers are available for online users at the SGRDIMSR Amritsar library. On all college working days, the central library is open from 8 am to 9 pm.



Lecture Facility-  

There are 5 lecture halls with 950 seats each, 2 exam rooms with 600 square metres and 600 seats each, and 1 common room with 168 square metres usable for both boys and girls separately.



Canteen-  

For the benefit of staff, students, and patients alike, SGRDIMSR Amritsar provides canteen and mess facilities. On the SGRDIMSR campus, there are three canteens and two mess facilities.

 Everyone has access to vegetarian and non-vegetarian cuisine at the hostel mess. For the personnel and guests, a separate dining hall is provided by the canteen/mess.



Medical facilities-  

The Sri Guru Ram Das charitable hospital Vallah - Amritsar is connected to the Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences. An average of 1,400 outpatients are treated daily at the SGRDIMSR Amritsar associated hospital. 

A cutting-edge medical facility with 992 beds for both teaching and clinical purposes and a variety of super specialties. 

The philanthropic Sri Guru Ram Das hospital has roughly 16 operating rooms, including 14 large operating rooms and 2 small operating rooms.

 The SGRDIMSR Amritsar Affiliated Hospital does 21 minor operations daily in addition to 34 major operations.



Sports-

To boost the students' physical health, SGRDIMSR Amritsar offers a variety of sporting activities. Indoor sports like table tennis, carrom, chess, and gym facilities are offered at the Sri Guru Ram Das Medical College. 

This college has playgrounds for outdoor sports including football, volleyball, badminton, and cricket for the advantage of the students.

overview:
DR. BR AMBEDKAR STATE INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES
The Punjab State Government is establishing the Dr. B.R. Ambedkar State Institute of Medical Sciences (AIMS Mohali) as its fourth medical school under the federally funded programme known as "Establishment of New Medical Colleges by Upgrading District/Referral Hospitals."’
The National Medical Commission (NMC), the institute's accrediting agency, has granted the institution its first letter of licence, and it has already welcomed its initial batch of 100 MBBS students for the academic year 2021–2022.

GOVERNMENT MEDICAL COLLEGE PATILIA
Patiala's Government Medical College is India's second-oldest medical school.

Library:
The Dr. N.S. Pradhan Memorial Library at the University of Delhi in Delhi is outfitted to satisfy the fundamental intellectual and academic demands of its patrons. It has two storeys, is roomy, and has undergone renovations to improve usability. A study-friendly environment is provided via the open access system and the large reading rooms. CCTV cameras are watching over the college library.
More than 150,000 books make up the library, which is well-stocked. Collection is regularly updated to reflect curriculum changes. It subscribes to numerous periodicals and newspapers.
The library has access to DULS databases and online resources thanks to its connection to the university's network.
overview:
BABA FARID UNIVERSITY OF HEALTH SCIENCES
A law issued by the State of Punjab's legislature in July 1998 authorised the establishment of Baba Farid University of Health Sciences. The University's mission is to open a window to the world for the nation's health professionals, health planners, health managers, biomedical and social scientists, and educators in the field of health sciences by fostering an intellectual, academic, and physical environment that encourages free exchange of ideas and information between the University's various faculties and between this University and other Universities of Health Sciences both domestically and abroad.
The Punjab government established it in July 1998 through an Act of the State Legislature in honour of the famous Sufi Saint Baba Farid.

A law issued by the State of Punjab's legislature in July 1998 authorised the establishment of Baba Farid University of Health Sciences. The University's mission is to open a window to the world for the nation's health professionals, health planners, health managers, biomedical and social scientists, and educators in the field of health sciences by fostering an intellectual, academic, and physical environment that encourages free exchange of ideas and information between the University's various faculties and between this University and other Universities of Health Sciences both domestically and abroad. 









The Punjab government established it in July 1998 through an Act of the State Legislature in honour of the famous Sufi Saint Baba Farid.

window in the room, bed, study table, and alcove—all the necessities for students.

Campus facilities-



Library-

The Dr. N.S. Pradhan Memorial Library at the University of Delhi in Delhi is outfitted to satisfy the fundamental intellectual and academic demands of its patrons. It has two storeys, is roomy, and has undergone renovations to improve usability. A study-friendly environment is provided via the open access system and the large reading rooms. CCTV cameras are watching over the college library.

More than 150,000 books make up the library, which is well-stocked. Collection is regularly updated to reflect curriculum changes. It subscribes to numerous periodicals and newspapers.

The library has access to DULS databases and online resources thanks to its connection to the university's network.



Lecture Facility-  

The building is now 100 years old. The new building is very well and nicely built. The classrooms have well lighting and ventilation and are fully air-conditioned. Classrooms are quite big.



AC Rooms-

 There are separate rooms with AC for PG. The hostel is good as compare, with daily room cleaning and laundry service. The Water filter system and geysers are also present. Anyone can even install AC in their rooms and can use any other electronic gadget properly.



Canteen-

 The canteen is student-cooperative. Medical facilities are available 24 hours. The hostel has a study room and badminton court. A parking area is available. Mess food is edible. The food quality in canteen is very good. 









Auditorium-

The 2000 square metre Medical College Auditorium was constructed with all amenities.





Medical facilities-

overview:
DAYANAND MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL
In Ludhiana, North India, Dayanand Medical College is a tertiary care teaching hospital. Dr. Banarsi Dass Soni, a former Captain in the Indian Military Service, originated the idea in 1934 and established Arya Medical School in a rented facility in Ludhiana's Civil Lines. The school was handed over to the Arya Samaj, Saban Bazar, Ludhiana, in 1936, under the auspices of the Arya Pratinidhi Sabha, Punjab.
The Arya Medical School became a full-fledged MBBS college in 1964, and the Dayanand Medical College & Hospital was born (DMCH). The management was taken over by the Managing Society of Dayanand Medical College & Hospital, Ludhiana, which was founded by industrialist Shri H. R. Dhanda.
DMCH was the first hospital in Punjab to establish a cancer centre.

full_content:
DAYANAND MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL



In Ludhiana, North India, Dayanand Medical College is a tertiary care teaching hospital. Dr. Banarsi Dass Soni, a former Captain in the Indian Military Service, originated the idea in 1934 and established Arya Medical School in a rented facility in Ludhiana's Civil Lines. The school was handed over to the Arya Samaj, Saban Bazar, Ludhiana, in 1936, under the auspices of the Arya Pratinidhi Sabha, Punjab. 

The Arya Medical School became a full-fledged MBBS college in 1964, and the Dayanand Medical College & Hospital was born (DMCH). The management was taken over by the Managing Society of Dayanand Medical College & Hospital, Ludhiana, which was founded by industrialist Shri H. R. Dhanda.

DMCH was the first hospital in Punjab to establish a cancer centre.

Students hostel- 



The hostels at Dayanand Medical College are located on the Ludhiana campus. DMC hostel rooms are available on a shared basis; each room can accommodate up to three people. 

There are 170 rooms available for boys and an additional 170 rooms available for girls. Nearly 480 students can be accommodated in the hostel. 







The male and female wardens patrol both dorms separately.



Campus facilities-  

Library-  

The air-conditioned library of Dayanand Medical College is 1650 square metres in size. This library has 18,284 books, including 71 Indian publications and 132 foreign journals. 

It has four different reading rooms for both staff and students, each with seating for 250 people. The DMC library has 25 computers available to its patrons.

 On all college working days, the college library is open from 9.00 a.m. to 12.00 p.m.





Canteen-  

The canteen/mess facilities at Dayanand Medical College are available to students, employees, and patients. 

On the college campus, there are 15 canteens and four mess facilities, as well as a separate dining hall for employees, students, and visitors. Students can eat both vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals at the hostel mess.

Hospital-  

The Dayanand Medical College & Hospital, Ludhiana (DMCH – Ludhiana) was the first hospital in the Punjab region to offer patients a modern level of advanced health care. 

For clinical purposes, Dayanand Medical College has an attached hospital with a capacity of 1300 beds, including 800 teaching beds. 

The hospital has renowned OPD consultants, including 32 doctors who are backed by more than 700 employees and 200 nurses. On an average day, the DMC Ludhiana affiliated hospital sees 2,800 outpatients.

SILCHAR MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL
Silchar Medical College and Hospital (SMCH), established in 1968, is a government-run medical college located in Silchar in southern Assam. It is one of the oldest medical colleges of North East India.
It is the only one referral hospital in the southern part of Assam which is also referred to as the Barak Valley.

PATNA MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL

                                              



Patna Medical Institution and Hospital (abbreviated as PMCH) is a medical college in Patna, Bihar, India, that was founded in 1925 and was previously known as Prince of Wales Medical College  .







 It is one of India's busiest hospitals. 

The average daily outpatient load in India is among the highest.

Health care-



The Patna General Hospital was completely transformed in 1925-26 after the temple Medical School was converted into a Medical College and the Hospital was raised to the standard required for training medical graduates. The Patna Medical College Hospital, which had 40 medical and 38 surgical beds at the time, is today known as the paediatrics department.



A completely new cottage The 18-room hospital was erected near the previous cottage hospital and has been in use since August 15, 1951.



Dr. Rajendra Prasad, India's late President, was honoured with a new surgical block in 1955.



Clinical medicine is a branch of medicine that deals with patients.

overview:
ARYABHATTA KNOWLEDGE UNIVERSITY
Aryabhatta Knowledge University (AKU Patna) is a public collegiate state university in Mithapur, Bihar, India. It was named after Aryabhatta, an Indian astronomer.
AKU is the state technology university of Bihar, and it has control over technical education across the state of Bihar, with a few notable exceptions. The AICTE has recognised and approved it. It is a legal necessity for all colleges in the state that offer engineering, medical, or technology programmes to be associated with the university.

 The clean, healthy, calm, and tranquil environment creates a great backdrop for students' exceptional academic performance and patients' good recuperation.

SHREE NARAYAN MEDICAL INSTITUTE AND HOSPITAL



Shree Narayan Medical Institute & Hospital Saharsa is one of Bihar's most prestigious private medical colleges. It began in the year 2021 and is located in the Bihar district of Saharsa. 

The purpose of the Medical Faculty is to provide high-quality education and a pleasant atmosphere for students in the field of medicine.

Within the campus of this Medical College, students can stay in hostels. SNIMS Hostel is a seven-story structure with 14 rooms on each floor. 

SINMS has 56 rooms for boys, 98 rooms for girls, 34 rooms for interns, 36 rooms for residents, 8 rooms for PG students, and 16 rooms for nurses in their hostel. 

Each student has a chair and table in their Hostel room, which has two or three inhabitants, a book shelf, and cloth cupboards.

There is a reading room on each floor, as well as a lift. A visitors' room is available on the ground floor of the hostel. 

A mess facility is also available. Residential quarters with a total of 67 rooms are provided for teaching and non-teaching staff. There are two gyms available: one at the Boys Hostel and the other at the Girls Hostel.



Campus facilities-



Library-

The library covers an area of 1605 square metres. In this college, there is an air-conditioned Central Library with seating for 450 people.

 The Library's openings are 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. It includes a good variety of resources, including 7017 textbooks, 70 national periodicals, 41 foreign journals, CD/DVDs, and bound publications, among other things.



Lecture theaters-  

This college has five lecture theatres, four of which have a seating capacity of 120 students apiece. These five cinemas are all air-conditioned and have gallery-style seating. 

Another is on the hospital side, which can accommodate 150 pupils. Overhead projector, slide projector, LCD projector, and microphone are among the audio-visual aids available.

 These Lecture Halls are shared by all departments on a regular basis. There is also an E-Class option. The examination hall is 800 square metres in size. It also functions as a concert hall. This hall can accommodate 700 pupils.





Canteen-  

Inside the hospital, there is a cafeteria. Both vegetarian and non-vegetarian high-quality food is offered in a sanitary atmosphere at a reasonable price, and it is served to students, faculty, and staff.



Medical facilities-  

The college also has its own dispensary, which employs one MBBS medical practitioner and a support team, as well as first-aid kits in each department.

 If a student has a medical condition, he or she can consult the institute doctor directly. 



Sports-

A recreation area with a television, a gym, music, and indoor activities is available. Within the college grounds, students have access to a well-equipped gymnasium with skilled staff and a sports field. 

Table Tennis, Chess, BasketBall Court, Foot Ball Court, Badminton Court, Cricket, and Volleyball are among the sports offered by this institution, which also has good trainers.

In Saharsa, Bihar, India, there is a medical school and hospital called Lord Buddha Koshi Medical College and Hospital. In 2019, MCI approved 100 MBBS seats at this college. The college is situated alongside National Highway 107 between Saharsa and Madhepura.



The Lord Buddha Shiksha Pratisthan, a society (Buddhist Minority) registered under the Society Registration Act 21 of 1860, Government of Bihar, with registration number 033/2002-0, was the brainchild behind the Lord Buddha Koshi Medical College & Hospital, Saharsa.

Lecture halls-

The lecture halls (Gallery Style Seating) are furnished with an audio-visual system, LCD projectors, overhead projectors, and other instructional tools like those found in a contemporary classroom. 

The E-Class feature enables lectures to be delivered from any location in the world.



Scholarships-

The college offers scholarships provided by several State and Federal government departments. For more information, please visit the student area. According to their parent's annual income, students from disadvantaged backgrounds (OBC, SC, and ST) receive scholarships that are equivalent to or less than the tuition cost. 

This is one excellent scholarship programme offered by the government. There are numerous scholarship options, including merit- and need-based awards that take into account students' abilities and achievements. 

Economically disadvantaged sections may also request a fee reduction, but all requests must be made at the start of the academic year.

The Patna-based Al-Karim Educational Trust built and maintains Katihar Medical Institution (KM C), a medical college in Katihar, Bihar, India .

It offers undergraduate and postgraduate studies in practically all clinical and non-clinical topics.

  3. Students hostel- 



Katihar Medical College has excellent hostel facilities for both boys and girls on campus, as well as a separate hostel outside of Campus 1 at Campus 2.

Hostel management makes every effort to meet the demands of its guests in order to make them feel at ease.

Security guards with Walkie-Talkies are stationed at each hostel, as well as wardens who oversee daily activities. Wardens can be reached at any moment during the day if you want to know how your loved one is doing.



Accomodation-



Students pursuing a bachelor's degree in medicine (MBBS) should consider the following: Available for boys and girls individually for Rs. 1,25,000 (Rupees seventy-five thousand) per year instead of Rs. 75,000 (Rupees seventy-five thousand).



Students of MBBS-



268 students can be accommodated in 179 rooms in the Boys Hostel.

288 students can be accommodated at the Girls Hostel.





Campus facilities-

Accommodation

IT services

Security

Sports and fitness

Student leadership programme

Support for the differently abled

Transport



Central Library – 

The main structure houses the college library, which is approximately 17500 square feet in size. It contains comfortable reading rooms and a large collection of modern text and reference volumes on all fields of medicine, as well as 112 national and international periodicals.

The library has a total of 17664 books.

In the last three years, I've bought the following new versions of books:





Canteen-



Canteen is high quality with very good food.

Canteens are hygienic and nutritive.



Auditorium -

The auditorium is magnificent with 3D art of design.



Wi-fi -

Wi-fi facilities are available in college. In the classroom, library, labs you will get internet connection free.



Health care- 

Regardless of how much fun university life is, we recognise that it can be challenging for both staff and students to keep up. 

As a result, we have experienced counselors on campus in the psychiatric department who are constantly accessible to help employees and students who are in distress.

Staff and students are welcome to visit the department during operating hours, which are Monday through Saturday from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. 

Because the medical college and hospital are part of the institution, medical facilities are available round-the-clock for staff and students in need.



 



Security-

Security Helpline Number  -   +91 9431498192

Katihar Medical College is happy to provide a secure and welcoming environment for all students, professors, staff, and visitors. Our commitment to safety is our top priority, and Katihar Medical College Security Services—a committed team of highly trained security officers armed with Walkie-Talkies who are ready and prepared to respond to crises 24 hours a day, seven days a week—helps to support this effort.



Bank –



There is a bank facility in college.Anyone can deposit  fee in the bank by also atm facility.

table Tennis











MBBS Internship provided-  



From day one, we are encouraged to build clinical abilities, and by the end of our internship, we will have developed a clinical sense that will enable us to practise as effective clinicians outside of the classroom. We are given extra credit for attending ward posting on a regular and timely basis.

Approximately 98 percent of students have been placed. The biggest package is around one lakh, while the lowest is around fifty thousand rupees. Approximately 60% of students were offered internships. Radiology, medicine, surgery, and paediatrics are among the top positions available.

Our college's placements are excellent, with nearly all students placed in excellent positions. The minimum monthly income is Rs. 55,000, and the maximum monthly salary is Rs. 2 lacs, which is comparable to a government college.

Dissection hall-



The 5000 square foot Dissection Hall is outfitted with small and large dissection tables, as well as human section models and charts for comprehensive instruction of dissected body parts.



Central Library - 



The Central Library, which is fully air-conditioned and has 150 seats for students and a separate reading area with 50 seats for staff, is located in the College Building.



The digital library is made up of 40 computers from the most recent generation, all of which are connected to the Internet.



More than 3000 books for reading and reference, as well as 15 Indian and 6 foreign journals, are housed in the stack area.



Canteen-

Health care-



The Madhubani Medical College Hospital is a 600-bed multispecialty hospital that was constructed to give the finest possible health care to the people of Madhubani, a district that lags behind other districts in the state in terms of quality health care facilities.

The hospital has all of the major departments with the best doctors available 24 hours a day, seven days a week. It has a built-up area of over 2 lakh square feet and all of the advanced facilities such as ICU, ICCU, NICU, 24 hour Emergency, Modular Operation Theatres, Blood Bank, 24 hour Pharmacy, and Central Investigation Laboratory

As like every college this college also maintains security policy. There are gate security guards , hostel security guards and also female security guards for girls' hostels.

All the medical students passing from our college will get a job directly into a medical college, hospital, or rural PHC according to their capabilities and skill.

Different states offer varied salaries to the medical officers ranging from Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 90,000.

Each student reading from our college has to do a compulsory rotatory internship either in the college or in other medical colleges of the country.

overview:
RADHA DEVI JAGESHWARI MEMORIAL MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL
A recently built institution, Radha Devi Jageshwari Memorial Medical College, Muzaffarpur, began operations in 2021. In Muzaffarpur, the gateway to India's northeastern regions, Nepal, and Bhutan, the RADHA DEVI JAGESHWARI MEMORIAL MEDICAL COLLEGE & HOSPITAL, a significant community campus, was established.
On July 16, 2009, Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, a former Indian president, laid the foundation stone for the Muzaffarpur campus. The government recognised the contributions of this organisation.
The Institute was honoured by the President of India in 2004, who gave it a national award for being the country's best institute. The center's top aim is to increase the provision of top-notch health and educational services.

The hostel is excellent with multiple buildings. Both boys and girls hostels are there. 
The infra is excellent and installed with lift, camera all around, parking facility, Security 24*7, mess facilities, common room, College ground, A very big TV in the common room, a sweeper for cleaning daily ,and clean toilets.

The Boys hostel facility is good for girls but not that much good. We get 4 or 6 seaters in 1st year whereas boys get 2 seaters from beginning.fee is the same for all 20000.
Boys get a good room with a balcony , almirah and washroom attached.
There are 2 hostels for boys and 2 for girls.

Indoor and outdoor games, a UCO bank office, a post mortem chamber, and a police station are all available on campus.

The ANMMC Libraries offer a number of instructional activities to assist students in improving their research abilities. From library orientations to advanced research topics, instructional programmes can be tailored to match a variety of learning objectives.

The library is air-conditioned, and there are enough reading rooms for undergraduate and graduate students to study in.

It houses a substantial collection of books and journals. It has an internet connection to bring readers up to date on medical discoveries.

The library has also purchased computers for use in research. This has provided postgraduate students and faculty members with a one-of-a-kind educational resource.

overview:
JAN NAYAK KARPOORI THAKUR MEDICAL COLLEGE
The JANNAYAK KARPOORI THAKUR MEDICAL COLLEGE & HOSPITAL is located in Madhepura, near the Jahat-Singheshwar road, in its own majestic edifice with a compound of around 25 acres of land.
In a strategic and scenic location near the renowned Coconut Park Madhepura, the college provides a learning environment. Only a few colleges in India have the kind of world-class modern infrastructure that this freshly built college does.
A good education system is built on solid infrastructure. J.N.KT.M.C.H Medical College & Hospitals makes sure that students have access to the greatest facilities available.
The campus is beautiful and cleverly built, from the design of the classrooms to a well-stocked library and a well-equipped audiovisual and computer lab. The new campus design will act as a template for other plans.

Library-

"Libraries store the energy that fires the imagination,"  written by famed writer Sidney Sheldon. They offer doors to the world and encourage us to learn, grow, and contribute to the betterment of our lives."The administrative Block's second and third floors house the college library.

 It is a completely air-conditioned hall with several of the most up-to-date amenities. The college has all of the literature, journals, and furniture. There are 3187 books in the central library and 278 in the Department library.

More books and journals have been added to the central and department libraries. We are now hiring for the positions of Librarian, Assistant Librarian, and Librarian Assistant. 

They subscribe to a carefully curated range of print and online journals and periodicals, as well as online libraries with conference papers and ebooks. 



Canteen-  

"First we eat, then we do everything else," according to M.F.K. Fisher, therefore our college provides a cafeteria to meet every human being's basic requirement.

 They give sanitary and nutritious food to our kids, faculty, and employees at a very reasonable rate.

They also provide labs where students can enhance their learning experiences at their best levels.



Medical facilities-  

The college also has its own dispensary, which employs one MBBS medical practitioner and a support team, as well as first-aid kits in each department.

If a student has a medical condition. Hospital opens for 24 hours.

Sports-

Cricket, volleyball, badminton, and football are all always played on the campus's sports grounds. Students have a variety of sports uniforms and equipment.





Extracurricular Activity

Inter-College SportsInter-college sports events are a great platform for students to showcase their sporting skills and abilities. It provides a healthy competitive environment and promotes sportsmanship among the students. It also helps in building team spirit and encourages students to lead an active and healthy lifestyle.

Onam CelebrationsOnam is a traditional harvest festival celebrated in Kerala. Onam celebrations in colleges are a great way to promote the rich cultural heritage of Kerala. It involves various activities like flower decoration, traditional dance performances, and traditional food. It brings together students of different backgrounds and fosters a sense of unity and harmony.

Teachers Day CelebrationTeachers play an essential role in shaping the future of students. Teachers day celebrations in colleges provide an opportunity for students to express their gratitude towards their teachers. It involves various activities like felicitation ceremonies, cultural programs, and gift giving. It helps to strengthen the bond between teachers and students and promotes a positive learning environment.

Birthday Celebrations of Father of our NationMahatma Gandhi, the father of our nation, was an influential leader and a role model for many. His birthday celebrations in colleges are a way to honor his life and his contributions to the nation. It involves various activities like prayer meetings, speeches, and cultural programs. It helps in promoting the values of non-violence, truth, and simplicity among the students.

Bhagat Phool Singh Medical College



Bhagat Phool Singh Medical Institution is a government medical college in Khanpur Kalan, Sonepat district, Haryana, India. It is one of the state's best medical schools.

Students, employees, and townspeople are served by the hospital. Al-Falah Hospital offers emergency services around-the-clock. Emergency patient transfers to hospitals in the NCR region are always possible because of complex maintenance ambulances.
 Under the supervision of 24 hour-a-day specialists in surgery, rehabilitative medicine, ENT, orthopaedic surgery, surgical critical care, gynaecology and obstetrics, ophthalmology, and emergency medicine, there are 4 operational operating rooms with cutting-edge technology.

Library:
With a collection of more than 3518 books, national and international magazines, and 265 DVDs, the Al-Falah Medical College Center Library opened its doors in 2017. 
Now, there are more than 4420 books in the library. The collection is completely computerised and set up to encompass all significant branches of medical research. The University of Al-Center Falah's for Medical Science and Research's instructional and research endeavours are the library's main goals. 
They are building an extensive collection of documents that will be helpful to the library's users. We anticipate that this library will rise to the top of the list of regional collections.

Al Falah University, which has operated institutions on campus, including the Al-Falah School of Engineering and Technology, founded the Al-Falah Foundation. The Haryana Legislative Assembly established the university.

 However, private schooling is quite affordable. For roughly 6000 per month divided between two students. However, one can also rent apartments in densely populated areas like Delhi if they so choose.

Hostel expenses per month 7k.





Campus facilities-



Library-

With a collection of more than 3518 books, national and international magazines, and 265 DVDs, the Al-Falah Medical College Center Library opened its doors in 2017. 

Now, there are more than 4420 books in the library. The collection is completely computerised and set up to encompass all significant branches of medical research. The University of Al-Center Falah's for Medical Science and Research's instructional and research endeavours are the library's main goals. 

They are building an extensive collection of documents that will be helpful to the library's users. We anticipate that this library will rise to the top of the list of regional collections.



Other Facility-  

One of the most gorgeous campuses in India is this university's WiFi-connected campus.

 It has lecture halls, seminar rooms, dorms, canteens, cafeterias, shops, sports facilities, libraries, and laboratories.

 It also has dormitories and canteens. The seamless interplay between nature and the constructed environment is the most noticeable aspect of the property.



Blood bank-  

Based on the patients' needs for blood transfusions, such as those for delivery patients, caesarean section patients, and major operation patients For the purpose of meeting the needs, it created a blood bank with all contemporary amenities and machinery.

 To collect, store, and donate whole blood for this reason, it also received a licence from a qualified authority. The blood bank is already up and running, serving the needs of inpatient hospital patients as well as providing blood donations to needy patients at nearby clinics and hospitals.



Medical facilities-  

Under the auspices of Al-Falah Foundation, a 378-bed Super Speciality Hospital was established in 1997 to serve the requirements of students, employees, and townspeople. Al-Falah University's 72-acre campus is where the hospital is situated.

Students, employees, and townspeople are served by the hospital. Al-Falah Hospital offers emergency services around-the-clock. Emergency patient transfers to hospitals in the NCR region are always possible because of complex maintenance ambulances.

 Under the supervision of 24 hour-a-day specialists in surgery, rehabilitative medicine, ENT, orthopaedic surgery, surgical critical care, gynaecology and obstetrics, ophthalmology, and emergency medicine, there are 4 operational operating rooms with cutting-edge technology.



Laboratory-

The hospital employees can provide information about directions and services, and the front office registration counters are entirely automated. 

General Medicine, Obstetrics and Gynecology, General Surgery, Paediatrics, D.P. & Respiratory, Dermatology, Psychology, Orthopaedics, Ophthalmology, ENT / Ear, Nose and Throat, Anaesthesiology, Radiology, and Dentistry are just a few of the departments accessible through the front office.

state-of-the-art indoor facility for students and faculty. Modern gym equipment, table tennis, carom, and snooker-billiards. There is a room adjacent to the gym that has a television with digital channel hookups and seating options.

full_content:
KALPANA CHAWLA GOVERNMENT MEDICAL COLLEGE



The Kalpana Chawla Government Medical College (KCGMC) is a coed medical school in Karnal, Haryana. It was named after Indian-American astronaut Kalpana Chawla and was established in 2017.

With an annual intake of 120 seats for MBBS, KCGMC is associated with PT. B.D. Sharma University of Health Sciences and is recognised by the National Medical Commission (NMC). 





Important information regarding this college-



Location-



Address –Model Town, Karnal,Haryana 132001



Campus Map Link:

https://goo.gl/maps/1SCxkzEACM4oLFc79



Campus size- 50 acres





Reaching KCGMC-



The Kalpana Chawla Government Medical College is located in Karnal, Haryana. With a travel time of 2.4 hours from Chandigarh Airport, you can get there (122 km). 

It takes 8 minutes to walk from Karnal railway station (3.2 km) and 8 minutes to walk from Karnal New Bus Stand (4.3 km).
