a medical facility

Cafeteria and mess facilities are also offered at a reasonable cost to students.

A departmental library, as well as an air-conditioned central library and lecture theaters, are available at the college.

With 48 journal subscriptions, over 2 million books are available.

A 1600-square-meter central library with 200 seats is available.

Auditorium-

Auditorium hall of 800-person capacity provided by the college to the students for seminar and cultural fest.





Hospital-

The connected hospital of Kalpana Chawla Govt Medical College now has 317 beds, which will be enlarged to 700 in the coming years. 

The hospital is well-equipped with all necessary equipment, such as computerized tomography (CT), computerized radiography, color Doppler, automated cell counters (ACC), and so on. It also has necessary departments, such as Pre-Clinical (Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology), Para-Clinical (Community, Forensic Medicine, and so on (Orthopedics, DVL, General Medicine etc.,)

Students Hostel –



The college campus hostel provides living facilities for both faculty and students.

The mess is quite good and we have all time security in case anything happens.

 Also our hostel is ragging free campus and the infrastructure of the hostel is also good.

 For students the cost varies from 8.5k to 20k for both boys and girls with the basic amenities and lodging facilities.





 

 



Campus Facilities-



 Campus life-



The Institute has a nice and magnificent campus. Tranquil and calm surroundings gives an ideal academic environment. Separate many housing is available for Boys and Girls for Undergraduate and postgraduate students.

 Various types of housing are available for students, faculty, and other staff. Shopping Complex, Departmental stores, Chemists Shops, 24 Hrs ATM Banking, PCO/STD, Post Office and Cafeteria are within walking distance. Spacious and comfortable living is also provided to all the faculty and residents.



Library-



The Institute includes a library with 42621 books on various branches of medicine.

Aside from that, the library has roughly 150 national and international medical journals on its shelves. The Institute's personnel and students have access to the Internet at the library.

During the summer, the library is open all day.

The library is undergoing an air-conditioning project at the moment. In addition, the library's Reading Room provides a tranquil and serene environment for students to study.

The library contains two reading rooms that can accommodate 600 people each. A reading room with a capacity of 400 students is available.





Sports-



Institute has excellent facilities that are given to students for sports and recreation activities. Students participate in many sport, athletic and literary events at state and national level and win prizes for the college.

 Students run a Literary and Magazine Club (LAM) and organize a number of cultural events every year.







Events-



Notable Events includes annual college festival- Spardha, Annual sports and athletic meet, Art Exhibition, Creative Writing, Literary and Poetry recital.

 The Resident Doctors Association (RDA) organizes Diwali Mela every year. Meritorious students are nicely honored with Gold, Silver and Bronze medals for best academic performances in respective clinical and preclinical subjects at the occasion of institute convocation.

 Metamorphosis is a PGIMS community development programme aimed at enhancing cultural and social interaction and catalyzing academic growth among PGIMS community, staff and students. Mission Excellence is a programme to enhance academic & professional excellence connects all faculty, residents and students and provides a platform for academic and research interaction.

Hasan Khan, Shaheed Mewati Government Medical College is located in the Nuh district of Haryana, India, on the outskirts of Nuh in Nalhar.

overview:
MAHARAJA AGRASEN MEDICAL COLLEGE, AGROHA
The Maharaja Agrasen Medical College, Agroha (MAMC) is a medical college in Agroha TAKEN to Pt. B.D. Sharma University of Health Sciences in  Rohtak and established by the Medical Council of India. It is named NAME OF a legendary Indian king of Agroha in India Maharaja Agrasen.
The college was located by Om Prakash Jindal while his wife Savitri Jindal is the current president of the society that governs the institute.
The Society was established at Agroha at the date of 18 April 1988 to provide health care services, medical education and research facilities to more than 2 crore population living in Haryana and joining states of Rajasthan, Punjab and Himachal Pradesh to fulfil the socialist ideals of the Maharaja Agrasen Ji.

The Institute's personnel and students have access to the Internet through the library. 

During the summer, the library is open from 8.30 a.m. to 12 p.m., while during the winter, it is open from 9.00 a.m. to 11 p The library is undergoing an air-conditioning project at the moment. 

In addition, the library's Reading Room provides a tranquil and serene environment for students to study.

Auditorium- 

In college there is a large auditorium and so many seats are also available.



Sports-

The institute boasts great sports and entertainment facilities. Students compete in state and national sporting, athletic, and literary activities, winning prizes for the college. Students run a literary journal (Srijan) and host a variety of cultural activities throughout the year.

Other facilities-

The Institute has a stunning and lovely campus. The serene and tranquil settings make for a perfect learning atmosphere.

For undergraduate students, separate housing is available for boys and girls. Students, instructors, and other personnel can choose from a variety of housing options.















A shopping complex, pharmacies, and PCO/STD are all within walking distance. All academics and residents have access to spacious and comfortable living quarters.

overview:
MAHARISHI MARKANDESHWAR INSTITUTE OF SCIENCES AND RESEARCH
Maharishi Markandeshwar Institute of Medical Sciences and Research is located in Mullana, Haryana, India, 36 kilometres from Ambala. [3] It is associated with Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar University and is accredited by the Indian Medical Council. A legislative act passed by the Haryana government established the school.
The university offers medical education to undergraduates (MBBS) and postgraduates (MD–MS degree).
The campus is part of Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar University. A hospital of 1020 beds, including 830 teaching beds, is part of the institute. Intensive care units with all resuscitation and monitoring equipment are available at the hospital. There are ICUs dedicated to neonates and coronary care. All types of procedures, including neurosurgery, urology, paediatric surgery, and endoscopic surgery, are performed in the hospital's well-equipped operating rooms.

full_content:
MAHARISHI MARKANDESHWAR INSTITUTE OF SCIENCES AND RESEARCH 





Maharishi Markandeshwar Institute of Medical Sciences and Research is located in Mullana, Haryana, India, 36 kilometres from Ambala. [3] It is associated with Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar University and is accredited by the Indian Medical Council. A legislative act passed by the Haryana government established the school. 



The university offers medical education to undergraduates (MBBS) and postgraduates (MD–MS degree). 

The campus is part of Mullana's Maharishi Markandeshwar University. A hospital of 1020 beds, including 830 teaching beds, is part of the institute. Intensive care units with all resuscitation and monitoring equipment are available at the hospital. There are ICUs dedicated to neonates and coronary care. All types of procedures, including neurosurgery, urology, paediatric surgery, and endoscopic surgery, are performed in the hospital's well-equipped operating rooms.

All the hostel rooms shall be furnished with beds, study tables, chairs and cupboards with ceiling fans and lights. Special facilities like air conditioners to be used on extra payment basis shall also be provided.

 The students interested in installing air conditioners will have to apply for consideration. Besides, for all its residents, the Hostel shall take off a common dining hall, a common recreation room with a T.V., incoming call facility, a Kitchen and common bathrooms. Geysers shall be given in all bathrooms.

Hostel accommodation shall be provided for once a year .



Campus facilities-

Infrastructure- 

Lecture halls, labs, libraries, a 500-bed hospital, faculty offices, seminar halls, conference halls, an auditorium, and facilities for sports and extracurricular activities are all part of the University's infrastructure.

Each classroom is spacious, well-lit, and equipped with cutting-edge multimedia teaching aids.

Wi-Fi is accessible across the campus. The university has constructed a state-of-the-art fully air-conditioned library with internet access for students, as well as a collection of national and international journals.

Transport Services-

The College has a fleet of approximately 15 buses that transport students and personnel on various routes throughout the NCR and its environs. Guests of the hostel can also use public transportation to get around the city. Students are charged the actual fees.

Seminar Hall-

The Seminar Hall is a perfect location for seminars and medical professionals to speak and provide lectures to our college's students in order to give them a better understanding of how their various areas work. 

These conferences and lectures not only provide students with first-hand information on how various fields work, but they also allow them to ask questions of our speakers, which allows them to clear up any issues they may have. 

The guests first give a talk about their work, the obstacles they confront, the difficulties they overcome, and so on, and then the students are given the opportunity to ask questions and clear any misconceptions they may have.



Play Grounds-

The College offers a variety of athletic activities and encourages students to participate in them in order to assure their overall growth. Basket Ball, volleyball, football, table tennis, cricket, and other sports have access to all facilities and playgrounds.

When a book is supplied for reading in the reading room or at home, the student must inspect it to ensure that it is not damaged or dirty, and that no pages are missing or torn. overview:
PT. JAWAHAR LAL NEHRU GOVERNMENT MEDICAL COLLEGE AND HOSPITAL
In 2017, the Pt. Jawahar Lal Nehru Government Medical College and Hospital in Chamba, which is located in the Chamba district, was promoted to a Medical College. Chamba is a historic town with a vibrant atmosphere, beautiful landscape, and a wonderful location on the banks of the Ravi River, some 120 kilometers from Pathankot.
It has a mild climate and a rich cultural heritage and history. The Distt. Hospital (Shyam Singh) was founded in 1890.

There a dining room in each hostel subscribes to a large number of magazines, periodicals and newspapers.

Each hostel also has a multipurpose common room, a TT room, carom and a gym, a volleyball court, football ground or a badminton court, depending on the hostel you find yourself in, along with a multi-purpose playground.

full_content:
GOVT MEDICAL COLLEGE SRINAGAR



The Government Medical College, Srinagar (Urdu: ) (Koshur: ) is a government medical school and hospital in Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir. It is also known by the acronym GMC Srinagar. In 1959, it was founded. 

The oldest medical school in Kashmir is there. The Medical Council of India has approved the college and hospital, which are associated with the University of Kashmir (currently National Medical Commission).

Students Hostel-  



Three students are housed in excellent, well-ventilated rooms; upkeep is your responsibility. This college's hostel is located in Bemina Srinagar, and it offers transportation services. Separate dorms are available for UG and PG students.

Hostel expenses per month 7k.





Campus facilities-



Library-

The college library has around 18000 books, subscriptions to over 270 international and national publications, and more than 14000 bound journals. 

Since the library is computerised with the SLIM software management system, any publication may be found and retrieved immediately.



Infrastructure-

The 46 acre GMC Srinagar campus includes a medical college, hospitals, and a dormitory and residential complex. Within a 5km radius, the entire campus is divided into seven sections.



Examination centre-

A total of seven examination halls, each with a capacity for 250 people, are located on the college site. 

Lecture theatre-

Both the College and the Hospital have the Lecture Theatre 07nos and o3nos, respectively. All of the theatres are gallery-style with seating for 180–250 people and excellent AV equipment.

Cultural facilities-  

The official student organisation at GMC Srinagar is called CASS (Cultural, Academic, Social, and Sports) Welfare Union.



The aforementioned four subcommittees are led by their respective Chairpersons and include faculty members.



Every year, the CASS holds general elections in December to choose the President and Vice President as well as the secretaries of each committee.

full_content:
JAWAHARLAL INSTITUTE OF POSTGRADUATE MEDICAL EDUCATION AND RESEARCH

                                                                              



The Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education & Research (JIPMER) which is a medical college situated at Pondicherry that is the capital of the Union Territory of Puducherry, in India. 

JIPMER is an Institute of National Importance (INI) and it is a tertiary care referral hospital which is under the direct administrative control of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India with autonomy .





Important information regarding this college - 



Location-



Address- Jipmer Campus Rd, Gorimedu, Priyadarshini Nagar, Puducherry, 605006



Campus map link-

https://maps.app.goo.gl/vi9yFTqDDxvUCr1B9





Campus size- 192 acres







Reaching JIPMER Pondicherry -



Puducherry railway station is the nearest railway station which is 6 km away from the campus and takes 7 minutes to reach.



Medical Courses offered by this college -



Courses

Eligibility

Streams offered

10+2

INI-CET

General Surgery

Student HHostels-



Including new hostels, there are a total of 8 hostels and this separate hostels for girls and boys and categorized into ug, bachelor pg and married pg hostels with great physician's names.

There will be double rooms which charge 9k per month and single rooms which charge 6k per month.

 For each student there will be one table, one chair and two cupboards.

With 2 TV rooms with a LCD TV in each of them there are 5 floors with 2 reading areas and a reading room on each floor. 













Campus facilities-



These are Facilities that are offered to the students who are interested to study here -

Sports facilities and gym-



Three basketball courts, a tennis court, a table tennis hall, and a social gathering area are all available. A well-known common blunder. 

There are also separate gyms for guys and girls. 





Central library-



 SC/ST scholarship students of JIPMER from all branches shall be granted books under this plan on a first-come, first-served basis if they apply in the specified format to the Library.

Depending on book availability and demand, each eligible candidate will be given a set of books.

Depending on the number of semesters in each year, each set is provided for a maximum of semester completion per year.

After the yearly exams, the books must be returned, and a no-due form from the Librarian must be presented before the next year's book purchase.

The Central Library is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week.







Wi-fi - 

Free wifi available for students and stuff.

In the hostel it has high speed and you can use wi-fi from anywhere on campus.



Canteen-



There are numerous messes in this area, including the common mess, Harvey's mess, kids' mess, and cafeterias.

You can either pay the entire month's mess costs and eat all three meals in the same mess, or you can pay separately for each meal and dine in different messes.

You can also switch up your mess on a monthly basis. That's what impressed me the most, because there won't be any repetition..





Health-care-



Outpatients with a monthly income of less than Rs. 2500 are not charged.

JIPMER students and Compulsory House Surgeons will be eligible for class C housing, and there will be no costs (including food).

 Except for diet expenses, Junior Residents will be entitled to free care in 'C'.

Physical education-

The Outdoor and indoor Facilities:

 Basketball court

Tennis court

Table tennis court

Lawn tennis court

Football playground

 Campus facilities-



Sports facilities and gym-

Central library- 



Indira Gandhi Medical College has a good and comfortable reading library in a five-story structure with 5000 books.

It has subscriptions to roughly 28 Indian and eight foreign periodicals, as well as 40 computer terminals with internet access.



Wi-fi -

laboratories and classrooms– 



The Laboratories are very well equipped.

 Classrooms were wide, air-conditioned and equipped with projectors and benches, but there was no Wi-Fi facility.



Canteen- 



The food available there is quite good, not so bad.

 Both North and South Indian menus are available.

 North Indian tiffin walas are also available, who provide home quality food.





Hospital  -



The hospital is now operating with 415 beds on the ground and second floors, with plans to expand to three floors in the future. The hospital is a state-of-the-art structure with 750 beds.

In the Teaching and Practical Side, we have well-trained faculty with excellent research and academic backgrounds.

Mahatma Gandhi Medical College and Research Institute

                                  



Mahatma Gandhi Medical College and Research Institute (MGMCRI) is a medical college and hospital situated  in the Bahour taluk which is the union territory of Puducherry, India. 

The institute is opened by the institution of Sri Balaji Educational and Charitable Public Trust (SBACPT) and taken by Shri M.K. Rajagopalan who is chairman of the group.

It has been ranked 21th among medical colleges in India by NIRF ranking.

The nearest Pondicherry Railway, a railway station, is roughly 15 km away and a 30-minute drive from the Medical College.

Hostel accommodation to all the students admitted to any course in the Institution is mandatory because of maintaining regularity, standard of medical education and discipline.

There is  air conditioning, cushioned seats, proper lighting, water supply, canteen, wifi with high speed data, fully updated library and laboratories.

Hostel fees per month Rs 14500 per month and AIQ reallocation fees Rs 10000.









Campus facilities-



Sports facilities and gym-



It has an open playground cricket and football pitches, indoor game facilities and badminton courts.

There is a communal lounge area, mini theater and gym.





Central library-



The library is located in an elegant multi-story structure with a carpet area of 4,400 square meters and eight reading rooms that are conveniently accessible to staff and students on campus.

There are access to more than 2500 e-journals and 10000 books.

Library is open for all day except national holidays.

In the library you will get a modern seminar hall, digital library and e-learning center.







Wi-fi – 

There is free wifi in the hostel, campus, and library everywhere.

Free internet for students, staff and professors. 



Cafeteria-



The campus has a separate mess for girls and boys both and apart from the general mess, the campus has 2 canteens. 

The cafeteria presents nutritious food and beverages at a reasonable price.





Faculty- 

Teachers are qualified, helpful, and knowledgeable in this setting. They use pre-clinical education, PowerPoint presentations, and paraclinical sessions with patients. 

In the last year, students are mostly exposed to clinical learning in the hospital, which includes examining patients and learning various examination techniques. 

The students will learn basic first aid, how to receive and handle a patient, and much more as part of the course curriculum.



Health-care - 



The Mahatma Gandhi Medical College and Research Hospital is a 1380-bed super speciality hospital that was established in 2001. It is the first Knob-accredited teaching hospital in Puducherry.

It provides emergency services as well as 24-hour full-time duty consultants in all specialties, including catering for trauma patients, neurosurgeons, orthopedic and general surgeons, plastic surgeons, and others.

SRI VENKATESHWARA MEDICAL COLLEGE HOSPITAL & RESEARCH CENTER, PONDICHERRY  

                    



The Sri Venkateswaraa Medical College Hospital and Research Center (SKMCH&RC) is a private medical college and hospital in Puducherry, India, that is accredited by the Medical Council of India, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare of the Government of India, and the Government of Puducherry .

The nearest railway station is Tirupati, which is 2.6 kilometers distant from the college that takes 6 minutes.

 In addition, the nearest airport to this college is Tirupati International Airport, which is 19 kilometers distant and can be reached in 40 minutes by taking NH71.

Student Hostels-



Boys and Girls have separate Premium and Regular Hostels.

College, Hospital, and Playground are all close by.

Attached balcony Rooms with centralized air conditioning are available in single, double, and triple configurations.Each hostel has a 100-seat Mess facilities.

Game rooms, recreation rooms, and a communication system are all provided.

Continuity of power and water supply

The campus offers a well-kept, sanitary canteen that serves a range of menus, including Indian and Chinese cuisines.

The college has its own garden with a wide range of plants and trees.A contemporary pond and an efficient rainwater gathering system support a big wastewater treatment facility at the college.











4   Campus facilities-



Museum and gym-



College has an excellent gym for students to stay healthy and fit. For boys and girls it has been separated.

It is one of the only colleges here where we can get to see museums.





Central library-



Library gives 12 hours service with supporting staff (8.30 am to 8.30 pm)

There is an e-library and browsing section with 40 computers present.

Anyone can get old books, semester books, journal sections with foreign and Indian journals with back volume.

There are separate departmental libraries.







Wi-fi – 



There is free wifi available  in hostels and on campus also .



Mess and Cafeteria-



There are 100 seated mess facilities in each hostel.

Campus always provides a well maintained, hygienic canteen which gives so many menu items including Indian and chinese cuisines.





Hospital-



The following medical departments will share these hospital beds.

General medicine has 220 beds, general surgery has 217 beds, paediatrics and Obstetrics and Gynecology has 150 beds, and orthopaedics has 137 beds.

In the departments of TB and Respiratory Medicine, Psychiatry, Dermatology, Ophthalmology, and ENT, there are almost 220 beds available

 



Sports-



Near the Hostel, there is a sports complex with facilities for football, cricket, basketball, and volleyball. 

There is also an exclusive Badminton court and Table tennis court within the complex. 

The second phase, which includes a gym, tennis court, and swimming pool, will be constructed in stages.



5. Internship provided-





Subjects

Compulsory time duration for Internship

Community Medicine.

Two months

Medicine including 15 days of Psychiatry

Two months

Two months

One month

E.N.T

15 days

Ophthalmology

15 days

15 days

15 days

12 months

21000 rupee Per month

overview:
GOVERNMENT DOON MEDICAL COLLEGE DEHRADUN (UTTARAKHAND)
The Government Doon Medical College in Dehradun is committed to improving the general health and well-being of Uttarakhand's residents by providing comprehensive patient care to the last patient in line, who come not only from Dehradun's immediate vicinity but also from far-flung parts of the state.
Govt Doon Medical is a new medical education and patient care centre that aspires to attain the greatest standards in education, training, research, and patient care.

overview:
HIMALAYAN INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES
The Himalayan Institute of Medical Sciences (HIMS), the state's first medical school, was founded in December 1995 with the goal of becoming a centre of excellence for teaching and training medical students.
The goal of HIMS is to develop morally upright medical professionals who are prepared to manage the nation's healthcare system. 150 MBBS and 120 MD/MS, Post Graduate students can be admitted each year.
The institute also provides paramedical programmes, PhD programmes, fellowship programmes, and super specialty programmes (M.Ch. in Surgical Oncology, M.Ch. in Neurosurgery, and DM in Neurology).

full_content:
HIMALAYAN INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES



The Himalayan Institute of Medical Sciences (HIMS), the state's first medical school, was founded in December 1995 with the goal of becoming a centre of excellence for teaching and training medical students.

 The goal of HIMS is to develop morally upright medical professionals who are prepared to manage the nation's healthcare system. 150 MBBS and 120 MD/MS, Post Graduate students can be admitted each year. 

The institute also provides paramedical programmes, PhD programmes, fellowship programmes, and super specialty programmes (M.Ch. in Surgical Oncology, M.Ch. in Neurosurgery, and DM in Neurology).

Campus facilities-



Canteen-

The on-site canteens deliver wholesome, hygienically prepared cuisine so that students can affordably eat like at home. 





Library-  

The four faculty library facilities at the Medical Institute, Management School, School of Engineering and Technology, and Nursing College of SRHU offer some of the greatest library services among private universities in India.

To support courses in medicine, paramedicine, and nursing, the university's central library, with a total seating capacity of 425 and a floor space of about 2400 square metres, was founded in 1992. It has now been operational for 25 years, with a monograph collection of over 17,000, over 1,000 international journals (mainly electronic journals), and 119 Indian periodicals. Over 5400 earlier journal volumes make up its extensive collection.



ATM Facility-  

SBI's fully functional branch on the college campus offers all types of banking operations for employees and students and has a core banking network, ATMs, and Internet banking capabilities.



Gymnesium-  

While in college, students have plenty of opportunities to cultivate healthy behaviours.



Transport facilities-  

The 59 vehicles in the wealthy transport department's fleet include 17 luxury buses, 7 ambulances, an internal workshop, and a gas station. The university provides transportation services for staff and students to go from their homes to the campus and back.



Guest house-

The University keeps the Guest House's air-conditioned rooms with the most luxurious boarding and lodging amenities. Parents and visitors can use the facilities for a small fee.

overview:
SHRI GURU RAM INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL AND HEALTH SCIENCES
The current Sajjada Nashin Sri Mahant Devendra Das Ji, the successor of the highest seat of the Udaseen Sect, catapulted Shri Guru Ram Rai Institute of Medical & Health Sciences, Dehradun, to success.
He named the institution after Sri Guru Ram Rai ji Maharaj, the eldest son of Sri Har Raiji, the seventh Guru of the Sikhs. Shri Guru Ram Rai Ji constructed his DERA in Doon Valley, which later became known as DEHRA, and hence Dehradun owes its name and history to Sri Guru Ram Raiji's "Dera" in 1676 in the area that is today known as Dehradun.

campus_facilities:
The institute's affiliated teaching hospital is the 200-bed "Govardhan Tiwari Government Base Hospital Almora," which is currently being expanded to 500 beds. The hospital is located about 7 kilometres from the campus of the medical institution and spans an area of 17.13 acres (6.85 hectares).
 For 100 MBBS admissions for the 2019–20 academic year, the institute has requested a letter of clearance from the Medical Council of India.

Library:
There are reading rooms open 24 hours a day, and this central library's outdoor reading room can accommodate. 
Staff rooms and reading areas are separate and available. 
There are 36,184 books available in all. 
There are 34 foreign journals and 72 Indian publications available.
overview:
SOBAN SINGH JEENA GOVERNMENT INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCE AND RESEARCH
The Uttarakhand government is committed to offering modern medical facilities to those who live in the state's isolated highlands. Following the successful establishment of State Medical Institutions in Haldwani (Nainital), Srinagar (Garhwal), and Dehradun over the past ten years, the State Government decided to open a second medical facility with a capacity of 100 MBBS admissions annually in the district of Almora in the Kumaun Hills of the State.
This facility will be known as the "Soban Singh Jeena Government Institute of Medical Sciences and Research Almora." Government of India sponsorship is provided to the institute.

SOBAN SINGH JEENA GOVERNMENT INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCE AND RESEARCH





The Uttarakhand government is committed to offering modern medical facilities to those who live in the state's isolated highlands. Following the successful establishment of State Medical Institutions in Haldwani (Nainital), Srinagar (Garhwal), and Dehradun over the past ten years, the State Government decided to open a second medical facility with a capacity of 100 MBBS admissions annually in the district of Almora in the Kumaun Hills of the State.



 This facility will be known as the "Soban Singh Jeena Government Institute of Medical Sciences and Research Almora." Government of India sponsorship is provided to the institute.



Important information regarding by this college-

Location: 



Address- pandey khola down, below Vikas Bhawan, near Vikas Bhawan, Almora, Uttarakhand 263620



Campus map link-

https://goo.gl/maps/ouH8Jxd2QR2cYcDf7



Campus size: 25 acres









Reaching SSJIMSR-



The college is only about 1.48 kilometres from the HDW HALDWANI railway station and can be reached in about 4 minutes.

Students Hostel-  



Two people will share a single room that will be provided. A single room for one may be obtained during the last year. All three meals will be provided by the hostel canteen. Aside from the hostel, there are a lot of single rooms near the hospital.

Hostel expenses per month 12k.





Campus facilities-



Library-

There are reading rooms open 24 hours a day, and this central library's outdoor reading room can accommodate. 

Staff rooms and reading areas are separate and available. 

There are 36,184 books available in all. 

There are 34 foreign journals and 72 Indian publications available.





Medical facilities-  

The institute's affiliated teaching hospital is the 200-bed "Govardhan Tiwari Government Base Hospital Almora," which is currently being expanded to 500 beds. The hospital is located about 7 kilometres from the campus of the medical institution and spans an area of 17.13 acres (6.85 hectares).

 For 100 MBBS admissions for the 2019–20 academic year, the institute has requested a letter of clearance from the Medical Council of India.



Mission-

The Soban Singh Jeena Government Institute of Medical Sciences and Research (SSJGIMSR) in Almora works to fulfil the government of Uttarakhand's mission to provide high quality, comprehensive health care that meets the needs of the people in Uttarakhand. It does this by providing excellence in medical education and training, research, and health-care leadership.

This college has triumphed in numerous state and national tournaments, winning championship shields in the process.
